From back of the box:
Each player is a dealer in new and used cars.  Players compete with each other to make car sales. There is a lot of friendly Wheeling and Dealing as players try to earn enough money to buy the Ultimate Car.

A roll and move game where players aim to earn enough money to purchase a BMW and have it complete a lap of lap of the board.  The includes opportunities to interfere with trades and sales other players are attempting to make.  This represents the car traders competing to get a sale by offering a discount.

A fascinating educational game that tells you all about the ecology and life history of our extraordinary Australian frogs (description from the back of the game board)

The game is a roll and move.  On about every third space there are instructions to follow (eg "Hot weather.  Have a rest. Miss a turn.", "Jump for joy.  You have found a mate already.  Advance to 'Egg-laying lilypad.'", "DANGER  You are eaten by a yabbie.  Return to start.").

Sometimes the instructions are to take a "Tadpole" or a "Frog" card.  These cards have a similar range of penalties and benefits as the board spaces with instructions.  The frog cards are much larger than the tadpole cards, in full colour and include some factual information about frogs on them.  A couple require the player to imitate a frog's call (described on the card) to receive the benefit.

There are two places where players get a choice of paths but other than that there is no choice in movement.

Party quiz game about (mainly) pop stars. Each of the 55 large format (11.5 x 8 cm) cards gives five facts about a particular pop star. A player rolls a dice and is read the clue that corresponds to the number rolled. If they correctly identify the pop star, they win the card. The sixth fact on each card is the name of the pop star, so if they roll a 6 they automatically win the card. The game is won by the first player to win 5 cards. There are 9 special power cards that allow, for example, an extra clue or the opportunity to 'steal' a card. These are kept by the 'reader' who picks them up.

description:

The game ends when one player has played all their wagons, but any player may call the game with 3 or fewer wagons, which may give the advantage of stopping other players from completing tickets.

boardgame: Murder Mystery Party: Murder in the Baths
description:
Who Killed Devius Taxus?

The year is 50 AD and the place is Ancient Rome. The last few days have seen the best Roman athletes, chariot racers and gladiators entertaining the vast crowds in the Colosseum and Circus Maximus. The Thermal Baths in Rome will be the venue for a lavish feast at which the cream of Rome's political and sporting societies will bathe and then feast. However, before the feast has begun Senator Devius Taxus is found dead in the plunge pool. Host your own party to discover how he came to meet such a steamy end.

Zogar's Gaze (2nd Edition) takes what worked well in the original version and adds new mechanics to improve the gaming experience for all!

Players take turns going to the market to buy items to equip their heroes with. Then they delve into dungeon one card at a time. Death is around every corner, but so is fame and fortune. How deep will you go on your turn? Will you risk it all for a possible pile of gold? Zogar, the evil wizard, is also watching over your every move. Attract too much attention and he will reveal himself, ending your turn, and possibly the game!

One of the over 50 implementations of the Box Quiz series, this one with the theme 'TV'.

The game consists of a tin box with 50 questions and answers within a category, here about TV. It is a small and simple quiz game, where players simply answer the different questions.

SHOOTERZ Hockey Card Game is fast-paced, head-2-head fun! 
Players pick through a special deck looking for combinations to shoot for a goal, while trying to keep their players out of the sin bin. Big goals and big saves!
 There are two teams, red vs. blue. The jerseys are centres, wingers and defence. Your team must collect one of each position to shoot for a goal. However, your opponent might be able to block the shot if his/her team jerseys have a higher total number.  There are special shooting and goalie cards for each shot. Do you know your opponent's tendencies?

Each time through the deck is a full period, with OT and shootout rules. GAME ON!

This is a card game, a variation of the English game Pairs (known as Memo or Memory in Europe) but has unique twists on that type of game. The game was given away in Quality Street boxes of chocolates in 2005.

Object of the game: To finish with property worth more than any other player.

The playing cards are placed at random face down on the table. Each player should also start the game with 7 Quality Street sweets!

Starting with the youngest, the players turn over two cards. If the property on the two cards is identical, the player keeps the cards and turns over two others. When two cards which do not match are revealed, they are turned over face down again, and the player’s turn is over.

Hidden in the playing cards are a number of ‘Community Chest’ and ‘Chance’ cards. If revealed, the player accepts their reward or fine, and if it is the first card turned over, that player still turns over a second card as part of their turn. These cards are removed from the table once revealed.

There is one ‘Go to Jail’ card. If this is revealed that player’s turn is over (even if it is the first card turned), and they must miss their next turn. Unlike the Chance and Community Chest cards, this card stays on the table, so you are wise to remember where it is!

The cards have a value on them (related to real values on a traditional English Monopoly board). So it is not the one with the most cards who wins, it is the one with the highest value of cards that wins.

boardgame: The Lord of the Ice Garden: Vuko Tiles Promo
description: Some promo tiles for the base game with special actions.

A fun and simple geo-themed game, where players put their tokens on a map, trying to be the nearest from the monument shown on the card. But beware, i bet you're not on the good place !

First, players draw a card from a pile, that displays a monument, a famous building, or anything else related to a specific place, sometimes accompanied by a flag.

The players should guess where in Europe this place is. The first player put his meeple where he think the answer is, on the European map/board. The next player take his turn and must do the same.

When all players have placed their markers, the card is turned over, revealing the right position. Now, using the benchmarks along the edges of the map, the players should deduct how far they are distant from the solution. The nearest player from this place score more points. If 2 or more player's markers are on the same position, the token that is under the others scores more points.

Then a new card is revealed. Game ends after 12 cards revealed.

Crazy Heroes is a game where players have to collect heroes and criatures to make the greatest team.

Each character gives the player a type of dice, that can use in the future combats.

Collect gold from your heroes and use it to buy more, or fight against creature to capture it and join it to your team. Get a team of 10 heroes (or creatures) to win the game.

It's Kelly's Birthday, but the presents have gotten all mixed up! Help Kelly match the presents to the friends who brought them. This game is part Memory and part set matching.

Includes 20 Friends Cards with the matching present shown in the corner; 20 Gifts for Kelly: 4 yellow stars, 4 pink hearts, 4 blue flowers and 4 purple squares with different gifts inside; 2 Kelly Cards; and instruction sheet.

Cards are dealt out evenly to players. If a player is dealt a Kelly card, she places it face up beside her and is dealt another card so that each player has the same number of Friends Cards.

The presents are turned upside down in the center. Players take turns turning over a present to see if it matches the present on one of the Friends cards she holds. If it does, she shows it to everyone along with the card and sets the two aside. If it does not, she shows the other players what is inside the present and turns it back over. If a player has a Kelly card, she can use it to turn over all the presents of one shape until she finds the present she is looking for. The Kelly card is then turned over and cannot be used again. The winner is the first to find all the presents matching their Friends cards.

A second way to play is to turn the presents upside down in the center and put the rest of the cards in a draw pile. Player turns one card over and tries to find the matching present. If the present matches the player takes the Friends card & present and sets them aside and turns over the next card. If the present does not match, the next player gets to turn over a present and try to match the card, continuing until a player matches the present to the card. If a Kelly card is turned over, the player turns the next Friends card over and uses the Kelly card to help her turn over the presents until she finds a match. Winner is the player with the most matches.

Monster Allegiance is a strategic fast-paced card game which challenges 2-4 players to compete for majority control over a horde of powerful and often unpredictable monsters. Tilt the balance of power in your favor and hold it until the end of the game, and you win! But beware... These are not mindless minions, but proud and dangerous creatures. Defeat an opponent’s monster, and you’ll earn it’s allegiance. But if one falls to your opponent… you’ll lose its respect and it’ll turn against you! As a Monster Commander, you must strategically decide when and where to place each monster, how to capitalize on the elemental strengths and weaknesses of each, and how to turn the tide with powerful item and element cards. Enter the Monster’s arena, but keep your mind sharp…. for if it dulls, your usefulness to the monsters will swiftly end...

-From Kickstarter description

Features:
-Beautiful colorful pixel art monsters.
-Simple but streategic battles- decided by number comparisons on 4 sides. 
-Playable with competitive personal decks or a single deck for balanced play
-Unique twist - All monsters brought into play by either player can swap teams. 
-Durable card material
-Multiple games to play - new game instructions released regularly. 
-Future expansions with guaranteed content - No random boosters required to obtain all cards.

boardgame: Rainbow Brite: A Color Matching Game
description:
Players spin & move around the board, collecting different color sprinkles and trying to fill their rainbow card with each color. Beware of Murky Dismall, who will take your sprinkles away. The first player to fill his rainbow card with color sprinkles, wins the game!

Comes with 4 Tokens, Spinner, 24 Color Sprinkles, 4 Rainbow Cards, Gameboard & Instruction Sheet.

Publisher's summary:

忍ジャン!! (Ninjan!!) is a children's card game from さとーふぁみりあ (Sato Familie). "This game is a fighting game between ninja. Young children can play this game. It is possible to rise quickly." The game is for two players.

The time is the Sengoku (Warring States) period. While Busho military commanders engage in fierce battles on history's center stage, ninjas risk their lives for their commanders. When two ninjas meet, one of them must die, because whoever discovers their identify shall not stay alive.

This is a one-on-one ninja battle game. The player on the offense plays Attack cards from his hand. The player on the defense guards against the attack with his cards. The player who manages to exhaust his opponent's Strength deck wins the game.
publishers: さとーふぁみりあ (Sato Familie)

description:
"It is known that from time to time Orcs perform races with steam cars, aka "Goratank Racing". Racers, riding pimped machines, run till death on a crazy race. The winner gets a championship title, a special "Trophy-Gun", fame and money. This competition is also known as "Orcorrida" (SpeedOrc)." - Lyzarbhi Chronicles

Orcorrida is a card game where you´re an Orc on a deadly race that takes place at the Fields of Gore World. Prove your courage and driving skills on a competition where anything goes. During the race, Orcs release all their wildness and skills with killing machines in an exciting competition on tracks through Orc Island.

It's a 3-6 player game where laughthing and fun is guaranteed.

Game Components: 54 cards and 1 "pole position" token

Game comes in 3 languages: Portuguese, English and Orcish

You're an orc racer and you have to play "road cards" to create your "path for victory". You start with 5 cards and use one of them at a time to make an action. You may have an "Orcrook" card to difficult the race: missiles, mines, bump actions or turbo to increase car speed are some of the tricks you can use against the other players.
"Road cards" have point values and you must play them to run for victory. The player with the biggest number of points is the Champion of this crazy race.

From Mancala World:

Alemungula is a mancala game played by the Wetawit (formerly spelled Wetaweat) in Ethiopia, around the towns of Asosa and Beni Sangul, towards the Sudan border. Wetawit is an ethnic group in Ethiopia and Sudan. They speak Berta, a Nilo-Saharan language. The population of this group is about 248,000.

The game is played mainly by youngsters, though sometimes also by adults in their leisure time. It is closely related to Um el Bagara ("the cow game") which is played by the Baggara in northern Sudan. However, Alemungula offers bigger challenges because singletons may also be moved.

Alemungula was first described by the ethiopist Richard Pankhurst in 1971. The game was implemented by Arty Sandler for online play at igGameCenter on December 19, 2010.

Rules

Alemungula is played on a wooden board which consists of 2x5=10 holes known as tisiya alemungula ("alemungula wood"), while the holes are called ful alemungula. Play is begun with five stones in each hole.

At his turn, a player picks up all the contents of one of his holes and then distributes, one by one, the stones into the following holes, the direction depending on the hole chosen. Moves from either of a player's two left-hand holes are made in clockwise direction, moves from either of the player's two right-hand holes are made in an anti-clockwise direction, and moves from the center hole may be made in either direction. The move is finished after a single lap.

A singleton which is played into an empty hole of the opponent's side, that is either the last or the first hole of the opponent's row), may not be played back in the next move.

If the last stone is dropped in one of the opponent's holes and makes a two or a four, these stones are captured. Should one or more of the opponent's holes immediately preceding this on the line of play, also have been increased to two or four stones these will likewise be captured.

The captured stones are removed from the board and stored.

The game ends when a player has no legal move. Each player then captures the stones remaining on his side.

The winner is the player who captured the greater number of stones during the game. If both players captured the same number the game is a draw.

Can you stand on your shoulders or hold a steady stance like a warrior? Increase flexibility and focus as you practice 48 yoga poses in this fun and simple card game. Your goal in Yoga Cards: Be the first player to correctly hold seven yoga poses that meet your mission and you win!

Your Goal: Collect the most pairs of matching Yoga Pose Tiles.

Beginner (Ages 3 and up)
Learn yoga poses as you search for matching pairs. Find the most matching pairs and - YOU WIN!

Advanced (Ages 6 and up)
After you find a matching pair, perform the illustrated yoga pose. Correctly perform the most yoga poses and - YOU WIN!

boardgame: Blood Before Richmond: Glendale & White Oak Swamp
description:
The second title in the Blood Before Richmond series recreates the Battle of Glendale, and its artillery-only side-show, White Oak Swamp.

The Union host is heading for Malvern Hill, a superior defensive position that will allow them to escape destruction and encirclement. If Lee wants to achieve those aims, he needs to cut off that retreat at a place called Glendale, which requires the careful coordination of his generals. But, par for the course, only A.P. Hill and Longstreet seem capable of doing what they’re told: Ben Huger and his men are too busy moonlighting as lumberjacks, while Stonewall Jackson fights a pointless artillery duel at White Oak Swamp - when he’s not napping, anyway. Difficult terrain and fresh, high-quality Union brigades mean that the weary Confederates have their work cut out for them. The optional Galena Rule gives the Union command-control problems of their own to contend with.

Like other titles in the series, a  modified 2d6 die roll determines which Confederate divisions activate on a given turn. Divisions that didn't pull there weight are less likely to show up than those that did most of the fighting. It's a simple, clever, and elegant way to recreate the difficulties that Lee had during the Seven Days.

Blood Before Richmond builds on the same "stacks of steps" combat system introduced in Tom Russell's Blood on the Alma (2012). Flipping a counter over when you take a hit just doesn't capture the feel of bloody infantry collisions or the horrifying impact of artillery fire. This system uses step counters stacked beneath an identifying counter to account for brutal attrition and devastating wipe-outs.
family: Theme: Wetlands / Swamps / Bogs / Fens / Marshland

description:
From the publisher:

The Isle of Coconuts is a beautiful place with lush palm trees and teams of Monkeys jumping from tree to tree competing for delicious coconuts. Roll the dice and count your chances to become the biggest team on the palm tree or be smart enough to stay away - either way, you win coconuts. Let your team be the first to gather 10 coconut tokens to win the game.

What They'll Learn

Quick Addition - Each turn, players add up the dice one of three ways to decide where to place Monkeys on palm tree fronds.

Risk vs. Reward - Players pick the palm trees where they can build the biggest team of Monkeys. Stay away from trees where there could be an all-way tie or where you might end up being the smallest team when all the palm tree fronds are full.

boardgame: モリヤウォーズ (Moriya Wars: Episode II)
description: モリヤウォーズ (Moriya Wars: Episode II) is a hidden identity game in which each player has different victory conditions. To keep it from being too easy to determine who is which character based on what you do in the game, players need to select actions based on the dice rolled in the game, so feel free to blame the dice when you "have" to do something that seems like it's leading someone toward victory.
family: Video Game Theme: Project Shrine Maiden

description:
Junkwars is a card game set in a post-apocalyptic future.
Each player will have to choose one of the Factions, represented by their own unique deck, fighting for the control over Neworld, the last city on Earth.

The strategy is based upon:
-the positioning of units on the table (each unit is represented by a unique card,and can be deployed on three different battle lines)
-the allocation of resources: the game has 2 main resources, Matter and Spirit. Matter is used for direct attacks while Spirit is used to unlock powers.

The objective of the game is to take down the enemy Overlord. This is the most powerful unit of the deck and is no deployed at the beginning of the game. In order to force the opponent to deploy his Overlord, the player must inflict consistent damages to the opponent's units. Doing so, the opponent will have access to buffs and, at the end, he will have to deploy his Overlord on the table, making it vulnerable to attacks.

The Buildings uses the gameplay found in Sky Scrapers, but creates new goals for new playing areas.

In each game, you start with pieces of the two players on the game board. Each turn, a player moves one of their own stacks a number of spaces equal to the height of the stack, stacking it onto of another stack. A stack is owned by the player whose piece is on top.

In the original Sky Scrapers game, included in this set, whoever has the most stacks that contain at least four pieces wins the game. A different game board crowns the player who has created the tallest stack. A third game board gives the win to whoever controls more of the six inner spaces on the game board.

Moon-Sun-Angel includes sixteen cubes, eight each in two colors, with each cube featuring the moon, sun and angel on three sides and having blank faces on the other three. Players takes turns placing one cube in the playing area, either on the game board or on another cube.

If a player can create a row of four cubes that feature the same symbol or color, that player wins. Alternately, a player can create two rows of three cubes to win.

After a successful launch at the Tokyo Game Market in November 2015, a new edition was produced in January 2016 under the name e-SOLO-e (絵揃), which is a Japanese word that means "match symbols". Three editions were produced, Spiritual (a copy of the original Moon-Sun_Angel edition), Japanesque (和風絵揃), and Cat World (Nekoten ねこの天使). The last had artwork by Shukoko Yamamoto based on her artwork of Cat Angels.

Formerly Bushido, the Card Game.

A Samurai push-your-luck, set collection game for 2-5 players. Take to the Dojo and Roll dice, allocating them to the available Virtues. Learn enough Virtues of the Bushido Code to impress one of the Daimyo in order to win!

For twelve years the Province of Tosa has been blessed with peace and prosperity. Peace and prosperity that has been won through valiant battle and sacrifice. That has left Tosa with little army, yet still what remains defends the earned prosperity. Until sea-faring invaders lay siege to Kochi, a capital port for Tosa, and threatens this lasting peace. With the invaders not willing to negotiate; the Shogun orders his many Daimyo to travel Tosa in search of recruits to train in order to bolster the dwindling ranks of Samurai for the inevitable military confrontation.

Bushi Tanto is an interesting blend of push-your-luck and strategy. Each roll of the die will leave you with a decision, collect virtues that are needed to win, or activate Daimyo abilities to alter the game to your advantage. Either decision, however small, will ultimately lead to your victory or failure.

Bushi Tanto doesn't lack interactive gameplay. Many of the Virtue cards dynamically alter the game when collected. Focus tokens can force a player to reroll any die result at any time - and each of the Daimyo has a special ability to help or hinder training!

Compete as samurai potentials to be the first player recruited!

B-Sieged: Heroes Set 2 introduces three brand-new Heroes to defend the Citadel of Modhelm: Waldo, the Monk; Kara, the Seer; and Rolf, the Executioner!
These three heroes are part of the "Defender Level Kickstarter Rewards".

Hvězdokupy: Hra plná hvězd (Czech for "Star Clusters: A Game Full of Stars") is an abstract logical game for 2 to 6 players. The players of this game become astronomers trying to find a given constellation. The goal is to be the first player to find the given constellation.

The game is played on a shared 10x10 grid board, which represents the night sky. Each player is assigned a unique constellation to look for on the board. During the game, players take turns to place stars on the grid based on dice rolls. The number rolled on the dice shows with how many stars the currently placed star must neighbour. The dice is 8-sided and each number is part of a pair, so the player may choose between 2 different results. The first player to find/construct their given constellation among the stars wins the game. Shared victories are possible.

A remarkable feature of the game is that the major game pieces glow in the dark, making the game suitable for playing at night, which contributes to its theme.

A long time ago in a galaxy far, far away Clone Wars raged. Stand on the path of light or dark side of the Force and turn the tide of the war. Each player can choose one of four characters: Obi-Wan Kenobi, Anakin Skywalker, Count Dooku and Asajj Ventress. Each character has their own abilities and the ability to wield power. Moving on the playing field - circuit galaxy - players will perform the tasks of their allies, and fight each other for influence over the planets and military bases. With the game cards and dice the player can influence the other characters enter the battlefield and more troops to crush the enemy's defenses.

Contents:


- High detailed play field
- 4 player's cards
- 128 game cards
- Count Dooku figure
- Obi-Wan Kenobi figure
- Anakin Skywalker figure
- Asajj Ventress figure
- 60 game tokens
- Dices (1-6)
- Board game manuals

As riders for the Pony Express, you'll risk life and limb to make a buck in the old west.

Spend 3 weeks in the saddle, claiming parcels and planning your route from Missouri to California and back. Make your deliveries and pick up helpful items along the way.

Riders of the Pony Express uses a unique low-bid auction to claim parcels every round, and then hazard tiles can spice up the routes between towns, forcing players to stay flexible with their plans.

The rider with the most cash at the end of the game wins!

This Limited Edition expansion adds 14 new cards to the game.

Anomalies (4 cards)
Every time you go back in time, anomalies are added to the game. More anomalies means more variability!

Future (5 cards)
Instead of going back in time, you have the option of going into the future. Futures add a bit more complexity to the game, as each future trip allows you to take a bonus action. If you play the future right, you can unlock some fantastic combinations!

Office (5 cards)
The Office action cards replace the simple Trash action cards in the game. Now, instead of merely removing components in order prevent the loss of points, you can time your actions to gain points.

These cards were included for free for all backers of Loop Inc. on Kickstarter. However, these extras are available for purchase separately. None are Kickstarter-exclusive.

This Limited Edition expansion adds 16 tiles to the game.

Performance Goals (7 tiles)
At the beginning of the game, each player receives one randomly dealt tile as a secret goal, hidden from the other players. Players who have fulfilled the goal listed on their tile score bonus points at the end of the game.

Waste Memo (5 tiles)
At the beginning of the game, one Waste Memo tile is chosen at random. During the game, component penalties no longer simply count as -1 points, but rather carry the penalties indicated on the tile. Components are left face-up in the penalty area to prevent confusion.

Butterfly Effect (4 tiles)
After shuffling these tiles, they are placed, one by one, face down above trip cards, starting with the trip cards that take travelers the furthest back in time. One tile is placed for each player in the game (i.e. in a 3-player game, the three furthest-back trip cards will each have one Butterfly Effect tile placed face-down above them).  When that trip card is visited, the player picks up the tile and places it face up above any trip card later in the timeline. No trip card may have more than one tile placed above it. A face-up tile affects both trips on a trip card, and rewards or subtracts points at the end of the game.

These tiles were included for free for all backers of Loop Inc. on Kickstarter. However, these extras are available for purchase separately. None are Kickstarter-exclusive.

Auction Heads nos pone en la piel de los subastadores de almacenes abandonados en Estados Unidos, al estilo de los populares programas de TV donde los participantes de la subasta de un almacén realizan pujas por quedarse con su contenido, del que solo pueden ver una pequeña parte, no pudiendo ver la parte de atrás del almacén ni tocar nada del contenido. De esta manera el que realice la puja más alta se quedará con todo el lote, que podrá darle beneficios o pérdidas dependiendo de su valor real.

ENGLISH

Auction Heads is a game of abandoned storage auctioneers, bidding on and winning the most bizarre objects, appraising the items found within, and selling the most lucrative and interesting pieces to gain the game. The player who make the highest bid will remain with the entire batch, and after the deck ends, the player with the most money wins. But, be carefully! There may be hidden illegal objects that can make you lose money!

boardgame: Thunderbirds: Devious Schemes Promo Card Set
description:
Expand both your Thunderbirds Board Game and Thunderbirds Roleplaying Game experiences with this bonus set of 5 Scheme cards to your Thunderbirds games. Also contains a reference card.

With these additional 5 cards you'll have an extra challenging Level I, II, III, IV and the first exceptionally hard level V Scheme. Prepare to launch as the Thunderbirds Devious Schemes are go!


     5 new Scheme cards bring tough new challenges to your Thunderbirds games
     Reference Card to show you how to incorporate the cards in your game


Included in the Kickstarter edition of the game.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: A twist on the usual trivia games; players ask questions personal to themselves.

Finding Wally is never child's play but it's even trickier when there's a board made up of four movable sections and a ticking timer to battle against! If you know a fan of the red and white jumper-wearing character, then they'll love the chance to show their stripes in this exciting fast and furious search and find game where they'll say goodbye to school holiday boredom and so long to rainy day blues. Great for girls and boys alike aged seven upwards (and fun-loving grown-ups too!), the Where's Wally? Board Game is a race against time where being eagle-eyed and speedy is a winning combination! Players must find the picture on the board which matches the picture on their card. It might sound simple but try doing that when the sections of the board have been rearranged and there's a constant tick telling you that time is running out. The aim of the game is to be the fastest player to find and cover Where's Wally? pictures on the board with a Wally token. The next player tries to do the same and so the game continues until the timer stops. A card with a number '2' on it means double the challenge - you have to match the picture on your card to the picture on the board twice before play moves on. And if you collect a card with an 'R' on it, the direction of play is reversed and the four sections of the Where's Wally? board must be rearranged too. Simply super stripy fun for Where's Wally? fans who like a challenge!

boardgame: ヘッジホッグジレンマ (Hedgehog's Dilemma)
description:
In ヘッジホッグジレンマ (Hedgehog's Dilemma), you and your fellow players are hedgehog breeders who are trying to encourage the little critters to get closer to one another on a cold day, perhaps to form a hedgehog couple if all goes well, so they need to be close enough to meet but not too close.

User summary:
The deck has cards numbered 1 to 4. Players are dealt six cards to their hand and a number of cards are laid face-up matching the number of players. Everyone chooses a card from their hand and reveals it. Cards that match up go to the player, everything else is discarded. Repeat this until the players' hands are empty, then score. Total up the whole of the cards you collected, the player with the highest total wins a token. Shuffle the whole deck over, play another round. The game ends when the fifth token is given out, the most tokens wins.

Matching cards simply means matching the number on the card, so if the table shows 1,3,3,4 and you're the only one to lay a 4, you get both 4s. But if more than one player lays a 4, you all get nothing. If two players each laid a 3, they'd each get a 3 from the table. If one player laid a 3, they'd only get one 3.

But if a 1 is matched in a turn, then the highest value table card is discarded, and so any matches are also lost. This brings in double-think, what are they other players likely to do? Do you go for the 4, or try a safer 3? What looks like a simple game becomes more cunning and watching what cards have already been played matters.

The card art is lovely, showing a variety of spiny creatures, sea urchins, lizards, turtles, porcupines. The cards are quite thin but the game is well made.

Each player tries to find and assign the fruits in the correct order.
When it's your turn, you first try to find an apple in the hidden tokens. For this, you return one of the chips that are on the table.
If you have returned an apple, you can give it as food to the caterpillar. On your next turn, you will seek a pear, then a plum, and so on.
Continue in this way to find, little by little, the food for the caterpillar in the right order.
The first player who have returned all six cards wins the game.
family: Series: World of Eric Carle (University Games)

description:
An apple, two pears, three plums? In the cards you see different images of fruits and with different amounts of numbers.
Mix all the cards and then You must try to correctly match the cards together.
The winner is the player with most matched cards.
family: Series: World of Eric Carle (University Games)

description:
"I know 3.029e+30 ways to drive you crazy."

Dive into the tumult and test yourself against the game and your friends in this frantic rush to prevent the disintegration of an otherwise nice and comely family. (Yeah, right...)

Tumult is a speed-puzzler that utilizes a random puzzle-generator mechanism to reach as many as 3.029e+30 different puzzles. All players solve the same puzzle simultaneously and compare results when time has run out. Every mistake causes tears, and whoever has caused the fewest tears at the end of a certain number of rounds wins.

Tumult reimplements Uluru/Pelican Cove, with some notable differences in the card set and gameplay. The physical and thematic design is also new; one particular improvement is that Tumult is playable even if you are colorblind!

boardgame: Frag Doch Mal: Das Clevere Spiel
description:
The oldest player begins by taking the top card from the pile and reading the question to the player at his left.

In the card the mouse, the duck and the elephant each offer a possible answer. Read these three possible answers and always say by whom the answer comes: From the Mouse, the elephant or the duck. You can check the right answer in the back of the card. There, the mouse, the duck or the elephant are displayed with a detailed response.

The game ends as soon as there are no more cards. The player who has the most cards wins the game.
family: TV Shows: Die Sendung mit der Maus

description:
In あやかし草子 (Ayakashi Soushi), players want to get rid of the tiles in hand quicker than anyone else. Tiles are hexagonal with a figure in the middle, and they feature borders of either a single unique color or two colors split evenly around the tile.

On a turn, a player places a tile on the board, triggering the special effect of the depicted figure if the border of that tile matches an adjacent tile. The tiles with unique colors each have a special power that takes effect when placed.

boardgame: Frag Doch Mal:  Das Kartenspiel Set 1
description:
The oldest player begins by taking the top card from the pile and reading the question to the player at his left.

In the card the mouse, the duck and the elephant each offer a possible answer. Read these three possible answers and always say by whom the answer comes: From the Mouse, the elephant or the duck. You can check the right answer in the back of the card. There, the mouse, the duck or the elephant are displayed with a detailed response.

The game ends as soon as there are no more cards. The player who has the most cards wins the game.
family: TV Shows: Die Sendung mit der Maus

boardgame: Frag Doch Mal: Das Kartenspiel Set 2
description:
The oldest player begins by taking the top card from the pile and reading the question to the player at his left.

In the card the mouse, the duck and the elephant each offer a possible answer. Read these three possible answers and always say by whom the answer comes: From the Mouse, the elephant or the duck. You can check the right answer in the back of the card. There, the mouse, the duck or the elephant are displayed with a detailed response.

The game ends as soon as there are no more cards. The player who has the most cards wins the game.
family: TV Shows: Die Sendung mit der Maus

description:
Ninja Ninja Ninja features a 54-card deck, with cards numbered 0-5 in three colors, with each card appearing three times.

Players have a hand of seven cards, and they play three at a time to create strong card combinations, such as three cards of the same number and color (strongest), three cards in sequence, etc. As they reveal combinations, they can cause damage to one another based on the strength of their attacks and defenses.

In this game for children, you need to move pawns into the holes with the corresponding shapes and earn the most victory tokens this way.
  Contents:
 1 gameboard
 12 pawns (in 4 different color red, green, blue,yellow, and 3 different shape triangle, brick, roll in every color)
 12 victory tokens
 4 color tokens (in 4 different colors)
 3 shape tokens
 2 dice
  When it's your turn, you need to take a step with a pawn. The goal is moving a pawn into a field having a hole with the same shape. When this occurs you can get a victory token. To take a step with a pawn, you need to roll with 2 dice. The first die identifies the number of steps, the second die identifies the color, so if you roll green and 2 you have to take 2 steps with one of the green pawns.
  If you roll a star with the color die, you need to select a color token from the face-down pile and a shape token also from the face-down pile and these identify which pawn you must move.
  The game ends when all pawns fell down in the holes.
 The player with the most victory tokens wins the game.

Marseille Sophistiqué is your guide to one of the most complex families of card games in the world! A 78-card deck, elegantly illustrated and faithful to the sensibility of its 15th century origins, updated with modern gameplay in mind.

The Tarocchi family of games is a historical set of point-trick taking games, played in a series of zero-sum hands. Most in the family include a signature fluctuating team system where one bids for the high-risk, high-reward position of the single opponent to the other players, who form a temporary team for the duration of the hand. Players may additionally bet on a series of secondary goals, to achieve bonus points along with those for the hand itself. A game is played to a variable number of rounds in which each player deals a hand.

Marseille Sophistiqué includes an introductory rulebook, containing historical game rules for
-Baronetti, a simple 2-player introductory rules set that focuses on the basic point-trick principles, also good for fast games and appropriate for children
-Scarto, a 3-player free-for-all
-French Tarot, the classic 4-player, team-based trick taking game, with variants for 3-6 players
-adaptations for 5-suited Poker

Free digital content extends these rules to include
-Droggn, a 3-4 player game with a smaller deck, more forgiving to the opponent.
-Hungarian Tarokk, a 4-5 player game with an advanced bonus system
-Illustrated Hungarian Tarokk, a variant for those who have mastered Hungarian Tarokk
-Rules variants reflecting some of the regional iterations of games

Rules light skirmish game for 28mm scale zombie survival scenarios, played with miniatures and terrain on the tabletop using 20 sided dice.  Suggested game size is 3-5 human survivors verses 36-150 zombies!

From the rulebook introduction:

ZOMBIES & SURVIVORS is the free downloadable fast play ‘fun’ Zombie game from Grekwood Miniatures. This game guide has all the information required to start playing the zombie apocalypse.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Age of Craft Expansion #2: Architecture Alliance
description:
This expansion for Age of Craft introduces Alliance Building cards, which can be purchased by neighboring players pooling their resources together. The Alliance Buildings are placed in between the two neighboring players and provide benefits to both.

This set also contains extra cards for use during 2-player games to make negotiation more effective, tweaked versions of base set cards, variant rules for a longer game, and additional cards for use with the first expansion.
publishers: Chicken Dice Games (チキンダイスゲームズ)

description:
First complete the puzzle about different terrains and animals.
Then take turns to spin the spinner and matching the different animals to the correct categories.
Who has placed the most tiles to the correct categories is the winner.

First complete the large world puzzle with different animals.
Then take turns to spin the spinner and matching the animals and fish to their correct habitats.
Who has matched the most tiles is the winner.

Maiandros is a 2-player connection game (such as Hex, Twixt, etc).

Games are always played in odd square boards with 9x9 being the minimum size recommended.In this game players alternate turns placing identical two-color tiles, each with an inverse side. Tiles must be placed in a way that its colours match the colours printed on the corners of each square (to ensure all tiles match each other). The goal of the game is to connect opposite sides of the board, be it horizontally (east-west) or vertically (north-south).

5Crests is a hybrid card game set in an alternate history, where the discovery of a magically imbued mineral called Villium gives rise to a stronger, more centralized criminal enterprise, the First Family. The family, in their quest to control this new power, created 5 different guilds, honoring the ways of their members to provide muscle and protection against any who oppose it.

You play the role of a fast rising street lieutenant in one of these guilds trying to carve out your own slice of the American Dream. Each player constructs a deck of various allies, weapons, trade fronts, and actions. Players can build and expand their arsenal by collecting more cards and trading with their friends to get more powerful allies and tools of the trade. Then you take to the streets and fight other street lieutenants for supremacy. Each lieutenant starts with a set amount of health based on their abilities, but winning requires that you bring the opposing lieutenants health to 0. Players expand their Villium trade by playing and controlling trade fronts. As these fronts bring in Villium, players are able to bring their allies and resources into the fray to battle with their enemies. Fights can be 1 on 1 street brawls, or all out mob wars between multiple players. Those left standing will continue their quest to gain control of the first family.

The family is calling, will you answer?
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: Nex is a variant on the game Hex. Like Hex it is played on a grid of hexagons. One player is trying to connect the top and bottom with stones of their color, while the other player is trying to connect the other two sides with their color. The mechanism that makes it different from Hex is the addition of a neutral color. Each turn a player has a choice between two actions. The first is to simply place a stone of their  color and a neutral stone. The second possibility is to replace two neutral stones with stones of their color and replace a stone of their color with a neutral stone. Neutral pieces are not considered part of any connection.

boardgame: Eternal War of the Candy Realms!
description:
A candy themed card battling game, where players compete to win real piles of sweet loot!

I noticed that there aren't a lot of games that both kids and adults would want to play.  Either games skew too young, and parents get bored... or the games engage only adults and are way too complex for a child.  So this one has a lot of references aimed at the 20-40 year age range, but is totally family-appropriate for most kids 8 and over!

The gimmick is that each player brings a pile of real sweets (or a candy substitute of your choosing) to fuel their warriors in battle against each other, with a "Land Card" mechanism that keeps it from becoming "pay-to-win".  Its perfect for gatherings with a lot of snacks, or for the holidays when there are lots of sweets to be divided up!

Why would you want to win a giant pile of candy?   Candy is awesome!   Who doesn't want first dibs on a giant pile of candy!?  Plus, you can share it with your friends and family, silly!

Danger lurks in the halls of Northfield Elementary. The school yard is no place for the meek. Only one man can protect the innocent children from bullies, cheaters, and thiefs. But unfortunately Principal Jones is usually too tied up with PTA meetings, in-service days, and keeping his office chair warm. That leaves Adin Simon, private eye, gumshoe, and sleuth to clean up after the fact. Fourth grade may not be safe, but with a bit of grit and determination he'll solve your case and bring your culprit to justice...or get wedgied trying.

Fourth Grade Confidential is a microgame of deduction, bluffing, and cunning, based on Neal Simon's comic book project called Fourth Grade Confidential. The premise is that a fourth grader named Adin is a private eye at his school, working to solve the great mysteries of elementary school, like who stole his sister's stuffed pink bunny.

In Fourth Grade Confidential one player is the Sleuth while 2-4 other players are the Characters. Each round the Sleuth is trying to solve a different mystery. The Sleuth has six Sleuth Cards and the other 12 cards are dealt evenly to the Characters. The Sleuth has six turns to try to figure out which Character has the Target Item for that round by calling out various attributes and seeing who has items with matching attributes. Through careful deduction and use of actions the Sleuth and Characters all try to be the player to end up with the Target Item.

The Sleuth: Each round a new player will take on the roll of the Sleuth. The Sleuth will be questioning the Characters and following up clues. If the Sleuth catches Characters lying about the items they have he'll get to perform additional actions to help him narrow down where the Target Item is.

The Characters: All other players are Characters in the current mystery. One Character will have the Target Item that the Sleuth is looking for. As the Sleuth questions Characters they will begin to reveal items they have to prove their innocence, or to throw the Sleuth off the track. Can you be cunning enough to keep the Target Item hidden from the Sleuth? Are you going to help the Sleuth figure out who is the Culprit? Maybe you're just out for yourself. Characters get to take actions if they reveal items the Sleuth is looking for, without giving up the Target Item. But if they are caught lying the Sleuth gets extra actions to help him find the Culprit.

The round ends when the Sleuth runs out of Actions or when he finds the Target Item. Scoring can be totaled for each round and then scores totaled after everyone has had a chance to be the Sleuth an equal number of times.

Launch a military style campaign to capture 270 Electoral College votes by using power and influence to sway the voters, turning hot-button political issues into weapons of mass destruction. Shock and awe your opponents with a devastating arsenal of lethal ordnance to capture Primary and Secondary Objectives and earn the 270 Electoral College Votes needed to win the White House.

Deck includes: 52 Playing Cards and 4 Intel Cards
2-4 Players
Kids 6+ / Adults

The object of the game is to WIN 2 CAMPAIGNS. There are 3 ways to win a campaign:


     Capture 5 PRIMARY OBJECTIVES for 270 ELECTORAL VOTES.
     Capture 5 SECONDARY OBJECTIVES for 269 ELECTORAL VOTES + 1 PRIMARY OBJECTIVE to exceed 270 ELECTORAL VOTES.
     Fight a WAR OF ATTRITION until all but one player runs out of cards and drops out of the game.


PLAYING THE GAME (Basic Overview)

Shuffle and deal out all cards evenly to all players (players do not look at their cards but keep them in a stack FACE DOWN on the table). During each round, players take turns drawing and playing their top card. Cards are placed FACE UP on the table.
When a player draws a PRIMARY or SECONDARY OBJECTIVE, the player continues to turn over cards until an ORDNANCE card (a numbered card) is drawn. The player with the highest card wins and captures all cards in play.

boardgame: Bugs Bunny Race in Space Game
description:
Bugs Bunny is an astronaut on a mission to deliver urgently needed carrots to an orbiting space station. The object is to be the first player to deliver four carrots to their matching space station.

Players spin and move their pawn from their planet with up to 3 carrots of cargo at a time to the space station with the matching color. Players may move in any direction during each turn but diagonal, but they may not simply return to the square they started on. If a player lands on a space occupied by another player, the other player must take all their cargo and put it back at their home planet. They must then move back to the planet to pick up cargo again. Players do not need an exact count to enter their space station (or planet) and drop off the carrot cargo. After dropping off the cargo, they must return to their planet to pick up the remaining cargo and deliver it to the space station. First player to deliver four carrots to their space station wins.

Shatra (шатра), also known as Altay Shatra (Алтай-шатра),  is a cross-over between chess and checkers, which is based on a traditional game of the Altay region whose exact rules were lost. It was re-created in 1960 by Saveliy Yakovlevich Pahayev, a professor of the Gorno-Altaisk Research Institute of History, Language and Literature. In 1974, some reasearchers of this institute "improved" the rules and, in the following year, the first tourna­ment was held in the capital of the Altay Republic. The game soon became a popular mindsport in the Altay region and is also play in Khakassia, Tuva and Buryatia. There were over-the-board  tour­na­ments in Russia (Besh-Ozek (Altay), Chernogorsk (Khakassia), Elo (Altay), Gorno-Altaysk (Altay), Kosh-Agach (Altay), Kyzyl (Tuva), Onguday (Altay), Shebalino (Altay), Ulan-Ude (Bur­yatia)), Ust-Kan (Altay), and Yabogan (Altay).

In addition, there is another "Shatra", which is a little bit more chess-like and, which is mostly played outside of Altay. It was invented by Vladimir Leonidovich Taushkanov in the 1970s who helped to finalize the Shatra rules in 1974, but went a step further. The older variant popular in the Altay region can be called "Shatra I" and the slightly newer variant, which is today almost exclusively played outside of the Altay region (mainly in the European part of Russia) "Shatra II". There is also a four-sided variation of Shatra I that can be played by four players.

The goal of the game is to mate the opponent's king (biy). Besides the king, Shatra I has 16 pieces, which move like Russian checkers men. In Shatra II there is a king (biy), a queen (batïr), two rooks (tura), two bishops (jalkïn) and 11 pawns (šatra). Capturing is in both variants mandatory and by jumping similar to checkers. The board has a very unusual shape and was influenced by Chinese Chess (the autonomous Altay Republic borders to China). It has a battlefield, a ditch, a gate and a fortress.

Shatra I can be played online at Abstract Games (a Russian site) and Shatra II at Chess Variants Game Courier. Recently a book was published about Shatra I. Draws are possible and might be substantial similar to checkers and chess, particularly in Shatra I, if the game is played by masters. However, in Shatra I tournaments at Abstract Games, which are played among weak amateurs from Russia, Belarus and Germany, only every 15th game ends in a draw. In Shatra I, there are 400 possible board positions after ply 2. The branching factor is similar to chess.

Simple 2 player card game. The aim of which is to use the differently numbered cards in your hand to achieve a random number on a designated card. The plyer who does this the quickest then gets to move the 'Death Star/millennium falcon' card away from them.

You and your friends are part of an order known simply as the Keepers. The Keepers were sworn to contain any horrors that keeps coming out from Portals of Evil that appear in dungeons from time to time. It is your job to not only prevent them from getting out, but also to destroy the portal to prevent it from happening again.

Monster Rush is a co-operative tactical dungeon crawling game for 1-4 players. Play different card combinations to create a different hero every game and prevent the monsters from escaping the dungeon. Strategize and come up with adaptive tactics every turn, as monsters mass up and threaten to overwhelm you and your fellow Keepers.

Players try rooting out witchcraft in their opponents village.
Each player has a deck of 9 character cards which they will use by drawing one and then playing one each turn in an attempt to locate the opponents witch card and avoid taking curses.

DamBeavers is the tile laying resource collecting game of beaver colony control and proliferation! Thwart hapless rivals by unleashing the wolverine, lumberjack or redneck farmer. Beaver tirelessly through the rolling seasons to make your colony reign supreme!

Begin by laying river tiles and placing initial dam pieces. Move through the three turn phases in each season. Lay terrain tiles, send out beavers to gather resources, build dams and lodges and watch your beaver colony grow. Fortify your lodges before winter or your baby beavers will perish. And at all times beware the wolverine! With each passing year when the river floods, the landscape changes more and more due to your damming activities.

When the final tile is plucked from the bag, the winner is the player with the most...DamBeavers!

The caterpillar is hungry and eats its way through fruits, vegetables and all sorts of other goodies. Children help him fight his way through the largest possible number of threats. This creates a tower of cards. But be careful! If someone is not careful, the tower collapses and the caterpillar must start from scratch...

Every card has a hole in it. The first player has to stack one card of his choice over the caterpillar, always leaving the caterpillar's head visible. All players do the same, increasing the stack, and trying not to collapse the stack.
If you collapse the stack, all the other players gain a butterfly chip except you. If the players manage to put all the cards in the stack without it falling, then all players get a butterfly chip.

The game ends when one or more players have won five "Butterfly" chips. These players are the winners!

Which head matches what legs? Which body is missing here? Who will be the first to assemble two matching Benjamin motifs correctly? A simple, entertaining game with the popular elephant.
The first player to assemble two Benjamin motifs with the tree corresponding cards, wins the game!

You are picture dealer of Japanese painting. You are being repaired the folding screen painting in cooperation with other picture dealer.
However, if it is possible to be repaired ahead of others, the painting can help you get.
Let's become a famous picture dealer by collecting more of the folding screen painting.

Rinpa is Rinpa theme of Japanese painting. And, it is a trick-taking game of the set collection.

				
				
					Summary of How to play
    Players, will do the trick-take by must follow. All cards of trick-take have been used and arranged all in the center.
    Cards, have been set for each 2 to 6 sheets - each set is folding screen picture - .
    The player who draws one of the last in each set, gets its set.
    Player to get the set is not a winner of each trick take. be careful!
    To play all of the hand, The winner is player to get the most of the cards!

Ogoola Karuta is a westernized version of a 400-year-old Japanese card game called Hyakunin-isshu Karuta. The game is sometimes described as intellectual martial arts. It is about listening and being extremely focused and fast, just like the Samurai with their swords. Only here you fight with cards instead.

The game is comprised of two decks of cards, the Reader deck and the Player deck. Cards in the Reader deck contain short poems or poetry quotations while cards in the Player deck contain only the second part of these poems. To begin the game, a Reader is chosen (or you may opt to use the Reader app) and the players are divided into two teams sitting on opposite sides of the table or play area. The Reader shuffles the Reader deck and the Players mix, split evenly the Player deck between the teams and place them face-up in 3-4 rows in front of them. The Reader will read the complete poetry quotation on their cards and the player that finds the matching card first takes that card and puts it aside. If the card taken was on your opponent's side, you must give one card from your side to your opponent. The first team that has no cards left on their side is the winner (you must get rid of all your cards first, to win).

The game is set in Japan during the Genpei war (1180-1185). In order to win the war, you need a strategy. Place the cards you are familiar with close to you (in the beginning it is normal that none of the cards are familiar). If you know what cards your opponent is familiar with, place those cards as far as possible from them inside the playing field. Since the reader or app will always read the complete quotation, you do not need prior knowledge of the poems. Strategically place the playing cards in a way that you can find and take them quickly such as laying them alphabetically or categorize them under the same first words.

Ogoola Karuta Poetry Game is not a translation of the original Japanese game. In this first edition, we have selected 50 of the well-known classical poems written in the English language. It is the first western version of Hyakunin-isshu Karuta, which means Karuta: 100 poems by 100 poets.

This starter set contains everything you need to learn and start playing Malifaux! Pit the forces of the mysterious Angel Eyes against the diabolical Dr. Grimwell in a series of starter games which teach you as you play. If you are new to Malifaux and want to know more, this box is for you. In addition to being a good starting point, every model has the "mercenary" characteristic, allowing you to use them with whichever Faction you decide on. Even if you're experienced with Malifaux, you may be interested in the brand new models for the Guild and Neverborn Factions this set contains.

Set Contains:

2 Crews (8 models)
    8 Stat Cards
    2 Upgrade Cards
    2 Quick Reference Cards

Quick Start Booklet
    Free Digital Rules Manual
    2 Fate Decks
    2 Measuring Tapes
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
It's a fast-paced race with Lightning McQueen and his friends. Bring your favorite heroes at the finish line first and win the thrilling race! 
But be careful, several obstacles are awaiting you during the route, before the winner can roll to the finish.

Each player chooses a pawn and places it in square one.
In your turn, place Lightning McQueen at the center of the action platform and press it flat. Look
as it turns, and in what hole he randomly ends.
Each of the 9 holes has a different action\power that you have to perform.
The first player to cross the finish line wins.

PLAYERS WILL EXPLORE THE UNSEEN WORLD OF MICROBES LIVING IN AND ON US

The American Museum of Natural History is introducing an original card game in conjunction with its exhibition The Secret World Inside You, which opens on Saturday, November 7. While working on the development of The Secret World Inside You, exhibition co-curator Susan Perkins, an avid tabletop game enthusiast, pioneered an idea to create a card game allowing players to learn about the community of microbes that live in our guts, known as the gut microbiome. Gutsy is based on the various ways we interact with those microbes on a daily basis and turns those interactions into a fun and educational card game.

The object of the game is for players to attempt to be first to create a healthy gut by diversifying their gut microbiome, which is influenced by what we eat and drink, whom we interact with, and the medicines that we take.

Two to four players draw from a common card deck, collecting microbes and pathogens to build their respective guts.

The first player to have six different kinds of gut microbes wins. The game is intended for players ages eight and up.

Perkins worked with the Museum’s Associate Director for Digital Learning, Barry Joseph, and high school students in the Museum’s Lang Science Program, educators, as well as Eric Teo, from the New York University Game Center MFA program, to create Gutsy.

Playing on the popularity of social card games like Sushi Go! and Killer Bunnies, the developers used illustrations, photos, and information created for The Secret World Inside You exhibition.
publishers: The American Museum of Natural History

description:
Recover the Summit, Rescue the Captive...

He’s been waiting, waiting for days. Hands tied, captured by enemy forces. He’s confident you will save him. You wouldn’t leave him to languish at the hands of his captors. Would you? Will he be rescued first? Or will his enemies destroy your clan to rescue their own captive?

You must make the treacherous journey across the Karrowlands. The Karrowlands are perilous; a vast and unending land stretching from the desolate desert to the arctic ice, from the rolling grasslands to the fields of lava. Recover the summit. It’s the only way. But who will be there waiting for you? Gwythral, the tameable dragon? Maneeri, the mountain giant known for taking captives of his own? Ragnash, The Wolf of the Underworld, who devours everything in his wake? Or perhaps your enemies have set up an ambush of their own? You must fight.

Control locations to gather resources. Deploy extra recruits. Battle the unsuspecting foes. Advance towards the enemy base. Will you team up with local kinsmen, or will you venture out alone? Choose your clansmen wisely.

He sits and prays for you, his compatriot, to come to rescue him; to save the day. He knows the journey will not be easy. Will you recover the summit and rescue the captive? Or abandon him to your foes. Only time will tell.

Nintori! is a Japanese game to win the tiles by rolling the dice.
From children to adults can play easily.

★ What game?

Nintori! It is handy DiceGame you can enjoy from children to adults.
Players, "Nezumi Kozo" [japan's robin hood] to longed (?) Sneaked in castle town Become a ninja apprentice of children,
Use the Ninjutsu using the treasure that was placed in hand and the dice.

★ Most people who use the Ninjutsu is Ninjutsu Master!

Who lot to get the treasure than, it will aim to use a lot of Ninjutsu!
However, that would be caught by scary officials and fail to sneak also ....

Players, Become a child ninja apprentice, you will go to sneak training to the castle town.
Two dice to use.
Using this, you can determine it is where to sneak.

Is from the tail out that crept, you will discover the treasures and items.
This is to become of course score at the end of the game, but you may come out lookout officials in because your attention!

Until the end of the game, the game is to compete a lot of scoring.

Because it is Ninja said that apprenticeship, hand to put the training point is scored by a successful Ninjutsu using an item, in this game called Nin-Point, you can be a lot acquisition.

● Convenient colorful purse to carry!
