Nintori! In comes with separately also add advanced rules tile from the normal rules of play even with a small child.

boardgame: Flames of War: Atlantik Wall – The German Defence of France June-September 1944
description:
This is a book which describes the forces that opposed the allied landings from 6 June 1944 to September of the same year.

You will be able to field the charismatic 21 Panzer Division with its captured equipment or attack the allied beacheads with the fanatic SS Tigers with Witmann on the lead.

This book is opposed to the one called Overlord, which covers the allied troops.

boardgame: Flames of War: Overlord – The Allied Invasion of France
description:
This book contains the forces that assaulted the French coast in 1944 to free it from the German occupation.

With this book, you can field parachute men from the famous 101st Airborne, men of the "Blue&Grey" 29Div. on bloody Omaha, british armoured spearheads heading to Villers Bocage to meet their fate or the powerful 2 Armoured Division as it crosses the Bocage breaking the front.

This book complements with Atlantik Wall, which is the book for the german forces.
A card game where you play cards to stacks and score points for having cards on your hand with the same last digit as a card on top of a stack.

George Washington and Abraham Lincoln are two of the most renowned figures in American history, but who would make a better President in 2016? In 1860? In 1792? Let the voters decide! Election! is an interactive family board game with a focus on America’s greatest leaders.

As a player, you have been granted the power to manage a political party that is desperate for control of the White House. In addition to selecting candidates, you may also take advantage of external factors to influence the election in your party's favor. Should you stay positive by gaining the support of a popular activist or attempt to smear an opponent's image by revealing a scandal? Do you take the high road and try to win ethically or attempt to sabotage your opponents through dirty politics? Should you throw in the towel or demand a recount? Votes are all that matters as you navigate your way to the White House.

GAMEPLAY

1) Determine the Election Year: Each round, randomly select an Election Year card that lists the most important issues of that time in American history.

2) Party Nominations: Each player selects 1 of the 5 Presidential Candidate cards from their hand.  Players nominate a candidate based on ratings that show the card’s overall polling strength of the issues being discussed.

3) Roll Dice to Determine Votes: Custom dice are rolled based on a candidate's rating. The rating corresponds to the important issues set on the Election Year card. Higher ratings often result in more votes.

Bonus) Red Tape and Public Opinion cards are wild cards that have the power to impact an election (for better or worse).

4) The player with the most votes wins the election and advances along the game board.  The winner is the first player to advance to the White House space at the end of the board.

After winning a series of elections with shrewd strategy and a bit of luck, the player who first advances to the White House space on the board wins the game and earns the right to be called Mr. or Mrs. President!  Mudslinging and dirty politics guaranteed!

From the rulebook (translated):

In Kaiten: Die Sushi-Bar every player takes the role of a sushi chef. Collect the correct ingredients, produce different sorts of sushi and take care of your guests!
Full people are happy people and the tip will be generous - sometimes.
At the end of the game, the player owning the most money wins, called the most successful sushi chef!

Players have to buy and combine ingredients (wooden cubes) following different recipes (combinations) to make sushi (earn sushi tokens). These will be placed on player-colored plates onto a sushi-belt (the board) to feed up hungry guests. The guests - represented by guest cards and mostly with unique special rules - take place around the sushi-belt wishing for a variety of different sushi types.
On turn, players can choose from different fixed actions like buying ingredients, making sushi or cleaning dirty plates. Or they can roll a die to perform special actions (for example changing plates places on the belt) or to activate events like new guests or moving all the plates on the belt one field clockwise.
The game ends instantly when the last guest leaves the restaurant and the player owning the most money wins.

The Avengers - Uniti per vincere is a cooperative game where players join together their forces to save the world from villains.
Choose your favourite Avenger with his skill. Play rolling one or two dice and moving your counter on the board. Make the action according to the square you land: collect a token, move to a square as you want, draw a card, exchange tokens with another player.
Collect the tokens and try to defeat the villains, together with the other players.
If you’ll defeat all the villains before the game ends you’ll win!

Competitive play: 
If you want, you can also play to be the best Avenger at the end of game.
At the start of game, each player receives 3 secret mission cards. Examples: “Defeat Loki” or “Have the most hammers”.
All players cooperate to defeat all the villains, and if they succeed, the player with the most points is the overall winner and the best Avenger.

As Lord Teddy's most prized toymakers, it is our duty to make the best bears possible for his approval. However, one must not shine too brightly as Lord Teddy is terribly insecure. The annual honoring of (and sacrifices to) Lord Teddy, known as "The Fluffy Rite", has come upon us once more and we must do our best — but not TOO good as we don't want to earn His wrath.

In the trick-taking game The Fluffy Rite, cards have only colors, not numbers, and each card can be used as either a head or a body of that color. Players combine two cards — one for the head, another for the body — to make one bear during each trick. The combination of colors of each assembled bear and the order of play will determine the winner of the trick. At the end of the hand, the player who has the second most trophies wins the hand and receives a game point; in a tie, all tied players receive a point. The first player to win two game points wins the game.

A promo character for Krosmaster games.

This version of Yugo is still a member of the Brotherhood of the Tofu, and has the ability - "Wakforce"

Attack spells gain Pierces armour if the targeted character is adjacent to YUGO

A stacking/balancing game with unique kinetic action. Draw a card to tell you which pirate to pile onto the ship and which water tile to use, then pull the water tile under the ship to make the boat rock on the waves. Try not to let your pirates fall overboard, but you might try to knock off your opponent's!  Each player gets one coin for each of her pirates remaining on the ship after movement of the water tile.

User summary:
サイノメコロシアム (Sainome Colosseum) is a card game where two fantasy characters fight each other. The publisher MR エンターテイメント (MR Entertainment) is the designer for this game.

Each turn, you may perform two of the following actions:
1) Fill your hand up to five cards
2) Equip yourself with cards in your hand and/or currently equipped cards
3) Play a card for a special effect
4) Attack your opponent

You have four slots to equip weapons, spells and/or items. In general, weak cards are versatile and can be equipped on any slot, but strong ones must be equipped on a certain slot.

When attacking, you roll a die to determine which slot is activated (roll of a 2 activates slot A, a 3 activates slot B, etc.), then damage and effect are determined. Used cards are discarded, so you will have to refill the slot, or your chance of a miss will be increased on your next attack.

Each character has their own special ability that goes well with specific cards.

You win the game when your opponent's life is reduced to zero.

Publisher's summary:
2人用の対戦型カードゲームです。
キャラクターに装備をつけサイコロを振り、出た目のカード効果で攻撃を行います。
最終的に相手のHPを0にした者が勝ちとなります。
publishers: MR エンターテイメント (MR Entertainment)

description:
VersuS is a board game for two to four players. Players take turns moving their pawns around a uniquely designed board, trapping and capturing other players' pawns until there is only one player left on the board, who wins the game.

Pawns move like castles in chess: up, down, left or right, as far across the board as the player wants, and can also push and pull other pawns or jump over pawns.

With three moves each turn and a new turn after every capture games move quickly and the winner is never clear until near the end.

Gagario (ガガリオ) is a two-player only game consisting of only twelve cards in which players try to exit the round at the right time based on what's been played to the table and what they think the other player has in hand.

The Waltzing Cat is a two-player game that consists of eleven wooden blocks, with each of those blocks depicting two differently-colored cats on opposite sides. Gold cats show up five times, red and blue four times, and white, black and green three times; no color is repeated on a block with another color that appears the same number of times, e.g., red doesn't share a block with red or blue, but it does with the other four colors.

To set up, shuffle the blocks without looking at them, then set them up so that each player can see only one side of each block. The player who sees more gold cats takes the first turn. On a turn, a player pushes a block away from them (so that it falls on the table), pulls a block toward them, or declares the end of the game. If you push a block and the face that lands facing up appears on another face-up block, then you keep the block that you just pushed over. If you pull a block — thereby revealing a face that you haven't seen — and the face that lands facing up appears on another face-up block, then you keep both matching blocks.

If you declare the end of the game, the other player takes turns either pushing or pulling blocks (claiming blocks when appropriate) until no further matches can be made; the player who claimed the end of the game then takes all remaining face-up blocks.

Players then tally their scores for the face-up cats in their collection. Each gold cat is worth 2 points, while each non-gold cat is worth 1 point. A pair of one blue and one red cat is worth an additional 3 points (for 5 total points). Whoever scores the most points wins!

Bugle (ビューグル) is a two-player only game of goblins vs. dwarves, with the two armies competing on a small battlefield and with the bugles determining in which directions an army can move on the subsequent turn.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
In the world of Tempest: Shards of the Gods, the apocalypse has come and gone. The world as it once was has been shattered into countless shards of reality. The only things that remain are the gods and what pieces they could salvage before the end came.

Take the place of a god of ancient myth: 

     Anubis: leading the Egyptians
     Athena: leading the Greeks
     Inari: leading the Japanese
     Heimdallr: leading the Norse


Tempest: Shards of the Gods features a number of unique mechanics:

     Discard any card for resources. Resources do not build each turn; instead, every card you play comes at the sacrifice of another.
     Tailor your deck to your opponent as you play. Split your resources between modifying and building your deck, or playing cards to fight your opponent.
     Tell the best story to win. Demigod and Tale cards have Labors that can be completed to score Victory Points. Reach 5 points first to win the game.


Fight for dominion over what is left, conquer enemy shards to piece together a universe for yourself, fend off attacks from rival gods and set your name in myth.

Yogyakarta or Jogja is an Indonesia city state in the heart of Java island. This city are well known for their historical places and arts. Performing Arts also one of the reason the tourist from all over the places came to visit.

In Pagelaran Yogyakarta, Players take role as a new appointed manager of the fictional theater that on the brink of bankruptcy. They are challenged to save this building by manage the show that would interest the tourists that come to this building.

On each turn, a player will going to choose one of three possible actions. Are they going to looking for a funding, looking for show to perform on the next turn, or to perform a  show from the show card that they have on their hand? Also there's a debt to be paid on the end of the month (every 4th round)
Dominion: Fan-Edition I is a German language collection box, including promotional cards, blank cards and additional material.

A roulette-style dare game where players take turns challenging each other to dares listed on black cards.

Each round, one player (The Challenger) draws a black card and challenges the rest of the players to do the task on the card. The rest of the players collectively decide what the odds are. On the count of 3, every player throws down one of their number cards. If your number matches The Challenger's, you have to do the challenge.

boardgame: Papecarz: The Papercraft Racing Board Game
description:
Papecarz is  a "roll and move" racing game,  a mix of strategy, placement & luck.
Two to six players control racing cars competing to win a single 8 lap race or a series of races.
As a driver, the player has to cunningly calculate every single move to make the most of the Caution Flags, restart, Slingshot Passes, Pushing and Shunting.
While as a crew chief, the player has to plan an effective and flexible race strategy and keep an eagle eye on the opponent's  tactics.

The race is made up of four main phases:
-Pre start: you choose your setup and decide the start grid.
-Start: you want to try to get a good position straight away.
-First 7 laps: here you need to get as many Boost points as you can and try to avoid the Damage cards.
-Last lap: put the pedal to the metal! it’s your chance to cross the finish line first and win the race.
At the end of each lap you get a card depending on your position; the higher your position, the better your card.
1st: one White card =2 Boost points
2nd & 3rd: one green card =1 Boost points
4th & 5th: one brown card =damage (Watch out! you could be in trouble!)
6th: one black card= serious damage (Oh no! you're in big trouble!)
And when you pit, this is where your strategy comes in;  each car has two possible setups.
It's the setup that tells you how many cards and kinds of cards you can turn over and convert.
Your setup also tells you when you can take a risk and roll the dice.
if the car in first place fails a dice roll...the yellow flag come out: CAUTION!
The race restarts  with all the cars now bunched up together and some player starts cursing , while other players cheer (Yeah!); It all depends on your pit strategy!

The players start at the big red space in the upper left corner of the game board. They have to spy on the enemy camp in the upper right corner and get back to the starting space near their own camp. At the red assembling points the players have to wait for all the other players on the same path, before they can move again. On the black spaces marked with a "T" the indians are trapped and have to wait for a trapper to come along and free them. Players of the same team may donate their die rolls to one another under certain circumstances. The first indian or team of indians to return home wins the game.

Before the start of the game, the payers agree on the starting harbor and destination harbors of the game. The destinations get marked with a lighthouse. At the harbors along the way the players may buy and sell goods according to the export-products-table while minding the loading capacity of their ships. Each player has to make a previously agreed upon number of excursions per train. When the players return to their home harbors, they sell the rest of their products, in which each subsequently arriving player gets less profit.

The game was published about 1900.

The rules of Alician Draughts were first described in the book "Les Jeux de dames non orthodoxes et autres jeux à pions" in 1956.

The standard game of International Draughts is played using two boards, A and B. The normal array is on board A; board B starts empty.

In turn, each player makes a single move on either board following these three rules:

1. A move must be legal on the board where it is played.
2. A piece can only move or capture if the corresponding destination square on the other board is vacant.
3. After moving, the piece is transfered to the corresponding square on the other board.

The original board (not the "mirror board") decides the game:

Whenever a player has no piece left on the original board, he loses even within a move. A move consists of up to three stages: Movement (which can be a jump in case of a capture), Transfer of the moved piece to the other board, Removal of any captured pieces. That is, if at any stage a player had no pieces left on the original board, he lost the game.

In practice, it is possible to play Alician Draughts on a single board, if the checkers have a piece of felt glued to their bottom. When a piece (men or king) is transferred to the "mirror board", it is just turned upside down.

Despite many similarities Alician Draughts is in fact a very different game. While in International Draughts more than 50% of all tournament games today end in a draw, in Alician Draughts a draw is impossible.

boardgame: Mascha und der Bär: Bühne frei für Mascha!
description:
Masha lives in a little cottage in the woods. With her fun, games and pranks, she really turns the idyllic life of her neighbour, a former circus bear, topsy-turvy. All the bear wishes for is a quiet life, but bubbly Masha keeps on causing havoc, making sure the bear’s life is full of action and adventure!
Today is Masha’s big day: the stage has been erected and all of her costumes are ready and waiting for her appearance. All of her friends are attending the show.
Arrange exciting scenes and make sure to pay attention since also you will play an important role in Masha’s play! Fans of Masha can playfully test their memory with three amusing memo games.

The game comes with three variants, and everytime you play, you choose the variant you prefer:
Variant 1 - Can you see the difference? 
Variant 2 - Memo
Variant 3 - True or false

The game ends as soon as each player has had four turns. The person with the most points after four turns wins the game.
family: TV Shows: Masha and the Bear

boardgame: Masha and the Bear: Trouble in the Garden
description:
Masha and Bear lavish care and attention on the fruit and vegetables in their garden: the strawberries, raspberries, carrots and all the other healthy delicacies. They love picking them and nibbling on them. But what’s THAT? Every night, uninvited guests get amongst the beds and help themselves to the fruit and vegetables. Who on earth can it be? Get your thinking caps on and start guessing!
Who knows which animal is going to cause havoc in Masha and the Bear’s garden next? The player to guess correctly the most times can advance his figure the furthest, and wins the game.

Each player selects one animal card. Each player places the selected animal card face-down in front of them. The other three animal cards are put to one side, also face-down.
Once all the players have decided on one animal and have put down the animal card in front of them, face-down, the youngest player takes the shaker, shakes it vigorously and then tips all the chips out onto the table. The chips must be separated such that the top side of the chip is clearly recognised.
Now each player turns their selected animal card face-up and compares it: How many animal chips can you see that show the same animal as depicted on your own card? You are allowed to move your figure precisely the same amount of fields up the game board.

The player whose figure is the first to reach the final field is the
winner.
family: TV Shows: Masha and the Bear

boardgame: Mascha und der Bär: Chaos vor der Party
description:
Tidy up the room along with Masha. Before the guests turn up, each and every object must be in its own place.

At the beginning of the game, each player is allowed to turn over one tile such that it is visible for all players. Once you have remembered your pictures, turn the tiles back face-down.
The youngest player casts the die and performs the action. The die is then cast in a clock-wise direction. Each die has a different action.
You all play together, so you are allowed to give each other tips.

Once all the objects are in their right places, face-up so that they can be seen, you have tidied up Masha’s room in time and, therefore, have won!
Once Bear, Rabbit and Squirrel reach the front door, you are allowed to turn over all the tiles. If all the objects happen to be in their right places, you have still won. If, however, you haven’t managed to tidy up in time, sorry but you have lost this round and the only thing to do is play another round!
family: TV Shows: Masha and the Bear

description:
You start the game with the enchanted witch broom. For this game, it's important to listen and react quickly, because the broom emits several sounds that are associated with different destinations.
Based on the destination cards and the enchanted witch broom you can play several different games of your choice where you can help Bibi Blocksberg break the enchantment of her broom.

boardgame: マタンガ！キッズ＆エキスパート (Matanga! Kids & Experts)
description: マタンガ！キッズ＆エキスパート (Matanga! Kids & Experts) allows younger players to get into the Matanga! challenge of marking out all the numbers on your scorecards first, with the scorecards featuring a wide variety of layouts for the numbers, including on clocks, the backs of bugs, and the midst of a whirlwind.

Throughout the history of mankind conflict is at the core of our evolution, evident in each generation with war and expansion of territories to show dominance of one empire over its rivals. Such aggression is not a choice of right or wrong, but is instilled into our DNA through the course of evolution.

This strategy game challenges players to execute military war tactics, allows for population growth, encourages development of city infrastructure to fuel the economy, forces long term rewards to be weighed against immediate benefits, and calls for the management of your civilization's resource expenditure. Winning is a combination of dice rolls and decision making. The key to success is using your economic engine to fuel growth and strategically engage in battles to weaken your opponent and strengthen your dominance on the map. Victory points are gained through killing enemy population, building a city capital, being the peace maker, acquiring special events, and conquering enemy land. No one strategy wins the game but is a combination of several of the victory points to win.

How the game is played section: Players have two choices at the start of their return, (1) Improve Economy or (2) Battle. Improve Economy allows 4 action items for the player; (1) Roll dice which generates resources for all players by collecting resource cards based on dice outcome and the location of their pieces on the game board, (2) Player draws their own event card which generates additional resource cards, (3) Using resources can purchase buildings, population, and increased military strength and placement of those pieces on board, (4) Tactical movement which allows for 1 unit to be moved on board.

Alternatively, Battle allows 4 action items (1) all military units can be moved on board, (2) Engagement in battle which results in offensive pieces on board attacking defensive pieces and outcome decided by die roll, (3) Acquire a special event card through dice outcome, (4) Using resources can purchase buildings, population, and increased military strength and placement of those pieces on board.

How are units killed and battle outcome decided section:
Offensive team rolls X number of dice based on the strength of their military, and outcome of dice is based on value rolled:
(1) capture civilian
(2) kill civilian
(3) kill military
(4) kill military
(5) nothing
(6) special event

Defensive team rolls dice, and outcome is based on these values:
(1) kill military
(2) kill military
(3) steal resource cards from offensive team(called Cost of War)
(4) nothing
(5) nothing
(6) nothing

Purchase of the following can be made:
Barracks; generates Military based on dice roll
Bank; generates resources cards based on dice roll
Fort; provide military defensive mechanism when being attacked
Fortification; provide first line of defense when being attacked
Civilians, allows for collection of resources based on dice roll
Infantry, allows player to initiate military attacks
Land purchase, allows player to control more tiles of board

How to win, first to 6(2-player game) or 7(3-player game) Victory points:
Manifest Destiny; placement of 6 civilians on 1 tile; 1 VP
Established Capital; all buildings + 6 civilians on 1 tile; 2 VP
Peacemaker; 2 unused special event cards, 1 VP
Conqueror; capture enemy owned tiles, 2 VP
Slaughterer; 10 kills by military; 2 VP
Genocide; 18 kills by military; 3 VP
Assimilation; capture enemy capital; 3 VP

セブン (Seven) is a reimplementation of 7 Symbols, and 7 Nations, a trick-taking game with a unique deck of 49 cards in which the cards are in seven suits with one suit going 1-7, the next 2-8, and so on up to the final suit of 7-13. Thus, the deck has seven 7s in it, and to win the game, you need to capture the most 7s.

セブン also includes rules to two other games played with the same deck.
In しまんちゅ (Shimanchu), players try to collect resources to assemble items that they can then sell to customers — assuming that the demand on the market exists, of course.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
You want to help Super 4 to defeat their enemies, the Black Baron, Shark Beard, Dr X and Baba-Cara? Your mission is to complete all ten tasks with Alex, Ruby, Gene and Étincelle in the four different worlds of Super 4 and to collect more gold in each of the worlds. But beware, in this game time is mercilessly against you. 
Super 4 is a cooperative game where you all win or you lose.
During the game, you can consult and help each other, however each player does not perform his turn alone, they do it together.
By throwing dice and moving in the board, you receive tasks that you have to fulfill one by one. Your objective is to fulfill all ten tasks.
If you get to complete the ten tasks and collect four key pads in gold, before time runs out, congratulations you have all won!

Description from the publisher:

The fight for the Morgan Cattle Company takes on a new twist!
Lula strikes a deal with the devil for the heart of the Morgan Cattle Company, someone is looking for Sloane and he won’t stop until he finds her, and the Eagle Wardens are desperate to reach Mayor Whateley. But why?

Dirty Deeds is Doomtown: Reloaded’s seventh Saddlebag Expansion. Featuring a complete playset of 21 new cards to customize your Doomtown decks, Dirty Deeds expands your options for takin’ over Gomorra, whether you’re looking to be shootin’, spell castin’, or just trying to show that you have more money and influence than the next person!

A simple kids' game about collecting monster girls of Monster High. The deck consists of 14 different girls, 4 of each. On your turn you play a card and draw a card. If you played a card that's already visible on the table (either in the centre or at another player) then you put the card on the top of the similar card(s) and place the stack in front of you. When a player starts their turn with a given number of stacks in front of them (2 players: 8 cards, 3 players: 6 cards and so on) the game ends and they've won!

S&T 301, Sept-Oct 2016

Kaiser’s War in the East is a strategic level simulation of World War I on the Eastern Front, 1914-17. This is a two-player game; One player controls the Central Powers, the other player controls the Allies. The concept of “victory points” (VP) is central to play of game. Players begin each scenario with a designated number of VP. They gain new VP as a result of achieving objectives; they lose VP when they lose units in combat. They may expend them to gain reinforcements and carry out certain actions. To win the game you must have more VP than your opponent, so play is a balancing act between expending VP and attaining objectives.

boardgame: LZ Albany: Ia Drang Valley 17-18 November 1965
description:
LZ Albany depicts the ambush of US air cavalry units by the North Vietnamese Army on 17 November 1965, during the Pleiku Campaign.

Turns cover one hour, with multiple impulses driven by a random chit draw. Additional chits add extra assets like artillery and air power. US and NVA platoons maneuver through areas of jungle, sawgrass, and clearings. Leaders magnify unit abilities but are vulnerable. The NVA player is bent on maximizing US losses before being overwhelmed by firepower. The US player must stabilize the situation, maximize NVA casualties, and evacuate wounded to prevent their falling into enemy hands.


Published with Modern War #24, Jul-Aug 2016
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
October War is a wargame of the 1973 Arab-Israeli War.

There are two Theaters of Operation: Sinai and Golan, both portrayed as a point to point system (since most of the major actions were fought over key points or lines of communications). There are two players in the game, one commanding the Israelis, and the other the Arabs, and the game emphasizes critical factors of the conflict, but allows for large units to break down into smaller units, representing organizational flexibility and cohesion. The game is driven by the drawing of event markers which represent major tactical and strategic factors that affected the course of the campaign historically.

Published with Modern War #25, Sept-Oct 2016

Modern War #27, Jan-Feb 2017

Modern Battles II is a grand tactical simulation of battles that were or could possibly be fought in the era since the fall of the Berlin Wall and into the near future. Players control actual and hypothetical combat units utilizing a wide range of technologies and forces to gain objectives. Modern Batles II includes two games: Kaliningrad, covering a hypothetical Russian clash with NATO forces at the Russian Baltic Fleet forward base, and Mosul, a clash between ISIS and Coalition forces in Iraq. There are two players in each game, commanding opposing forces. Both games use a main body of shared rules, and each also has its own scenario special instructions.

boardgame: Pirates Code II: Pirate Alliance and The Curse of the King
description:
User summary: 
パイレーツコード 2　-海賊同盟と呪いの王- (Pirates Code II: Pirate Alliance and The Curse of the King) is an expansion to Pirates Code, but you can also play this set alone.

This expansion adds the "Alliance Battle". This is a co-operative game players work together in pairs to decrypt the code. Alliance Battle rules are for four players only, but when you decipher a code in co-operation with a partner, the sense of accomplishment that you get becomes higher than just playing the base game.

In addition, the card called "Curse" appears in this set. It interferes with the action of the opponent so that you gain advantages, making Pirates Code even more fun to play.

Publisher's summary:
「パイレーツコード」の拡張セットです。拡張ではありますが、このセット単体だけでも遊ぶことが出来ます。
この拡張では、新しくペアで協力して暗号を解き明かす「同盟戦」ルールが加わります。 同盟戦ルールは4人専用となりますが、仲間と協力し見事推理を的中させたときの達成感は今までの勝利より、さらに一層の達成感を得ることができます。 また、「呪い」という自分に有利な行動を取るように相手の行動を制限する意地悪な効果を持つカードが登場し、パイレーツコードをより一層楽しいものにします。

boardgame: Deception: Murder in Hong Kong – Kickstarter Promo Pack
description:
This promo pack was available as a free bonus for kickstarter backers and at the Essen 2015 convention. It will not be in the retail edition of the game. It was also made available during the 2017 kickstarter campaign Deception: Undercover Allies expansion as an add-on.

Includes the following:

1 new role: Consulting Detective
2 new clue cards: Password and Website
10 new means cards: 
Game Pieces
Shipping Container
Dice Tower
Microphone
Spoiled Food
Poisonous Mushrooms
Lab Sample
Anthrax
Ladder
Elevator Shaft
family: Game: Deception – Murder in Hong Kong

description:
Once this country knew of a genius merchant called the "one-eyed merchant", who regularly combined two products into a single auction to cross-sell them to the public, a process by which he built up enormous wealth.

In 片目の商人 (One-Eyed Merchant), each player bids on "cross-sold" commodities at the market, trying to gain the most money and become something of a "one-eyed merchant" themself.

User summary:
ブライトゥンザライフ (Brighten the Life)

DNA damage pouring on the earth. The survival of the fittest. Fierce and merciless battles between living creatures. How will you survive? By breeding, preying upon others, or evolution?

Whether by breeding or preying upon others, living creatures manage to thrive by making the most of their abilities. Furthermore, in the long history of evolution, they've managed to evolve by overcoming the DNA mutation in the ever-changing global environment. We've made a game with a theme of such life.

The future changes every moment according to the surrounding environment. Living creatures must simply choose the best move.

Brighten the Life is the game to make all living creatures shine most brilliantly.

Publisher's summary:
地球に降り注ぐDNA ダメージ。弱肉強食。生き残りを賭けた生き物同士の無慈悲で壮絶な争い。生き残る術は増殖か、捕食か、進化か。
生き物は増殖が得意なもの、捕食が得意なもの、それぞれ自分の持つ能力を最大限に生かすことで繁栄を勝ち得ている。また、長い進化の歴史の中で、生き物は地球環境によるDNA 変異を受けながらも、それを克服することで進化してきた。そんな地球上の生き物の営みをゲームにした。
未来はとりまく環境によって刻々と変化する。いま生き物ができることは、いま最良の行動を選択するのみ。
生き物を最高に輝かせるゲーム「Brighten The Life」
publishers: ワイビーワイゲームズ　(YbY Games)

description:
User summary:
スペースメンカム! (Spacemen Come!)

To protect the Earth from the attack of the irrational aliens from outer space, it's vital to detect your own talent and make it bloom!

When you manage to unlock your innate talent and master your way, you'll rock the universe.

Publisher's summary:
常識が通用しない宇宙人の襲撃から地球を守る。頼れるのは自らの才能を見抜くこと、そしてその才能を開花させろ！
才能が開花し、その道を極めたとき、宇宙は震撼する。
publishers: ワイビーワイゲームズ　(YbY Games)

From the publisher's website:

The Fleet mini-expansion includes 8 Salty Captain Cards, 8 Inuit Fisherman Cards, 3 Wharfside Casino Cards, and 3 Nunavut Weather Authority cards.

description:
600 is a Yahtzee-like dice game played with six regular dice.

Players are trying to earn the highest score in 18 rounds in 4 categories: Numbers, Rungs, Clusters, and Patterns. On their turn, a player rolls all 6 dice up to 3 times and then enters the result in any free row. After 18 rounds have been played, the player with the most points wins.

Included in Reiner Knizia's book Dice Games Properly Explained.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The Frostbyte Ravagers warband box brings the unparalleled brutality of the orcs to Super Dungeon for the first time! Rampaging through the Frostbyte Reach, the savage hordes have spilled out into Crystalia. Combining potent offense and a hardy resilience, the orcs are driven into further frenzy by the ferocious Rage Fiend Mini-Boss. Standing in their way is the Lightning Mage, whose crackling bolts arc through multiple foes with a single blast.

CONTENTS:
Lightning Mage Hero Model
Rage Fiend Mini-Boss Model
14 Orc Monster Models: 2 Skull Stone Spawning Points, 4 Neck Splitters, 4 Face Beaters, 4 Knuckle Smashers
Game Cards: Hero, Classic Monsters, Arcade Monsters, Treasure Card

The Crown Guard warband represents the doughty men and women who join their local village militias, town watches, or regional military. Devoted to combined arms and defense, the Crown Guard can protect weaker monsters around them, shielding them from attacks. Occasionally, the Crown Guard are even forced to maintain the peace against rambunctious Heroes, or—in more dire situations—turn to the service of the Dark Consul. While orcs are ferocious, they are not unintelligent, and the Crook-Eye Tracker has recognized the threat of the Dark Consul, lending his considerable survival and hunting skills to the good of the land.

CONTENTS:
Crook-Eye Tracker Hero Model
Royal Knight Mini-Boss Model
14 Crown Guard Monster Models: 2 Watchtower Spawning Points, 2 Sergeant-at-Arms, 4 Longbowmen, 4 Swordsmen, 4 Pikemen
Game Cards: Hero, Classic Monsters, Arcade Monsters, Treasure Card

The Midnight Tower, the Arcade Mode standalone game reveals the dreaded armies of The Midnight Queen. Armies of baroque-armored Nether Elves issue forth from the corrupted Tower of the Goddess. Within their ranks, cursed Spider Speakers summon scuttling hordes of spiders, spinning webs of shadow and poisoning their foes. Alongside them march powerful Knights of the Tower on spectral steeds, while Tower Stalkers lurk in the shadows weakening the mightiest Heroes, before their fellows strike the final blow. Three new Heroes take up the call to adventure: the rugged Hearthsworn Tincan, sneaky Cat Burglar, and brutal Riftling Warrior.

Super Dungeon: Arcade is a mighty fusion of The Midnight Tower Level Box, Midnight Tower Dungeon Tiles, and Super Dungeon: Arcade rules.

boardgame: Secrets of the Lost Tomb: Fate's Fortune
description:
Carpe Diem and Vae Victis Brothers and Sisters of the Order!

For thousands of years Fate has had a plan for you. As a member of the Eternal Order of Perseus you know your destiny has been written… your path chosen. You are tied to the of ones who came before… you’re A Bastion of Hope… A Beacon in the Darkness... an Ender of Doom, a Saver of Souls, you are Fate’s Crusader… you’re on a quest… and you’re the one who can sense what needs to be done to reach your true potential.

Fate can not be chosen, it is set and those who fight against fate betray their destiny… if destiny is betrayed then your quest becomes tainted and you may become a Traitor!

In Secrets of the Lost Tomb: Fate's Fortune, as you explore the Tomb, each adventurer will begin the game with a Fate Card sending you on a secret quest, that upon completion, unlocks your Adventurer’s True Power! On the back of each Adventurer Sheet, there is now a unique Ascended Adventurer with their own Unique Equipment Card, Upgraded Attributes, Abilities, and Courage Track! In addition, all Ascended Adventurers have their own unique alternate artwork and standee, rewarding the player’s successful completion of their quest!

But be warned, some of these quests can optionally be “Traitor Quests”, when this player completes their quest a harsh crisis will fall upon the other Adventurers as the newly revealed Traitor turns on their former friends and joins the side of Evil in the active scenario.

Are you brave enough to fight for your future... are you prepared to meet your destiny when faced with… Fate’s Fortune?
family: Game: Secrets of the Lost Tomb

description:
A challenging and fun game with many quizes developed in collaboration with the editors of Focus Junior.
Over 1000 questions designed for children in school age on many subjects like Science, Geography, Curiosity, Sports, Entertainment and more.
Very original and surprising game mechanics where everyone must bet, in fact, in the response of the opponent!

Tinker Tactics is a free, abstract microgame for 2 or 4 players and takes around 5-10 minutes to play.

Rules:


     1D8, 2D6, 2D4
     Each player rolls dice but hides them from opponent
     Each player announces score. All players adjust dice to match the highest score (except highest scorer)
     All players arrange dice as they wish on their board
     Players reveal and connect board to each other
     Players take it in turn to move each die. Die move the same number of spaces as pips on the die
     They can move across filled spaces but must land on empty space
     Dice cannot move diagonally, nor are diagonally situated dice affected by enemy dice
     Any enemy dice adjacent reduce number on die by 1
     Any dice reduced to 0 are removed from the board
     A player is eliminated from the game when they have only 1 die left
     The last player remaining is the winner

boardgame: The Three Pillars: GameHoleCon Promo Card
description: Promo Card for The Three Pillars: A Paranormal Card Game. Game text reads, "Plays in your evidence pool. This card is worth +1 O at the end of the game. May not be canceled." This was a promo card available to attendees of GameHoleCon 2015.

User summary:
決闘師カードゲーム (Kettousi Cardgame) means The Duelist Cardgame in English.

In the game of Kettousi Cardgame, two characters fight each other with character-specific decks of 18 cards each.

You play a card to move yourself or force your opponent move in 5 x 5 square grid board.

You win a round if your opponent cannot play a card, and you win the game if you win three rounds.

Publisher's summary:
それぞれ18枚、非対称のデッキを使う2人用対戦カードゲームです。
カードをプレイすることで自分のコマを移動。または相手のコマを移動させることができなくなったプレイヤーがそのラウンドを落とします。
先に勝利点カードを3枚手にしたほうが勝ちます。

User summary:
    
In the game of ほとり (Hotori), you are a commander of a battle and your goal is to gain strategic advantages over your opponents. You earn points by deploying soldiers and building citadels in the right places. The player with the most points wins the game.

Publisher's summary:

場に並べたカード「戦場」に兵士コマを配置して、
その配置した地形の価値を上げる。
もしくは価値の高いカードに兵士コマを配置することで得点を競うゲームです。

From the box:
"Learn about the exciting, mysterious and adventurous world of literature and the arts as you play this fast-paced quiz game!  Each card contains five clues.  Answer on the first clue and you earn five points.  If you answer after all five clues (including a picture clue), you still earn a point!  The player with the most points at the end of the game wins!"

One player starts by drawing a card depicting a famous artist or work of art (concealing the picture on the front and covering the clues one the back).  He or she reads the first clue the player to the left.  If that player answers, he or she earns five points.  If not, the first player reads the next clue to the next player.  This sequence continues until a player answers correctly, earning a decreasing number of points depending on how many clues have been revealed.  The final clue is the picture on the front of the card.  Once a card has been solved, the player earning the points draws the next card, and play continues until all cards are used.  The player with the most points wins.

Animal Match Puzzle Cards is a matching game designed to teach reading skill to young children. It consists of a series of cards depicting animals and matching puzzle pieces showing the written names of the animals. The picture cards are placed face-up on the table. The name pieces are stacked face-down. Players take turns choosing a name piece. They must then read the word and correctly match it to its animal picture card. If the puzzle piece fits the card, the player is correct and keeps the pair. If not, the name piece is placed back on the bottom of the pile and the animal card is returned to its place. Play continues until all pairs are claimed, and the player with the most pairs wins.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Jack O' Lantern Promo
description:
The 2015 Halloween Promo Gear card was included with the 2015 Halloween Special Pinup Twilight Knight and available only during the 2015 and 2016 Halloween weekends.

The figure itself is not part of the game.

Gear Recipe
Cost: 1x Skull, 1x Scrap, 1x Organ
Innovation Requirements: Momento Mori

From the box:
"Tired tigers, ruffled rabbits, wiggly worms and others get together to make a very funny learning game.  Your children will play again and again by themselves by themselves or with friends, at home or in the car.  They listen to the Tongue Twisters on the cassette and match them to their lotto board.

To play, each person takes a lotto board and nine coloured tokens.  Start the cassette at any point on either side for a different game every time.  Find the picture of the Tongue Twister and cover it with a token.  Cover all your pictures to win.  For a real challenge, use the letters only side of the board."

Player of the Game Basketball is designed to provide a quick method of resolving a basketball game between any two teams. Where this game is truly unique is in its ability to not only provide you with a final score, but it will also give you individual point, rebound and assist totals for each player that participates in the game.

Once you understand the concepts behind the game, you’ll be able to play out a full game in 5 minutes for the basic game, and 7-10 minutes for the advanced game.

The game offers two levels of complexity. For those gamers that wish to use Player of the Game Basketball in conjunction with another basketball title that resolves games in a possession by possession manner, the basic version will more than likely provide you with all of the detail that you require. Since the basic version plays quicker, it should be a great tool for single team replayers that want to get a snapshot of the rest of the league.

The advanced version of the game is designed for gamers that wish to conduct a series, or a complete season using only Player of the Game Basketball. The advanced version will provide you with hot and cold streaks for players, as well as a realistic game pace factor that will provide you with a realistic method of reproducing the high and lower scoring affairs that occur in basketball, all with their real-life frequency. The advanced version is a great method of playing cross-era teams to see who was truly better.

You will need 2 D10, not included with the game.  You use the d10 rolls to determine which players on a given team will play in a game and what his potential scoring total is.  Additional rolls are used to calculate adjustments due to defense, home court advantage and other factors.

This game is a snakes and ladder kind of race with a twist.
Set inside a computer, the goal is to race from start to finish. Special face-down tiles are placed on the board with various chaotic effects. Movement is driven by cards and players will get attacked or powered up by viruses along the way.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: In Authentic Professional Football Game, players use a stack of cards to simulate the plays in a football game. The offensive player chooses his play and then picks a card. Each card lists a result for all possible plays. The player checks his chosen play and applies the result by moving a football marker on a football field board. Downs, first downs and scoring work as they do in American football. Kicks (kickoffs and punts) are resolved by drawing one card for the kick distance and another for the return distance. When a play results in a penalty, interception or too-short field goal, another card is drawn to show the result. When all the cards in the stack are used up, a quarter ends. The deck is then shuffled and the next quarter begins. The second half (or third quarter) begins with a kickoff. As in a typical football game, the player with the most points after four quarters wins.

1964 Canadian abstract game with a colorful hexagonal board.

Each player has pieces of three different colours, which behave differently if they are on "home territory" or "enemy lines" (different colours).

The goal of the game is to send your pieces to the central "fortress" hexagon, but you can be sent back to your home square by an "assault" of an enemy piece.

Cthuhulu awakens, promptly dies from pollution, and his corpse washes up on the Jersey shore. You're a boardwalk bro looking to impress some babes, but these girls are demanding you grab some tentacles off that nasty dome and don't care about the stuffed banana you won at ring toss! Keep grabbing tentacles until you either give up or go crazy in this pub style push your luck card game.

There are three types of cards: tentacles, junk, and madness. Players want to get as many tentacle cards as possible to win the round, but anyone who draws a madness card goes crazy and is out. Dawn cards are kept face down, so players don't know how many of anyone else's cards are tentacles, and how many are junk. First to win three rounds, wins.

The Kickstarter Edition includes the Metropolis Expansion, which comprises 40 new building cards and 2 extra Reputation cards.


In Islebound, you take command of a ship and crew. You sail to island towns, collecting resources, hiring crew, and commissioning buildings for your capital city. Each building has a unique ability, and your combination of buildings can greatly enhance your strength as a trader, builder, or invader. You also recruit pirates and sea monsters to conquer towns, which, once conquered, allow you to complete the town action for free, and charge a fee to opponents if they want to use it. Alternatively, you can complete events that give influence, which can be used to befriend towns.

There are many routes to success. Will you be a ruthless conqueror, careful diplomat, or shrewd merchant in your race to the top?

The player with the most wealth and most-impressive capital city will win the game!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Le zug is played on the dark squares of a 17x17 board (the four edge squares must be dark). A game starts with 12 pieces per player. Nine pieces of each color are placed on the first and the last row. In the first move (each player's first turn) the players put their three remaining pieces on the row next to their other pieces in any order they want them.
 After that players move at their turn one of their pieces diagonally forward to an adjacent empty square. Players also can jump over their own pieces or over those of their opponent (even combining both colors) similar to Halma but only diagonally forward. However, they don't have to jump, if they don't want to, and they can stop a jumping move anywhere, not making the largest jump possible. After the last jump they are allowed to move the piece, which did the jump, one square diagonally forward to an adjacent empty square. Again, this is not mandatory. No pieces are ever captured and the jumped pieces remain untouched.

The first four rows of each player are called their "railway station" ("gare" in French; "Bahnhof" in German). Players are not permitted to enter their opponent's railway station, before they have vaca­ted their own one. After a player has entered all of his pieces into the opponent's railway station, he is allowed to move or jump backward to reach the final goal position, but only as long as all of his pieces remain in the opponent's railway station.

The object of the game is to place nine pieces on the last row and the remaining three pieces anywhere on the penultimate row, that is to recreate a valid start position on the opponent's side of the board.

The name of the game is French and German mixed together ("le" is the masculine French article; "zug" means train in German). The game is meant to celebrate friendship between the French and the German peoples after centuries of wars fought between them. It is related to Halma and Salta. Le zug was first described in the book "Le livre de tous les jeux" (Edition Solar), which was published in France on January 1, 1989.

This 76 page supplement includes additional scenarios and rules for the Skirmish Sangin miniatures game. The book includes six new scenarios, additional rules for JTAC’s, Helicopter, Drones and Combat Engineers. It also contains a new 16 page Taliban ORBAT (order of battle) that includes everything you need to know about building a Taliban Insurgent force, including Chechen allies.

boardgame: Maalintii Rangers: Day of the Rangers – A Skirmish Sangin Battle Book
description: This 84 page Battle Book includes additional scenarios and rules for the Skirmish Sangin miniatures game. The book includes the key scenarios and all the additional rules to re-enact the Battle of Mogadishu - a battle made world famous, first by the book, then the film of Black Hawk Down.

Congregate is a social political game where players use various Representatives to accomplish their hidden Agendas in a series of votes. Each Session (round) a player will choose a Proposition for all others to debate and vote on. Players augment their votes with different types of Representatives, from the highly influential Councilors to the destructive Spies. Whatever the approach, players must do anything in their power to accomplish their hidden Agendas and amass the most wealth.

This game is a variation of the classic Memory.

With the stroke of their paintbrush, the old masters changed the art of painting around the sixteenth and seventeenth century. But also in the centuries preceding and following that, they pioneered with their characteristic painting styles and emotional depictions on canvas. The power of paint, colors, brushes and spatulas, combined with the genius of the painters created a new world, illusive in between reality and abstraction.

Old Master Memory Game challenges you to expand your knowledge and refresh your memory regarding the old masters. In the Old Masters Memory Game you seek and match two different portrait paintings from the same painter. One set of two cards always offers a clear relationship or clues. These vary from painting style to visible attributes such as colors, flowers and head dresses, or color and facial expression.

Clinic Expansion: Medical Dossier 3, the third expansion for the CliniC base game, contains:


     5 Virus + new orange and red cubes
     Public works tiles
     Pregnant women
     Satellite dish tiles


The ICU PnP tiles and rules are included in this last dossier as well as an official solo variant.

boardgame: For Queen and Planet: The Imperial Wars of Earth and Mars 1845 thru 1930 – The Compendium
description:
The sweep of the red Sudanese desert, scattered with thorny trees and pierced by black basalt outcrops is the setting for this cinematic rule set. The whirling dervish, the fanatical Ansar and the lonely British foot soldier far from home are the combatants.

With these rules, you can take the role of the British commander tasked by the Queen with maintaining crown rule in distant, inhospitable lands or the local emir, committed to exercising his own free will over the natives who will fight with all their blood, sweat and tears.

For Queen and Planet is designed to balance the mass and ferocity of the local peoples against the technology and training of the Colonial powers and to provide a fair and reasonable struggle for both imperial General and local Emir.

For Queen and Planet - Making Colonial Wargaming fun!
This rule set includes:

All rules necessary to play either a colonial or VSF version of the game.
An alternative timeline for VSF genre play.
Unit lists detailing British, British-colonial, Ansar, Beja and Cephalopod forces.
A nine-scenario campaign that will allow you to play your way through the Mahdi’s initial rebellion against Egyptian garrison forces all the way through to the siege of Khartoum.
A quick reference sheet for most common information.
A one-page how to do things to help you get comfortable with the rules.
A blank unit command sheet.

CAMPAIGN SCENARIOS

Each of the scenarios in this rulebook is written to be fought as a pure colonial battle, but it is very easy to add a VSF flare to any scenario or battle you fight with this rule system. I have included an addendum (in italics) to each ‘colonial’ battle to allow you to make it a VSF battle. Play balance will not be affected by converting colonial battles to VSF battles.

The Rise of the Mahdi Scenarios:

1) First Battle of El Obeid - Dec 1881 Egyptian vs. Ansar
2) Second Battle of El Obeid (or Hick's Folly) - Nov 1883 - British lead Egyptians vs. Ansar
3) First Battle of El Teb (or Baker's Teb) - Feb 4, 1884 - British lead Egyptians vs. Beja
4) Second Battle of El Teb - Feb 29, 1884 - British vs. Beja
5) Battle of Tamai - March 13, 1884 - British vs. Beja
6) Siege of Khartoum - ~May 1884 - Ansar sieging British lead Egyptians
7) The Battle of Abu Klea - Jan 17, 1885 - British vs. Ansar
8) The Battle of Kirbekan - Feb 10, 1885 - British vs. Ansar
9) The Battle of Tofrek - March 22, 1885 - British vs. Beja/Ansar

Join the quest and help the monkey pirates find their long-lost buried treasure. Feel for the hidden treasure shapes within the cloth island and guide them out through beach, waterfall or jungle openings. Quickly collect your treasures before the volcano erupts!

Train Line: Chicago Edition is a multi- player card base strategy board game that will test your wits, competitive spirit, board navigation, and card management skills. You utilize 3 elements on two boards on you quest to get the required amount of pawns in seated spaces before your opponents. You use the boarding die to determine entry to train or lost of turn, number cards to move along board spaces or action cards; cards with specialized abilities to increase your chances of being victorious.

This is a very simple, child memory game, which is based on Hungarian nursery rhymes. There are different figures of nursery rhymes on the game tokens.

Contents:

36 tokens
5 big tokens
1 die

In the setup you get 5 tokens and place them on the game field, and then you cover them with 5 big tokens.

If it's your turn you need to roll the die. Then you need to guess which token is under the big token that has the same color as your roll. If you can guess it, you get the token, if you make a mistake you don't get anything.

The game ends when all tokens are given out.

The player with the most tokens is the winner of the game.

Teblo is an abstract strategy game. It is played on a square board (12x12). Each player has a pawn and 25 square pieces.

The board shows 4 colored starting squares. Players start their pawn on their own starting square, diagonally opposite to each other. Their goal is to reach to starting square of the opponent.

On his turn a player can either move his pawn horizontally or vertically or place one of his square pieces on the board.
These pieces can only be placed on the white squares. Pawn can enter the colored squares too.

The square pieces block the opponents route to the goal square, but not your own (you can place your pawn on top of it and pass your own piece in the next turn).

A player may never block the goal square completely.

You win as soon as you reach the goal square with your pawn.

Adijuh (Адиюх) is a rather new game, which combines the rules of Russian checkers, stacking and a unique way to win. The game was created by Viktor Pankovich in 2007. It has become quite popular in Russia, where a lot of discussion is going on at several game forums. There are postal tournaments and numerous problems have been composed recently that show the beauty of this game.

The game has basically the same rules as Russian Checkers. However, captured pieces are placed beneath the capturing piece and so columns are formed (similar to Bashni). A king can only move if it captures. After the capture the king becomes a normal piece again unless it ends its turn on the opponent's back row. The game stops if a player cannot make a move. The winner is the player who has got more pieces of either color in the opponent's half of the board. The sum of all points is always 24.

Adijuh is played with a standard Chess board and Checkers pieces.

To play Bid, Bluff & Buy, you don't have to "know the facts" to win. . . you just need to be able to bluff, cajole and have fun. The game consists of 20 different auctions in five different genres: Music, Movies, Television, Sports and History. Players begin each auction with $30,000 and try to accumulate the most wealth by buying items in the auctions with the highest value. The items in this game were actually sold in a real auction and the Estimate, Opening Bid and Sold Price were the prices recorded at the auction on the date identified on the card and in the catalog.
 Whoever has the most wealth at the end of the auction wins the game. However, if the Auction House has the most wealth, then the House wins!

Pyramid Arcade is a compilation of 22 games that can be played with Looney Pyramids and assorted other components. Looney Pyramids are colored pyramids in three sizes that can nest together.

Pyramid Arcade focuses on games that can be played with three or fewer trees of each color, and includes ten colors of pyramids (ninety in all). It also includes numerous boards, nine dice, and a rulebook explaining all the games.

The individual games are:

     Black Ice
     Color Wheel
     Give or Take
     Hijinks
     Homeworlds
     IceDice
     IceTowers
     Launchpad 23
     Looney Ludo
     Lunar Invaders
     Martian Chess
     Petal Battle
     Petri Dish
     Powerhouse
     Pharaoh
     Pyramid Shambo
     Treehouse
     Twin Win
     Verticality
     Volcano (v3.0, aka Fiesta Caldera)
     World War 5
     Zark City

We are pressed on all sides and the enemy is closing in. Word has come down from the Commander-in-Chief and our only order is "Attack!"

From designers Mike Selinker and Sean Brown comes the Attack! Deluxe expansion for Glenn Drover's Attack!, which adds to the base game by setting the stage for combat on the Pacific Front. Attack! Deluxe, which replaces the out-of-print Attack! Deluxe Expansion, places you squarely on the frontlines as you vie for world domination. Speak softly or swing your big stick in order to conquer your neighbors and expand your economy.

Attack! Deluxe introduces bold new mechanisms with expanded Naval Combat, player government styles, new technology research, and seasonal combat. As the leader of a world power, you can choose to control trade routes on all seven seas or you can blitz the sovereign shores of your nearest nations by land: build the biggest army and crush your enemies before they crush you, or choke the life out of them by cutting their economic supply. All is fair in love and war after all!

The cards in Attack! Deluxe replace all of the Attack! base game cards to ensure uniformity, with these cards having better quality base card stock and linen finish with playing card varnish. Attack! Deluxe includes a plastic insert tray to hold all of the pieces from both Attack! and Attack! Deluxe. To use Attack! Deluxe, you need these components from Attack!:


    Western Theater game board
    678 Army Unit miniatures (six sets of 113 pieces in six colors: infantry, artillery, tanks and planes)
    8 Red Army battle Dice
    2 Blue Order dice

Hauska Kiusa ("Funny Tease" in english) is an childrens card game from early 1980s. The idea is to divide the cards evenly between two, three or four players leaving the "Kiusa"-card at the center of the table.
The players, in turns, play the cards by rules. The first player without any cards in hand wins. Who has "Kiusa"-card in hand loses (he/she gets minus twenty five points), other players get one minus point for every card they have in hand.

boardgame: 猟奇耽異-キュリオシティ・ハンティング (Curiosity Hunting)
description: 猟奇耽異-キュリオシティ・ハンティング (Curiosity Hunting) is an expansion for Phantom Detectives that provides personalities for each of the twelve suspect cards, with those personalities translating in game terms to special abilities.

Stunde Null is the German language equivalent of "zero hour", a term taken from military planning that indicates the beginning of some operation or event. In Germany, Stunde Null has a particular reference: midnight on May 8, 1945, when the capitulation of the Nazi forces' high command came into effect, marking the end of World War II in Europe. The period immediately thereafter became known as the Nachkriegszeit ("time after the war").

In this game, the players asume the role of the reconstruction of Germany after the World War II, managing action cards, construction cards and event cards, that come into play using dices. At the end of the game, the player who has built more prestigious buildings will be the winner.



SPANISH:

Stunde Null es el equivalente en alemán de "la hora cero", un término prestado de la planificación militar que indica el comienzo de una operación o evento. En Alemania, Stunde Null tiene una referencia particular: medianoche del 8 de mayo de 1945, cuando capitularon las fuerzas nazis, dando fin así a la Segunda Guerra Mundial en Europa. El período inmediatamente posterior se conoce como Nachkriegszeit ("tiempo después de la guerra").

En este juego, los jugadores asumen el papel de reconstruir Alemania después de la Segunda Guerra Mundial, mediante la gestión de cartas de acción, construcción y sucesos, que se ponen en juego mediante el uso de dados. Al final de la partida, aquel que haya construido más edificios de prestigio será el vencedor.

Set during the Edo period, 御用達 (Royal Warrant) pits the player merchants against one another in a castle town rules by a tiger lord. Players need to bribe influential people with their five bidding tokens (numbered 1-5) in order to acquire goods and deliver them to samurai.

Publisher's summary:
Players will be merchants of the animal land in fictional Japanese Edo era. In this game, they compete for the leader position. Each player has chips that are assigned value of 1-5 and they bid a chip, hiding the value each round.

In turn, each player puts a chip on any area of the board. After each player has put all their five chips on the board, the value of chips are revealed and the totals compared in each area. The player who put the highest value can use the special effect of the area. The effects are various. For example, you can get goods, you can win victory points, and so on. Once six rounds are over, the player with the most victory points is the winner.

Publisher's summary:
江戸時代の動物の国の商人になって、地位を競うゲームです。
各自1～5のチップを持っており、それを使ったブラインドの競りを毎ラウンド行います。手番が来たら自分のチップを1枚ボード上の好きなエリアに置きます。
全員が5枚のチップを置いたら、各エリアの数字を比べて大きい人がそのエリアの権利を得ます。商品を獲得したり、それを使って勝利点を獲得したりとエリアの効果は様々です。
6ラウンド終了時に、最も勝利点を獲得しているプレイヤーが勝者です。
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Cards Against Humanity: Eight Sensible Gifts for Hanukkah was a holiday themed subscription in 2015 in which participants received 8 gags and gifts in the mail and worked together to solve a massive online puzzle.  The gifts include 2 packs of cards - Jew Pack Part One and Jew Pack Part Two.

Night 4: Jew Pack Part One

     White: 13
     Black: 2


Night 7: Jew Pack Part Two

     White: 12
     Black: 3
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
This supplement for the epic space strategy game Quantum includes alternate rules, bonus maps and answers to Frequently Asked Questions about Quantum.

Contains:

     Rules - Quantum Entanglement rules
     Expert F.A.Q. - additional F.A.Q.
     Command Card Tips - several useful tips for Command cards
     New Maps - 6 new maps specifically designed for shorter, intense games and for use with the Quantum Entanglement alternate rules.
     Map Revisions - revised BETA SECTOR and NEXUS maps
     Curious - revised Curious Command card


These new rules and revisions (including the Command card) were included in the revised edition of Quantum. If you have the revised edition, you do not need this supplement.

In Volcano Cove two warring nations discover gold mines within a disputed cove. To win the game, sink all enemy ships, or be the first to earn 7 gold. But watch out for the volcano. If your opponent’s prayers to the volcano gods are successful, lava could engulf your fleet.

Volcano Cove is a beautiful 2-player game that packs a large punch. Games last 10-15 minutes while providing drama and strategic decisions around every turn. Since there are multiple paths to victory, you’ll need to watch your opponent carefully and look for weaknesses you can exploit.

Both players share a zero-sum Gold Track. When you earn 1 gold your opponent loses 1 gold, and vice versa. This means when you buy extra ships or movement, you are giving your opponent gold in exchange for the advantage. You earn gold by sinking enemy ships, occupying gold mine spaces, having 3 Battle Coins at the end of your turn, and by praying to the volcano gods and successfully causing an eruption.

You start your turn by building your fleet, sailing, and declaring any battles you’d like. When fighting, your Battle Strength is equal to the number of coins that make up your ship (ships can be built up to 3 coins high) plus any Battle Coins you commit. Three Battle Coins float between both players throughout the game. If you commit a Battle Coin to a battle, you have a higher Battle Strength for the current battle, but you must pass any committed Battle Coins to your opponent.

After the Battle Phase, your opponent decides if he or she wants to pray to the volcano gods. If your opponent prays, he or she shakes 3 Prayer Coins. The number of heads shaken moves the Volcano Track marker, which can cause an eruption. Ships within the Lava Danger Zone when the volcano erupts take damage depending on the severity of the eruption. After the Pray Phase, you end you turn by earning gold from any gold mine spaces you still occupy. Then play passes to the next player.

To create your free print-and-play copy of Volcano Cove, print the board and rules at VolcanoCove.com and find 10 pennies, 10 nickels, 5 dimes, and 3 quarters. You now have everything you need to challenge someone to your first game.

EQUIPMENT: one 8-sided die; 4 playing pieces of four different colors; and the board, consisting of an oval, three-lane track with a separate, ten-space track in the center. What follows is my reconstruction of the rules.

SET UP: players group their pieces on the 'start' space of the central track. Turns are in alphabetical order of the colors.

GOAL: to get to the opposite side of the central track, the 'finish' space, a mere nine spaces away. An exact throw is not needed.

MOVEMENT: players take turns rolling the d8, moving that many spaces counter-clockwise. Several spaces are connected by arrows-if you land on the end of one, go to its head; if on its head, go to the tail. If you land on another player, that person goes back to 'start.' Landing on the fifth space of the central track also sends one back to 'start.'

EQUIPMENT: a 48-card deck consisting of four suits: Fly Away Jill, Fly Away Jack, Come Back Jill, Come Back Jack. Each contains 12 cards ranked (lowest to highest) 1-9 plus A, B, C.

SET UP: shuffle the cards and deal evenly to all players. Person to left of dealer goes first.

PLAY: The first player lays down a card and the other players must follow suit-the highest card played (of the leading suit) wins the 'trick' (all played cards) and leads the next card. When all cards have been played scores are tallied. ONLY Fly Away Jacks are counted.

GOAL: To be the person with the LOWEST score after three rounds-meaning, having taken the FEWEST Fly Away Jacks.

boardgame: Castle Assault: Sir Mitchell Greencastle Promo Card
description:
Promo Card for Castle Assault.

Epic Neutral Unit
Cost: 3
Stats: 2, 4, 0, 1
Ranged Linear (1-6)

boardgame: Castle Assault: Dagger of Ill Omen Promo Card
description:
Promo Card for Castle Assault.

Epic Neutral Command (Attachment)
Cost: 1
Target Unit gains: Loses 1 Health at the End of its turn

boardgame: One Hit Kill: Death & Lore Expansion
description:
One Hit Kill: Death & Lore Expansion brings two new components, two associated special cards and 16 new weapons to build. Expansion pack supports up to six players.

Every expansion pack requires the Core deck. Most weapons in an expansion pack use a mix of core classes and expansion classes.

To use an expansion pack, shuffle its white cards in with the Core white cards. Do the same with the weapons. Rules and game objective same as Base game.

This is a Colorforms game where the object is to be the first to completely fill your Treehouse with all four missing Norfin Trolls. The trolls are Colorforms that stick on the Treehouse. Comes with 4 Treehouse cards with stands, Board with built-in spinner, and Colorform pieces.

Player spins so the spinner points to one of the four Norfin Trolls. Player adds the matching troll to their Treehouse. If there is no matching troll or they already have the matching troll, play moves to the next player. The first player to fill their Treehouse wins.

Snowball Fight is a micro deckbuilding and deduction game for two players that only contains 18 cards.  In the game players go head-to-head with their arsenal of snowballs in an attempt to be the ruler of the Blizzard Realm!

In Snowball Fight each card has two possible actions, one on each half of the card (top and bottom).  The action a player takes each turn depends on the direction they play the card. The game is played in two phases, a quick Drafting phase where players build their initial arsenal and then the Snow Assault phase where there is all out war! Play is simultaneous and players try to attack each other with an arsenal of snowballs, slushballs, and the dreaded iceballs and snow covered rocks while also trying to dodge attacks and build up their weaponry!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Expedition Planning is a hand management and blind-bidding game for 2 to 4 players.

You are the Captain of a prestigious explorers' guild. As their leader, you will send your explorers on various expeditions in order to retrieve the earth's most valuable treasures. All of this in order to increase your guild's prestige. You will have to do this while avoiding or fighting off as many dangers as possible because losing against those ferocious creatures would tarnish your reputation!

Each of the items you are after is part of these ancient collections. If your explorers are able to retrieve all treasures in order to form a complete collection, they become even more valuable!

Ready your explorers, but make the right choice on who you send as other explorers are after the same Treasures! Whoever has the most prestigious guild in the end wins at this game!

Goal of the Game

Over the course of 4 rounds, you will play Explorer cards from your hand in order to win Treasure cards that are worth Victory Points (VPs), while avoiding Danger cards that are worth negative VPs. At the end of the 4 rounds, the player with the most Victory Points is declared the winner.

Car Wars Arenas 2 gives hardcore autoduelists more exciting locations to showcase their skills and blow away their opponents. The bagged set contains two double-sided 22" x 34" arena maps and a booklet detailing the special features of each arena.

The arena designs in Car Wars Arenas have appeared in The AADA Duel Circuit: L'Outrance and Autoduel Quarterly, but only as scaled-down maps. In this set, they come out of the box ready to play in full Car Wars Classic scale.

Life is Card is a deceptively simple social card game where everyone playing decides on a scenario and goal and then proceeds to attempt to complete it using six fundamental actions. A game can last from five minutes to an hour and is appropriate for most ages.

The game can be played cooperatively using one person as the antagonist or, more commonly, competitively.

Gameplay: The players collectively decide on a scenario that includes a goal and a limitation or two (the rule book describes several scenarios). They then draw six cards and pick one to play all at the same time, action order resolves by the numbers printed on the cards from highest to lowest with each player describing their action. The next turn all players pass their cards to the player on the left and draw back up to six. A winner is determined when the scenario is either completed or impossible to finish.

Cards: The six action cards are Grab, Use, Position, Plan, Rest, and Delay
  Grab: You pick something up, pull something down, restrain something, etc. Anything having to do with grabbing.
  Position: Anything related to movement: yourself, a shopping cart, your laser pistol. You can only position something you already have in your possession or grabbed earlier in the game.
  Use: Activating something, whether or not it is the item's primary intention. Turning on a stove, throwing a grenade, pushing that shiny red button.
  Plan: Just like a wild card, you can use a Plan to pull any card out of the draw pile and immediately use it. The card you drew keeps the number from the “Plan” card for action order.
  Rest: This card does not have numbers. Leave this card in front of you, sit back, and relax this turn while the others blunder away. On any later turn, you may discard this card to play two from your hand (be sure to draw back up to six cards). These two additional cards still adhere to the action order based on their numbers.    
  Delay: These cards also do not have numbers. If you play one, you can pick someone to delay their action until they are last in the action order after they have stated their action. You may also play another action card. You cannot Delay a Rest and you cannot play another Delay card as your additional action card.

In Taluva, players attempt to successfully settle a South Sea island slowly emerging from the ocean waters as volcano after volcano erupts.

Each turn, players decide to either have a new volcano erupt along the shore, increasing the size of the island, or to have an existing volcano erupt again, increasing the height of the land around it (and possibly destroying parts of existing settlements). They do this by placing a new tile, consisting of one volcano and two other types of landscape. A tile must always touch at least one other tile, when placed at sea level, or be placed on top of at least two other tiles (without any gaps under the land being created), with the volcano being placed on top of an existing volcano.
Next, the player will place one or more wooden buildings; huts, temples or towers. Settlements must always start at the lowest level, by placing a single hut. From there on, existing settlements may expand by placing huts on all hexes of a single type of terrain around the settlement, with temples once the settlement takes up at least three hexes, or with towers, placed at level three or above.

The game ends when all tiles have been placed. At that point, the player who's placed most temples wins. Ties are broken by towers, then huts. Ultimate victory - and an immediate end to the game - waits for the player who manages to place all their buildings of two types. Immediate defeat is also possible, when no buildings can legally be played during a player's turn.

A lot of strategy results from the various placement rules. Volcanoes may never fully destroy a settlement, so single huts can block volcano placement, protecting other settlements. Alternatively, a well placed volcano can split a large settlement in two, creating the opportunity for both to expand more rapidly than a new settlement would. Limiting your opponent's growth potential is at least as important as preparing the terrain for you to expand upon...
