The Deluxe edition contains the basic Taluva game at increased size, as well as the following new expansions:

     Ship expansion: A new wooden piece, which can be placed in lagoons, adjacent to settlements
     2-Hex tiles expansion


Some of the Deluxe editions also contain the deluxe game board and three-volcano start tile.

Da Scramble Pidgin Crossword Game is a tile-laying word game using the Pidgin language, particularly as spoken in Hawaii. A pile of letter tiles is placed face-down on the table. Each player in turn rolls two six-sided dice, takes that many letters from the pile, and tries to make a Pidgin word or phrase from them. All drawn tiles are kept face-up. If unable to place a word, the player passes his or her turn to the next player who rolls, collects letters and tries to lay down a word. Once a word is laid down, all words must connect in crossword fashion similar to Scrabble. Although tiles may not be moved once played, players may steal tiles from other players to complete words or phrases (but be careful! This can help them). The game ends when all letters have been drawn and either one player plays his or her last letter or no more words can be made by any player. The player with the fewest tiles left wins. The game comes with a small pamphlet-size Pidgin dictionary, but players are encourage to use their knowledge of the language to settle disputes.

The game comes packaged in a cardboard tube.

From the box:'
"Play your way to punctuation know-how with BINGO! Beginners and experts alike have an equal chance for BINGO, and everyone wins punctuation pointers every round."

Punctuation Bingo is an educational twist on traditional Bingo. Each player has a bingo card with two rows of four spaces each. On each space, a sentence with a missing punctuation mark is printed. The caller chooses and reads a card showing a punctuation mark and describing its use. Players must then search their bingo cards for a sentence missing that punctuation mark and mark it. When one player has completed the chosen pattern, he or she says "bingo," the marked sentences are checked, and if correct, that player wins.

An expansion for Taluva (Deluxe edition), consisting of a double-sided game board, with "Beginner", "Player" and "Expert" zones marked on it. The different zones give different limitations to the size to which the island of Taluva can grow, adding another layer of considerations for tile placement.
Another game ending condition is introduced: The game also ends if a player can't legally place a tile within the chosen zone on the game board.

An expansion for Taluva (Deluxe edition), consisting of a single tile showing three volcanoes, allowing maximum freedom with swiftly building up to a second level. As with regular Taluva tiles, no buildings can be placed on these volcanoes. This tile is always placed first, separately, before the first player takes their regular first turn.
A 5th player expansion for Taluva Deluxe. Contains 20 huts, 3 temples, 2 towers, 12 tiles, all in black.

From the box:
"The intoxicating game of skill and chance! Cheers! It's always happy hour when you play this outrageous party game! Take a chance and roll the dice--this may become your latest vice!"

Shotzee is a Yahtzee-like game (hence the name) that incorporates drinking but can also be played without it. In the game, players roll dice to try to assemble either a full house, strait or a Shotzee (five of a kind). On his or her turn, a player rolls five dice. Each die will show one of six symbols, which may instruct the player or another player to drink, or may display a "party foul" for which no one drinks. The player will then look as his or her dice and decide which ones he or she wants to save (in order to try to get one of the winning combinations) and then marks those saved dice on a score card. Players then refill their glasses and play passes to the next player. When the original player plays again, he or she rolls however many dice were not "saved" on the first turn, trying to complete the chosen combination. Play continues until one player completes a winning combination of dice. The game comes with six tiny plastic shot glasses.

Flaptacular is a Disney Fairies themed card game, played with  a deck of 36 cards numbered 1 to 9.  Cards are dealt evenly to all players.  Each player then places four cards face-down in a row in front of him- or herself. Remaining cards are kept face-down as a draw pile.  Each player turns the top card of his or her draw-pile face up and places it in the center of the table.  When the dealer says "go," players turn over the four cards in a row and try to place these number cards on any of the number cards placed in the center of the table. Cards must be placed in numerical sequence, either higher or lower. 1 and 9 can be played on one another. When a card is played, another card may be turned up from the draw pile and placed in the face-up row, so players may always have four cards face-up to play. If no player can play a card, each player turns another draw pile card face up onto the stacks of cards already played. The first player to play all the cards onto the middle stacks wins.

Wizards and creatures wander a dark land where only the strongest survive and amass large, powerful armies to conquer.
Madstone is a card game with power crystals that are collected by players to either capture or kill creatures and warriors.  Capturing them adds power as they join your army.  Killing them gives victory points.  The strategy comes into play as each player decides when to kill and when to capture.
Players can attempt to kill or capture other players' armies, but only when darkness falls. 
The game ends when the deck is exhausted, the player with the most points showing on killed creatures is the winner.

This unique game takes the classic motif of the haunted school to new levels. In Jinmenken Haunted School Escape Game, your character must move around the scary school board, all while being chased by a so-called Jinmenken, a dog with a human face that is a staple of Japanese urban legends. Collect three matching items that spring from the clock tower to various rooms in the school, and then escape back to the starting point. Whoever gets there first is the winner! Spin the clock tower face to see how many moves you can make each time. But the clock may tell you to move the Jinmenken, who has three different speeds! If he catches your character, it's game over for you! There are also numerous other tricks and secret gimmicks depending on where you land on the board. This is a great game for Japanese language-learners and fans of Japanese ghost stories.

An expansion for Arcade by Néstor Romeral Andrés.

Situation: Units advance on us too quickly.

Solution: Deploy the arachnoids.

The arachnoid is a new type of ground unit that can shoot spiderwebs to immobilize enemy units. It can also sting other ground units adjacent to it.

An expansion for Arcade by Néstor Romeral Andrés.

Situation: We need explosives to punch through tank armour.

Solution: Deploy the minelayers.

The minelayer sidecar is a new type of ground unit that moves around the zone at high speeds and deploys powerful explosives.

boardgame: The Addams Family Creepy Mansion Action Game
description:
From the box:
"It's another crazy day with Gomez, Morticia, Pugsley and Wednesday! This spooky mansion is full of creaky surprises! Make it all the way to the top and the family fortune is yours!"

Players choose one of the four Addams Family playing pieces, roll the die and make their way up the plastic "mansion". If player lands on a white space, they must press down. Some white spaces trigger special traps that will knock the player back to start. The first player to make it to the top of the mansion wins.

The cold wind blows across an expanse of lake frozen solid by the subzero temperatures.  You gather your equipment and begin the journey to find the perfect location to set your shelter on the perfect patch of ice.  Will you land the trophy catch or will you go home empty handed?

From the first cracks of the ice to the excitement of catching the prize fish, Ice Fisher recreates the experience.  Variable settings to simulate differing ice thickness provides scalable playing time.

Players alternate turns.  On a player turn they will need to strategically choose a location to attempt to catch fish.  Utilizing deduction and hidden information analysis they will try to end their turn with a successful catch.  Each failed casting on the line provides information about fish habits and should be considered on future turns.

In Escape from Alcatraz four inmates are working together to try and escape from Alcatraz prison and eventually the island.  The inmates only have 60 days to accomplish this goal before they are transferred to another prison.  To win the game at least three inmates must successfully escape the prison and get off the island without getting caught.

Each day is broken into two phases: morning and afternoon.  Inmates will either spend the entire day at a specific location or can visit two separate locations; one in the morning and another in the afternoon.  Players control the actions of each of the four inmates during the two phases of the day and determine what actions they will take.

Escape from Alcatraz is 1-4 player Co-op worker placement game where all the players work together to break out of the prison and escape the island. If any one of the players gets caught the game is over. At least three players must escape the prison and island to win the game.

Once upon a time.....

Their was a great King that ruled over all the kingdom.  This was the land of the fairy tales of long ago.  The land where all the stories told at your bedtime came to life, where all your favourite childhood heroes and all those fearful villains coexisted in harmony.  But with His Majesty’s sudden disappearance, this once tranquil kingdom was thrown into chaos.

Forgotten Kingdom provides players with a unique and fully customizable gameplay, with a rule set that allows for shorter and longer games.  Certain character combinations YOU choose compliment each other by enhancing their abilities.  This provides a different and challenging gameplay experiance each time.

Before you begin, ensure that each player recieves an equal set of identical cards. Each player takes their deck of characters and shuffles them, drawing eight cards. During each turn of the game, players will assign a character from their hand, placing it face down on the table.

Players will simultaneously reveal their character. Each player resolves the forgotten ability (Discard) first, followed by their character ability in play. The player with the total power after the forgotten and character abilities are resolved, wins the round and adds ONE to the total points earned on the score tracker.  Both players discard their character to the forgotten and play begins again.

The first player to get the desired victory points decided on before the game begins wins!

In Good Cop Bad Cop: Undercover, the second expansion for Good Cop Bad Cop, you are leaving your badge and uniform at home as you go undercover as a common criminal to get to the bottom of who's honest and who's crooked around here. Good luck!

In Good Cop Bad Cop, your identity is determined by three cards in front of you. You're either Honest or Crooked and you need to find the leader of the opposing team so that you can take a gun and take 'em out.

This expansion adds a unique ability for each player (their undercover assignment), twelve additional Equipment cards, and a Cover, which lets you protect one of your Integrity cards from investigation.

boardgame: Escape: The Curse of the Temple – Queenie 11: The Cursed Gems
description:
Escape: The Curse of the Temple – The Cursed Gems (Queenie 11) is an expansion for the Escape base game that includes 6 cursed gem tiles and 6 gem location tiles.

The players have picked up cursed gems that they need to return before being able to escape the temple. Each player must find the exact chamber tile their individual cursed gem goes before they can escape!

This expansion was sent for free to all backers of Escape: The Curse of the Temple – Expansion 3: Traps during the Escape: The Curse of the Temple – Big Box campaign.

boardgame: Black Hat: Celebrity Status Promo Deck
description:
The Black Hat: Celebrity Status Promo Deck was included in Kickstarter and Nordic editions and is made available from BGG store.

It adds a new dimension to the Black Hat game, by changing the winning conditions completely. Instead of aiming for lowest points, with Celebrity Status cards, you aim for your own score. The person who gets closest to his score wins.

boardgame: Car Wars: The Card Game – Out of Control Promo Card
description:
A Promo card that was released as part of the Car Wars Arenas Kickstarter Campaign. It was a reward to the $40 and higher pledge levels.

The card's ability is this:

Out of Control

Play against an opponent IMMEDIATELY after they play a Spin, Swerve, or Bootlegger Reverse. That player loses their next turn.

boardgame: Car Wars: The Card Game – Body Armor Promo Card
description:
A Promo card that was released as part of the Car Wars Arenas Kickstarter Campaign. It was a reward to the $40 and higher pledge levels.

The card's ability is this:

Body Armor

Play at any time, up until your driver takes damage from an Attack card. This acts as Armor (3) against the first such Attack played against you. If played IMMEDIATELY after the attack, it also works against that attack.

Doctor Who: Companions is a challenging cooperative boardgame based on the well-known BBC TV Show. Each player will assume the role of one of the 13 last companions of the Doctor and try to protect 4 regions from famous Doctor Enemies such as the Daleks, the Cybermen, or any of the 9 types of enemies you might encounter.

During the game, players will draw cards and use symbols on theirs cards to perform various actions such as move across the boards, travel through time, fight, meet famous characters, rest, train population, search special sites for items,call the Doctor for help... They'll have to watch for the enemies moves and special plan, and time their actions to counter them.

Players win the game if they manage to clean the board from enemies before the activation deck is depleted for the second time. If not, the world will be submitted to an hostile alien race...

In Complots: Saint Barthélemy, each player is assigned a team (prostestant or catholic) and they are forced to work together until one team is eliminated. Additional rules add the ability to convert a player to the opposite team at a cost. The extension includes 8 dual-sided cards to mark each player’s team, a new Hospice card and a set of rules.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Note: Not to be confused with Monopoly: Here and Now from 2005 and similar later editions.


Travel around the gameboard visiting America's most exciting places.
Pay for the stamps of the places you land on and put them in your passport.
Charge other players vistor fees to stop at places you have stamps for.
The first player to fill their passport wins!

Different than traditional Monopoly (even previous "Here & Now" Editions) - there are no houses, hotels, or even title deeds.  The goal isn't to bankrupt other players (in fact, the rules say if you are out of money, just don't pay - but keep playing). The goal of each player is to fill their own "Passport".

This game plays far more like Monopoly Empire than regular Monopoly.
An edition with places from over the entire world, also exists: Monopoly: Here & Now World Edition (The World has voted - powered by BuzzFeed).
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: Gogglebox is a fun-packed TV trivia board game based on the BAFTA winning observational documentary series. Gogglebox can be played by up to four players or in teams. Each starting on the nearest ‘Start’ space to them the players must successfully negotiate their way around the coloured squares on the board answering TV trivia, ad break questions as well as questions on the Goggleboxers themselves to try and win Gogglebox tokens. Each player takes it in turn to roll the die and move onto the appropriate space, different colour spaces represent different types of question or activity. Landing on a yellow space can mean having to return some of your Gogglebox tokens or steal some extra ones! The TV Trivia includes questions such as ‘Who called the Queen a ‘top bird’? and ‘Name the island where Father Ted is set’. The Gogglebox space means it’s a multiple choice question on the Goggleboxers themselves! This fun-packed game is perfect for after dinner entertaining or just for playing with friends and fans of the show. It is certainly guaranteed to test your TV trivia knowledge!

The American southwest: 1881. The “iron horses” are operating in these parts regularly now, and the turn of the century is near. The rise of cities out here is clearly on the event horizon, as ever-increasing numbers of “civilized” easterners move in. Yep, the old ways are about to end. Still, law and order largely remain in their formative years. Wanted posters circulate throughout the region, and bounty hunters are plentiful….

In Bounty Hunter: 1881, you are one of these rough-and-tumble men. You’ll obtain wanted posters and travel by train, stagecoach and on horseback to get to outlaws’ hideouts. Once located, you’ll try to apprehend your quarry, by fair means or foul. Be aware, however, that bounty hunters start out with a bad reputation. If you bushwhack too many wrongdoers you’ll be considered just as bad — or even worse. You’ll also have to be careful when apprehending your prey, since most of them are quite capable of getting the draw on you! So saddle up, pard’ner and let’s make some money — bounty hunter style!

Game Play (simplified): After a quick setup, a player will choose one of several possible actions, usually selecting one of the three Travel cards on display at the bottom of the board, along with its associated bonus. These bonuses are, left to right: draw an Outlaw card, draw an On The Trail card, take a silver dollar from the display. Each type of card has a hand limit, and players may need to pay dollars in order to discard unwanted cards. Players travel to the location where an outlaw was last seen via Train cards, then play an On The Trail card along with the Travel shown cards on it to try to apprehend an Outlaw card in his hand, which is accomplished via rolls of the dice, and may be attempted by fair means or foul. If successful via the latter method, one or more Black Marks are taken, but there is a limit. Players can be wounded by their quarry and lose turns. The game ends when the Travel deck has been exhausted three times– so shuffle the discards after it runs out the first and second times. The rewards for the outlaws captured/ bushwhacked are totaled and $300 deducted from the player with more Black Marks. The player with the higher total wins.

From the Publisher:

This is a 2 in 1 game. The youngest members of the household may begin playing dominoes with these cool pirate drawings, but can also play the puzzle on the other side. They have to join all the chips together until they end up with a super pirate illustration. And after they are finished playing, they can save it all in its lined cardboard box until the next game.

This is a cardgame where the cards have a drawing on one side, that depicts a Dutch proverb. On the other side the proverb is listed, along with an explanation, example phrases, and a point value. Each turn a player takes one card (with picture visible) and tries to guess the proverb. If possible the explanation can be given. If a player is right, they get the card/points. Whoever has the most points wins.

In 2006 part one appeared.

The Year of the Goat expansion celebrates 20 years of equal opportunity butt-kicking with the Shadowfist card game. Choose one of the game's six factions and have your Secret Warriors battle for control of the world's Feng Shui sites.

This set consists of six 55-card pre-constructed starter decks, one for each of the factions: the Ascended, the Dragons, Eaters of the Lotus, the Guiding Hand, the Jammers, and the Four Monarchs.

Pigs in Pants is an Educational Take that style matching game. The main aim of the game is to match "pants cards" in your hand with "pants cards" in play. The winner is the player how has the most cards at the end of the game. The pants cards and player boards are very thick card stock more akin to tiles than cards.

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – Flight of the Stormcaller
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Game: The Lord of the Rings – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
Simple Campaigning is a 48-page A5 full colour booklet containing Campaign frameworks for Ancient and Medieval warfare, which can be played as boardgames or as bases for miniatures battles. Includes Solo Campaigning (the Rise of Rome), Angevin Empire, Across the Steppes (Mongol Conquests), The Millennium Campaign (11th Century Europe), Crisis of Rome (3rd Century AD), The Wolf on the Fold (Assyrian Empire), The Calamitous Fourteenth Century (late Medieval Europe).

Each of the seven Campaign wargames contains rules for re-creating the conflicts of a historical epoch and region, with counters on a map and using either a combat results table or miniatures.  They vary widely in complexity; some have minimal rules and can be played very quickly, while others have detailed political and/or economic rules.  The games are designed to entertain both historical boardgamers and miniatures buffs.  Several can easily be played solitaire.

The point of each game is to determine by die rolls where conflicts arise, fight the battles and compare the outcome with history.  Several include victory point tables so that players can measure their success against each other or, if playing solitaire, against the historical outcome - for example, can the player outdo the Lionheart and King John in holding the Angevin Empire together?

Some of the games can easily be completed in an hour, if using a combat results table; others take considerably longer, while all can last for many sessions if the battles are fought on the tabletop.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: History: Wars in the Ancient Near East

boardgame: Rise of the Gods: Tides of Infamy Expansion
description:
Rise of the Gods is an expansion for Tides of Infamy, a mythological universe with a pirate theme.

In Rise of the Gods the Forge of the Gods has been awakened and with it the Ore of the Gods has started to flow.  Temples from which the Gods of the Seas derive their power are nearly complete.  Help complete construction by offering up the Ore of the Gods as tribute at a Temple of your choice to gain favor among the Sea Gods.  Use your otherworldly support to increase your acts of piracy as the most infamous Pirate of the ages.

Rise of the Gods adds the following:

     Acts of Piracy (Cards)
     Power of the Gods (Cards)
     Sea Monster (minifigure)
     More Map Tiles
     More Pirate Luck Cards
     More Pirate Ships - can play up to 6 players (or 8 players with Kickstarter Exclusive edition of Tides of Infamy) 


Acts of Piracy are tasks taken on by Pirates that upon successful completion yield Marks of Infamy.

Power Cards are received by paying tribute at a Sea God Temple. They award special abilities to your Pirate Ships.

The Sea Monster roams the board (using the same mechanic as the Ghost Ship in Tides of Infamy) engaging in battles.

Have we found your Tipping Point? The high stakes quiz game the whole family can enjoy. Based on the highly popular TV game show, Tipping Point combines general knowledge questions and answers with nail-biting unpredictability.

Compete as teams or individuals. By answering questions correctly, players win tokens to drop into the electronic table top tipping point machine. Which of the four drop zone do you think will give you the biggest payout?

As the machine slides back and fourth, let your token fall and see whether that will start a chain reaction. One single correct answer could trigger a cashcade that changes everything!

Which team can amass the most counters to go forward and compete in the final jackpot round?

boardgame: Hide and Seek Audio: The Friendly Farm
description:
The new Hide and Seek Audio excites the senses for a stimulating game of discovery.

Two ways to play:
Conventional game: The game comes with several boards, each depicting a scene scattered with various different objects. The objects are hidden within the images so that they mingle with the background, making them hard to spot. One of the boards is chosen for the game.

On each turn, a card is turned face up and players try to spot the object as fast as possible. The first one to do so wins the card. The winner is the player who accumulates the most cards.

With the DVD: Be the first (or play as a group) to find the animals and other things that correspond to the 30 sounds on the DVD.

The following is a rough translation from the Dutch manual. Some terms just cannot be translated without loss of meaning, sorry about that.

KOEkeloeren is a Moohingly exciting (LOEIspanned) cardgame. Play along with Kittie Cow, Pleuntje kitten, Mistie mouse, Pepper pup, Gagger goat and Bibi piglet.
To help the play KOEkeloeren right, they have put on their glasses.

Contents: 
72 playing cards (2*6 cards for each animal)
8 combination cards (showing how to score)
2 dice

Strictly, the box "contains" 5 games, that all use the cards in the box.
These five games are: KOEkeloeren [a8; p3-6], GABBERen [a5; p2-6], BIGgen ("pigletting") [a5; p3-6], Doe de PEPPER (Do the PEPPER) [a4; [3-6] en Pleuntjes Bluf! [a5; p2-6].
Per game, the minimum age and number of players variate slightly, I have added in square brackets after the name of the game, e.g.: [a5; p2-4] would mean ages 5 and up, number of players 2 through 4. Playing time also differences per game.

The manual and 8 combination cards are in Dutch, the playing card are language neutral.

boardgame: The Red Dragon Inn: Compulsive Gambler's Grog
description:
The Red Dragon Inn: Organized Play Season 1 (2015) participation promotional card. This card was given away if you won an RDI organized play.

Begin a Round of Gambling as though you just played "Gambling? I'm in!"

The winter of that Round of Gambling gains 2 alcohol Content.

Players may Ignore this Event to avoid the Round of Gambling. (However, players may no longer do this once the Round has begun.)

The best gamblers know when to stop!

The Organized Play Kits each contained 4 Tournament Prize Packs, consisting of twenty participation cards to be handed out to anyone who attends (this one and Deirdre's Detoxifying Draught) and three premium cards to be handed out as prizes.

The 2023 re-release of the Organized Play Kit contains only 1 Tournament Prize Pack.

A tasty fun play set for 1-4 pizza bakers

This pizza bake sets contains two games. 
Pizza presto:
A red hot pizza duel for two children, flip a card the first kid take puts the right toppings on the pizza and puts their pizza in the oven wins the card. Best of 9.

One Pizza Margarita, please!
A crunchy listen-well game for 1-4 pizza bakers and an adult

On a little bakers turn the adult reads the ingredients on the card, if the pizza has the correct ingredients the little pizza baker gets the card. Most cards wins.

Party game for 2 to 6 Players.

The players get task cards which give them tasks which they have to solve with knead.
There are different kind of cards. 

     creating special objects
     building in duel with another player (first wins)
     all build the same in a sandglass time
     building a little game, e.g. a bowling game and every player can try to hit all pins (with 3 ball throws).


The player who gets first 20 points wins the game.

boardgame: Deutschlands Häfen u. Küsten: Ein Reisespiel
description:
The players start at space 1 (Borkum) and have to reach space 50 (after Königsberg). Movement is determined by one die. On some spaces the players are hindered or benefited by certain events. The first player to reach space 50 wins the game.

The game is played with nice pewter ships and was most likely published before WWI.

Referred to by the publishers as a "role-playing party game", Johnsonville: Cutthroat Capitalism is a dice-rolling thematic game set in a small 1950s town.

Players start with a small pool of money that may be depleted every turn, and the object of the game is to have completed the most "citizenly tasks" (objectives drawn at the start of the game) by the time all other players have lost money. Player interference to deplete the money of other players is encouraged.

Actions in the game can be completed in two ways- through rolling against the Game Master, or "Johnson", or by drawing "Kit Cards", fictional cure-all products. Players interact by describing actions and rolling or drawing to attempt to complete them. The positive(or negative) effects of these attempts are described by the Johnson in a humorous fashion based on the numbers rolled or card drawn.

Bitwy Okrętow (War of Ships or Battles of Warships) is a quick, easy wargame about battles fought between XVII-XVIII century ships. Players may choose and customize their fleets, creating big number of small ships or small number of the bigger ones. Rules contains maneuvers, wind dependency, boarding and burning enemy ships.

Game was published in the 40 issue of "Taktyka i Strategia" magazine in both Polish and English editions.

Crecy 1346 is a game about most famous battle of the 100 years war fought between Kingdom of England and Kingdom of France. The battle was fought on 26 August 1346 near Crécy in northern France and was the major victory of the English army under the king Edward III.

Game was published in the 40 issue of Taktyka i Strategia magazine, in both Polish and English versions. Is uses Medieval (Sredniowiecze) games mechanic.

Update: Toast! was not funded on Kickstarter, but the design was taken up by Horrible Games and developed into Raise Your Goblets.

Toast! is themed around ruthless medieval nobles trying to poison each other during the toasts at a royal function. The King has decreed a bizarre etiquette to be followed, in an attempt to discourage this behaviour. However, instead it has just turned the practice into an art and a sport, with each (surviving) noble having developed a particular expertise.

In a round of play, goblets are arranged in a circle in the middle of the table, one in front of each player, such that players can’t see their contents. Players take turns to secretly place wine, poison or antidote tokens into the goblets,  switching the goblets around, or using their characters special abilities. Each player has a target, and their aim is to ensure that, when the round is ended by someone calling for the Toast and the players all ‘drink’, their target’s goblet is poisoned and theirs is safe!

The game supports 2-12 players. The main rules are for 4-6 players, there are special rules to extend up to 12 players (where pairs of players play as a noble and their wine-taster), and there is a separate game mode for 2-3 players (a sort of Russian Roulette with goblets).

A new array of adventures awaits the brave heroes of Mistfall. Mistfall - Encounter Pack 1 brings 18 new encounter cards to increase the replay value of Mistfall. They can be added to the base game without restrictions.

This Pack is already included in the Kickstarter Edition of Mistfall, and contains 18 Encounter cards (numbered 406-423).

New enemies rise from the dark mists, threatening an already perilous world with their fierce axes and powerful magic. Mistfall - Enemy Pack 1 brings 18 new enemy cards to increase the replay value of Mistfall. They can be added to the base game without restrictions.

This Pack is already included in the Kickstarter Edition of Mistfall, and contains the following cards:

Green Enemies:
Fellstalker x3
Ice Reaver x3

Blue Enemies:
Ravenous Draugr x3
Undead Loremaster x3

Red Enemies:
Blackwood Ambusher x3
Twisted Lasher x3

How much of what you say is actually made up of your words?

Emotional IQ is a hilarious new party game for 2 players on up to party size groups. It’s easy to learn and easy to play. There are 2 types of cards: the Emotion Cards, and the Sentence Cards. You’ll choose 1 card from each stack and then deliver the sentence aloud for everyone to hear.

How do you say, “Great news everyone, I got engaged today” in your best sad voice? ...or... "That guy just sneezed all over you" in a voice full of Happiness?

Other players will have to guess which Emotion you are using. Here’s the catch – no one except you knows which Emotion card you drew, the Sentence and Emotion rarely match, and you only get 1 shot at your delivery.

It's a science experiment of your brain vs your mouth. Emotional IQ is designed by Bryan Sloan and illustrated by Roman Shipunov.

For the heroes who have already mastered their skills and have successfully battles and defeated the first four villains from the mists, two new quests await. Defeat Sycra, the Black Crusader and Karnas, the Betrayer to become the most praised hero of Mistfall.

Mistfall - Quest & Nightmare Time Cards features two new special enemy cards with their quests and setup cards as well as 13 new time cards for nightmarish difficulty.

This Pack is already included in the Kickstarter Edition of Mistfall, and contains the following cards:

13 Nightmare Time cards (numbered 426-438), replacing the included Time deck.

2 Quest Info cards (numbered 439 & 440).
2 Special Encounter cards (numbered 424 & 425).
2 Special Enemy cards (Sycra the Black Crusader & Karnas the Betrayer).

Here are the rules...

Give each player 10 cards (1 pack of card needed)

The winner is the one who get rid of all their cards first (jokers are free cards and are best to keep until the very end to surprise the other players)

You moove one dice at a time. Not like a combination. This will make you end up on 2 different square.

Avoid the "C" spot, if you end your move on one,  You'll have to take another card in your hand.

If you end up on a square with a drawing of a dice, you have to moove your pawn by that number of square in any direction you want.

You can only moove one direction with the first dice no come and back. But you can choose to move back with the second dice in any direction. Or even use a different pawn for the second dice.

You need a 6 on one dice or on a combination of the 2 dices to put a pawn into play. You have tree role to get it otherwise you loose your turn.

If you got a 6 on one dice you can move your pawn by the number of the other dice. You can move on any direction.

You get rid of a card by ending your moove on a card symbol identical as your card. The card symbol count as a square. You can only enter that card symbol square by the exact number and by the only way make with an arrow. This is a safe square and the only place your safe to be eaten by an another player.

You get eaten by a player if they end up their moove on the same space as you.

The Poison Dragon turns the streets of Arcadia into a hazy gauntlet of deadly fumes, weakening heroes as he snuffs all life in the city. In MountTen (テンガロン), players want to rid themselves of cards in hand first in order to win the round. The deck consists of green cards numbered 1-40 and red cards numbered 10-30. Players start with ten cards in hand with 2-3 players and eight cards with 4-6 players. Set the deck nearby with the top card revealed.

The start player plays any card to the center of the table. The next player must play a card at most ten lower or higher than this card, e.g., playing 14-34 if the 24 was lead. If the second-played card is lower (higher), then all subsequent cards must be lower (higher) — but also always within ten of the most recently played card. If a player cannot or doesn't want to play, they pass and draw the top card of the deck, then reveal the new top card. If all players pass in turn, then whoever played the most recent card clears the table and leads a new card.

Two exceptions:

1. If the next player in turn can play a green card exactly ten lower or higher than the topmost card on the pile (no matter the direction of play), that player can call "Mount Ten!", play the card, then clear the table and lead a new card.

2. If any player can play a red card exactly ten lower or higher than the topmost card on the pile (no matter the direction of play), that player can call "Mount Ten!", play the card, then clear the table and lead a new card.

When one player has no cards in hand, the round ends. Each player with cards in hand scores -1 for each green card and -2 for each red card; the player who went out scores positive points equal to all the negative points scored that round. Complete as many rounds as the number of players, with the start player rotating each round. Whoever then has the highest score wins!

User summary
In the game of モーモートレトレ (Mo Mo Toretore), you are an alien Primary Schoolchild. During a school outing to the planet Earth, your teacher told you to capture cows in the field.

The teacher specifies how many cards you must play, and you play that number of cards. If the cards' total face value equals a cow's number, you can capture the cow. (Your arithmetic skills are tested because you are a Primary Schoolchild).

If you are the only one who played your number, you get the whole points, otherwise points are divided among players who played that number.

With cute illustrations, this card game can be enjoyed by all, from children to adults. This game is especially good for those who frequently play with Primary Schoolchildren or new gamers. The first edition is published in Japanese but English rules are available online.

Publisher's summary
モーモートレトレは、宇宙人の小学生となってウシを捕まえるのが目的のゲーム。
先生の指示の枚数のカードを使って手札の数字とウシの数字をあわせよう。（簡単な足し算引き算が必要です）
自分だけが出した数字なら独り占め、他の人も出してたら分け合おう。

かわいらしい絵柄で、お子様から大人まで楽しんでいただけるカードゲームです。
特に小学生くらいのお子さんやゲーム初心者の方と遊ぶ機会が多い方にお勧めのワイワイと楽しめるゲームに仕上がりました。

A new epic fighting card game to experience! Build your own fighter, complete with weapons, armor, masteries and origin stories and fight to see who built the best one! Karacterz features 77 unique artworks of 63 artists from 23 countries. Play with a fresh experience every time.

Build your character to specialize in either Magical, Agility (martial arts) or Physical (medieval warfare), have a combination of two of them or all three! Players also build up their character's combat deck to fight with and buff them up with stats. There is no Life Points or Maximum Health in Karacterz so deal as much damage to your opponents as possible! Player with the least amount of damage on them is the victor.

Karacterz is a great game for gamers of all styles and skill levels. This is a perfect game for skilled gamers to play with more casual gamers. Karacterz features many small game mechanics that interact with each other, perfect for experienced gamers, but not necessary a must to win. Those who find the depth of Karacterz overwhelming need not play with every feature to have a great time and beat up their friends!

Playing with a large group? Karacterz features a Teamwork/Betrayal system that guarantees to make group play interesting and more fun! Wanna check out more about Karacterz? Go to our links!

description:
User summary
ペンタトリックス (Penta-Tricks) is a game about the power struggle between aristocrats in the 14th Century France, during the reign of Louis XIV. It combines trick-taking and area-majority.

During the game, changes occur to both the trick-taking trump and the value of areas for area majority points.

The game has Dice in the suit colours set up on a pentagram showing the value 2 at the start. The trick taking follows the normal pattern, with players following the suit lead if possible and the highest card winning. If there is a Trump suit, highest Trump card will win. But after each trick, the dice values change. The winning suit rises by 1 (or 2 if it was Trump). And one of the dice opposite that winning dice falls by 1. After this change, the lowest dice (if any) now becomes Trump.

But this change in dice values also affects the players' scoring. Before the trick winner adjusts the dice values, the losers place one or two cubes against the dice of the suit they played (or its neighbour). NOTE, the winner of the trick does not get to place cubes, but does choose which dice will drop 1 point. At the end of the round, majority cubes score the face value of the dice (except the Trump die), second most gets half rounded down. Tricks won are also worth 2 points each.

So the game becomes more than just winning tricks. Players want to lose tricks so they can place a cube (2 cubes if you lose on a Trump card) and gain majorities. But they also want to win tricks to control Trump or No Trump and lower the points on another dice.

Publisher's summary
ルイ14世時代のフランスが舞台の、貴族達の権力闘争をモチーフにしたゲームです。
トリックテイキングゲームとエリアマジョリティゲームを一つにまとめたような作りになっています。
トリックテイキングの切り札がゲームの途中で変わります。
エリアマジョリティのエリアの価値もゲームの途中で変わります。

Welcome to your last semester of college!
 Dropout is a humorous take-that card game for up to four players who try to raise their G.P.A in order to graduate.

Unfortunately, there are so many distractions to your studies: parties to attend, movies to watch and beer to drink.  Each one of these distractions will lower your G.P.A.  However, if you study, do your homework and avoid temptation, you might be able to raise and your G.P.A and graduate!

Each turn players will place activity cards to increase their G.P.A and distraction cards on the other players to lower theirs.  After four rounds of triumphs and defeats, one semester has passed.

Earn the highest G.P.A in four semesters and become the one who doesn't Dropout

Welcome to Robert Houdin’s theater! The most famous magicians in the world come here to prove themselves. You will have to show your skills in card, coin and stage magic. Will you gain the audience’s favor or will this be your last performance?

Alakazam! is a fast-paced card game for 2 to 6 players. Players are dealt a hand of cards with a set of three magic tricks in each. In every turn, each player has to play a card that has a better trick than the one performed by the previous player. They must pass if they don't have a card with a better trick.

The first player to perform all his tricks wins the game.

You always suspected that they were psycho, now it is time to prove it.

In All my Friends are Psycho you become the psychologist and use original designed Rorschach cards to find out just how demented and gross your friends really are.

The Rorschach card deck is set in the middle face down. Flip the same number of cards that you have players, face up.

Without touching the cards, each player chooses an image to analyze keeping their interpretation a secret.

The psychologist picks up the cards and one by one asks each player what they saw. The psychologist has to match the player's interpretation with the correct card.

If the psychologist guesses correctly, both the player and psychologist gets 1 sanity point. If not, they score 1 psycho point.

Each player takes turns being the psychologist. After this the sanity and psycho points are added up. The player that has the closest number of sanity and psycho points (balances out) wins.

The game comes with 27 Rorschach cards and requires a genuine, original (neither genuine nor original) pad and pen.

In Medieval Might, two generals are pitted against each other in a battle. Each leads their army against each other to become the supreme commander.

Players take turns placing their units, represented by cards, on the battlefield. Each card builds up the strength of their forces, and use tactics to weaken their opponent's forces.

Use your knights and banner-men to greatly increase your strength, while deploying your scouts to gain an advantage over your opponent. If they start to take the lead, use your marksman to eliminate high value targets or ambush their forces to reduce their effectiveness.

The army with the higher value at the end of that round is the victor of that round. The game ends when one player loses two rounds, and the winner is crowned the supreme commander

MATCH! is an abstract family game in where you compete to build chains out of matches.

Search simultaneously for matching cards, which use the Stroop effect, a scientific principle that creates interference in the player's reaction time.

Don't let the cards manipulate you as you build the most chains of cards before all the matches are gone.

The player that makes the most matches wins.

Download Print & Play on:
http://www.trickzbunny.com/match.html

There's a house filled with hungry zombies and your team is in the middle of it!!!

Around each corner there are different terrors.  Super fast zombies, giant zombies and viscous stalkers are the least of your worries.

Mad House is a nail biting card strategy game for up to 5 players. It features a unique Noise Tracking mechanic; if you're team makes too much noise they will be attacked by horde of zombies!

The only way to survive is to keep everyone alive. Kill the required number of zombies to escape this Nightmare ...

Can you escape the Mad House?!

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 1 – Nieuport Ni.28
description:
From the publisher:

The Nieuport 28 was a French biplane fighter flown during World War I, designed by Gustave Delage. Owing its lineage to the successful line of sesquiplane fighters as the Nieuport 17, the Nieuport 28 continued a similar design philosophy of a lightweight and highly maneuverable aircraft.

A shortage of SPADs led to the production of Nieuport 28s. It became the first aircraft to see operational service with an American fighter squadron. It was flown by famous aces such as Ralph A. O’Neil, Harold E. Hartney and Edward Rickenbacker.

Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW1 Wings of Glory Airplane Pack is a ready-to-play model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW1 Wings of Glory game product. In each pack you will find all you need to play with the airplane: a special base with gaming stats, a variable altitude flying stand, and a specific deck of maneuver cards.

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 1 – Macchi M.5
description:
From the publisher:

The Macchi M.5 was an Italian single-seat fighter flying boat, designed and built by Nieuport-Macchi at Varese. It was extremely maneuverable and agile and matched the land-based aircraft it had to fight.

The M.5 was operated by five Italian maritime patrol squadrons as a fighter and convoy escort. By the end of World War I, these aircraft were flown by both United States Navy and United States Marine Corps airmen. The three versions of the airplane proposed include the model flown by the Italian pilot Domenico Arcidiacono, an American plane flown by Willis B. Haviland and a captured Austrian plane piloted by Friedrich Welker

Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW1 Wings of Glory Airplane Pack is a ready-to-play model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW1 Wings of Glory game product. In each pack you will find all you need to play with the airplane: a special base with gaming stats, a variable altitude flying stand, and a specific deck of maneuver cards.

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 1 – Hannover CL.IIIa
description:
From the publisher:

The Hannover CL.III was a German military aircraft of World War I. It was a two-seat multi-role aircraft, primarily used as a ground attack machine.

Like the other Hannover "light-C-class", or "CL" designated aircraft, it included an unusual biplanar tail, allowing for a greater firing arc for the tail gunner. Until the introduction of this model, such tails had only been used on larger aircraft. The Hannover CL.IIIa in WW1 Wings of Glory is presented in versions flown by Rudolf Hager/Otto Weber and Johann Baur/Georg von Hengel, as well a Luftstreitkräfte model.

Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW1 Wings of Glory® Airplane Pack is a ready-to-play model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW1 Wings of Glory game product. In each pack you will find all you need to play with the airplane: a special base with gaming stats, a variable altitude flying stand, and a specific deck of maneuver cards.

Wormhole! is a space themed board game for up to 8 players that turns the traditional rules of game play inside out. Everyone starts out at the edge of the board on a spiral track. Every turn you roll the dice, move closer to doom and draw a card. That card may be good, it may be bad, but it will certainly be unpleasant for someone. Red event cards go into effect immediately and may move you forward or back, swap you with the nearest ship or even make you take another turn. Blue mass cards are...mass cards. Mass is also the most common card type in the deck and comes in 25, 50, 75, and 100 unit variants. Mass is bad, because for every 100 mass you accumulate you have to roll another die on your turn. So if you’ve got 300 mass sitting in front of you, you’re rolling 4 dice every turn (that's your original die plus 3 more). And that’s just not going to end well. Green action cards are like the red event cards, but you can hold onto them and use them at your discretion. Use one to send your highest mass card to the person behind you...or if you happen to be that person behind you can use a different one to dodge that mass and let it keep on heading back. There’s even an action card that will let you skip your own turn! When a player inevitably reaches the center of the wormhole, they are out. Flung into the deepest recesses of time and space most likely. When that happens, they mix up their remaining action cards with their mass cards and each remaining player takes one at random (going in order along the track). Play continues until only one player is left on the board, allowing them to safely escape the wormhole and making them the winner.

Crop Circles is an abstract puzzle game for 2-6 players. Taking the role of aliens warning Earthlings of impending doom, players etch glyphs into crop fields with their spaceships, according to changing rules about adjacency between glyph shapes and glyph shading in order to score points for effectively following the rules set forth by the Intergalactic Communications Council.

Each turn, a player places any number of glyph tiles from behind their screen onto the board in an unbroken straight line, connected to glyph tiles that are already on the board. That player scores points for the sum of the values of only the glyphs played (affected by on-board multiplier squares) multiplied by the number of glyph tiles already on the board that were adjacent to their play. Thus, a long string of glyph tiles played parallel to a previous play can net massive points, so players must balance taking advantage of opportunities for huge points with preventing such opportunities for other players. Finally, that player then draws exactly three new glyph tiles either among five face-up glyph tiles next to the play area or from the bag of remaining glyph tiles, regardless of how many they played on their turn, as long as they played at least one glyph.

Glyphs come in four shapes (circles, squares, triangles, and stars) and four shadings (empty, solid, striped, and crossed). Each shading is permitted only to certain shapes, and when a player places glyph tiles on the board, they must satisfy the requirements of both the glyphs being places and the glyphs they are playing adjacent to. The more complex a shading, the more difficult it is to place on the board and the more points it is worth: empty glyphs have no restrictions about what shapes they may be placed adjacent to and are worth only one point; solid glyphs may be adjacent to only three of the four shapes and are worth two points; striped glyphs may be adjacent to only two of four shapes and are worth three points; and crossed glyphs may be adjacent only to a single shape and is worth four points! However, the four active rule cards that determine these permitted adjacencies will get replaced throughout a game, providing new opportunities to play glyphs. Players must plan ahead, acquire useful glyphs, and play them on the most opportune turns.

User summary
海パラダイス (Umi-Para-Dice) means Sea Paradise in English.

Umi-Para-Dice is a dice game where players collect cute sea creatures (actually pencil erasers!) and make their own aquarium.

One player rolls a number of dice equal to twice the number of players, and players take turns taking one die. Each player collects a sea creature that corresponds to the two numbers of their dice taken, and place that creature onto their personal board.

The game ends when dice are rolled 12 times. Each player scores according to the types of creatures collected. Bonus scores are added by multiple creatures of a kind and positions on the personal board. The player with the highest total wins the game.

The game is for 1-2 players with one copy. Up to four players can play if two copies are combined.

Publisher's summary
サイコロを振って海の生き物を集めていく、箱庭的 水族館ボードゲーム。海の生き物は可愛い消しゴムで出来ています。サイコロを手番の人が人数×2個振って、手番の人から時計回りに、2週1つづつとります。取った2つのサイコロの目に該当する海の生き物を手に入れます。同じ海の生き物を集めたりすると、ボーナスが貰え、最後にサイコロを振って加点出来ます。
1セット1・2人用ですが、2セットで3・4人で遊べ、みんなでワイワイと遊ぶことが出来ます。

User summary
樹ブロック (Ju:block) is a collection of blocks, made of three different woods (Japanese cypress, Japanese big-leaf magnolia, and Japanese red oak).

Different woods have different density, so even blocks of the same shape have different weight.

Using these blocks with various shape and material, you can play three different games (competitive balance, co-operative balance, and chess-like strategy game).

This game has several expansions. With these expansions, you can play other  types of games, such as territory-occupation game and maze game. You can also play more technical balance games.

Publisher's summary
ヒノキ、ホオノキ、赤樫の3つの重さの違う木材で作られたいろいろな形のブロックを使って、バランスゲームや、チェスのような思考型ゲームなど3つの遊び方が楽しめます。
別売の追加セットを購入することで、陣取りゲームやさらにアグレッシブなバランスゲームを楽しむことが出来ます。

The publisher of KUSUKUMA is Lycée Classique Echternach. Kusukuma is the result of a student game development class (main game idea by Charel Bintz) under the guidance of Dennis Kirps (teacher) and the help of Christian Kruchten (prototype development). The class itself was originated by professor Carmen Schares.

The objective of the game is to push the opponent off the board by cleverly pushing the Kusukuma discs, so that the final player keeps at least one Kusukuma disc of his own on the board. However, as the die determines the colour of the next move, a player might have to push his own disc(s) off the board. The question arises: “Who will be left?”

PLAY
The active player throws the die and chooses exactly one of the following three moves:

1.    If one of the four colours is cast, the player has to move one of the discs corresponding to the cast colour by exactly one square horizontally or vertically (not diagonally), even if said colour does not correspond to his own! 
2.    If the joker is cast, the player can push any disc, his own, his opponent’s or even the joker.
3.    If the blank side is cast, the player cannot make a move, and it is the next player’s turn.

END OF GAME AND WINNER
The game is over when:

     there is only one disc or discs of the same colour left on the game-board. The player representing this colour wins.
     there is only the joker left on the game-board. In this case everybody wins.
     in a game of two or three players, there are only neutral discs left on the game-board. In this case there is no winner.

The Speed-puzzle game.

One who puts the most pieces in to the game, wins.
Players build the same puzzle with their own pieces.

The rules include three ways to play - a straightforward skirmish, a retrieval mission and a "High Ground" scenario, adding extra replay value.  The winner is the first to meet his objective, or the last with characters still standing.

A handful of miniatures, a couple of six-sided dice and a bit of imagination are all that's required to enjoy Alien Worlds.

After finishing a campaign, the players will get a new load out of Level 6 Upgrade cards, with a budget that is based on the amount of medals each guild completed during the campaign.

This last part of the fight doesn’t have PvP Quest Cards and all players have to kill the Dragon before he wrecks too much of the city! This is a fully cooperative fight, in which the players win as a group if they manage to kill the Dragon and they lose if too many parts of the city have been destroyed, or if the Dragon kills Heroes so much that they can’t find the resolve to continue the fight. Sector Prime is a fast paced, light 4X board game using a balanced dice rolling mechanic that avoids a runaway winner and also keeps the game exciting and balanced right to the end. The unique design of the tiles provides a completely new game board each time, requiring players to adapt the strategies each time they play!

Each player represents a mining faction seeking to control the mining rights to the sector. You need to control enough planetary systems to mine the needed materials to complete the construction of your Mining Station
 In each turn there are 3 phases:

(1) Explore. 
By a roll of the dice move your mining drones to new planet tiles.

(2) Expand or Exterminate. 
You must choose to either invade and attack another mining faction OR to build defenses, new routes of exploration or a section of your Station thus bringing you one step closer to victory.

(3) Exploit.
In this phase you gather the results of your mining operations. You can simply gather the minimum OR you can gamble by pushing your drones produce double. Be careful, because this isn't always successful.

The first player to complete construction of their Station wins the game!

boardgame: Portugal (fan expansion for Age of Industry)
description:
This Portugal map has been primarily designed for 3 players (though also playable with 2 or even 4, though super tight) and features Portugal during the height of its industrial development (1855-65).  This map adds quite a few nuances to the base game such as:

a.) Banks were hesitant to invest in the industrial sector so loans return $1 less for each additional loan after the first.

b.) Railway expansion was highly encouraged - certain rails are worth $4 at the end of the game instead of $2 and there are bonus VP for the player who finishes with the most VP.

c.) Government debt was mounting rapidly due to poor management so to simulate this waste, corruption, and poor economy, there is always a region or industry where you CANNOT build.

...and many more!

Players must navigate the map's tightness - don't forget the option to combine both actions to build anywhere! - and suffocating economy to try and build a prosperous network.

Battle on the high seas or in the crowded streets of the Spanish Main as a Pirate, Pirate Hunter or a simple Merchant in this minatures game!

Using the Chain Reaction System found in all of Two Hour Wargames, you field a group of characters with very simple stats: Reputation and Weapons.  Activation rolls tell you when you are able to go (higher rolls go first, but only activate higher Reputation groups) and then you may move and fire/charge.  That's it!

Of course, being the reaction system, the other figures on the board won't be sitting and waiting for you to finish in a IGO-UGO fashion, but will react to your moves using a series of tables that may have them running from you, drawing pistols and firing, or charging into combat swords shining!

Be the last pirate standing and win!
Or play a campaign and retire with the most gold to win!

Rules to adventure ashore?Check!
Big Land Battles to sack Panama? Check!
Ship combat with boarding actions? Check!
Big Sea Battles to fight fleets? Check!
Treasure maps and buried treasure? Check!

boardgame: The Struggle for Portugal: Peninsular War III, 1810-11
description:
From the publisher:

The Struggle for Portugal - Peninsular War III contains four battles in Portugal and Spain during 1811: Barrosa, Fuentes de Oñoro, and Albuera.

The Struggle for Portugal - Peninsular War III is one of the games from OSG's The Library of Napoleonic Battles series. Details on the series are below, as all the games share a common rules set and game system.

BATTLES SIMULATED

Barrosa, 5 March 1811
A reduction in the besieging forces gave the garrison of Cadiz an opportunity to break out. Loading on to naval transports, they debarked at Tarifa and moved to engage the siege lines from the rear. The French, under the command of Marshal Victor, placed one division on the road to Cadiz, while his two remaining divisions fell on the single Anglo-Portuguese division under Sir Thomas Graham. Following a fierce battle, the British succeeded in routing the French but could not prevent their reoccupying their siege lines.

Fuentes de Onoro, 3 - 5 May 1811
Wellington first secured Portugal and then set about re-taking the fortified frontier cities of Almeida, Badajoz, and Ciudad Rodrigo. Whilst Wellington besieged Almeida, Massena reformed his battered army and marched to relieve the French garrison in the city. Wellington chose to check the relief attempt at the small village of Fuentes de Oñoro.

La Albuera 16 May 1811
Wellesley sent Sir William Beresford to retake the border town of Badajoz. Soult rapidly gathered a new army from Andalusia and, joining with the troops retreating before Beresford, he marched to relieve the siege. With the Spanish army under General Joaquin Blake approaching, he planned  to turn Beresford's flank and interpose his army between his position and the Spaniards. However, the Spaniards had already linked up with the Anglo-Portuguese corps, and his 24,000 troops now faced a combined Allied army 35,000 strong. The opposing armies met at the village of Albuera. Both sides suffered heavily in the ensuing struggle and the French eventually withdrew on 18 May.

DESCRIPTION

The Library of Napoleonic Battles - formerly known as the Napoleon's Last Battles (NLB) Series:


     480 meters per hex, 
     1 hour per turn, 
     400-800 men per strength point. 


Each Approach to Battle game lasts about 22 turns. The Full campaign links the individual battles.

The game is played in three rounds where all teams meet each other. Fast glossary, loud laughter, tongue-tied, blackout and wild gestures succeed each other in a fast pace! A brand new party game that never gets old!

description:
Form on Admiral's Wake is a quick-playing miniatures game of fleet naval battle during the age of sailing warships.  It allows for the play of larger naval battles with a small number of players who will each control one or more squadrons of ships.  The game is typically played with miniatures on a hex grid.

The game system uses an action deck to determine which ships can activate and what actions they can perform.  The action cards have limitations based on a ship's fleet, sail state, wind orientation, crew quality, and damage level.  The more favorable the condition and status of a ship, the more cards it will activate on.

One card is flipped each turn and ships that meet the requirements of the card may perform actions as indicated in the instructions.  The actions allowed vary by card, but include moving one hex, sideslipping one hex, turning in a hex, and reloading cannons.  Once fired, cannons require two actions to reload.

After all actions from the flip of a card are completed, all ships with loaded cannons may fire simultaneously.  As a ship takes damage, its status reduces from undamaged to disabled to crippled.  As the status changes, the ship will have less chances to activate.

Additional rules cover critical hits, grappling, fires, forts, anchoring, and fire ships.

The game is essentially a modified deck of traditional playing cards, but featuring cartoon parodies of popular rap artists.

Each player is dealt a hand of 12 cards from the deck, and a game lasts 12 rounds. The player to the left of the dealer goes first.

Each round the top card of the deck is revealed, and the color of that card becomes the "trump" color. Each player plays a card from their hand of the trump color if they can. If the player does not have a card in the trump color he may play any card. The round ends when each player has played a card. If trump colored cards have been played, then the highest trump colored card played that round wins. If no trump colored cards were played, then the highest card of any color wins.

The winner of the round scores 10 points. The first player to score 187 points or higher is the winner of the game.

(It should be noted that, since a game only lasts 12 rounds and players only score 10 points for a round, it is impossible to score the required number of points in a game to win)

This was the last expansion published by Daedalus. Lots of cards for the Dragons and Architects of the Flesh, as well as the Jammers, finally making them a viable faction to use.

From the booster package: "The Architects of the Flesh are raising a sinister Arcanotower in the South American jungle - and it's up to the kung fu-kicking, gun-toting Dragons to save the day! More than 140 new cards, plus bonus info cards with background notes, Q&A, and extra goodies! The war is on - and the jungle explodes with action!"

You are investigating alien ruins on Mars. Just as you are getting ready to take all the artifacts back to your ship, disaster strikes! A meteor storm sweeps across the planet! Your equipment is damaged! Your oxygen is running low! And to top it off the dust kicked up by the meteors has obscured the location of your ship!

What had been days worth of air is now only a few scant hours. You have to make it back to your spaceship - but what about all the artifacts you have already found? Decide what to take and what to leave, but remember, if you take too much, you run out of oxygen faster!

The game is played by moving your character across the barren Martian landscape, exploring tiles, attempting to find any Martian artifacts, more air, and most importantly, your ship.  Your spacesuit has three randomly selected abilities that can help your character navigate the hostile terrain.  Abilities which come into play when Event cards are drawn after moving.

The player only wins when:

     The player does not run out of air.
     The player finds the spaceship.
     The player has at least one alien artifact upon reaching the spaceship.


The Mars Exploration mission fails if no items from the Martian Ruins make it back to Earth.

This was an entry in the 2014 Solitaire Print and Play Contest.  It won first place for:

     Best Large Game
     Best Thematic Game
     Best Greyscale Printed Game

In this game, the player adventures through the Temple of Heryshaf, the Ancient Egyptian god of Primordial Waters.  The goal is to reach the apex of the temple - Heryshaf’s Inner Sanctum!  But beware!  For the temple is filled with traps, and the passageways are winding!

At the start of the game, choose one of the five cards at the base of the pyramid to start the path.  Each turn take the token representing your character and move it onto a card.    The token may only move to a new card if the room you are in has a path which leads to that card and the card you are moving to has a corresponding path.  When you attempt to move to a new card, flip the card over and sees what effects are activated.  There are both traps and helper cards you may find inside the temple.  You may choose to backtrack, but if you do, one card on the board is discarded, and a new unknown card takes its place - which may help or hinder you later.

The player wins if they make it through the temple, avoiding the traps or disarming the traps, to arrive safely at Heryshaf's Inner Sanctum.

This game tied for first place in the 24 hour contest for September 2014.

boardgame: Better Me: The Game of Growth and Friendship
description:
Better Me is a new approach to relationships and personal development. It challenges people to take action to improve themselves and their life, and strengthens relationships by helping people understand each other in a deeper and more meaningful way.

The game brings up interesting topics that don't normally come up in daily conversation, allowing players to quickly learn about themselves and their fellow players. And all in an encouraging and fun atmosphere, with no pressure or judgment permitted. When was the last time you told a group of people about a past accomplishment that you're proud of, or had a game challenge you to write a thank you letter to a loved one?

Better Me also harnesses the power of accountability. Any player committing to an action must find an accountability partner to help make sure they follow they follow through on their commitment. Both players write down the action to be taken, and the target completion date on their game sheet. This accountability strategy is key to insuring that players take action and grow.

Players move around the game board and draw cards which prompt all sorts of positive action and discussion. Players race to fulfill each of their five life areas, heart, mind, body, people and tangibles.  The game is won by the first player to "fulfill" (3 points) each of the five categories; heart, mind, body, people, and tangibles.  Alternatively, the game may be played to a time limit, and if no player has fulfilled all categories by the end of the time limit, the player with the most total points is the winner.

In addition to being played by groups of friends and family, Better Me is also popular with counselors, schools/universities, youth mentoring programs, recovery programs, hospitals, etc.

It's a new and engaging way to improve yourself and your relationships with friends, family, and coworkers, all while having fun!

Yakitori, the famed Japanese street food on skewers, is now a game in which players are skewering tiles, placing dice, and manipulating a fickle market to vie for the most Yen from hungry customers.

In the game, players roll dice, select from actions such as Cooking, Market, and Influence. Food tiles are bought, skewered, and sold at just the right time to net a healthy profit. Clever planning can pay off in this family-friendly game that will also intrigue seasoned gamers looking for a thinky game that integrates theme with mechanisms.

boardgame: Kyoui no heya: Geijutsu ni kakusareta allegory
description:
User summary
驚異の部屋：芸術に隠されたアレゴリー (Kyoui no heya: Geijutsu ni kakusareta allegory) in English means 'Rooms of Wonder: Allegories hidden in the art'. The publisher also refers to it in German as Wunderkammer.

Allegories are symbolic items which are drawn in several Western paintings to imply an abstract or spiritual meaning.
In the game of Wunderkammer, you spot such symbolic items drawn in a Western painting, and collect corresponding item tiles in your room. When a certain number of them are collected in your room, you complete the painting and score one point.

When you collect an item tile from opponent's room, you have to give one item tile in exchange.

You should watch your painting precisely, and collect item tiles faster than your opponents. The player who completed most paintings wins the game.

Publisher's summary
プレイヤーは西洋絵画カードに描かれたシンボルを探し当て、該当するシンボルチップを他のプレイヤーよりも早く、自分の部屋に蒐集します。 しかし、蒐集するには自分の部屋のシンボルと、相手の部屋のシンボルを交換しなければなりません。 絵をよく見て、シンボルを奪い合いながら、たくさんの絵画カードを獲得したプレイヤーが勝者となります。

Welcome those from other lands with their own place to stay in your city thanks to Dice City: Embassy, and gain the benefit of their knowledge and abilities.

Dice City: Embassy is a mini-expansion for Dice City that consists of three copies of the same card.

This designing of this game was commissioned by the Dutch "Rijksgebouwendienst" (government building service). It is an attempt to make the changing in their organizational structure more transparent through a boardgame. During the game, player collect points by completing projects en cooperatively realizing the construction of a building. Completing projects is accomplished by negotiating with other players for the right "people". The game contains 6 types of people, each with their own special skills, represented by game chips.

In het spel moeten de spelers punten verdienen door projecten uit te voeren én gezamenlijk een gebouw te realiseren. You are the captain of a crew of workers, abandoned by your superiors on the planet Rhodon V, which you once mined for resources. Now, a cargo ship has crash landed nearby and its crew has offered a ride off of the planet to anyone able to repair their ship – but you aren't the only one to respond to the distress beacon. Will you be the first player to collect all of the Repair Parts to fix the cargo ship and escape the planet?

The goal of Scrap is to be the first player to collect three different Repair Parts and deliver them back to the Start Area. Repair Parts can be collected by passing over specific spaces on the board or by looting them from your opponents after defeating them in battle.

Each player operates a unique vehicle, monstrous tank-like machines that have been constructed from salvaged mining and military equipment. Each vehicle starts with a special ability, different stats, and a set amount of upgrade slots. As players traverse the board, they will collect Resource Cards through different means, which are used to build upgrades, collect Unique Upgrades, trigger Events, and more.

A high quality deck of playing cards made up of letters. With it, you can play hundreds of word games and card games. Instead of numbers, sets and runs, you use letters and make words.

Letter Playing Cards are for all ages. Whether you’re just starting out learning your letters and words in pre-school, or a scholar of the English language, fascinated by words. Whether you're a backpacker travelling light and meeting new friends, or a parent with too much luggage.

It's a whole new dimension on the phrase ‘Let’s have a game of cards’.

Here’s the short list of some of the more popular games you can play with Letter Playing Cards.

Word Poker; Texas Spell ‘Em; Rummy / Gin Rummy; Brag (3,4, 5 card or 9 card!); Patience / solo games; Hangman; Snaffle - the letter grab game; Old Maid; Murder (or Mafia); Word Black Jack; Letter Cheat; Word Speed or Spit; Go Fish; Hearts; Indian Word Poker; Moogle; Spoons; Letter Whist; Anagrams; Crosswords; Ghost; Flash Cards, Spelling tests, Word Bridge.

In the distant past, a starship from a faraway world appeared in the sky. Damaged in battle, the craft broke apart and traced lines of fire across the horizon. These falling stars crashed to the surface, and in the ages to come, became enshrined in legends as the Lost Legacy. Discover where the Lost Legacy can be found and win the game!

Lost Legacy: Orb of Prophecy can be played independently or mixed together with other Lost Legacy sets to create a unique custom set.  This version was released at AEG Big Game Night at GenCON 2015 and won't be sold for retail.

As for how to play, Lost Legacy is a game of risk, deduction, and luck for 2–4 players. You start the game with one card in hand from a deck of sixteen cards. On a turn, you do the following:


         Draw: Draw the top card from the deck and add it to your hand.
         Play: Choose one of the two cards in hand to play and place it face up in front of you.
         Effect: Carry out the played card's effect, after which the card is considered as discarded.
         End: If there is at least one card left in the deck, the turn goes to the next player; if not, the investigation phase starts. Using the investigation speed indicated on the card each player has in hand, players take turns guessing which player (whether yourself or someone else) holds the "Lost Legacy" card; this card might also be in the "Ruins", a location that holds one card at the start of the game and possibly acquires more cards during play. The player who guesses correctly wins; if no one finds the Lost Legacy, then everybody loses.
    


By combining different Lost Legacy sets (while keeping only a single Lost Legacy card in play), up to six players can compete at the same time.

description:
The game consists of two packs of 30 cards. Each card has a picture on one side and a description on the other.
 One pack of cards is spread out picture side up so that all pictures can be seen. The other pack is stacked picture side up. The game starts by turning over the top card of the stack so that the descritipion can be read. Somewhere amongst the pictures showing is the item matching this description but finding the match may not be as easy as it seems as the pictures have been chosen so as to appear to be something else and a little imagination may be needed to make the match.
