A dice-based game where you try to advance your pawn to the winning space by rolling 10's, 11's, and 12's.  It seems to be a thinly-disguised gambling game.

boardgame: Imperium Chronicles: In Harm's Way Tactics – Streets of Regalis
description:
Streets of Regalis is a campaign setting for In Harm's Way Tactics, the turn-based tactical combat game. In a massive expansion of the original game, Streets of Regalis introduces players to the capital city of the Imperium, including three separate districts with distinct missions and factions. With this new campaign, characters can enjoy hours of gameplay in the Imperium Chronicles universe.

(Disclaimer: Not affiliated with the In Harm's Way line of RPGs published by Flying Mice Games.)

Welcome to CitiesUP! 
County officials just offered new opportunities for land purchases, and you are now an official investor for a new city. Build, develop, and increase your income. 
Your job is simple: outperform all the other investors in the game. But don ́t be a shark! And remember the little people: the inhabitants of your new city need services to be satisfied! 
Let ́s build this city!

CitiesUP is a 2-4 player board game. Every player acts like an investor. Build residential, commercial, bank, farm, or industrial buildings, and then supply them with electricity, water, and services. 
Every player collects tax revenue from their buildings, which each have to be supplied with different services to thrive.
Your goal: purchase as many buildings as possible and supply them with water, electricity, and services to reach a higher score than the other players. Buildings have to be supplied properly to generate taxes for the owner. Taxes, bonuses, and upkeep each generate points for the owner. When the last demand card is turned face-up, the round is complete and the game is over. 
If two players have the same amount of points, the tie will be broken by counting each player's money tokens.

In Ghost Hunter: Monsters players become Infernal Club investigators engaged in a cryptozoological big-game hunt. The Club members are spread across the globe chasing the beasts of world folklore. Pride is at stake and the title of ‘Master of the Hunt’ will go to the biggest catch of the season.

This is a card game for 2-6 players that should last for 10 – 30 minutes. Players take turns laying higher and higher cards on a central stack. If a player can’t lay an equal or higher card on a stack then they must pick up the stack. If a player lays a set or run of 3 cards at once then they get to capture the stack. The first player to play all of their cards ends the game. Players count up the scores of all of the cards they have captured minus all of the cards they didn’t manage to get rid of. The player with the highest score wins the game.

Ghost Hunter: Monsters is a standalone game but it is also one of a series of compatible Ghost Hunter decks. You may take cards from any of these decks and shuffle them together to create new and interesting versions of the game.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
A compilation item containing both Munchkin Fu and Munchkin Fu 2.

Munchkin Fu won the Origins "Gamer's Choice Award" for the Best Card Game of 2003. How do you follow something like that? With more mooks, more mayhem, more monsters, more munchkins, more monks - especially more monks - in Munchkin Fu 2: Monky Business!

Learn new styles like Kong Fu, Fee Fi Fo Fu, Sna Fu, and the ever-perilous Stomach Fu! Pick up dangerous new weapons like the Auspicious Ivory Gutting Hook and the Tofu Tonfa. Garb yourself in the Hong Kong Sarong. Slay foes like the Sex Gaki, Clamera, and Genghis Cong... and take their stuff!

The team of Origins-Award-winning designer Steve Jackson (I) and mad cartoonist Greg Hyland (Lethargic Lad, Munchkin Fu) are back again for Monky Business. It's got 110 evil and overpowered cards, plus two blanks to create your own. And of course, like all other Munchkin products, this supplement is completely compatible with the original Munchkin and all its supplements and spinoffs, including Star Munchkin, Munchkin Bites!, and Munchkin Blender.

Following the fall of Rhodes, the Knights of St John rewarded Suleiman’s magnanimity by harassing Turkish shipping and commerce from their new home in Malta. In 1565, Suleiman sent his generals to finish the task he had left incomplete more than 40 years previously. The Turks laid siege with a force that reached 60,000 in number. They would exterminate the 500 Knights of St John defending the island and wipe the Order from the face of the earth – or so they thought.  If ever there was an ‘against the odds’ situation then this was surely it.

Waning Crescent, Shattered Cross (WCSC) recreates the incredible events that occurred on this small Mediterranean island and changed the course of European history. Using cards to drive events, WCSC is a relatively quick playing, moderate complexity simulation. Players must decide whether to try to build their hands to a size that might deliver a decisive blow or to use their cards to gradually wear down their opponent. And the play of cards complements the management of resource including supply, artillery, men and even honour. Acts of treachery, torture and atrocities will contrast starkly with acts of heroism, rousing speeches and even the celebration of Saints Days in the midst of horror and terror.

Along with the full 2-player campaign scenario, there is also a short/learning scenario and a 'bot' controlled solitaire variant.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: History: Classical Age of the Ottoman Empire

boardgame: El Gaucho: Fodder for the Cattle
description:
Contained in Brettspiel Adventskalender 2015 for Day 21.

This mini expansion introduces feedbags, which you can use to increase the value of a Herd when you sell it.

Carcassonne: Winter-Edition: Die Kornkreise – which is playable only with the Winter-Edition of Carcassonne – consists of six new tiles and rules. A corn circle tile allows a player to place a second follower on a feature that they have already-claimed or return an already-placed follower back to his supply.

Carcassonne: Winter-Edition: Die Kornkreise debuted as a promotional item at Essen 2015.

Also contained in Brettspiel Adventskalender 2015 for Day 23.

There's a big mess on the table! Who can clean up the cards the fastest, while also lining up cards that match one another?

In Kuddelmuddel – German for "hodgepodge" – players try to put together chains of cards in order to score points. To set up the game, the players scatter the 80 cards in the deck face down on the table and place the bell within reach of all players. Everyone then simultaneously starts looking at face-down cards one by one. You can place the first card you see face up to start a row in front of you. To place another card next to this first one, whether on the left or right, the newly placed card must have at least one image in common with the card already in place.

As soon as a player has seven matching cards in a row, she rings the bell. If she goofed up, she sits out the round until another player creates a row; if she did everything correctly, she scoops up those cards and places them in a pile beside her while all other players reshuffle their cards into those that remain on the table.

The game ends whenever the last card is taken from the center of the table. Whoever has collected the most cards (i.e., rows) wins!

---

This is a small, promotional edition of Kuddelmuddel containing 32 "cards" to cut out yourself.
Includes both the original Munchkin game – albeit the revised 2011 edition with color cards and updated text – and Munchkin 2: Unnatural Axe.

From the publisher:

BATLHEX (pronounced bat'l-heks) is a novel strategy board game played by two opponents who battle against one another with game pieces on a hexagonal board. To win a player must be the first to capture all of the opponent's pieces. Each player’s ability to move or return a piece to the board is determined by rolling a die. This highly replayable game, which features a game board, die, twelve game pieces, and a rule sheet, incorporates a unique method of play that makes the game both easy to learn by the youngest players and a challenge for mature gamers. (Level: Beginner-Intermediate)

BATLHEX Commander is a variation of the basic game that removes the die and adds a Commander piece to both sides. The object is to capture the opponent's Commander first. All of the pieces (with the exception of the Commander) share the same unique movement capability, and both player's have a chance to bring a captured piece back during a turn if they occupy a certain hex. BATLHEX Commander allows both players to think several turns ahead and develop various long-term strategies. (Level: Intermediate-Advanced)

A deck of Invention cards have a picture and the name of the invention on one side and the year it was invented on the other. Four Invention cards are placed on the table with the year face down. Each player has four player chips with first, second, third and fourth in them. Players place their chips face down next to the Invention cards to indicate the order they think those 4 cards were invented. For each chip placed next to the correct invention card, players receive a point. These points can be wagered in future rounds. Whoever has the highest score after 5 rounds, wins!

The Invention Deck has one Invention per year with no duplicate years. They include a wide variety of inventions in consecutive years spanning from the late 1890's to the 1970's. Inventions are easily recognizable but just obscure enough that each game will be a challenge!

This is a promotional playable character for the Krosmaster series of games, released at PAX 2015.

This character has his name in red, which indicates that he is only meant for fun and non-competitive play. He won't be legal in tournament, and won't be available in the online version of the game.
This is a promotional playable character for the Krosmaster series of games, first released at PAX 2015.
This is a promotional playable character for the Krosmaster series of games, first released at PAX 2015.
This is a promotional playable character for the Krosmaster series of games, first released at PAX 2015.

This is a promotional playable character for the Krosmaster series of games, released at PAX 2015.

This character has his name in red, which indicates that he is only meant for fun and non-competitive play. He won't be legal in tournament, and won't be available in the online version of the game.

Koromo Japanese workshop is an educational game of Lean Management. Players take on the role of the owner of the garage, which is still working to bring the greatest gains. The game allows you to familiarize yourself with concepts such as efficiency, quality and continuous improvement.

In each round, players perform moves in two rounds. In the first round followed by perfecting his workshop in three categories (quality, productivity, order). Such an action requires an adequate number of points of knowledge from the player. Points knowledge obtained at the beginning of the game and wins throughout its duration. In the second round, each player receives one car repair order, which is placed on the player board (workshop). The player uses their moves (moving pawns) to repair the damaged car best. The game ends when one player accumulates the appropriate number of experience points (20). Experience points are awarded for the improvement actions and repaired cars.

These 8 cards were included as promo decks #1 and #2 with the Twilight Struggle digital Kickstarter.  It comprises of 4 card sheets with descriptions of the cards on them, an alternate Space Race Track board, a special Zaire counter to be used on the board if the "Mobutu Sese Seko" event card is activated, and 8 cards:

Non-Aligned Movement
Mobutu Sese Seko
Berlin Wall
Stanislav Petrov
Who Lost China?
"Don't Wait for the Translation"
"Kremlin Flu"
"First Lightning"

Best Friend S is a party game.

Guess my favorite travel destination!
Ask a question to the other players.
As an answerer, come up with something that will please the giver.
Show that you "care" and understand the giver to win the place of BEST FRIEND!

100 question cards included.
For example, "Guess my best answer for what I'd want to do on my last day on earth", or "Guess my best answer for what I was in my previous life".

Including new infinitely-rewritable "Pixel slates" & Special pens, so players do not have to prepare writing materials.

Grisa is a mountainous land!
The Ogres are friendly. They have a very strong team spirit but their strength and love of others are often a problem for the opposing team. In fact seeing Ogres gobble up a piece of their opponent is not a rare sight. Before winning, the opponent needs to stay in one piece! "
In this box, you’ll find the necessary to play the Ogres Team :
o    9 pre-painted miniatures, Player Cards, POWER Cards, …

This is an expansion box. It needs the standard HELVETIA Cup box to be played!

The box contains :
o    1 miniature cup.
o    2 prepainted miniature stars (the Medusa and the Genius).
o    The player, POWER and LEGEND cards in relation to the Stars.
o    4 new goals with a beautiful new design.
o    40 boosts in form of potions.

This is an expansion box. It needs the standard HELVETIA Cup box to be played!

In this box, you’ll find the necessary to organize Tournament
The box contains :

     A second TOTOMAT.
     2 more dice D20.
     A time marker.
     A memo to record your score and team progress.
     A normal game board. At the bottom, you’ll find a tournament desk for the Cup and Tournament. You’ll be able to write progress of the Championship on it with an easily erasable pen.
     Number indicator tags, to make the numbers easier to see on the field.  
     1 more balls.

Each player has a set of pawns that start in his or her corner of the board. The goal is to move the pawns around the board to the "home" spaces. Movement is determined by a die. All players move around the board on their own track in the shape of a triangle. All 4 triangles together form a star, so the players may capture each other's pawns at the intersections. Captured pawns are returned to their player's corner and must start their journey over. The winner is the first player to move all pawns "home".

The game was published between 1923 and 1937.

boardgame: La boîte de la langue française
description:
La boîte de la langue française is a game of various questions about French language.

The first edition contains 300 questions and the second edition 500 questions.

32 cards, four of each family (color) that make it possible to play two games:

Action Game:

The object of this game is to get rid of all your cards as soon as possible. Each player gets 5 cards and the rest go in a pile face-down with 1 card turn up next to it. In a player turn he must play a card with the same colour, number or symbol as the face-up next to the draw pile. When a card with a symbol it's played, the action related to that symbol must be performed. Then the next player goes.
When no card can be played, the player must take the top card from the face-down pile. If that card matches it can be played, if not the turn moves for the next player.
You win if get rid of all your cards.

Family Game:

The object of the game is to get the most families of minions you can (4 of each colour makes a family). One player ask for a type of card to any other player. If he can form a family he displays it in front of it and can continue until one player can group all the families he has in his hand at any given moment.
If a player asks a card and the other player does not have it that player's turn ends.

This new specialist card expands the solo game variant.

When you choose Harlekin for a building period, do not shuffle it with the other cards. Put it at the bottom of the shuffled stack instead. Harlekin will always be one of the last two cards from which you can choose one to play for both actions.

His first ability clears all remaining buildings in a row on the central board.

His second ability gives you 3 of a certain resource, determined by rolling a die.

This expansion, originally available as a promotional item in Spielbox 2015/7, appears in the Glass Road base game starting with the 2021 editions.

Put down your weapons! You fought your battles, now hire heroes for the hard work. Players bid to complete quests and earn rewards.

Each player begins with gold that they use to secretly bid for Heroes to complete their quests. The player with the highest bid wins the Hero's service. Their quest and hero is set aside until the required number of days (turns) pass. The Hero then returns with gold. Journey tokens add twists that may help or hurt players. Play continues around the table with new quests and new heroes. After 10 turns any heroes still out questing come home and all players count their gold to see who is richest!

The objective of Kust Ma Seda Kuulsin? ("Where did I hear it?") is to guess the quote: which movie, song, novel etc. is it.
Player who guesses correctly moves forward on the game board.
Who finishes first is the winner.

From the box:

"A fast-paced guessing game of delicious deduction.

Take turns playing customer and server. As the customer, make three food selections. Place your order by displaying the menu clue cards. Flip the timer! The server rushes to fill the order by using the clues. Ring the bell when the order is correct!

The more food items you serve correctly, the more money you earn! Switch roles and play again!"

This game for young children features cardboard food items, matching clue cards, and a screen to allow the customer to secretly choose three food items. The server can see only the clues (one for each item, describing color, shape, or temperature) and must figure out the customer's order using logic and process of elimination.

1% is a deckbuilding game where not only are you building your deck collecting point and/or useful card while also trying to take over sites that give you powerful abilities. Collecting three of these sites instantly wins the game so players must be aware, battle opponent's crew, and interact more than your usual deckbuilder. 
A drafting mechanic while setting up allows you to customize your gang members prior to play so you feel like you have actually customized your crew from actual strategy and choice.

Röda Tråden is a quizgame. The game comes with 80 cards with two subjects on each card. Every subject have 10 clues. One of the players reads a card and knows the answer, tells the other players the subject, and then, one by one, going thru the clues. The players can try to answer on high levels and get a higher score, or wait to answer and get lower score. Every player starts each subject with 10 points, and loses one for each new clue. The winner is the player with most points at the end of the game.

Footy Feud! is an Australian football themed-card game.

Players are dealt numeric cards, and choose the highest value for scoring.
The objective is to have the highest score at the end of the game (played over 4 quarters).

4 cards in the deck allow stealing or score multiplication.

Do I need to know about Australian football to play the game?
No; Footy Feud! has tested very well with people of all ages who have no interest in Australian football.
If you are familiar with the Australian football scoring method it may make the game initially easier to grasp.

From the publisher´s website:

In a small town far-far away, everyone is excited for Christmas. There are holiday lights in all the windows, the houses await the holiday all groomed up. 
The kids are walking home from Christmas celebration. The way is long and slippery. And you have to stop so many times, to look at shop windows, talk, pick up lost hats, scarves… but maybe they have time to help the angel, too… no?

Players roll die and move forward. If they reach "Ice"-space the slip another space. "Deep snow" make You move slower. If more than one player is one and the same place they chatting and only 5 or 6 allows You to move again. And many other things.

Player who reaches home first is the winner.

It is the board game version of the popular tv show "Still Standing".
In this game every player can win! Answer correctly to the quiz, and you can keep your chance for the "money". Because if you are wrong other player wins.
All of the 10 characters will play until he/she "falls down".

In Draftica, each player is a Celtic chieftain assembling a tribe of 18 cards. Combinations of cards score points, and the player with the most points at the end of the game wins.

Players do this by collecting (or "drafting") both common cards in the colours red, green, blue and yellow, and rarer special cards that can provide a substantial reward for specialising in certain types of card.

Unlike some other drafting games, there is no lengthy playout phase to determine a winner - the game takes place entirely in the drafting phase itself, with many opportunities to outwit and outmanoeuvre your rivals.  May the best drafter win!

New version of Alias which challenges players to a rap battle! Two teams go against each other and try to beat each other with the power of words. Alongside with traditional Alias, Rähinä Alias also includes a Battle phase where players attempt to figure out rhymes from the given words.

The players must find the ghosts in the forest, by catching them in headlights of their cars.

The game is played on a five by five grid of tiles. Players taking turns moving their car on the grid, turning their headlights on after each move. This allows them to flip over the tiles in a straight line in front of the car - until they reach a ghost, or the end of the board.

The player that finds the most ghosts wins the game.

The object of Orator is to deliver the best orations on a variety of topics without making any blunders within a two minute time limit. Players attempt to avoid blunders commonly made in spoken English today, such as, 'umm', 'aah', 'err' etc.

Players may resort to bluffing to complete their talk however successful detection of bluffing and making of blunders incurs penalties. The player who is given the highest score by the other players (the audience) wins the round. The winner of Orator is the player with the best overall score for the game.

User summary:
シラストピア (Sirasutopia) is a competition to be the most charming girl in the class between two girls: Sirasu, an ordinary Primary School girl, and Pia, an avatar of Rabbit Tribe who is pretending to be a school girl. Players plan their days by choosing three out of five action cards.

Each card has various effects, such as raising their statistics (intelligence or dressing), attracting a boy they like, and interfering opponent's actions.
After the third round, they gain victory points if they meet the conditions for a charming girl.

Publisher's summary:
平凡な小学生しらすちゃんと、ウサギ族の化身ぴあたんが 
クラスのイケてる存在を目指すゲームです。
プレーヤーはしらすちゃんかぴあたんを演じ
5枚のアクションカードのうち3枚をセットし日々の予定を立てます。
すると、学力やオシャレ度が向上したり男の子の気を引いたり
相手の行動の邪魔もします。
それらを3ラウンド行い、最終的にイケてる存在の条件を達成し勝利点を得ます。

From the publisher's website:
Have you ever found yourself...

Not knowing what you're doing at the gym
Being too drunk at a wedding
Farting in yoga class

Just Four Kicks is a hilarious party game where anything can happen through creativity, quick thinking, and general debauchery. Watch your friends try to act out “being too drunk at a wedding” or use someone else’s body to depict “farting in yoga class.” Bring it to your next game night and it'll be guaranteed to bring your friends together through countless laughs and just a touch of embarrassment.

from the back of the box:
Do you like fun? Do you enjoy laughing? Are you interested in having an all around good time? If you answered yes to any of these questions (hopefully all) grab a copy of Just Four Kicks and prepare to have the time of your life. Inside jokes included in the box.

Now I'm sure you're thinking, wow, this game sounds amazing, how is it played? Start by finding at least three friends (or strangers) and splitting into two equal teams. Just Four Kicks has four rounds of play that will get progressively more intense and hilarious, so choose your teammates wisely. Each player has 30 seconds to get their team to guess as many cards as possible by describing the topic, doing charades, or using someone else's body as a puppet. The team that guesses the most cards wins!

Close Counts is the party game where the best guesstimate wins! How many Coco-Cola products are sold every day? What did the world's most expensive painting cost? How many DeLorean sports cars were manufactured? How many pennies would it take to circle the world? Don't know!?!? It doesn't matter!!! You don't need to know the answer, you just have to be closer than everyone else - now that's what counts! There are six categories of statistical fun: Time Traveller, Numbers and Nature, Dollars and Cents, Records and Feats, What Year Was That? and No Way! New 2015

From the publisher:

Thrones is a two-player 9x9 board game where each players set their tiles and pieces secretly, screened from their opponent's view, until the screen is removed, the middle fifth row is set, and the battle's combat begins.

Gameplay:
Each player controls an army of 13 and a homeland of 36 tiles, including 1 Fortress.

A screen separates the players as they set their sides of the battlefield in secret.

Before the screen is removed, both players choose positions for the tiles in a middle row between their two sides.

Once the screen is removed, the middle row tiles positions are resolved, and the remaining empty space is occupied by a neutral Fortress.

The game begins! Tiles affect their piece’s ability to move, attack, and defend itself- sometimes to their benefit, sometimes not. Players must battle their opponent’s army while preserving their own, but will lose if they lose their Throne piece.

A player instantly defeats their opponent by moving their Throne piece into their enemy's Fortress- no matter what pieces either player controls.

boardgame: No Quarter Presents Forces of Distinction
description: No Quarter Presents: Forces of Distinction compiles all the “Forces of Distinction” articles from No Quarter magazine through issue #56 in one volume—over thirty theme forces with original fiction. No Quarter Presents: Forces of Distinction also includes two all-new theme forces unique to this product.

boardgame: You & Me: a game of love and intimacy
description:
A fun game for lovers promising exciting encounters and romantic inspirations. With 90 innovative ideas, sexy suggestions and adventurous activities, You & Me is a game where the taking part really is almost as good as winning!

Roll the dice to see who will enjoy the intimate attentions of their lover as they perform one of the many passionate pleasures prescribed by the cards.
The player performing the action is the one who receives the points. 
The winner is the first player to reach 30 points.

In this game based on the animated series players attempt to collect since, toxins, viruses, and bacteria matching the colors on their player board. During their turn a player spins a spinner and takes the corresponding color of standee.

boardgame: Cluedo: Doctor Who – The Hunt for Gallifrey
description:
The Doctor may have defeated Missy and stopped the Cyberman army released from the Nethersphere, but his old friend continues playing her twisted games, toying with the Doctor’s mind. In her final moments, she reveals that she knows a secret he cannot resist…the location of Gallifrey.

But the coordinates she gives him just lead to empty space and in Cluedo: The Hunt for Gallifrey, you play the part of the Doctor, visiting nine recent locations, with six inventions to meet six recent enemies and uncover the true coordinates of your lost home.
family: Game: Cluedo / Clue (Parker Brothers, et al)

description:
This extension was on the back of the catalogue of the Haba Family Games. (In .PDF Format)

General rules:

The 4 bonus tiles are placed separately in the middle of the table at the beginning of the game. The first player that fulfills one of the four requirements receives the corresponding bonus tile. If two or more players fulfill the same requirement in one round, the bonus tile is removed from the game.

1 “Fastest treasure collector” bonus tile
The first player to collect at least 1 gold nugget or 1 crystal in every adventure receives this bonus tile. 
Value: 2 victory points.

2 “Best treasure collector” bonus tile
The first player to collect at least 4 gold nuggets or crystals in any one adventure receives this bonus tile. 
Value: 3 victory points.

3 “Best route builder” bonus tile
The first player to use routes to connect all the adventurers with temples that match their color receives this bonus tile. 
Value: 3 victory points.

4 “Fastest runner” bonus tile
The first player to move all their adventurers at least once receives this bonus tile. 
Value: 2 victory points.

This expansion was on the back of the catalogue of the Haba Family Games.

General rules:

The 4 tiles are set out face down and shuffled in a stack. If a player picks up an herb tile with the value of "2" they can immediately trade it for one of these bonus herb tiles. To do so, the player picks up the top tile from the stack of bonus herb tiles. The traded-in herb tile is removed from the game.

1. "Gold sword" bonus herb tile
2 dice (instead of just one) can be used for the gold sword. The tile is removed from the game after it has been used.

2. "Platinum sword" bonus herb tile
3 dice (instead of just one) can be used for the platinum sword. The tile is removed from the game after it has been used.

3. "Magic gold herbs" bonus herb tile
Combat strength is increased by 5 points when the magic gold herb tile is used. The tile is removed from the game after it has been used.

4. "Loyal wolf" bonus herb tile
Combat strength is increased by 2 points when the wolf tile is used. The wolf remains at the side of the player as a loyal companion and can be used in every battle.

Simple roll and move game. The first player to reach space 60 on exact count wins the game. The red numbered spaces represent events from the fairy tale that hinder or benefit the players.

The game was most likely produced between 1918 and 1922, since it doesn't show the "ABC Verlag" imprint.

It was also part of at least one game compilation.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Hansel and Gretel

description:
A fully cooperative survival miniatures board game. Mission-based with a highly modular game board.

Every player takes the roll of a survivor to fight against bad hunters and mutants, causes through the outbreak 5 years ago.

Red Fall offers a great combat system where positioning is the key to survive. You have to find Items, weapons, crafting upgrades with components to manage your character. You can learn skills to improve your chances to survive.

Beside the combat system one of the great and innovative game features is the enemy AI. Every enemy acts by itself. They have movement paths and smart combat mechanics.

In the survival game turned on its undead head Dead By Night, you are part of the legions that now hunt and stalk the remnants of humanity that survive in this shattered and ruined city. Each day still weakens you, and some mortals even attempt to hunt you down as you race against rival beings who are seeking the power to rule the night itself.

Hand management and consistent player interaction make this a fun and casual, yet deceptively strategic risk vs reward-style game.
Typical spin and move game. The first player, who cross all the route, wins the game.

This cardgame plays a lot like "Werewolves of Miller's Hollow", but with a twist.

The settings is the wild west, in a town called Tombstone. Two outlaws and a wannabe outlaw are hidden amongst the cowboys, and each night they kill an innocent cowboy. Their goal is to bring down the number of cowboys untill they outnumber them. But each morning the cowboys gather and hang someone, hoping to eliminate the outlaws.

Each player is secretly assigned a role : you may be the sheriff, the barber, the doctor, the priest or the hero. You can hide this identity, but if you want to use your special ability (like healing a shot person if you are the doctor, of taking the confession of another player if you are the priest) then you need to reveal your card. Of course, this is a risk if the outlaws consider you dangerous.

Unlike the werewolves, the outlaws can have a special character too. The priest, or even the hero, can be an outlaw. This makes the paranoia even bigger: when the cowboys decide to hang a person, why does the judge decide to let him free? Why doesn't the doctor heal the priest after being shot? This make the game much more intriguing. No more "hey i'm a special character i'm innocent"...

Another difference is that you can play the game with lesser persons. There are rules to play the game with up to 25 players, but slightly different rules to make it playable with 13-24 players, 4-12 players, and even 2-3 players. In the case of 4-12 players each player has more than one character, which means you are not eliminated out of the game as soon as one character dies.

A card game to exerce memory. Each player in turn plays a card and either says the specified word or acts according to the specified action. The game continues until someone makes a mistake and forget the words or actions on hidden cards. Whoever has the highest number of cards wins!

Un jeu de cartes pour faire travailler sa mémoire. Chacun à son tour pose une carte et dit le mot ou fait le geste indiqué. Le jeu continue jusqu’à ce que quelqu’un se trompe et oublie les mots ou les actions des cartes cachées. Celui qui a le plus grand nombre d’« avions à réaction » gagne la partie !

From the publisher (translated):
In this game, the two faces of the cards are useful and each has a different animal. To win, nothing more simple: Get a hand of cards of the same animal. But be careful because each card played imposes an action that could work against you!

From the publisher:
Dans ce jeu, les 2 faces des cartes sont utiles et chacune comporte un animal différent. Pour gagner, rien de plus simple : Obtenir dans sa main des cartes d'un même animal. Mais attention car chaque carte jouée impose une action qui pourrait se retourner contre vous !

77 TENZI games in 3 variations: Regular, pattern, and non-speed. Each of the 77 cards in the deck have its own pattern or rules you can use to modify your TENZI game.  The pack also comes with 3 blank cards you can write your own rules on.

Adding this to your Tenzi dice is paramount in making the game fun for multiple rounds.  Always yell "Tenzi" to signal that you've won.  All the pattern cards use 9 dice to create the pattern but don't forget to roll a 6 on the final dice in order to win! The non-speed rounds change the game up greatly.  You either take turns rolling the dice in rounds or all roll your dice together.  If no one has won then continue on to the next round.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
From the box:

Taking place against the backdrop of the brutal Sanctus Reach campaign, Stormclaw pits the Space Wolves of Krom Dragongaze against the Orks of the barbaric Warboss Grukk Face-rippa. Inside this box you will find a fantastic collection of Citadel miniatures representing the key warriors of this conflict. In addition, there is a narrative book and a compact version of the Warhammer 40,000 rules, allowing you to read about and reenact the pivotal battles.

ENGLISH
Starfleet Legions is a card game for 1-4 players which takes us to a science fiction universe where humanity is threatened by the alien Insectoids: A highly hostile race intended to end our specie. You'll be in command of a infantry squad, traveling through the galaxy and eliminating the threat. Starfleet Legions is a competitive game. The squadron with the most Victory Points at the end of the game wins. You'll need get the most from your 4 soldiers, equip them with the best possible weapons and use cunning tactics.

During combats, each player will be drawing and allocating result cards for the members of their squads, trying to maximize casualties and considering their strengths and advantages. All the result decks have the same proportion of cards: critical success (1), success (2), partial success (3), partial failure (1), failure (2) and fumble (1). When a card is assigned to a soldier, it can no longer be changed during that round of combat. All results are evaluated at the end of the round depending on the skills and equipment of each soldier in the squad. Requiring enemies are eliminated before those who has survived attack the squad members.

ESPAÑOL:
Starfleet Legions (Las Legiones del Espacio), es un juego de cartas de 1 a 4 jugadores, que nos traslada a un universo de ciencia ficción en el que la Humanidad se encuentra amenazada por los Insectoides alienígenas: Una raza altamente hostil, que pretende acabar con nuestra especie. Estarás al mando de una Escuadra de infantería de Las Legiones del Espacio, encargada de viajar a lo largo de la galaxia, eliminando a la amenaza Insectoide allí donde se encuentre. Starfleet Legions es un juego competitivo, en el que la Escuadra con mayor cantidad de Puntos de Victoria resultará vencedora al final de la partida. Deberás sacar el máximo partido posible a tus cuatro Soldados, equiparlos con las mejores armas posibles y utilizar sus tácticas con astucia.

Durante los combates, cada jugador extrae y asigna cartas de resultado para los miembros de su escuadra, tratando de maximizar las bajas y teniendo en cuenta sus fortalezas y ventajas. Todos los mazos de resultados tienen la misma proporción: crítico (1), éxito (2), éxito parcial (3), fallo parcial (1), fallo (2) y pifia (1). Cuando se asigna una carta a un soldado, ya no se puede cambiar durante ese asalto de combate. Todos los resultados son evaluados al final de la ronda en función de las habilidades y equipo de cada soldado en la escuadra. Los enemigos que se requieran son eliminados antes de que, los que ha sobrevivido ataquen a los miembros de la escuadra.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Holiday Special 2015

In uncertain times mysterious objects appear in the void of the galaxy.  Rumored to be sent through space/time rifts by the Elders, these caches contain wonders that help the civilizations through the dark.  They can be used for great benefit by those who are able to share the contents.

Eclipse: Gift of the Elders is a print and play expansion.  It adds Elder Gift Tiles which will come available in a randomly determined round during a special Gift Phase.  Each Gift Tile has a unique effect like gaining resources, gaining ships, gaining VPs for certain features.

boardgame: Kemet: Skills and Black Power Tile For Ta-Seti
description:
3 new skills to play with the path to Ta-Seti and a new black power tile originally distributed as promo items during Essen 2015.

Contains the follwing gaming material:

1 black power tile:

     Piety: awards one additional permanent VP if you control at least 2 temples at the beginning of the night phase


3 new skills available on the path to Ta-Seti:

     Immunity: ignore any damage outside of combat
     Neth Shields: +1 protection during battle
     Offering: after battle, gain 1 prayer point per opposing unit destroyed in this battle


Note: "Kemet: Ta-Seti" expansion is required in order to use these new power tile and skills.

3 new power tiles originally distributed as promo items during Essen 2015.

Contains the following gaming material:

1 blue power tile:
- Protective aura: ignore any damage outside of combat

1 red power tile:
- Night Expedition: one priest can move to the next location on the path to Ta-Seti during the night phase, without benefit from the bonuses between the locations nor taking any object or skills token on the location

1 white power tile:
- Reminiscence: At the end of battle, if the opponent has played at least one DI card, you may take one of them in your hand

Note: "Kemet: Ta-Seti" expansion is required in order to use these new power tiles

Orig. - "Fahrt ins Mühltal", eng. - "The trip to Muhltal (a municipality in the district of Darmstadt-Dieburg, in Hesse, Germany)". 
The game consists of a board with holes in. Each hole scores a certain number of points. At the back of the board, a three-dimensional 'slides' is erected. Into the top of these slides, a player in his turn, drops a ball. The ball will bounce around the 3 separated slides and come out at the bottom and score points when it settles into one of the holes.

The points available range from 1 to 10.

The one who earns the most points, wins.

Path Finder is an education game designed to help teach measuring and comparisons of length and area, along with logic and reasoning.

To play the game, players take turns rolling a die and placing a piece of the corresponding color (if able) in the carved wooden path.

In the Level 1 variant the goal is simply to fill up the path.
In the Level 2 variant, each player gets an equal number of pieces and tries to be the first to place them all.

History of Civilizations is a card-driven civilization game for 2 to 8 players that depict the rise and fall of empires from 4000BCE to 1900CE. Each player leads his empires to Glory through conquest, trade, science, religion, wealth, culture and many other means. The player with most Glory at the end of the game is the winner.

History of Civilizations differs from most other civilization board games as each player controls several civilizations at once. The game is divided into 5 Ages and takes place on a map spanning the entire world. Each round, players will take turns playing cards from their hand to build cities and wonders, move units, adopt religions, gain powerful leaders, trade with other empires, produce new units and many other things. Each card also depicts an empire that can be started if the game has reached a certain age and an event. Events can be played to affect the game in many ways; everything from natural disasters, generals, spies, revolts, golden ages and usurpers is included in the game.

Each empire has an individual set of Glory conditions that define what that particular empire must achieve in order to earn Glory points (victory points). For example, the Mongols want to be the largest empire in the world and conquer other empires, whereas England wants to have the most colonies and the largest navy. At the end of each round, all empires in play gain Glory according to their empire card. If an empire is performing poorly, it can simply be discarded to free up that empire slot, allowing you to play another empire later. This way, new and more modern empires keep coming into play as the game progresses.

History of Civilizations has many small and simple parts that make up a complex and highly dynamical game, where no two games will play out the same way. Imagine western history without the Romans and Greeks. What if the Aztecs and Incas held out against the Spanish conquistadors? What would the world look like if you made all the decisions?

Ta bouche is a card game from 1 to 5 players.

Includes: 110 cards (50 'Thoughts' cards, 50 'Retort' cards, 10 scoring cards), 1 dice.

Each player draws a card. One of the players read out loud one of the Thoughts lines: the other players, each at their turn, must use one of their 'Retort' lines to snap back the silliest or funnniest retort.

The player who fails to do that, or gives the lamest retort (after a decision by the reader), is shouted out: "Ta bouche" and loses 1 point on the score track.

At the end of a round, the player with the most points loses the game.

This is the familial variation of the game "Taggle". It's different from the classical version in the sense that the thoughts lines are adapted for the youngest, while "Taggle" is more 'adult' and could not please all parents.

boardgame: The Big Book of Madness: 2016 Greeting Card – Evil Santa
description:
The Big Book of Madness: 2016 Greeting Card — Evil Santa is a promo gift grimoire-page-as-half-of-a-greeting-card for The Big Book of Madness. The text:

THE SPIRIT OF CHRISTMAS
Shuffle all cards in the support pool and players' hands. Starting with the active player, deal them in clockwise order back to players' hands.

[defeated monster]
BETTER TO GIVE…
Choose a player. Each other player places 1 card from their hand on top of the chosen player's deck in any order. Then, each player except the chosen one draws a card.

[monster victorious]
…THAN TO RECEIVE!
Each player draws a random card from the player's hand to their right and places it on their own discard pile.

boardgame: Rock Stage: A Path to Fame
description:
The world's first board game about heavy music, which brought together the experience of the leading concert and promotional agencies.

The player forms a band from the deck of over 100 musician cards. With the band the player attempts to gain as much fame as possible with the money they earn from the band. The player with the most fame at the end of the game wins.

boardgame: Krypton Factor Interactive DVD Family Board Game
description:
The Krypton Factor Interactive DVD game is a mind-bending fun filled challenging board game the whole family can play.

Use all your powers of Mental Agility, Observation, Intelligence, Physical Agility and General Knowledge to answer questions, solve puzzles and take physical challenges in order to progress around the infamous Krypton assault course.

Gain Stamina tokens on the way to overcome the 13 course obstacles that await you. Will you be the first across the finish line and be able to call yourself the Krypton Factor Champion?

The North Sea Runesaga is an epic competition for 2-4 players to collect the most Runestones and earn the highly coveted title of Chieftain. Players will progress through each chapter of the North Sea saga (Shipwrights, Raiders and Explorers).

Each chapter offers 3 unique Runestones for players to collect. These are awarded to the first player to achieve a particular objective. Gaining these Runestones also awards players advantages in successive chapters. Each of these Runestones also have 1 or 2 inscriptions (Shield, Horn and Hammer). The first player to gain 3 inscriptions of 1 type collects an additional Runestone for their efforts.

Players also gain 2 Runestones for winning each chapter or 1 Runestone for coming a close second. However, Runestones collected this way do not award any in-game advantages or inscriptions.

Once all 3 chapters have been played, the player with the most Runestones is the overall winner and takes the title of Chieftain!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Series: The North Sea Trilogy (Garphill Games)

description:
Abstract game where players will build the dungeon rooms with cards from their hands until reaching the exit of the dungeon. Players will earn victory points matching cards played with position of their pawns.

The goal is to arrive to the exit but be sure to have enough points to win.

boardgame: Dungeon Storming: Il Nido di Phirion
description:
From the back of the box:

"Alone, in the depths of the dungeon, surrounded by monsters. A Barbar and a Ranger against a host of fierce Draconians. Rich treasures await you, with which you can get new weapons, armor, spells and scrolls. Will you become so powerful to face the Dragon Phitrion and become heroes?"

A collaborative game for two players or solo. It can be played with more players by combining two or more sets of Dungeon Storming!"

The game is played by moving the miniatures of your heroes on the map, room by room, battling the monsters with a simple roll of a die, dodging the traps, collecting treasures and magic objects, until the heroes reach the last room and try to overcome the boss monster (the heroes can exit the dungeon and buy new objects with the money found).

The map and the layout of the rooms for this dungeon are fixed (all the tables for the encounters, treasures and magical objects are printed on it), but it can be played with four levels of difficulty.

The nobles of Rolldovia have struck a rich vein of gold, and now it is time to put it to good use! The competition to be the new capital has now taken on a new twist, with not just new architecture, but the power to inspire through great craftsmanship or simply extravagant displays of wealth. But beware, there is a limited supply. It is time to show off all that glitters; it is time to once again roll, build, and win!

Dice City: All That Glitters includes three new locations of each type — military, economic, cultural and civic — as well as a new location available throughout the game: the Gold Mine, a Commodity location. This expansion also comes with a supply of gold tokens with which to play.

The find and hide game of chickens and eggs!

Cluck! Cluck! What an “egg-cellent” memory you have!

A “My First Game” for your clever little farmer. With constant motion, Peek-A-Doodle Doo engages your child's memory and concentration in a farm fresh way!

Fast-moving fun; game pieces are in constant motion!

First, use memory to collect all 12 eggs and return them to the crate. Then, use memory to cover all 12 eggs by chickens
Free range chickens feature fun rubbery beaks, combs and tails
Components of silicone rubber, plastic, reinforced printed cardboard.

Sporttable Box is a fusion between boardgames and Physical Activity.

On the board, you have the possibility of playing three different games: pursuit race, tug of war, and team sports. The gameplay is much alike that of party-games: the rules are easy to follow but, in order for you or your team to make strategic progress on the board, the players need to perform some active challenges (physical challenges with different levels of difficulty, by the way). These were carefully designed to create fun moments of challenge either against a goal, the timer or the opponent team.

There are more than 60 challenges divided into 4 categories: strength, dexterity, speed and mime (this one also provides a funny moment as it is about sports). So... roll the die, pick a card and let's get started! We are... SPORTACULAR!

description:
For the first time, an Advanced Squad Leader module steps outside of the traditional World War II era to bring you Forgotten War, covering the Korean War of 1950-1953.

New rules in Forgotten War cover Steep Hills (reflecting the difficulties of movement and combat in the rugged Korean terrain); Bayonet Charges; Proximity Fuses; and enhanced Air Support featuring Tactical Air Control Parties, airborne Forward Air Controllers, and the jet-propelled aircraft of the era such as the F-80C Shooting Star and the F9F-2 Panther.

Forgotten War includes several new nationalities, each with its own distinct characteristics, and expands some existing nationalities. Forgotten War nationalities include the following:


    United Nations (U.N.) Forces:
        R.O.K. (Republic of Korea);
        O.U.N.C. (Other United Nations Commands), representing forces from such countries as Colombia, Ethiopia, France, Thailand, and Turkey, all of whom contributed to the U.N. effort;
        U.S. Army and U.S. Marine Corps;
        British and Commonwealth (Australian, Canadian, and New Zealand) forces
    



    Communist Forces:
        K.P.A. (Korean People's Army);
        C.P.V.A. (Chinese People's Volunteer Army)
    


Forgotten War components include:

        seven countersheets
        four 8" x 22" maps (80-83)
        sixteen ASL scenarios
        Chapter W rules for the Korean War
        Chapter H rules for American, Commonwealth, O.U.N.C., R.O.K., K.P.A., and C.P.V.A.
        two chapter dividers
        one box and lid


Forgotten War is not a complete game. Ownership of the Advanced Squad Leader Game System is required for play.

(from MMP's website)

boardgame: World At War: The Texan War of Independence
description:
In late July of 1985, two months before the dramatic events depicted in America Conquered, a brief but bloody and consequential war was fought: The Texan War of Independence. It marked the first time in 120 years, since the Civil War, that Americans took up arms against each other.

The circumstances were desperate. The United States had been struck by nuclear weapons. Many cities—Washington, D.C., New York, Los Angeles and others—had been annihilated, but none in Texas. Texas stood strong. Initially, Governor White of Texas sent convoys of relief supplies to the imperiled areas of the country, but soon learned that the convoys had been intercepted by a skilled and tactful, but unknown, enemy. President Saunders, upset that Texas would withhold aid to the rest of the country, sent military forces to Texas to intimidate its single-minded Governor in to resuming the flow of aid. Governor White responded by sending the Texas National Guard to the border, to meet and stop them.

As these unnatural enemies fought against one another, like brother against brother, a new threat loomed. Both Texas and the United States itself needed to confront the post-nuclear world. They could do it independently or together.

The Texan War of Independence contains four gripping scenarios. Forty new counters bring four formations of Texans to the front lines. The fearless Texans ride toward all enemies in Abrams and M60-A3 tanks, and their well-armed infantry are highly mobile, with plenty of M-113s and Dragon support weapons at hand. Texas had once been its own republic. In the Texan War of Independence you’ll find out if it happens again.

The Texan War of Independence is a World at War series expansion module; to play it, you will need to own America Conquered.
family: Series: World at War (Lock 'n Load Publishing)

description: Spin the spinner and fill the floating space station with cosmic cargo - but watch out! The Stellosphere is barely balanced and a lopsided load will send it crashing to Earth in a nanosecond and you must collect anything that has fallen off! Use your dexterity skills and superstellar strategy to win by stacking all your pieces on the space station before anyone else!

Radar Command is a 90-120 minute strategy board game. Start by building your own fleet, choosing from ships, islands, a radar base, air fields and etc... Once your fleet has been picked place your pieces on your map.

Once your pieces are in play it's time to search and destroy your opponent. May the best admiral win!

By using special interrupter cards you can add reinforcements, repair damage or retreat from a particularly intense battle.

With Radar Command's unique playing boards you can write directly on the playing board and easily erase for the next game. Have fun each game by choosing different ships and bases and by varying your game board set-up.

Publisher's description: In this exciting dice game, you go on a thrilling journey through Arendelle and make your way to Elsa's Ice Palace with all your favorite Frozen characters! With a little strategy and luck of the dice you will be the first player to reach the Ice Palace and win the game!

The rules are short and easy to learn.  The idea is simply that each time a player has the right to take a reward, they take a Lying Politician Card along with that reward (if they chose to take the reward). This addition to Police Precinct adds significant difficulty, variety, and replayability. The twists and turns of this new expansion are endless!  It consists of a deck of poker sized cards, and begins with "Set A" which has 28 playable cards.  More sets will be added in the future with the expectation that the total number of cards will eventually exceed 100.

User summary:
シガラミ (Shigarami), meaning "bond" or "shackle" in Japanese, is a boardgame about the business world.

The players, as well-established businesspersons having strong connections with various industries, move between 5 areas, including the government office district, business district, and bay area, and invest in enterprises to gain "performance points".

The game's most distinctive feature is the system whereby "the players have various special abilities at the start of the game, but they must discard them in order to gain points."

Among the 10 types of special abilities available for the players, only 7 of them are used during the game. The game development is also influenced by the abilities applied in the game.

This game is recommended for relatively heavy-weight gamers.

Publisher's summary:
「シガラミ」は、ビジネスの世界をテーマにした本格ボードゲームです。
プレイヤーは、社会的に成功を修め各業界と太いパイプを持つビジネスマンとなり、官庁街・商業地区・湾岸地区など５つに分かれたエリアを行き来しながら、事業に投資して「業績ポイント」を稼ぎます。

最大の特徴は、『プレイヤーはゲーム開始時に様々な特殊能力を持っているが、得点を稼ぐためには、特殊能力を捨てなければならない』というシステムです。

プレイヤーが持つ特殊能力は、10種類用意されていますが、ゲーム中には７種類しか使いません。どの能力をゲームに採用するかで、ゲーム展開が変わってくるのも魅力の一つ。

本格的なボードゲーム好きな方にお勧めの一品です。

boardgame: Trivial Pursuit: Family Edition Featuring the Magic of Disney
description: Trivial Pursuit Featuring the Magic of Disney Family Edition Master Game from Parker Brothers and Horn Abbott.  Game includes two sets of questions (one for kids and one for adults). This is a complete boxed set including the cards and a Disney themed board.

An expansion for Leaving Earth, adding the giant outer planets of the Solar System (Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune).

New missions lead you to survey the outer planets and their moons.  Depending what you find there, more missions may become available, leading to further journeys to the outer planets.

Roll and move game set in the Star Trek TOS milieu

From the rulebook:

Travel the game board of uncharted systems and planets to compete in Starfleet Academy's 'Enterprise Challenge." Carry out board actions, gain phaser tokens, and acquire access codes to enter deep space in order to collect federation tokens. Be the first cadet to complete your score card by returning all 4 unique federation tokens to your dormitory and win the prestigious 'Enterprise Challenge.'
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
These adorable animals will steal your heart in this crazy card drafting and dice stealing game. You will have to think on your feet to make the most of the animal cards you are given.

Overview
Each player chooses an animal to play.  The cards are dealt and dice are rolled.  Then players draft cards each turn and place their dice to try to get the highest score.  Each card has two possible effects depending on which player drafts the card.  To win the game, players will have to choose the best combos of dice and cards.  After 5 rounds the hand is done and players add up their scores.  See the tutorial link for more information.

From the publisher:

Hexlinx is an intriguing game of geometric strategy in which players start each turn with 6 tiles, then score points by trading, switching, and connecting  them to build a honeycomb grid of interlocked rings.  Each ring must include 6 different color tiles.  When all the tiles have been drawn and no player can form any more rings, the game is over.  Highest score wins. 
 Uniquely simple yet challenging, this exciting game of logic requires strategic planning (and a little luck) to win.  For a variety of gameplay, some optional Rules can be used -- and because there are 2 methods of scoring points (standard and advanced), Hexlinx is FUN for children and adults of all skill levels !
Players race to spell words combining the consonant cards in their hand with the vowel dice in the Dice Slide. The first to state their word wins the hand.

Busted is a push-your-luck dice game played with eight dice. After each throw, one or more dice must be "locked" and the player can then choose whether to roll again or to bank any accumulated points. However, if a player eventually spells out “B-U-S-T-E-D“ on the dice, they lose any accumulated points for that hand. Players roll the dice for as long as they can to drive up their score toward the goal of 1000 points.

boardgame: Poland (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
This map is an unofficial fan made expansion. Symbols & Graphics Copyright © 2004-2015 Days of Wonder, Inc. Produced with permission of Days of Wonder, for non-commercial use only.

This follows the principles of the basic Ticket to Ride game on a special board with special cards and rules. The object of the game is to gain the most points by travelling various routes and outwitting other players. You will need the train pieces and the train cards from either the US or Europe base games (or you can buy them from the Days of Wonder shop. You can use stations if you wish.

There is an extra set of Chance cards: some are for the cities, each with a value to be earned by visiting that city:others give you extra facilities to complete your tickets. These cards are dealt/picked up alongwith tickets in the same quantity and with the same 'keep/dispose' rules. Some famous names intervene to add interest.There are also up to 9 bonuses for the end-of-game scoring.

The board does not have any grey tracks or parallel tracks, though the famous names can help with these.

There are many new options in the rules: exchange all your cards at the start but keep all the new ones: discard 5 cards and pick up 5 new ones, as your turn: earn double or triple points: and many more. You can also call time when your wagon stock reaches 3, if you want, giving you the advantage of stopping other players completing tickets.

Compete with your family or friends to see who has the biggest musical culture of yesterday and today.

Nostalgia and laughter guaranteed ! Young and old have their chance with nearly 1600 questions. Answer the questions of the cards and try to recognize visual album covers, complete an unforgettable words or recognize who owns accessories from famous ...

And without realizing it, you will sing on tubes that have marked you.

2-6 players or teams of players.

Players are sentient dice that are evolving and battling at the same time.

Dice Mutants: Evolution Battle is a dice drafting game.  At the beginning of a round, all the dice in the game are rolled and then players take turns claiming one of the available dice.  The dice players draft are then assigned to improve their attack, defense, guile, mutation, and speed.

Every round, each Dice Mutant attacks every other Dice Mutant. Once a player has lost all their life points, they are out of the game.  Last player left alive is the winner.

Game Background:

You are the leader of a village in the forest of Na~TACE. Your expansion views require resources and pushes you to explore the surrounding areas.

Your lumberer will quickly discover areas for the development of agriculture for your farmers or mining for your blacksmith.

The wood harvested and will allow you to build farms, forges, fences or barracks for your soldiers.

But will your farmers and your hunter be able to provide enough food for your soldiers? No problem, send your merchant to other villages to share valuable resources.

Many other surprises await you in this game of resources gathering, war and colonization.

Finally, attack enemy villages to colonize the entire forest and win the game!

How to play:

The goal of the game is to eliminated other players by destroying their village with your soldiers.

24, 36 or 48 hexa cards (forests) are placed in a predetermined shape according to the number of players.
Each player choose a village and pick 3 ressources card.

Player 1 will advance his lumberer pawn forest by forest and discover 3 areas by flipping the forests he passes on. Underneath, he may discover a regular forest, a farm land, a mind land or a special land.

The lumberer will pick 3 wood cards and will use them to build farms (placing a pawn on the farm land), mines (placing a pawn on the mine land) or barracks (placing a pawn on the village).

For each farmer, the player will pick 1 farm card per farm land with a farmer. The food will be use to feed the soldiers (1 per barrack). Soldier pawns will be moved and advance to eventually attack the other villages or soldiers.

When a soldier or a group of soldiers attack, a D6 is used + all bonus cards the attacker player may have collect. Defense is fixed by the number of soldiers or what type of building is attacked + any defensive bonus the defendant player may have gathered.

From the box cover: "Collect the eggs - but watch out for the crazy dino!"

From the publisher's web site:
"A fantastic educational game from the multi-award winning Chimp and Zee range of games and puzzles. Each player races to collect Dinosaur eggs from the Dino's den on the rock, whilst trying to avoid the tail of the revolving dinosaur; which can knock all the eggs away. The player who has collected the most eggs wins the game. If there is a tie - the player with the most brown eggs wins.

Dodge the Dino is great fun for children and easy for parents to explain. The game can help your child learn through play. Easy to set up you can start playing in just a few minutes and everyone has a chance to win. Only takes ten minutes to play and is even great fun when it's not your turn. Dodge the Dino was created to help children with colours, shapes, matching, and co-ordination along with developing the ability to play together. The game is presented in a quality cardboard carton complete with carry-handle and glitter printing."

Excellent fun and educational game of chance for young kids, equally fun for adults. A witch has turned the players into frogs and they must race through her lair to find a cure (cure is a cauldron spinner which will either leave you as a frog or transform you back to human form).

The movement mechanic is where this game shines. 54 cardboard tiles (6 ghosts, 6 spiders, 42 footsteps with 1, 2 or 3 steps each) are used as a movement pool. To move players can turn over as many cards as they wish but try to avoid the ghosts and spiders. Players movement is equal to the number of footsteps they turn over. If they flip a ghost they must move backwards. Spiders stop you in your tracks.

The realm is strife with conflict as armies of Humans and Dwarves battle against the swarms of Goblins and Undead.  The Barbarian Horde, a neutral 5th army may also enter the fray to fight for either side.

Player's will build large armies, multiple cities, and infrastructure which will help boost their economy and fuel their war machine.  Also, each army has four heroes that can be used to explore dungeons, fight monsters, gain valuable treasure and ultimately advance levels in an attempt to lead their armies to victory. Each faction has unique units, spells, heroes, and upgrades at their disposal. It is only through the careful balance of limited resources that can win the day. There are many paths to victory.  The struggle is real. The carnage is devastating. The conquest has begun!

Conquest and Carnage are the next in the line of the Warparty series.  Lock'n Load Publishing will not be publishing this game.  You can find the first game Warparty series at the link below.

Players take turns playing the editor who reveals a headline and the other players select a comment card that they think is funny in the context. The editor selects the one he thinks is the funniest and that player gets a point.

boardgame: Under Both Flags: The American Navy in the Civil War
description: Rules for American Civil War naval battles in miniature.  Scaled for 1/600 miniatures, but easily adaptable to other scales.  Tactical level, 1 turn = approximately 3 minutes, with one miniature = one vessel.  Includes historical and "what if" scenarios, as well as charts for most vessels in action during the ACW.

Promotional Expansion for Xenoshyft: Onslaught.

Cards:
"Hades' Gaze" Plasma Launcher × 4

Eleventh Hour Setup × 2
