description:
From the publisher´s website:
Professor Bugsy loves bugs. To be able to observe them he wants to catch as many of them as possible. But what bug would want to get into his matchbox? Life is much better outside the box! The bugs are on holiday right now. They’re relaxing and swimming in their summer houses. When the fun ends, sadly they have to go home. The road is dangerous, because the professor is lurking on the path leading to the city… How many bugs will get home to Bugsville?

Players roll the dice. First die shows the bugs who left the town or being catched by professor or escaped from the matchbox. But if magnifying glass is rolled the matchbox moves as many steps further than the other die shows to catch all the bugs.

Players lost the game if 4 or more bugs are catched and there are no bugs left at the summer house. Otherwise players win.

From the publisher´s website:
The first entry into the history of car racing, the victory of horsepower without horses. Go ahead gentlemen, the world awaits you! The racers speed along trackless roads and deserted landscapes to win the most exalted of prizes, and the glory for being the most daring of drivers. The first and greatest race in the annals of history can only be won by someone who, defying all odds, manages to drive his automobile through the finish line. We can admit though, that beside their skills the racers have to posses a small amount of luck as well.

Players play simultanously the cards with numbers 1-6. Now player reveal the cards and starting to move their cars in increasing numbers on their cards. If Your car ends the turn on the place where is another card or obstacle you must put Your car back  to the space from which they started the movement.

Player who finishes first is the winner.

Sabotile: Isles of Hexoté is a competitive 2-6 player capture-the-flag game with tile management based progression.

As a chieftain of one of the six tribes on the island of Hexoté, you will start in your people's village outskirts and attempt to bring back your sacred totem, stolen by the god of chaos Mesba, from the center temple. However, it has been mixed with the other tribes' and you must deduce yours by carrying totems to villages and using the ever-changing land to bolster yourself and impede the rest. Influence the board by placing or taking tiles with various powers to affect the game and take back what is rightfully yours!

Moving across tiles, any time you or another tribe chieftain ends movement on one, it activates, the resulting effect taking place. This simple mechanism opens up all sorts of opportunity for strategy and sabotage.

Marienkäfer, flieg: The players start on the orange spaces on the outher ring. The first player to reach the center space wins the game. Movement is determined by a die. Direction of movement from each space follows the direction of the antlers of the pictured lady bug. If the antlers point to another ring, the player changes rings and moves in the direction of the first space on that ring.

The game board has another game on the backside: Kinderglücksspiel. To win this game the players have to move from one of the outher starting spaces to the center space. Movement is determined by two dice of which the player may chose one to make his move. The players move their meeples one space in the direction that matches their chosen die. 
If a meeple gets on a space that is already occupied by another meeple, the later one must start anew.

The game was published in the 1980s.

Cacao: Chocolatl features four modules for the Cacao base game, and these modules can be used individually or in any combination with the other modules.

1. The chocolate module includes three chocolate maker tiles, three chocolate markets and twenty wooden chocolate markers. Chocolate is made out of cacao at the chocolate maker tiles and can be sold at a high price but only on special markets.

2. The huts module includes twelve double-sided huts you can acquire during the game. They give their owner special powers during the game or extra points when fulfilling specific conditions.

3. The irrigation module includes three special plantations where you can harvest a lot of cacao fruits but must pay with water.

4. The maps module includes a preview board for two more tiles that are visible near the draw pile. Each player receives two map tiles at the beginning of the game, and for each tile spent they can also choose from the preview board when filling up spaces on the board.

Beep Boop is a kid-friendly, strategic, competitive card game about battling robots. Why? Because who doesn't love robots?

In the game, players play cards to build and upgrade their robot while sabotaging other players. When a robot is built, players can begin attacking each other. The last robot standing wins!

When a clown cries we all cry. In this game of Crying With a Sad Clown you must try to cry the most by playing cards from your hand and gathering the tear tokens.

Game Contents:
60 Cards of Four Suits (Man, Woman, Child, Clown)
24 Tear Tokens

Game Setup:
Shuffle and deal all the cards to all the players. Every player should receive the same amount of cards.

Game Play:
The starting player chooses 1-3 cards from their hand and plays at least one face up in front of their play area. All other players in order play 1-3 cards either face up or face down in front of their play area. After all players have played a card,flip over any face down cards.

Score each “trick” as follows: The man (blue) and woman (yellow) act as a group. The baby (purple) and clown (red) are a group. The group with the least amount of cards wins the “trick” and takes a tear token of the same color as the card they played in player order. If the groups are equal in size, no player wins a token. After each trick, the starting player is the next player in order. The game ends when 3 of the 4 color tear tokens are depleted or when all of one player's cards have been played. The winner is the player with the most tokens within the widest variety of colors.

In each opening round of the trick-taking card game Union, players take turns using their voting tokens that are randomly drawn from a bag for starting player, trump, super trump, number of cards discarded, and the value of tricks taken.  Once the starting player is determined, that player chooses a partner, then the teams play out the round based on the rules they collectively chose.

In this unique three suited trick taking game, players also have the option of playing -5 point cards out of sequence that will outcome the trick.
Billionaire Banshee: Australia Booster consists of ten new Perk and ten new Quirk cards for Billionaire Banshee that feature Australian-based traits.

It is a period of war. The intergalactic empires of Enate and Kurth have grown desperate as scare resources have forced them to aggressively expand across the universe. They use planetary nodes as instruments of war, consuming entire star systems in the blink of an eye. Now the two empires compete for the final goal: Complete domination of the universe.

In Star Duel, you control strategic galactic nodes to gain Energy, Minerals, Population, and Influence, then use those resources to crush your opponent.

GAME OVERVIEW
In Star Duel, players take turns activating, exhausting, and moving their nodes while also attacking the nodes of their opponent. The player who best manages his network of nodes will emerge victorious.

TURN OVERVIEW
At the beginning of each turn, the players choose two of their Node Programming cards, orient them to match their coordinates for a node, and reveal the cards to the other player. The player that programmed the lower number goes first. After the first player completes his actions for the turn, the second player performs his actions and then a new turn begins.

On a player's turn, the player may perform each of the following actions one time and in any order:

Execute Program [target a node, replace a node, or produce energy]
Exhaust node;
Move node.

WINNING THE GAME
A player wins immediately if his opponent is ever reduced to 0 influence or 0 population. Additionally, a player wins if he controls all available nodes at the end of a round.

Build your fortune as you buy, sell and trade properties from the Kensington neighborhood in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. Don't worry  it's classic Monopoly fun! Take a ride on Huntingdon El Stop, buy a neighborhood store, or buy up All of the stores — it’s your turn to get rich! You’ll take a spin around the board. Roll your dice and move your favorite game piece. Prove your skills as a deal maker. Pass YOUSE, collect $250.00, take a Destiny card or head over to Fate space as you wheel and deal your way to the top. But remember to stay out of The Sweat Box! Bring back those old neighborhood memories.
Build your fortune as you buy, sell and trade the properties. It’s like classic Monopoly fun!

Slash is a card-based party game in which players compete to create the best romantic fan fiction pairing. Everybody gets a hand of their favorite characters from pop culture, history, and literature. The matchmaker selects one from her hand and places it face up on the table. For a bit more storytelling, the matchmaker can request that each player narrate some portion of the relationship they are attempting to create. Maybe it's the first date, or their wedding day, or the moment when they realized they belonged together.

Each player in turn selects a possible 'ship for that character using a card from their hand, narrating as necessary and hoping that the matchmaker chooses theirs — but if she doesn't, you can always narrate the tale of this destined connection to win the favor of the table and overrule the matchmaker. Play continues until one player has accumulated 20 points worth of characters.

Slash: PAXspansion 2015, which works as both a standalone game and an expansion for Slash: Romance without boundaries, has forty new characters and five blank cards, including Terrible Teenage Poets, Gary Gygax, and a $20,000 gaming table.

Play continues until one player has accumulated 20 points worth of characters.

Slash: Nerdlesque GGC 2015, which works as both a standalone game and an expansion for Slash: Romance without boundaries, has forty new characters and five blank cards, including  Xenomorphs, Dr. Watson, and the classic android Maria.

Play continues until one player has accumulated 20 points worth of characters.

Everybody gets a hand of their favorite characters from pop culture, history, and literature. The matchmaker selects one from her hand and places it face up on the table. For a bit more storytelling, the matchmaker can request that each player narrate some portion of the relationship they are attempting to create. Play continues until one player has accumulated 20 points worth of characters.

Slash: Shipwreck October 2014, which works as both a standalone game and an expansion for Slash: Romance without boundaries, has forty literary characters and five blank cards, including Faceless Old Woman, Giant Space Octopi, and Clint Eastwood. Can you find them a love connection?

boardgame: Slash: There's a Fan Fic for That...
description:
Slash: There's a Fan Fic for That... is an alternate set of rules that provides a new way to use the character cards in Slash: Romance without boundaries or one of the Slash spinoff sets.

Each player selects a character card, then takes a role in a scenario set in a location such as a coffee shop, the old west, or high school. The player must act out scenes from a typical fan fiction while being both the character (e.g., Orphan Black, Commander Shepherd, Veronica Mars) and the role (sheriff, jock, cheerleader).

King's Forge: Glassworks adds glass as a new material with which to work in King's Forge. The expansion includes new "glass" material dice, new items that require glass to craft, and new places to gather glass for your workshop.

boardgame: Durch Nacht und Eis zum Nordpol
description:
"Durch Nacht und Eis zum Nordpol. Im Zeppelin mit Dr. Eckener".
One of several roll-and-move games that came out between 1890 and 1910 as the public was enthralled with expeditions to the arctic.

The board features illustrations of the trip north, by ship, dogsled, and skis. The dice rolling journey covers the journey north and the return home.

Copied from Rules of Play insert:

The Treasure Dive Game was invented by Ronald C. Ketchum, an internationally certified Scuba Instructor.  He created the game as a fun means of review of scuba safety skills for his diving students in Australia.  The game has since been revised to include a basic level for children and an advanced level for scuba divers, so that both may play the game at their own level.
INTRODUCTION... Imagine you and your friends each discovering eight pieces of 8 coins on a beach.  You know a treasure ship must lay offshore, with a treasure chest containing fabulous wealth.  The race is on to be the first one to reach the treasure and claim exclusive salvage rights. 
OBJECT...The object of the game is to become the first diver to reach the sunken treasure and return to the surface, thereby staking claim as the Legal Salvor.  Descent to the treasure is made by earning various levels of scuba certification.  Hazards about, and a knowledge of first aid and diving safety will help you reach the treasure safely.

After a boat tour, you crash on an uncharted island where you find five castaways willing to help you. The problem is that others on the island — other players, that is — are also using YOUR people, and they are not being honest about where they are or what they are doing. You might want to get them exiled to insure you fare the best.

In Marooned 5, use the five castaways to help build your raft and get off the island; lounge at the resort sipping drinks with a famous actress; gather and monetize water; or exile all the other players. The player with the most victory points or the last player standing wins.

Players are dealt one worker and one location and use these two cards to create worker placement. You can be honest and safe, or get ahead by claiming the best roles, locations and combos.

Darkness surrounds the shores of the world and seeps from caves deep beneath the Earth. The maddening howl of eldritch beasts echoes on the night air. Dangerous artifacts from strange eons call to you at all hours of the day. Your quest is nearly complete. So what if bands of enterprising “heroes” have started snooping around. The Old Ones have heard your prayers. Now all you need to do is finish your plans and set them free before these Investigators find you!

Rise of Cthulhu: Dark Secrets is the first expansion to last year’s successful Rise of Cthulhu card game. ROC: DS adds all new Monsters, Artifacts, and Old Ones for players to use in their shadowy path to victory. In addition, the expansion introduces a brand new mechanic: the Investigator. These “would-be heroes” set out to hunt down rumors of your corrupting influence and seek to stop you from awakening the Old Ones. When they appear, they force both you and your opponent to deal with the threat they bring, lest they halt the coming of the age of madness you seek to unleash.

When an Investigator card is drawn, it is placed on the corresponding Location printed in the upper right hand corner of the card. When an Investigator lands on a Location without an Old One, it banishes all the Cultists assembled there, effectively returning all Cultist cards in play to their owner’s hands. Any Monsters or Artifacts that are in play on that Location are removed from the game. The Location then resets to neutral (turns sideways so that it’s not facing either player).

Each Investigator has a number in a red circle in the upper left hand corner of their frame. This represents their resources they bring to bear against both cultist players. Players must play Cultist cards to equal or exceed the resource value listed to remove the Investigator from the Location. Until the Investigator is removed from the Location, players may not play any Cultist cards on any other Location except that one; furthermore, cards played on a Location with an Investigator present on it do not cause the Location to shift to face the player with the highest total value of cards. Once the resource rating is met or exceeded, the Investigator is defeated and removed, at which time the Location would face the player with the highest total Cultists in play there.

Lastly, this expansion adds a new player aide to the card, something the community asked for following the release of the core game.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Setting The East Ablaze!: Warfare in the Back of Beyond 1917-1926
description:
Setting The East Ablaze! contains miniature wargame rules for warfare in Central Europe, Russia, Central Asia, China and elsewhere from the years 1917-1926.

These rules are designed for medium-sized games (c. 60-70 figures per side), but they do work just as well for smaller skirmish-level games, and for larger games of up to 500 figures per side.

Rules use standard six-sided dice. Figures are grouped into units, ranging from 8 (cavalry units) to 20 (irregular mobs) strong. An action card system is then used to play the game, with considerable potential to customize the card deck depending on the game you wish to play.

Appendices provide further information on basic points value of units, tanks and armored cars, optional rules, fire and bomb templates, and army morale.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Ophidian 2360: Survival of the Fittest – The Art of War vs. Otherworld Allies
description:
Ophidian 2360: Survival of the Fittest is a non-collectible expansion to the Ophidian 2350 CCG arena combat game as well as a standalone game that pits futuristic gladiators against each other using unique and thematic mechanisms.

In the game, each player brings a team of up to four gladiators to the table, along with a customized deck that's driven by the selected gladiators. Gameplay revolves around a momentum-based system that allows the player with the momentum to continue to take actions until they lose momentum, at which point their opponent picks up and continues.

Players use resources to supply their gladiators with equipment and minions (support characters). The crowd gets involved through cheer and also provides an alternate victory condition. Win the crowd, and you win the game!

Mix and match cards from any Ophidian 2360: Survival of the Fittest decks:

     Ophidian 2360: Survival of the Fittest – Mental Block vs. Warrior's Resolve
     Ophidian 2360: Survival of the Fittest – War Machine vs. Bio-Hazard
     Ophidian 2360: Survival of the Fittest – The Art of War vs. Otherworld Allies


Playing with four players will require a total of four playable decks.

Many character options, 4 different win conditions, and a large number of in-game choices/options makes this a truly unique and engaging game!

The Nerf Rebelle Disc Target Game was released as a toy included in McDonald's Happy Meals.  It is part of the Nerf Rebelle line, designed to appeal primarily to girls.

This game has a two-sided board and can be played in two ways.  One side of the board is a combination of a dexterity game and tic-tac-toe.  The board is laid out on the floor or a table and players take turns throwing plastic discs, trying to land them in one of the spaces on the 3x3 grid.  Players may of course knock other players' discs off the board.  Like tic-tac-toe, the first player to get three discs in a row wins.

The other side of the board is a target game.  Players take turns throwing their discs at the board, trying to land them in scoring circles.  Smaller circles have higher point values.  After each player has thrown all his or her discs, the highest total score wins.

Because the rules are minimal (and conveyed only with pictures), player will have to create house rules to answer lots of questions, such as whether players may pick up and rethrow discs if no one get three in a row in the tic-tac-toe version and how much of a disc must be inside a scoring circle to score points in the target game.

Description from the publisher:

The last war of the Enlightenment broke out in July 1778, following the death of the Elector of Bavaria. Austrian Emperor Joseph II sought Bavaria for himself; Prussian King Frederick II wished to block this acquisition. Their conflict became known as the War of the Bavarian Succession or, more colorfully, as the Potato War. Armies of both sides marched about Bohemia, scouring the landscape for food (potatoes in particular) while the diplomats hashed out a solution.

Soldier Kings: The Potato War brings this worldwide interlocking conflict to Soldier Kings, as Britain struggles with a growing insurgency in her American colonies, France fans those flames and supports Austria, Prussia seeks to block the annexation of Bavaria and Russia supports Prussia. Happy Austria has once again married her way to more territory, but still has to take possession.

The Potato War is a 64-page book supplement for Soldier Kings, a multiplayer game of the Seven Years' War. In addition to the new Potato War scenario and its pieces, there are new pieces (with optional rules for their use) for the Seven Years' War scenarios of Soldier Kings, plus some additional options.

Description from the publisher:

Napoleon's 1803 coronation as Emperor of the French touched off a worldwide series of wars, that lasted until his second and final exile in 1815. Soldier Emperor lets you play out the campaigns in Europe, while Indian Empires covers those in, you guessed it, India.

Soldier Emperor: Dreams of Empire is a 64-page book that brings those two games together, with a linking map covering the Persian Empire and the Central Asian khanates of Khiva, Kokand and Bokhara, plus some pieces of Afghanistan and other regions and new pieces to represent the armies and leaders of these distant lands.

The book also includes additional scenarios for each game. For Soldier Emperor, it's the war that would have broken out in 1815 had Napoleon not returned from Elba to re-unite his quarreling enemies. For Indian Empires, there's the 1760 Afghan-Maratha War, which resulted in the massive Battle of Panipat, and the Seven Years' War in India featuring the conquests of Robert Clive.

And there are more new variants for both Soldier Emperor and Indian Empires, like National Aspirations to give each power their own special victory conditions and special abilities for each major power. These make every country different to play, and not just because of their geographical position. There are new Imperial Power rules, and a whole assortment of additional special rules just because they make the games even more fun to play.

In Hero Flix, you take on the role of a movie director. Your task is to complete a production and earn Production Points toward a winning score of 10 points. More complex productions have a higher Production Point value but also take longer to complete.

While working towards Completing a Production, there are opportunities to increase the value of your production or impact the production of other players. Some examples include:


    Joining in their success (joining them as a Co-Producer),
    Lowering the Production Point value (through a Smear Campaign), or
    Preventing them from Completing the Production this turn (via an Injunction).


Hero Flix enables you to change the level of interaction by removing some cards from the game. We break these styles into three “Budget Levels”:


     "B Movie" - there is a low chance of influencing another player,
     "Major Motion Picture" - you have some chance for influencing another player, and
     "Summer Blockbuster" - another player production will get influenced unless you are doing it wrong. This is how the game was designed to be played.

boardgame: Broken Legions: Fantasy Skirmish Wargames in the Roman Empire
description:
The Roman Empire ruled the civilized world with an iron fist, seemingly all-powerful and limitless — yet the power of Rome is secured not by its mighty legions, but by small bands of warriors and agents fighting a secret war. Tasked by the Emperor to explore ancient temples, forgotten labyrinths, and beast-haunted caverns, they seek out artifacts hidden by the gods themselves, hunt creatures of myth, and face enemies that would use dark magic against the empire.

Broken Legions: Fantasy Skirmish Wargames in the Roman Empire is a set of fantasy skirmish rules for a war unknown to history, fought in the shadows of the Roman Empire. Various factions recruit small warbands to fight in tight, scenario-driven battles that could secure the mystical power to defend — or crush — Rome. A points system allows factions to easily build a warband, and mercenaries and free agents may also be hired to bolster a force. Heroes and leaders may possess a range of skills, traits, and magical abilities, but a henchman's blade can be just as sharp, and a campaign can see even the lowliest henchman become a hero of renown.

Mark Latham's blog, which provides additional rules, discussion, and FAQS.

https://thelostvictorian.blogspot.com/

boardgame: Poseidon's Warriors: Classical Naval Warfare 480-31 BC
description:
Poseidon's Warriors: Classical Naval Warfare 480-31 BC is a set of war gaming rules for large-scale naval actions between fleets of classical galleys from the Greek and Persian clash at the battle of Salamis to the battle of Actium that decided the fate of Rome. With so many of these battles taking place around islands or in narrow channels and shallow waters, sneaky tactics and cunning maneuvers are a hallmark of warfare of this era, and the rules use an integrated turn system to allow a commander to position ships to go in and ram without being rammed in return, or to employ feints and traps to tempt the enemy out of position and leave his ships vulnerable to a follow-up strike.

With data for ships throughout the period, rules for famous admirals, historical scenarios, a campaign system and a brief historical summary for those who wish to refresh their memory of the era, Poseidon's Warriors offers everything players need to bring to the tabletop the battles and campaigns of the first great age of naval warfare.

The years’ 1965 and you’ve just gotten a job in one of those brand new “Data Centers”. Your job is to feed endless piles of punch cards into different computers and tally the results. Problem is, you kinda all overstated your experience with these machines and you don’t really know what you’re doing.
You and your colleagues will feed the computers with punch cards and hope that everything goes well and that no computers jam and overheat!
Who knows, maybe you’ll get to be the supervisor!

Gameplay:

Players get number cards.
With these cards, they need to combine these to form equations (addition, subtractions, divisions) or to form whole numbers.

Then they get to enter these values in one of the computers, in various bases (base 2, base 3, prime numbers, tec...)

For each values that match. the player puts one of his cubes in the column, to gain majority and points.

The player with the most points wins and becomes the supervisor.

User summary:
エイジオブサモナー ～大召喚時代～ (Age of Summoner Dai Shoukan Jidai) Age of Summoners is a card game of elemental conjuration. As Summoners, players gain victory points by summoning their elementals.

In this game, each card has three usages: an elemental to be summoned, power source to summon an elemental, and an action number to play a specific action.

Your action is determined by the total action number of two cards, the one from yours and the other from one of your opponents, so the opponent can limit your available actions to some extent. You should plan your moves considering your hand and the card the opponent sent to you.

Who will be the second coming of King Solomon by summoning many elementals?

The artwork for this game was taken from copyright free images.

Publisher's summary:
エイジオブサモナーは、「精霊の召喚」をテーマにしたカードゲームです。
プレイヤーは召喚師として様々なカード（＝精霊）を登場させ、得点を稼ぎます。
このゲームでは、召喚のためのコストも、行動を起こすための力も、全ては手札に集約されています。
１枚のカードに、アクション、魔力、精霊と３通りの顔が存在するのです。
さらに、アクションは自分ひとりで完結する訳ではありません。
他プレイヤーから送られたカードと自らのカード、２枚の組み合わせをよく考え、効率の良い行動を設計してください。
数多くの妖魔をあやつり、ソロモン王の再来となるのは一体誰でしょうか？"
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
publishers: Chicken Dice Games (チキンダイスゲームズ)

boardgame: Saints and Sinners: The Gallery of Polish Kings
description:
Saints and Sinners: The Gallery of Polish Kings is a two-player card game in which the players impersonate the powers of good and evil struggling for the souls of Polish monarchs. The aim of the game is to make three characters either "saint" or "damned" by means of playing sin and virtue cards, which are specific for each faction. The game includes sixteen double-sided monarch cards — twelve of them are always in play — that possess different special abilities depending on whether the card has the good or evil side up, and multiple copies of seven deadly sins and seven heavenly virtues cards.

The artwork is inspired by a series of paintings by Jan Matejko, a famous Polish painter, under the name "The Gallery of Polish Kings and Dukes" (created 1890–1892).

User summary:
エイジ・オブ・リバイブ (Age of Revive) is a construction game in which players try to reconstruct and revive a city devastated by a long war.

Initially, the city is full of rubble. You clear and reuse it into significant buildings. Constructed buildings form a ring-shaped world. Players move along the ring and collect resources for their next building.

The winner is the player who has collected most victory points by constructing glorious buildings and receiving King's favor.

The artwork for this game uses copyright free images.

Publisher's summary:
Age of Reviveは、復興と再生をテーマにした建築ゲームです。
舞台は長く続いた戦乱から解放された大都市です。
はじめはいたる所ガレキの山が重なるのみですが、これを撤去、再利用して立派な建築物に変えてゆきます。
建築された施設は円環状の世界を作り、プレイヤーはその上を転々と移動しながら、次の建築のための資源を集めるのです。
壮麗な建物と、王の支持をもっとも積み上げた人物が、勝者に選ばれます。
publishers: Chicken Dice Games (チキンダイスゲームズ)

description:
User summary:
ペンギン十字稜 (Pengin Jujiryou) means "Penguin Pyramidion" in English.

One day, a Penguin suddenly claimed that he is the "Emperor" of all the Penguins. The entire Penguin nation, which once lived peacefully, became strained and is currently in the midst of struggle. Each species has to flourish by increasing their population and producing offspring. Weak species will become extinct naturally, but even strong species may be made extinct by misfortune. Only one species may survive in the end!

Players roll total of 55 dice, and build a five-tier pyramid. You have to name a species which will be the summit of the pyramid. Which species will win the generations-long survival game?

The artwork for this game uses copyright free images.

Publisher's summary:
ある日突然、一匹のペンギンが『帝王』であると名乗りを上げました。
平和な群れに緊張が走ります。いままさにペンギン達は、競争の真っただ中です。兄弟を増やして繁栄し、後の世代に子孫を残さねばなりません。 弱い遺伝子は淘汰され、強い遺伝子も運が悪ければ絶えてしまいます。
最終的に生き残るのはたった一種類のみです！
プレイヤーは55個のダイスを積み上げてピラミッドを作ります。そのてっぺんに座ることになるペンギンを推理せねばなりません。
世代を超えて繰り広げられる、サバイバルゲームに勝利するのはどの種族でしょうか？
publishers: Chicken Dice Games (チキンダイスゲームズ)

boardgame: Tales from the Taverns: Legends of Goblins Past
description:
Step into a world of epic storytelling. Take on the role of the traveling bard, regaling the tales of diminutive heroes, long forgotten. Embellish the stories, challenge your rivals, share the lore and the liquor!

The stories of heroes are well established in the annals of bardic lore. A select band of bards have traveled the earth acquiring the legends of the most unbelievable legends of Goblins past. The stories of their conquests are unlike traditional legends, crafted from improbable accomplishments, incalculable risk, and a nearly absurd amount of luck. The epic tales of these forgotten heroes belie their diminutive stature. You have the responsibility of keeping their memory alive by telling their stories to weary travelers at taverns across the land. Creatures of darkest myth, artifacts of untold power, diabolical traps, and recovery from catastrophic misfortune fill your mind and flow from your tongue as you compete for the attention of the audience. Your story-weaving talents will be put to the test as other bards tell the tales of their own chosen heroes and heroines. Keep it lively ladies and gens’ fortune is a fickle mistress!

Each round a player may perform two actions from the following list:

May discard two Lore cards from their hand to draw either one Lore card from the discard pile or one Lore card from the top of the Lore deck, as a free action.

May place a card from their hand to the play mat. (this can be done twice, one for each action)

May draw two cards. (May be done twice, once for each action)

May beg for tips and receive two gold from the bank.

May pay one gold and get one bardic talent card.

At the end of 10 rounds,add the score of all the Lore cards on your Storyboard. Add 5 Lore for each Epic Embellishment card on your Storyboard. Add 1 Lore for every two Coins remaining in your possession (rounded down). Determine if you earned any of your Bardic Talent reward(s) by calculating and comparing necessary rows, columns, or other criteria. Add Bardic Talent reward(s) to your Lore score if earned. The player with the highest Lore score wins the game. Ties are resolved by awarding victory to the player with the most Lore earned from Bardic Talents. Secondary ties are resolved by remaining Coins. If a tie still remains, toast each other’s success and share victory.

Legends of Goblins Past conjures the confabulist in us all. Your flair for the dramatic will be put to the test, as will your constitution. Drink and be merry!
Spaceteam: NSFS Expansion is a 48-card, somewhat adult-oriented expansion for Spaceteam that includes cards such as "Ethanol Probe" and "Greased Apparatus".
Spaceteam: Triangulum Expansion is a family-friendly expansion for Spaceteam that adds 54 cards to the game, which thereby allows up to nine players to play at the same time.

Drecksau: Sauschön, an expansion for the Drecksau card game, lets players decide how they want to participate in the pig parade, whether with full makeup and styling fit for a beauty pageant or a more traditional wallow in the mud.

You work a thankless office job at a faceless corporate entity. Its stark, lifeless cubicles bleed grey into your very soul. Before you can even start your day, an old co-worker wanders into your workspace. He's been long dead but that doesn't stop him from being a distraction. You manage to blow his brains out with a modified staple gun and get back to work. Maybe you can make it to Friday. Maybe.

The object of The Working Dead, a negotiation-driven strategy game, is straightforward: Be the first to earn $250,000 by the end of the week by negotiating for fellow co-workers! Check in on Monday, manipulate others to do your work for you, grab a beer on Friday. Don't let the undead get you down. Use them to your advantage or die trying. Always follow corporate policy — or don't.

Catan: Big Box contains the classic Catan board game, along with the expansion that allows up to six players to compete on the base game, the Frenemies scenario, the Helpers of Catan mini-expansion that gives each player a character with a bonus ability, the Mallorca scenario (printed on paper), and the Deutschland scenario (printed on game board, with the city landmarks also being included).
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description: Players in Monster High: Monsterkrasses Such-Spiel want to collect the eerily beautiful images of those in school in order to complete their photo album — but which monster fits where? Only those quick enough to find the proper photo album page for the images will collect them, and whoever first collects six images wins!

In Hot Wheels: Track Attack, players build a custom track together, then grab a car, and start racing. Luck with the die won't be enough on its own to become champion of the race, though, as players also need to avoid dangers on the track. Whoever first crosses the finish line wins!

boardgame: Barbie: Wer packt zuerst seine Koffer?
description:
What a nice surprise! Barbie's girlfriends have invited her on a short holiday, and they're already on the way to her house to pick her up — but her dresses are all mixed up and the two suitcases that she needs for the holiday are still empty.

In Barbie: Wer packt zuerst seine Koffer?, you need to put together the perfect holiday outfit for Barbie before her girlfriends show up at the door. Can you do it?

boardgame: Rund um die Welt: Eine interessante Rekordfahrt
description: The player wo rolls highest begins and choses a continent to travel (green track). Every player has to chose another continent. Movement is determinated by a die. After completing their continental journey the players go to Berlin to start their race around the world (red track). The first player to return to Berlin wins the game. At some cities certain events hinder or benefit the players.

Toy of the Year Finalist for Rookie of the Year, as the only board game!

First player to place their Sphere on the Bullseye is the winner. How hard can that be? 
Well, you have 1, 2, or 3 opponents with their sets of pieces trying to stop you, and take you out of the game. 
Your Sphere has no defense and at the center of the board there's a Red Zone where everything changes….

Geometry Strategy is a strategic and tactic board game where even the element of luck is tactics. 
A brand new classic game, with a Red Zone.

Gameplay:

     Each player aims to be the first to place their Sphere on the Bullseye to win the game. 
     The Sphere must be protected from other players who will try to take it out and thereby put you out of the game. At the same time you must stop the other players from reaching the Bullseye with their Spheres.
     For defence as well as offense, each player has 9 pieces besides the Sphere to strategically and tactically play with as the game starts. All pieces have different rankings in challenges between them; though they all are able to capture the opponents’ Spheres.
     If you think this doesn't sound hard enough, add a Red Zone where any player can enter and change the conditions of the game. Completely. Now, either you get blind sided by these changes every time, or you prepare yourself and play your game tactically. 
     Before the game starts, all players place their pieces on their quarter of the board. The Sphere and the Helix, your trump piece, have to start at your base line but the other pieces can be placed on squares of your choice. A Red Zone card is flipped to get the conditions for the Challenges; attacks, by showing the present rankings for the pieces.
     Players take turns in a clockwise order. First player starts by moving one optional piece of their own color; one or two steps in any available direction. It is then the next player’s turn, and the game is on. Is your strategy to move your Sphere toward the Bullseye as soon as possible? Or to use your pieces to stop the other players to get there and then run for the goal in the end? 
     When two players’ pieces are within reach of each other it can lead to a Challenge, or attack, where a player can lose a piece. The piece with the highest ranking according to the present Red Zone condition wins the Challenge and takes the losing piece out of the game. 
     The players can choose to enter the Red Zone and change the conditions for the challenges. 
     As soon as a player enters the Red Zone, a new Red Zone card is flipped and the conditions of the challenges can be changed with new ranking orders for the pieces! It can be a good thing for one or more players, but it can also mean any advantage you’ve enjoyed is stripped away.
     If a player’s Sphere is challenged, game is over for him/her.
     Although, if you think you’re losing many pieces, as long as you have your Sphere you can still win the game.
     First player to place his/her Sphere at the Bullseye is the winner!

Eye Know is a visual trivia betting game. At the start of the game, a number of cards are laid out. On a turn, you choose one of the cards and identify the image on it. (This isn't the hard part, but rather a precursor of what's to come.) If you correctly identify the image, you can then choose to answer a trivia question relating to the object named on the card. Questions come in three varieties — true/false, multiple choice, and open ended — and the payouts are higher for the tougher questions.

Eye Know Mitbringspiel includes one hundred picture cards that can also be used as an expansion for the Eye Know board game.

boardgame: Vom kleinen Maulwurf, der wissen wollte, wer ihm auf den Kopf gemacht hat.
description: Vom kleinen Maulwurf, der wissen wollte, wer ihm auf den Kopf gemacht hat. is a memory game featuring the Little Mole, the butcher's dog Hans-Heinerich, and other animals from the children's classic book. Who can find the most pairs?

boardgame: Defenders of the Realm: Winds of War
description:
Winds of War is an optional variant for Defenders of the Realm that adds a tracking board and 16 cards to the game. Anytime a Special Hero card is played for its special power, or an "All Is Quiet Darkness Spreads" card appears, it is placed on the tracking board. As soon as the third card is placed, then a Winds of War card is drawn and must be followed. In most cases a negative effect occurs unless the team decides to prevent it by following the demands of the prevention on the card.

The contents of this expansion are included in the second edition of The Dragon Expansion, and the rules for this expansion are contained in the second edition Dragon Expansion rulebook.

In Psycho PAF: Channel Wars, you are the president of a TV station, you create your own programming schedule by buying TV shows and their presenters. While trying to gain the highest ratings, you run into scandals, gossip, intrigues and back-stabbing from other players.

The goal of the game is to have the best channel (victory conditions to meet -- see below).

Each turn, the players play 4 actions (among which you can play presenter and TV show cards, take the top card of a deck and discard it, move shows on your schedule, etc).
You can play the positive and negative gossip cards on your own game or on other player's games. The better your TV rating is each turn, the more actions you get to do on your next turn.

You win when, at the end of a turn, all your 8 time slots for the week are filled (including 1 "best evening") and a presenter is assigned to each show. In case of a tie, the highest TV rating designates the winner.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Emprise is a multi-player fast-paced, real-time card game where players race through dungeons looking for treasure and glory. While only one player can hold the top spot, they must rely on their teammates in order to succeed or all will fail.

The object of Emprise is to have the most experience points after a series of levels, culminating with a boss battle.

Each player has a deck of cards with their own crest on the back to play plus their character cards which represent individual powers they can use while playing.

At the start of each round, the players flip over a new level, read the appropriate rules (if any), and deal out the indicated number of monsters for the start of the round. The players then deal out there focus pile and stamina cards, leaving the remainder of their cards as their luck deck.

To clear your focus deck, players simultaneously play with four zones gaining experience points and treasure. These zones are the level (where the monsters are milling about, ready to fight along with the level cards), their focus, stamina, and luck decks.

When a players focus deck has been cleared, unless otherwise noted by the level, the round ends. Players count up the cards played into the field, subtract any remaining focus, and take their hidden treasure which is revealed at the end of the game.

The player with the most experience points at the end of the game, including their hidden treasure, gains bragging rights until the next time the adventures play.

It is time to hunt the Beast of Balka! The Sun Clan and Moon Clan are competing to be the first to slay the Beast and earn the favor of their god, and they must navigate a jungle full of monsters — and enemy hunting parties — to do so. Will you be able to use your superior tactics to claim your quarry, or will the other clan get the best of you?

Beast of Balka is a fast-paced abstract strategy game that challenges each player to outwit their opponent, taking into account positioning as well as probability to emerge victorious. However, destroying your enemy is not your goal: simply an obstacle to it.

Each player takes turns traversing the forest, honing in on their prey and trying to hinder one another. The more each hunting party encounters danger, the more bloodthirsty they become; but they must face ambushes, misdirection, and — worst of all — stampeding beasts!

boardgame: Survival: Frogs of South Eastern Australia
description:
Survival: Frogs of South Eastern Australia tells the struggle of five endangered Australian frog species: The Southern Corroboree Frog, Spotted Tree Frog, Baw Baw Frog, Stuttering Frog, and the Growling Grass Frog. In this cooperative game, the players try to return and establish stable populations of each species to the wild.

All player's hands are seen only by them, but they cannot play cards from their own hand. Instead they must use the action cards to find out information about the cards held by other players, all while trying to avoid threats to the frogs and their habitats.

The game was developed and designed by 5D (in 2014), 6A (in 2015) and their teacher at Berwick Fields Primary School. Wanting to do something to help endangered frog species, the class launched a two-year learning journey and this game is the product of that. Along the way, the class was awarded the Zoos Victoria Love Your Locals 2014 winner for its work on the game and was inducted into the Zoos Victoria Hall of Fame for Young Wildlife Advocates of the year in 2015.

This is a collection of three games intended to be suitable for playing while on a journey.

     Game 1  "Lost Sheep" is the classic SOLITAIRE (1697) played with magnetic sheep.
     Game 2  "Battle Sheeps" is the classic BATTLESHIPS (1931) on a 10×10 board with different size groups of sheep instead of ships.
     Game 3  "The Ewesual Suspects" is a sort of WHOSIT? (1976) played with a set of 24 (sheep) character cards where any question with a YES or NO answer may be asked.

After the destruction of most of Manresa during the War of the Spanish Succession, the four main classes of the city start working to rebuild it: the bourgeois and the traders build a palace that will house the city hall, once the old ones affected by the 1713 fire are demolished; the artisans and the farmers rebuild the burnt houses from most of the city; the clerks request the manufacturing of a new altarpiece for the Seu (the city’s cathedral) because the old ones were burnt, and the bourbonic army strengthens the city’s defenses by fortifying the barracks in Puig Mercadal.

The game is a slightly asymmetrical worker placement, where players try to be the first to finish the three reconstruction phases of their buildings.

Each round, the players send the workers outside the city to the production placements; then some misfortunes could stop the production of resources (fires, droughts or collapses); after that the players gather their production (stone for the walls, salt to pay workers’ and artisans’ salaries, water to make mortar or wood for the scaffoldings); finally they use the resources to finish if possible one of the reconstruction phases.

There is an advanced variant adding a fourth reconstruction phase (the new banner of the city) and a different skill for each class.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Organizations: Board Games Association of Creators in Spain (LUDO)

description:
Take to the sky commanding a squadron of bi-planes in this exciting head-to-head dice game! Sharpen your aim as your competitors dodge your attack. Call up your flying Aces to take down your opponent’s planes and claim your victory!

Squadron Dice is a game of elimination. First, players roll a set  of dice to gain position over other players. Next, the highest and lowest positioned players roll dice again, forcing the lowest positioned player to remove one of their Squadron dice from the game.

A player is eliminated from the game when a player cannot replace a Rookie die with an Aces die after a loss on an Evade roll during a Resolve Phase. When all other players have been eliminated from the game, you win.

Brettspiel Easter Basket 2016 contains eight new expansions for eight games from eight publishers.

All expansions take the form of punch-out counters, except the colt express expansion, which is a mini game board.


     Snowdonia: Wagons Expansion - a collection of 9 wagon tiles, which you can buy using the build action. Each awards a different end-game bonus.
     Antarctica: Penguin Expansion - 8 identical counters, representing the extra point earned by the player who comes first in an area.
     Haus Der Sonne: Lava Tiles Mini Expansion - 6 lava tiles.
     My Village: Vagrants Expansion - 4 vagrants, distributed randomly to players grant a unique one-shot special ability, and a 5th "wandering" Itinerant Preacher tile which accumulates story points and passes to whichever player had the last death in their village.
     Imperial Settlers: Council Promo - During the production phase, if all players agree put a meeple on the council tile, each player draws 1 faction card and the meeples go back to the supply.
     Colt Express: Rock Promo - This Rock decoration can be used to place your common deck during your Colt Express games.
     T.I.M.E Stories: Beacon Promo - 3 "beacon" tokens which you can spend to skip spending time points when you change location.
     Dream Islands: Package Trip Promo - Each player gets a set of 3 tiles, each tile can be played once per game, and affects your move


This package also contains the microgame WH1CH GAM3 from Jürgen Karla and Oliver Grimm that can be played in order to determine which game should be played next.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Polyversal is a science-fiction mass-combat miniatures game. The game is for two or more players and is set in a plausible near-future Earth, but also lets you create a setting with its open-source approach. Polyversal is compatible with all 6mm-15mm miniatures and is fully customizable. Boxed sets of the game can be purchased with or without miniatures. Sets with miniatures feature models from multiple 6mm and 10mm miniatures manufacturers including Brigade Models, Dark Realm, Plasmablast Games, Microworld Games, and Hawk Wargames (Now TT Combat).

Polyversal also allows players to design their own Combatant Tiles using a free web application called Arsenal. The input is a player's miniature and design choices- the output is a playable game tile. All tiles contain stats for units in the game and they are also used to form battlegroups- a visual command structure. A points-based system balances everything out based on the selected options, weaponry, armor, class of unit, etc.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Battle for Arnhem is a low-complexity board-wargame on Operation Market-Garden.  Designed for two players or solo play.

The Allied player drops his paratroopers ahead of the ground advance, hoping to capture key bridges and cities.  Meanwhile the tanks roll in from the south and fight their way through the German counter attack.

This is a small game with simple rules, suitable for beginners, but play is tense and often comes down to the last turn.  The Allied player has two ways to win which forces both players to plan ahead.

The files are available here on BGG.

Download the following:

     Game Rules
     Counter Sheet
     Map File
     Game Schedule


You can also optionally download the Designer Notes.

For the counters, separate the front and back portions of the counter sheet, then use the aligning holes to ensure proper alignment.  Glue the front and back sheets to chipboard and cut using a glue stick or spray adhesive.  A rotary cutter works best for cutting, but scissors will do.

For the map, print out both map pages and tape or glue together.  The full map is 22" x 8.5" with the pages joined end-to-end.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
In the knitting game Threads, you collect yarn in your stash, trade with other's destash, and turn that yarn into the best knitting patterns possible.

In more detail, players collect yarn and use it to knit various projects. Each time they complete a stitch, they are rewarded with victory points, with additional bonus points coming upon the completion of each project.

Complete the most valuable projects and you will win, being honored as the best knitter in your group!

boardgame: Isle of Man (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
This map is an unofficial fan made expansion. Symbols & Graphics Copyright © 2004-2015 Days of Wonder, Inc. Produced with permission of Days of Wonder, for non-commercial use only.

This follows the principles of the basic Ticket to Ride game on a special board with special cards and rules. The object of the game is to gain the most points by travelling various routes and outwitting other players. You will need the train pieces and the train cards from either the US or Europe base games (or you can buy them from the Days of Wonder shop. You can use stations if you wish.

There is an extra set of Chance cards: some are for the cities, each with a value to be earned by visiting that city:others give you extra facilities to complete your tickets. These cards are dealt/picked up alongwith tickets in the same quantity and with the same 'keep/dispose' rules. There are tunnels, ferries,flights and a mountain railway.There are also up to 9 bonuses for the end-of-game scoring.

The board does not have any grey tracks or parallel tracks - these are in the hands of the Gods. Thus there are God cards but also Goblin cards!

There are many new options in the rules: exchange all your cards at the start but keep all the new ones: discard 5 cards and pick up 5 new ones, as your turn: earn double or triple points: and many more. You can also call time when your wagon stock reaches 3, if you want, giving you the advantage of stopping other players completing tickets.

Zombies in our neighborhood is a solitaire card game using grid movement, dice rolls and resource management. It’s the zombie apocalypse and your neighborhood has been overrun by the living dead. You are ex-military and you just returned home from war when you realize the world has gone to hell in a hand basket. Your family is scattered throughout the neighborhood and you must rescue all of them. Your wife and two kids are holed up in different house throughout the neighborhood and you need to find them and bring them to safety; or as safe as you can be in a zombie apocalypse!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
The Colt Express: Marshal & Prisoners expansion contains three new components:


     It adds another character "Mei", a martial arts lady.
     The players can free prisoners from the prison wagon that is added to the end of the train.
     One player can play as the marshal, who has two decks of bullet cards and three specific items.
     The Marshal has got four hidden and random objectives, he must achieve it to win the game (i.e. shoot every bullet from one gun, catch a Bandit, Shoot at every Bandit, ...)
     Freeing junior bandits from the prison car gains their help (i.e. a secondary ability for your character)

In American Dynasty you will play as one of the grand American political families: the Roosevelts, the Kennedys, the Bushes, or the Clintons. To assert your family’s supremacy you will employ the time­-honored tools of civic discourse, such as manipulation of the media, tactical legislation, calling in favors, back­stabbing, stabbing, and the accumulation and distribution of money. You must build your political engine, sabotage your opponents, and bully and barter your way into the hearts and minds of the American electorate, ignorant of the closed door meetings, and deliberately impassioned by carefully orchestrated media events. By getting your candidates elected to congress, passing bills, and hopefully becoming president, you will crusade against your rival dynasties, sacrificing resources and morality in equal share. Anything, for the good of the family.

GAMEPLAY: The game is played over the course of 5 Terms, and the goal of the game is to collect the most Electorals by the end of Term 5. Each term, players take turns taking 2 actions each (playing Core Action cards or Media Event cards, introducing Bills for voting, and other special) until an Election is triggered. In an Election, players with competing candidates roll dice and add on bonuses they prepared before the Election to determine winners of each vote. Winning candidates then take office and can vote on Bills and other issues. Electorals can be gained in multiple ways, including winning elections and passing bills. Negotiation, trades, bartering, bribes and table-talk are strongly encouraged.

Terra on Tour takes players on an adventurous journey of knowledge around the world — but if the players don't know what's being asked about, they can still score by being close to the correct answer. What's more, players also assess the answers given by others, allowing you to score from the knowledge (or lack thereof) of opponents.

Terra on Tour is a standalone game, but the cards included can also function as an expansion for Terra.

Welt der 80er quizzes players about the world in the 1980s, with a record-shaped game board highlighting a media format that disappeared shortly after that time. Players select questions from seven categories and test their knowledge: When did Katarina Witt win a gold medal? What show did Rudi Carrell host in the 1980s? Who was the German chancellor?

With Jan & Henry: Das Memo-Spiel, youngsters can immerse themselves in the stories of Jan and Henry while trying to find where Henry tucked away the birthday cake or where the bed went to. In the end, whoever finds the most pairs of tiles wins!

Description from the publisher:

Who has quick-witted answers to the really important questions in life and can make their fellow players laugh in reaction to their profound but absurd answers? You'll find out in Klappe!, but if you overdo it, you can quickly talk yourself into trouble...

Description from the publisher:

Monsters upon monsters! But everybody has brought a different musical instrument for the night-time monster concert. Each card is played in front of the young conductors and covered by the next card. Who will be alert enough in RaBATz! to know which monsters have already been on which stage?

What's more, once a trio has performed and has been recognized correctly, the search changes. Now players need to find three of the same musical instruments!

Trends sometimes change faster than you can move!

Which colors are recommended by Guido the trendsetter? What's the new rallying cry? In Shopping Queen: Das Kartenspiel, you're trying as quickly as possible to compile a matching five-piece outfit. But be careful! Taking unwanted fashion advice from other players can make you look pretty stupid...
family: Versions & Editions: Card Versions of Non-Card Games

description: A promo added by the publisher to every copy of the base game until it's out of stock, designed by Mateusz Żbikowski. A set of 4 tiles - one for each player, depicting a termite queen in their  colour. The queen lies in player's supply from the beginning of the game. During their turn, in the reproduction phase, a player can - once during the entire game - place their queen on any hex (except for water or stone) instead of a normal termite. When on the board, the queen cannot be attacked, moved, does not attack or support attacks. A player may move through/over the hex with their queen. The quuen can be replaced by any available termite tile belonging to the same player as the queen.

Description from the publisher:

The candidate of the day is traveling back and forth across the city while you're working in her home, trying to use the funds available to build up the wardrobe of this candidate to match the three special goals before the candidate returns. Who will manage to roll the best outfits in Shopping Queen: Das Würfelspiel?
family: Versions & Editions: Dice Versions of Non-Dice Games

boardgame: Die Legenden von Andor: Der Unbekannte Krieger
description:
Der Unbekannte Krieger (The Unknown Warrior) is a blank hero for Game: Legends of Andor. You may fill in its dice, rank, and special abilities. It differs from the first blank hero, Legends of Andor: The Stranger, by having four willpower rows (like the Tarus from Legends of Andor: New Heroes).

A standee (but no board) for this hero is included with Legends of Andor: Journey to the North; the board is available as a free PDF download here: https://legenden-von-andor.de/blankoheld-der-unbekannte-krieger/.

boardgame: Fun to Go: Fragen über Fragen
description:
Description from the publisher:

Based on the familiar traveling game of "I spy with my little eye", Fun to Go: Fragen über Fragen challenges most of the players to guess what the active player is seeing on their card by asking only questions that can be answered either "yes" or "no".

Description from the publisher:

Where is a red car, a blue bus or a yellow truck driving? Whoever finds the sought vehicle first gives a yell and scores points for spotting the target. Whoever watches most closely and spots the most cars in Fun to Go: Auto Lotto will win!

It plays like Riichi Mahjong, but needs a whole different set of tiles.

1/4 of the tiles are opaque and 3/4 are translucent.

So, the strategies are a lot different since you may see about 3/4 of the tiles in your opponents hand.

Variant from the Manga Akagi, and there are sets made exclusively to play this particular variant of the game.

Description from the publisher:

Jan and Henry have once again been hearing strange noises! In Jan & Henry: Das Geräuschespiel, players assume the role of one of the meerkats and describe to the other players the sounds they hear or they imitate the noises themselves. Who can guess the most noises?

Roller Derby Final Jams (RDFJ) is essentially a race game based on the sport of Women's Flat Track Roller Derby.

RDFJ components include skater cards depicting jammers and blockers from two rival teams. Players will use custom dice and team assist disks to navigate their jammers past their opponent’s blockers in order to score points before time runs out in last few remaining jams of a closely contested derby bout.

Optionally the game can be customized by removing the skater cards that come with the game and then substituting trading cards available from some roller derby leagues. This allows players to simulate competition between actual roller derby teams.

Kill the Overlord: Anarchist is a promotional character for Kill the Overlord that bears the following power: "Give 1 gold to the Overlord during the Income Phase. If you have no shield cards, you may play any book card as if it was Revolution - all Active Players randomly switch roles and the Execution ends."

Kill the Overlord: Conspirator is a promotional character for Kill the Overlord that bears the following power: "At the start of the Round, all other players close their eyes. Players with book cards raise their hands."

Description from the publisher:

A bank heist in Berlin-Tegel, a painting theft at the Stuttgart state gallery, a corpse in the inner harbor in Hamburg — these cases and 22 others from 25 cities in Germany, Austria and Switzerland are really demanding. The mysterious crimes in Crime Master can be solved completely only by the cleverest investigators.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Description from the publisher:

After months of investigation, there is finally enough evidence against the notorious "Godfather" to put the cunning criminal behind bars for good. The question remains, however, how he can be arrested.

In Die Villa des Paten, only a team of smart as well as resolute policemen can manage to eliminate the ruthless gangsters and to penetrate the lion's den.

Description from the publisher:

What regulates the drawstring? What was the lowest amount a candidate on Shopping Queen ever spent on her outfit? How often did Guido Maria Kretschmer give zero points? You'll find these and other questions in Shopping Queen: Das kleine Quiz. Learn the detailed answers and exciting knowledge about one of the most beautiful themes of world.

Description from the publisher:

Death Grip is kidnapping innocent citizens in the city, but there is hope! A new hero – Crimson Crush – is in town, and he will use his special powers to put an end to Death Grip.

In Crimson Crush, you play your cards smartly to score points with the hero, while trying to have Death Grip kidnap citizens to give negative points to other players.

Wine IQ is the ultimate game for wine lovers, letting everyone shine and have a good time. The two levels of difficulty and the ability to play in a team make it a very sociable game. In the first round it comes to your knowledge; in the second to your memory. From the classics to the forbidden wine napa in Casablanca, from the wine to the glass, with this game you will discover fascinating things.

—description from the publisher

Grand Tour Europe is the game for travel lovers who want to discover the countries and cultures of Europe. The players answer questions using their cards that represent countries, but no need to be a know-it-all to win. The two types of questions — speed and knowledge — make the game fun and accessible. Correct answers let the players advance from country to country and beat the other players to win playing pieces and victory points. The player with the most points at the end wins.

The Bullshit Quiz is a game about populism and right-wing extremist positions. It's satirical and tries to sensitize the players for extremist views or statements. The populists in the game and their statements are original ones from current social debates in germany. The game is designed by the authors of a famous satirical news site. They've also created the Minderheiten-Quartett.

From the publisher (translated and abbreviated):
Welcome to the Bullshit Quiz - the interactive crashcourss in Populism.

The game consists of 2 phases:
In the Quiz-Phase quotations of right wing populists are read out, the players must guess who said it (out of a selection of 4). The winner takes a bullshit card.
In Phase 2 players must a create a funny sentence with their bullshit cards, which is then rated by the others.
The game includes cartoon drawings and short biographies of the 30 populists.

From the publisher:
Herzlich willkommen bei Bullshit Quiz - dem interaktiven Crashkurs in Rechtspopulismus.

Haben Sie das Gefühl, nicht von Ihrem Normalsein zu profitieren? Haben Sie es satt, dass sich Politik und Medien immer nur mit den Problemen von Minderheiten beschäftigen? Sind Sie auf der Suche nach einer beruflichen Veränderung?

Werden Sie noch heute Rechtspopulist!

Dazu müssen Sie nicht in teure Ausbildungscamps nach Russland oder Sachsen reisen. Sie können das Populisten-Handwerk auch ganz bequem und spielerisch im heimischem Wohnzimmer erlernen: Vom Sesselpupser zum Opinion-Leader in nur 60 Minuten!

Das Spiel besteht aus 2 Phasen.


     In Phase 1, dem "QUIZ" gibt es abhängig der Spieleranzahl unterschiedlich viele Runden. Ein pro Runde wechselnder Quizmaster


liest ein zufälliges Zitat vor. Die Spieler müssen nun aus einer Auswahl von 4 Populisten wählen, wer diesen Bullshit von sich gegeben hat.
Die Spieler die richtig geraten haben, bekommen eine Bullshit-Karte.


     In Phase 2, dem "Bullshit", kann jeder Spieler nun den Populisten nacheifern.


Aus den erhaltenen Bullshit-Karten bildet man einen möglichst lustigen Satz, der von den anderen dann bewertet wird.
Die Wertung der Mitspieler + der Bullshit-Faktor der Karten werden addiert und bilden die so eine Endwertung.

Durch die Quiz-Runde erfährt man viel von den Populisten (Steckbriefe enthalten).
Die Zitate sind gut ausgewählt und mit Quellenangabe hinterlegt.
Die Populisten sind durch schöne Karikaturen dargestellt. Die Spielanleitung ("Book of Shame") ist klar geschrieben und einfach verständlich.
A new location for Spyfall. The Spy will surely be stumped when he finds himself amongst a crowd of board game geeks!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Paris 1800 allows players to play as the great figures of the nineteenth century Romantic and Modern, to decide the future planning of the capital. Modern transform the city by expanding its boulevards, creating monuments in iron and glass. Romantics will preserve the old quarters of bourgeois ambitions by using the dissatisfaction of the people of Paris.

One player is the Modern ans plays Haussmann and two police chiefs
Others players are the Romantics (1 Romantic per player)

Turn Sequence :
1 - Rewards : Modern gains money ; Romantics gains  "Inspiration".  Romantics draw 1 Revolution card when the Time Cube (grey) is on a Revolution dot 
2-Move : players can place their tokens on the board
3-Actions : Romantics do their Actions first. They can:
a-Go out of France (Exil)
b-Go in Fontainebleau Forest
c-Pay for deliver a Romantic in the Fortress
d-Raise the angry of the Parisians by put an "Angry token"
Bonus : if there are 5 adjacent Angry tokens , 1 of them can be substituted by a Revolt token 
e: Find Haussmann and gain 3 VP
f : If Romantics succeed their Revolution goal, they gain 8 VP

Modern can :
a-Go to the City Hall to obtain a building permit
b-Go to the "Beaux-Arts" Academy to have a Project
c-Renove a district. Haussmann must  be in this district., and this district must match with the Project requirements and the building permit. This action also costs some money and Modern gains victory points (VP)
d. Pacification : a  Modern token can discard an "Angry token". On a Police token can be in a revolted district and remove the token. It costs 2 millions.
e : Jail a Romantic : can be done by a Police token which is on the same district than a Romantic token, which is placd in the Fortress.

All the players can : Open/Close the Magazine in Montmartre and Go to the Observatory to change the Move Order.

Victory : there's a score track at the left of the board. The winner is the faction which have 20 VP before the end of the game or its depends of the position of the scoring cube on the track at the end of the game (1910).
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Hi-Jack is an abstract strategy game, played in a 6x6 or 8x8 square board. It is a territory game. Players take turns placing pieces in the board; those pieces exert influence (i.e. claims) over adjacent spaces. Players score at the end of the game for the territory rhey influence more than their opponents. Highest score wins.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Borg Cube With Sphere Port Premium Figure
description:
Star Trek: Attack Wing – Borg Cube With Sphere Port Premium Figure is two expansions in one, with one of them being an oversized Borg Cube (20cm on each side) that also has ship cards and tokens for Cube 384 and the accompanying Sphere 936.

The other half of this expansion is a cooperative scenario in which 1-5 players can battle against Cube 384, whose actions and attacks are automated and random!

The Powering Up! expansion takes Tesla vs. Edison gameplay to a whole new level by infusing exciting new systems and introducing both a sixth player and a solitaire variant! Fans of the game will delight in exploring:

     Building a headquarters. What is a good electric company without state-of-the-art facilities such as a laboratory, works, office, and studio? Each option offers different bonuses and rewards to the enterprising player!
     Responding to historical events. Dipping into the rich treasure trove that was the 19th century War of Currents, 21 real-life events add variety and flavor to every play. Exciting opportunities to fight for! Catastrophic calamities to recover from! Woe is the unprepared electricity capitalist!
     Empowering the women. Seven historical ladies join the gentlemen to engage in the War of Currents. Important female entrepreneurs, engineers, and artists are now available to draft for each company. Plus, one of these luminaries, trailblazing philanthropist Madam C.J. Walker, becomes the sixth player!
     A solitaire robot challenges the solo player with dynamic play!


The enhancements offered by the Powering Up! expansion bring new layers of strategy and fun to the Tesla vs. Edison experience without significantly lengthening game time.

So get ready to power up!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Fairy Tale Chess is for people who want to add an air of uncertainty to their chess games.

Unlike normal chess, Fairy Tale Chess uses a deck of Chance Cards that are drawn at the start of every player's turn, with results ranging from the helpful to the disastrous.

Follow the basic rules of Chess except where stated.

Harry Houdini is throwing a dinner party for the world’s greatest magicians. To make this evening more special, he has prepared a unique delicacy — poutine! Unfortunately for everyone at the party, Houdini is a terrible cook. Each player must use their skills in magic to escape from the party without hurting Houdini's feelings.

Houdini's Poutini is a competitive game in which each player represents a group of five magicians. They must collect a combination of item cards with which they can perform magical escapes from the party. The Houdini card will mingle about the party, making it more difficult to escape the party without hurting his feelings. You must use your skills as a magician to get your entire group free from the party before time runs out and the poutine is served. When only one player remains or time runs out, anyone who was unable to escape must politely choke-down Houdini's ill-prepared Franken-dish.

A variation on rummy with a splash of Old Maid with lots of screwing over your opponents, Houdini's Poutini is a fast-paced and exciting card game for any with a strong mind and stronger stomach.

Waterloo Squares is 2-player entry-level wargame of the battle of Waterloo 1815 played on a map which represents the historical battlefield.

One player assumes the role of Napoleon and takes command of the French, the other player represents the Wellington/Blücher combination and will command the Allies (British, Dutch/Belgian, minor German states and Prussian).

The Light Blue and Dark Blue counters represent the French forces, the Grey counters the Prussians and the Red counters all the other Allied forces. All counters contain information denoting their type (infantry or cavalry), combat strength and historical designation - which is only used during setup.

The map is divided into 100 squares, each of a single terrain type - Clear, Woods or Buildings - each of which has a somewhat different effect on movement and combat.

Each square can only contain a single counter. Movement and combat between units is regulated and triggered by playing cards from a personal deck which can be manipulated according to a player's chosen strategy.

Players take turns until one player achieves one of his victory conditions at which point the game ends and he's declared the winner.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Farándula is a central pool deck-building game where each player take the rol of a participant in a Yellow-Program. In this yellow program, Famous are willing to do anything for fame. False pregnancies, homophobic remarks, car accidents ... Nothing is too low or vile as to not use it either to score points for yourself or to try to sink the other players' score.

But do not let the subject scare you. In essence, Farándula is a deck-building game with more than enough complexity to keep you entertained. Build your deck from a central pool with juicy exclusives and fill other players' decks with everything you do not want. To win you just have to have more Fame points at the end of the game. With 6 different audiences to pamper, Farándula offers a variety of playstyles to try each game.

So, whether you have a dark sense of humor or you just like deck-building games, you should take a look at Farándula!

Politics is the art of the possible.
War is politics by other means.
Peace and war. War and peace.

Take the role of the leaders of European major power in the times of Napoleonic Wars. Try to negotiate the most favourable peace, but remember sometimes war might be the only way, leading to it.

Players form partnerships or alliances, which then compete for assets: political influence. Those could be gain through diplomacy, (spending diplomatic actions) or war (spending military actions). Players move their playing pieces to vie for influence on the board representing 19th Century Europe. Influence can also be gained by completing diplomatic or military missions, shown on cards.

The alliances are not very stable, so each turn may see other set of players cooperating.

The winner is the player, who can fulfill set of ambitions of the Empire he or she is representing.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Other than being colorful and fun, this game has no relation to the hit Pixar film. UP is a unique abstract game for 2-6 players that combines the strategy of limited actions, with the luck of dice, and the joy of building blocks. Games last about 30 minutes. Basically, you're all building one stack of multicolored dice and moving your pawns up the stack along paths of color that match your pawn.

You start out with a handful of dice. There's no numbers on these dice, just 6 different colors; and the dice themselves are different colors. You have to get rid of all your dice to win the game, but you also have to be the highest pawn on the stack after you've used up all your dice.
