The color that you roll determines where a die can be placed on the stack (that's the luck part), but rather than spending a die, you can use your turn to move or rotate a die that's already on the stack (that's the strategy part). You might want to move a die to open up a path for your pawn to climb higher, or rotate a die to make it harder for an opponent.

The competition gets hotter as the game progresses, and there seems to be a good "catch the leader" dynamic, so that by the end, everyone is often at the edge of their seats to see if a lucky roll or a tricky maneuver will win the game.

(wrong description below - the game is similar to trivial pursuit and asks all sorts of questions to players)
There are 60 cards with 12 different animals and 5 cards with humans.
Each player gets His/Her own animal. Shuffle the cards and start drawing Your cards. If You draw and show the animal You represent be the first to shout the sound of this animal to get the card as a point. If the human is drawn the first player who shouts homo sapiens gets the card as a point.

If all cards are played the player with the most points is the winner.

boardgame: Star Fleet Battles: Commander's Rulebook Volume III
description: Update to earlier volumes I & II rulebooks, with rules pages intended to replaced previously published but now outdated pages

Explore the new world as a dragon hatchling! Players compete with each other to see who can amass the greatest horde of gold. As they gain experience and grow into larger and more capable dragons they will also face the challenge of slayers. With only 9 rounds to play it will be a tight race to see who can be most infamous Wyrm!

On your turn you will take actions that include search, eat, move, explore, raze, and fight. The explore action will allow you to draw new tiles to expand the play area. The search action will let you draw an event for a specific tile, which may be a fort, ruins, or a farm. These farms and forts may be razed for gold, or you can eat the livestock they produce to grow and gain experience.

Experience can be used to grow your dragon, from whelp to drake, and then to a mighty wyrm! You will also use experience to unlock and upgrade skills for your dragon. Each dragon has several unique skills that only they can use, no two dragons are exactly the same.

Each new round will start by introducing a new slayer to the board. The player who's turn it is to go first will control this slayer for the rest of the time it remains on the board. These slayers come in a variety of forms, from the simple bandit chieftain, to the mighty heroes of legend. Defeat these slayers and you will earn gold and experience, fail and you will have some of your actions crippled for a time.

Throughout the 9 rounds there will be new objectives introduced that will give players chances to earn more gold as they complete them. Whoever ultimately ends up with the most gold at the end of the game wins!

THEME
YEAR = 2025
WORLD POPULATION = 8 Billion
GLOBAL TEMPERATURE RISE = 1 degree C
As the first quarter of the century comes to an end, world leaders have yet to achieve any meaningful reduction in global carbon emissions. Food production is being disrupted around the world, and demand is surging for dwindling resources. In the decades to come there will be countless crises, like economic collapse, political revolutions, disastrous weather, failed states, disruptive technologies with unknown consequences, and much more. The only certainty is that when people are faced with famine, they will always migrate and raid their neighbors before starving.

Set in the near future, the players take on the roles of the USA, Western Europe, Russia, China, and India. Collectively, these five major global powers dominate the world’s economic, military, political, social, and technological powers. By cooperating and strategizing, the players must put their collective powers into actions to lower carbon emissions while resolving the many crises around the world.

HOW TO PLAY
The world is divided into 15 regions, each with its own population, food capacity, carbon emissions, sociopolitical stability, and likely number of people that would suffer when hit by famine. At the start of each turn a number of crises are revealed to all the players. Each crisis is associated with one of the 15 regions. The players must then decide what actions they will take, unilaterally or jointly, to resolve which crises by implementing their economic, military, political, social, technological powers. Alongside dealing with crises around the world, the players must quickly reduce global carbon emissions and prevent their own regions from becoming a failed state. This can be done by implementing green energies and developing advanced technologies. At the end of each turn the global temperature rises, and if it exceeds a new threshold then food capacity is reduced in every region worldwide. If any region loses its ability to feed its population then famine strikes, starting a chain reaction of refugees, conflicts, failed states, and megadeath! Players must devise innovative solutions to tackle Earth’s most urgent problems. Act too selfishly, and you accelerate humanity’s demise. Act too altruistically, and you could be overrun by climate refugees and chaos.

EVERYONE LOSES WHEN…
If the global temperature rises by six degrees Celsius, there won’t be any way to stop the runaway greenhouse. It will result in Earth’s sixth mass extinction, possibly the greatest one in geologic time! Still, a rising global temperature alone is not the only thing that can bring the end of human civilization. Some regions of the world and its people may be forsaken, mired in conflicts, epidemics, and anarchy. If the chaos consumes most of the world then there will be no safe-haven left for anyone, and all nations will inevitable become failed states. If either condition is met before the end of this century then it is a Global Collapse . . . the beginning of the end of all human civilization!

YOU WIN WHEN…
If the two conditions for losing are not met by the end of this century, and your region is not a failed state, then humanity is saved and your civilization leads the world into the next century.

boardgame: Ph. D. The Game: Dr. Dinosaur
description:
Included as a Limited Edition Card for KS backers of Dino Dude Ranch and KS Backers of the original Ph. D. game, it is a Dinosaur Dude Rancher as the image but in the style of Ph. D. The Game.

It adds a new Lab member card to the original game

Keep your Heroes of Normandie games fresh and interesting with regular new content! The fifth quarterly Gazette includes new rules, new scenarios, and an exclusive punchboard filled with equipment, options, and surprises.

This gazette has 4 scenarios, one punchboard and a giant poster that represents the Arnhem bridge.

Scenarios require Pegasus Bridge and SS Panzergrenadiere expansions. You will also want the UK Army Box if you want to use the UK Action Deck and Order tokens.
family: Series: Heroes System Tactical Scale World War 2

description:
Football game is an "educational game" aimed at children and families.  Players take turns rolling the die, advancing their player down the pitch.  The first player to score a goal wins.

Score the first goal to win the game!

A roll and move game similar to snakes and ladders. However the game also come with two spinners to simulate the decision of the umpire's whistle (can be good or bad) and the attempt of the goal keeper to block the shot on goal.

Each player has their own player board and football counter.

Game description from the box:
Watch the ball pass backwards and forwards as you race to score the winning goal in this exciting game of football!

Hunter's Island is a last man standing card game. Each turn the players will search the Island for better cards, equip the cards they find or attack the other players. While the player searches the island animals are also there to attack.

Can you out hunt the other players?


For 2-4 players, this card game pits players on a lonely island against the island's dangers and also each other to the last Hunter standing.

Starting with the ability to escape and attack, players take turns searching the island to gather equipment or encounter animals and other dangers. Found cards are placed in the player's Storage, where they can be picked up rather than searching the Island or Attacking.

Players must decide which of three areas to search, either safely by camp, the deep island, or crossing the ocean water to another dangerous island while the island's dangers whittle away their life points and equipment. Soon, the opportunity to be the lone Hunter will arise.

Hunter's Island Thieves is a last man standing card game. Each turn the players will search the island for better gear, equip the gear or attack the other players. While the players search the island animals will attack the players. Be the last man standing to win.

This game is a stand alone expansion of the game Hunter's Island. Combining the games will allow for more players and more situations.

Hunter's Island Zombies is a last man standing card game. Each turn the players will search the island for better gear, equip the gear or attack the other players. While the players search the island Zombies will attack the players and if they kill the player, the player becomes a zombie and for rest of the game is attack the other players to make them zombies as well.

This game is a stand alone expansion of the game Hunter's Island.  there is also Hunter's Island Thieves expansion, combining the sets will allow for more players and more situations during the game.

The game contains 36 fishes in mixed colours and two dice. From the beginning, all but three of these are upside down. On your turn, one die is rolled and show your action. Either you are allowed to fish, everyone are allowed to fish, you can turn one fish around or you miss your turn. If you are allowed to fish, the second die is used showing which colour you are allowed to take. The same goes when everyone are allowed to fish. The players then compete to grab the fishes with the correct colour. The game ends when all fishes are taken and the player with the most fishes wins.

Cap de colla és un joc de taula de cartes i daus que representa i difon la cultura castellera d’una forma divertida, dinàmica i enginyosa.

Cap de colla is a boardgame that uses cards and dice to spread the human towers culture in a funny, dynamic and clever way.

The purpose of the game is scoring the most points by doing human towers typical of catalan culture. The game is played in five regular rounds plus a column round. Each regular round is divided in two phases.

In first phase, called "assaig" (something like "erzatz"), cards are dealed and traded until most players have assembled enought cards to build a human tower.

Then a new phase called "a plaça" (literally "to the square", but it means performing), each player must perform the towers by using the dice : thay have to roll over difficulty of their tower to build the tower, and if the tower is succeful, they score a "trial" token and can try to "unbuild" the tower. The trial tokens earned in past rounds can be exchanged for rerolls. Each round has a scoring process : first player to complete a tower, succesful builds and unbuilds score points according with the difficulty of the tower. After the five regular rounds are played, players can try to build human columns (called "pilars") with excess trial tokens and score points according to column difficulty.

The player that scores most points, adding score of all rounds is the winner.

Fábulas Fantásticas é um jogo de storytelling, isto é, através das imagens contidas nas cartas, os jogadores se unem para contar e criar uma história pra lá de fantástica.

O jogo é formado por um baralho de 54 cartas dividido em 3 tipos: Persona, que define o protagonista e os coadjuvantes da cena, Locações, que define o cenário onde a cena ocorre, e Acessórios, itens que trazem o conflito motivador da cena.


Fantastic Fables is a storytelling game, that is, through the images contained on cards, players come together to tell a story even more fantastic.

The game uses a deck of 54 cards, divided into 3 types: Persona, defining the protagonist and the supporting cast, Locations,which sets the local where the scene takes place, and Accessories, items that trigger the conflict scene.

boardgame: Escola de Dragões: Intercâmbio no Oriente
description:
The Gran-masters decided to take their students on a vacation exchange to practicing against a different school. But it seems that will not be easy.

This first expansion for School of Dragons extends the a number of players to 5 (base deck + expansion) and adds 26 cards with Orient-themed illustrations (art by Jersey Giuliano) and two new powers found in two cards:

Old Master - His power change the direction of the table (from clockwise to counterclockwise or vice versa).
Troll Kabuki - Allows the player to see 4 cards from the hand of another player.

The game continues with the same simple features, uniting experienced and novice player while keeping the fun.

From the publiser (Portuguese):
Os magos da Escola de Dragões decidiram levar seus alunos em um intercâmbio de férias para que praticassem contra uma escola diferente. Mas parece que não será fácil.

Na primeira expansão de Escola de Dragões amplia a quantidade de jogadores de 4 para 5 participantes e adiciona 26 cartas com ilustrações com temática oriental, todos os desenhos com a belíssima arte de Jersey Giuliano, e dois novos poderes encontrados nas duas cartas:

Mestre Treinador - Permite que o jogador veja a mão de outro participante
Troll Kabuki - Sim! O terrível Troll também foi para o oriente! E resolveu participar de aulas de teatro! Seu poder é inverter o sentido da mesa. Se o jogo está no sentido horário ele passa a ser anti horário! Ou vice versa.

O jogo continua com a mesmas características simples mantendo sua pegada de unir jogadores experientes e novatos sem perder a diversão.

User summary:
ケルベロス (Cerberus) was developed from ideas that appeared whilst designing the game ヘッジホッグジレンマ (Hedgehog's Dilemma).

Players are dealt six cards. You play a card each turn, whose value is either 1, 2, or 3. If the card's value matches a card in play, you score that value. But if you score more than five points, you lose all the points scored so far.

Publisher's summary:
1-3の数字が書かれた6枚のカードを任意の順番で出し、
場のカードとペアを作り得点を獲得します。
6点以上の点を獲得すると0点になります。
出したカードと同じ数字のカードが場にあると必ずペアになります。

Vagadas is a mancala inspired game of wandering. After setup of the 18 modular land tiles and placement of goods on each tile, players take turns moving the tokens around the board to gain points based on the tokens they are moving and where those tokens end the turn.

Contents:
81 tokens (9 each of cargo, fish, food, gold, love, people, stone, water, and wood.)
1 bag
18 Land Tiles
5 Player tokens

Setup:
Shuffle the 18 land tiles and make a 6 x 3 pattern (You may vary this modular board as long as there are at least 2 rows of tiles). Draw 4 random tokens from the bag and place on each tile, following the rules of each if needed.

Game Play:
Players will take turns going North/South or East/West depending on which side of their player token is face up. Players will take all the tokens on a tile, and moving in the desired direction, place one token on each tile until all the tokens in hand are exhausted. After all tokens are placed, scoring will happen depending on which tile the player ended placing tokens. Play repeats until there are no legal moves and the player with the highest score wins.

User summary:
ござるござる忍者でござる (Gozaru Gozaru Ninja de Gozaru) means "We are NINJA!" in English.

Ninjas are trying to steal your secret documents. Even high fences and heavy security cannot prevent them from infiltrating. Once you let even one of them sneak in, the entire territory will be overflowing with them and all the documents will be stolen. Counter-intelligence is the only way to protect your documents. You must mislead the Ninjas away from your documents.

The game is played with 2-4 players. Each player has four secret documents hidden in a 5x5 square grid gameboard. Your goal is to place Ninja tiles on squares where opponents' documents are hidden, while protecting your documents. During the game, players take turns placing a Ninja tile following placement rules. At the end of game, you score victory points if your document square is not occupied by a Ninja tile.

Are you smart enough to protect your secret documents?

The artwork for this game came from copyright free images.

Publisher's summary:
ボード上に忍者タイルを配置して、自分の密書のマスを守り抜きましょう。
しかし、どんどん増えてゆく忍者を前にしては、簡単な事ではありません！
あなたは大事な密書を守れるでしょうか？　

「密書」が狙われている。
どんなに高い塀も、どんなに厳重な警備も、忍者の進入を阻む事は出来ない。侵入を許せば、不思議な術で部屋はたちまち忍者で溢れかえり、根こそぎ奪われてしまう事だろう。
忍者から密書をまもる為には、情報戦しかない。忍者をミスリードしてあなたの密書から遠ざけるのだ！
publishers: Chicken Dice Games (チキンダイスゲームズ)

description:
FRICO is the most popular game of the culture war that is ironically playful parochial controversy that often pits the city of Udine and Trieste on the market. The military confrontation-humorous takes shots of traditions and cultural elements characteristic of the two cities, the background scenario with a regional part of the municipalities of Friuli, Trieste across the districts, separated from Bisiacheria, neutral buffer zone. 
The aim is the conquest of the region's most sought-after in the world: the Friuli Venezia Giulia.

The Turn of each player consists of:

     Draw a card from their decks of belonging;
     Acquire reinforcements through the number of territories held;
     Moving a group of armies in another adjacent territory
     Attack other players using a simple rule of combat comparison the result of a dice for the attacker and one defender, the higher of the two wins. Players can stick with the desired frequency.
     Use the cards to take advantage or disadvantage the enemy.



FRICO' è il più popolare  gioco di guerra culturale che tratta con ironia la scherzosa diatriba campanilistica che spesso contrappone le città di Udine e Trieste disponibile sul mercato. Il confronto bellico-umoristico avviene a colpi di tradizioni ed elementi culturali caratteristici delle due città, sullo sfondo lo scenario regionale con da una parte i comuni friulani, dall'altra i rioni triestini, separati dalla Bisiacheria, zona cuscinetto neutrale. 
L'obiettivo è la conquista della regione più ambita al mondo: il Friuli Venezia Giulia.

Il Turno di ogni giocatore è composto da:

     Pescare una delle carte del proprio mazzo di appartenenza;  
     Acquisire rinforzi attraverso il numero di territori tenuto;  
     Spostamento di un gruppo di eserciti in un altro territorio adiacente
     Attaccare altri giocatori usando una semplice regola di combattimento di confronto il risultato di un dado per l’attaccante e uno per il difensore,il più alto tra i due vince. I giocatori possono attaccare con la frequenza desiderata. 
     Usare le carte  per avvantaggiarsi o svantaggiare il nemico.

User summary:
狼と七日間 (Ookami to Nanokakan) is a worker placement game, called Wolves and Seven Days in English.

In the game of Wolves and Seven Days, each player leads a clan of werewolves. They try to infiltrate a village and control it from inside.

There are six villagers in the village. You send your henchman werewolves into these villagers to control them. You gain victory points by controlling these villagers, and the highest scorer wins the game.

A game consists of seven rounds. Do the following each round:
1) Players play an initiative card simultaneously. The player with highest initiative value goes first. But if there's a tie for the initiative value, all tied players lose the opportunity to play this round. (In Japan, the mechanism that has the same cards or the tied values cancelling each other out is called "butting".)
2) Among non-tied players, in the initiative order, place your henchman on a villager. You may then activate that villager's special ability.

After seven rounds, for each villager, total your henchmen's influence points on that villager. You gain that villager's victory points if you have the highest influence points on that villager. But "butting" is applied here again; you cannot gain victory points if your influence points are tied with any other's.

Although this game is inspired by Werewolf, it is not a werewolf game. You send your henchmen in like worker placement, and determine the controller of each villager by majority of influence. And the "butting" and a little bit of bluffing are the keys to enjoying the game.

Publisher's summary:
「バッティング」とちょっとした「ブラフ」が楽しい人狼モチーフのカードゲームです！人狼一族をひきいて人間の村を支配することを目指します。
村には得点の異なる6つの地区（エリア）があり、自分の狼を配置していくことでその地区を支配することができます。
支配したエリアの合計得点が最も高いプレイヤーが勝利します。
また、各エリアにいる、特殊能力を持つ村人たちを扇動することで、さまざまな能力を利用することができます。
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
From the publisher's website:

Story Time Dice are back! Roll your way into a spooky and supernatural world of storytelling. These seven jumbo polyhedral dice illustrate 72 classic horror concepts and combine to roll over 6 million story combinations. And the best part? They glow in the dark!

Pit twelve intrepid heroes against twelve menacing monsters across eight scary settings. Overcome eight obstacles with the help of twenty tools, but stay wary of the six troublesome twists! Will your heroes survive? The answer is just a roll away!

If you need a little help getting started, every set of Story Time Dice: Scary Tales comes with a handy dice guide with 8 suggested games to play. They range from simple storytelling games for beginners to complex, turn-based party games with lots of rolling. There's even a special crossover game for owners of Story Time Dice  and Story Time Dice: Fairy Tale! Are you brave enough to play them all?

In Zauberstein players try to reach the center of four circular paths on the board. Players can only move from one circle to the next with the help of another player. A six sided dice determines the movement; if a player cannot make use of his dice throw he can donate it to another player, thus helping the ones behind. Nevertheless, there is a winner: the first to bring his 3 pawns (2 in a 6 player game) to the center wins.

See-Schlacht is a card game based on battleship. Each player receives a couple of empty sea cards, 3 single ships (red ship cards), 1 small fleet (2 brown ship cards) and 1 large fleet (3 yellow ship cards). First each player lays out his sea cards in a grid defined by the players (e.g. 4 x 5 cards). Then each player replaces secretly empty sea cards by his ship cards. Single ships are placed by its own, fleets need to be placed on adjacent spaces. In order to trick the oponents he can also replace empty sea cards with other empty sea cards. The game consits of players taking turns in naming a card in an apponents area (e.g. 3rd row, 4th card). The named card is flipped. As sea cards have identical sides players should try to remember which cards have been named. Each player whose ships are all turned face up are out of the game. The last player with hidden ships wins the game.

Players take turns removing marbles of the same colour from the board. You can take as many marbles of a single colour as you wish but all marbles need to be accessible, meaning they are free and not blocked by other marbles. The player with the most marbles at the end wins.

Ironsides is a moderate complexity game on the decisive battles of Oliver Cromwell and the “Ironsides” of the New Model Army at Preston in 1648 and Worcester in 1651 which won the Civil Wars and established the English Commonwealth. Philip Jelley uses the same area movement design as Lilliburlero: The Battle of the Boyne, July 1690 (ATO 40), with units representing the regiments and batteries that fought the battles. Each turn represents one or two hours of time, with weather and nightfall affecting command and control and unit activations. Scenarios are included for both battles with historical deployments, free-set-up, and for Preston a short introductory scenario and a three-day campaign game. There are optional rules for units that might have fought at the battles, and a random events table. Ironsides contains 216 counters, two maps (11” x 34” for Preston and 17” x 22” for Worcester) and a nine-page rulebook.

PRESTON 1648 King Charles I was defeated by Parliament in the First Civil War, but secretly made an Engagement with the Scottish Duke of Hamilton to restore him to the throne. John Lambert’s 4,000 Parliamentarians could only slow Hamilton’s 20,000 invading army as it joined 3,500 Royalists under Sir Marmaduke Langdale and marched through Lancashire. When Langdale encountered Lambert near Preston on 17 August 1648 it was dismissed as another delaying tactic, and Hamilton continued south across the River Ribble. What they did not know was that Oliver Cromwell had forced-marched 4,500 “Ironsides” of the New Model Army 300 miles to join Lambert. Langdale desperately tried to hold off the assault, but was overwhelmed, and Hamilton’s divided and disorganised army was destroyed in a three-day running battle. With the Royalists defeated the Army purged Parliament so they could bring “Charles Stuart, that man of blood, to an account for that blood he had shed”.  His trial was a forgone conclusion and the King and Hamilton were beheaded.

WORCESTER 1651 In 1650 the Scots invited King Charles II to succeed his father, so Cromwell invaded Scotland, destroying their army at Dunbar and taking Edinburgh. Charles II raised a new army, but with little chance of victory in Scotland he slipped past Cromwell and marched south to Worcester, hoping to rally English Royalists to his cause. Cromwell followed, joining the county militias raised by Fleetwood and Harrison, marching on the 16,000 Royalists with 31,000 men, intent on a battle of annihilation that would end the Civil Wars for once and for all. On 3 September 1651 Cromwell crossed the River Severn with a bridge of boats, dividing his army in order to encircle the Royalist army. Charles seized his chance and attacked the militia east of the river, but was repulsed, and the Ironsides chased his army through the bloody streets of Worcester, killing 3,000 and taking 13,000 prisoners. Charles fled to France, famously hiding in an oak tree to escape capture, while Cromwell would overthrow Parliament and make himself Lord Protector.

• Two Mapsheets (11″x34″ and 17”x22”) 
• One Countersheet (216 9/16″ counters) 
• One Rules Booklet 
• Two Players Aid Charts (one for each player)
• Two Generals Display Charts (one for each side) 
Two six-sided dice are required to play the game, but are not included.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Battlemind: A Game of Battle Strategy
description:
Battlemind is a medieval-themed battle strategy game in which two players, each with 10 character cards, try to capture/kill the other player's King. Inspired by the game from George R. R. Martin's A Song of Ice and Fire fantasy novels (HBO's Game of Thrones is based on this series), Battlemind combines the strategic elements of Chess, Stratego, and Blitzkrieg to create a an easy to learn but hard to master game with infinite replayability. This game is for anyone who loves strategy games - not just fans of the books!

What makes Battlemind special? Instead of being played like a typical card game (dealing/drawing), it is played more like a board game, with players setting up a "board" made of terrain cards and moving character cards across them.

Like chess, each character card has a certain number of spaces in which it can move. But that's where the similarities end. Each character has a strength value (1-8) which dictates whether it can capture/kill another character as well as certain advantages and disadvantages over other characters. From the lowly Rabble (rabble is like an angry mob) to the powerful Dragon, every unit has a purpose.  How you use them is up to you!

Finally, each terrain type can benefit or hinder your character. Use terrain to slow your enemy down, protect your fortress, and even hide from Dragons!

Ultimately, how you set up your terrain, arrange your army, and advance your units will determine whether you will be the victor or the vanquished. You'll laugh at your opponent when he places a crucial unit on his front line that you are able to capture easily, but will be crushed when that same placement allows him to capture your dragon on his first move in the next game!

Will you create a defensive stronghold and make your advesary come to you?  Or will you storm the front lines in hopes of catching your opponent off guard?

It's your decision.  The fate of the Kingdom rests on your shoulders. Fight for honor.  Fight for glory.  But remember: in the game of Battlemind, every move can mean victory or crushing defeat.

Expand your favorite Kaosball team's roster and boost their scoring potential with these brand new Kaosball Ringers. Ringers are powerful, all-star players that can fight, steal, score, and even use unique special abilities. Combine the right Ringer with your favorite team and unleash an unstoppable force onto the Kaosball field.

boardgame: E. Kensingworth Pinfield's New Game of Circuitous Meandering
description:
Subtitled: "an Adventure, From Beginning To the End.


        From the back of the box: "A Game of Luck, Chance, Serendipity, Fortune, and Happenstance, Intended as an Entertaining Amusement For the Youths and their Elders."


A roll and move game. There are 51 spaces, and all even spaces have a programmed instruction. Up to 6 players roll dice, move and follow the instructions until one player successfully arrives at the end space by exact count.

Many of the instructions are witty, silly or entertaining.

States Game is an educational dice game that teaches math skills and U.S. States. It is played with four six-sided dice, with the number one replaced by an outline of the United States.

States are numbered 1 to 50 based on their date of entry into the union. Each player picks a state, and then each player picks one state for each other player. In turn, players roll four dice, declaring which of their assigned states they're aiming for before they roll. Any United States pictures rolled are wild, and may be used as any number from 1 to 6. The player who rolled must then use any combination of addition, subtraction, multiplication and division to achieve the target state's number. If the player is successful, he or she earns a point.

The number or rounds is equal to the number of players, and players may not try for a state they've already scored. After each player has taken all his or her turns, the player with the most points wins.

If the game is tied, all non-tied players choose a state. Each tied player has one roll to get the state's number. This continues until one player wins.

A roll and move game for two players.  One player is the mammals, and the other the reptiles.  Players roll a special die to move their team of animals along a path to complete a journey to the other end of the path.  However the mammals are travelling the opposite direction of the reptiles.  No cohabitation of a space is allowed.  If two different animals arrive at the same space, one must restart their journey from the beginning.

The game uses special dice, and if the special icon is rolled, an animal must move one space backwards.  The board features a few "safe" spaces.  The game is won by advancing all of your mammals/reptiles across the path first.

Remainders Game is a math game, designed to help students learn division. The game is played with a six-sided die marked with the numbers 3 through 8. Each player has a board with 24 squares, each with a three-digit number. In turn, players choose a number, place a marked on it, and roll the die. The player then divides the chosen number by the rolled number. Any remainder is added to the player's score. After all number on each player's card have been marked, the player with the highest total score wins.

boardgame: Squeak, Squawk, Grunt, Whistle: the Animal Soundtrack Bingo Game
description:
Squeak, Squawk, Grunt, Whistle is a bingo derivative that teaches about wild animals and their sounds.  Each player gets a 3x3 board with pictures of various wild animals.  A cassette tape is played.  When players hear the sound of an animal on their card, they cover it with a marker.  The first player to cover all of his or her animals wins.  In order to make the game replayable, players may fast forward the tape to at random place to change the sequence of animal sounds. A list of the order of sounds is included so winners can be checked.

The other side of the tape identifies all the sounds so that players may learn to identify them before playing.

The year 2000, the year of the Dragon in the Chinese Zodiac, saw the re-launch of the Shadowfist game from publisher Z-Man Games.

The set consisted of five 50-card starter decks for each of the five main factions: the Dragons, the Eaters of the Lotus, the Ascended, the Guiding Hand, and the Architects of the Flesh.

Blurb from the starter box: "Shadowfist is the award winning Trading Card Game that's as fast paced as the Hong Kong action films it's based on. Enter a world where factions battle for control of Feng Shui Sites, mystical places that guarantee power and prosperity for those who control them."

This was a standalone reimplementation of the original Shadowfist. A version of the original is not needed to play. Many cards have been reprinted with fixes to wording for clarification and fixes to gameplay functions. However, it can be integrated with the original game with "play as printed" and "most recent printing" rules to resolve any conflicts.

boardgame: Cthulhu Wars: Great Old One Pack One
description:
Description from the publisher:

Cthulhu Wars: Great Old One Pack One adds five new Great Old Ones to Cthulhu Wars, and all five are available to each faction on a first come, first served basis. If you awaken Mother Hydra first, then no one else has access to her...unless they can kill her and reawaken her for themselves. Each Great Old One has its own special ability, plus its own unique spellbook, which is earned separately from your own.

• Abhoth is, of course, the horrible thing which is the source of all uncleanness. When you awaken Abhoth, you gain the power to place Filth tokens around the map. The Filth tokens "fight", participating in battles in their areas, and any damage they inflict goes onto both sides of the battle. However, since the Filth itself is immune, it gradually makes the world a more and more polluted cesspool.

• Chaugnar Faugn gives you the ability to alter the way the Elder Signs work (to your advantage). In addition, he provides you with a new action: Miri Nigri (cost 1) select an area in which you have units; a battle immediately begins there, and all your cultists in the area add +2 to their combat.

• Cthugha is difficult to get as you have to sacrifice a Great Old One, but it changes the way battle results work, allowing you to transform your kills rolled into Elder Signs. Of course, this spares your opponent, but hey, Elder Sign! Also, his spellbook, Fire Vampires, earns you Power for damage inflicted on YOU.

• Mother Hydra gives you the ability to sting your enemy out of the sea, reserving them for your own use. Her Zygote spellbook permits you to spawn multiple cultists at once at a low cost.

• Yig makes your cultists poisonous, so if they're eaten by enemies, those enemies die. In addition, when he earns his spellbook, each turn your foes must decide whether to donate power to you or have you earn doom points.

Boom Chaka Laka was an expansion set sold as boosters for the Shadowfist game. The set perfectly captured the style, movies, and music of the 1970s.

Blurb from the booster box: "The 1970s. It's cool! It's hip! It clashes! And the Secret Warriors find themselves clashing in a big way. Superfly martial artists, dirty cops, nefarious hoods - it's one big throwdown in San Franciso's Chinatown. So strap on your Saturday Night Specials and boogie on down to the fight, cause the Jammers are about to take Disco Inferno literally!"

Number Play is a card game designed to teach numbers, counting and basic math to young children.  There are direction for several activities and games included.  In addition to versions of Memory, Snap and Go Fish, there is  a game called Collect 10, which is a simple set collection game in which players must choose cards from faceup or facedown piles in order to try to collect a full run of numbers from 1 to 10.

boardgame: Get up and Go with the Power of Oats
description:
This game was printed on the back of a Honey Nut Cheerios box.  It is a "flip and move" game where players flip a coin to move along a track, collecting oats along the way.  Heads moves the player one space, tails two spaces.  Some spaces grant the characters a number of oats, from one to fifteen.  Some spaces move the player forward or cause loss of a turn.  The player who finishes with the most oats wins.

The rules do not specify if the game ends when one player reaches the end, or when all do, but it would make sense to end the game when the first player finishes, otherwise losing a turn would have no negative effects.

La Ruperta Fantasma ("The Ghost Ruperta") follows the rules of Battleship. There is a grid with lines and columns identified by letters and numbers. The contestants have to choose a spot from the grid. It could contain a "slip", a "sneak pumpkin" or the "Ghost Ruperta". Getting in a "slip" give the contestants a question. If they answer correctly they go on playing, if they fail you loose your turn The "sneak pumpkin" opens all the spots around the sneak. The "Ghost Ruperta" makes the contestants the winners. There is only a single Ghost Ruperta on the whole grid

boardgame: Hawaii (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
This map is an unofficial fan made expansion. Symbols & Graphics Copyright © 2004-2015 Days of Wonder, Inc. Produced with permission of Days of Wonder, for non-commercial use only.

This follows the principles of the basic Ticket to Ride game on a special board with special cards and rules. The object of the game is to gain the most points by travelling various routes and outwitting other players. You will need the train pieces and the train cards from either the US or Europe base games (or you can buy them from the Days of Wonder shop

There is an extra set of Chance cards: some are for the cities, each with a value to be earned by visiting that city: others give you extra facilities to complete your tickets. These cards are dealt/picked up alongwith tickets in the same quantity and with the same 'keep/dispose' rules.There are also up to 9 bonuses for the end-of-game scoring.

The board does not have any grey tracks or parallel tracks - colour choice and parallel routes are in the hands of the Gods. Thus there are God cards to help you.

There are many new options in the rules: exchange all your cards at the start but keep all the new ones: discard 5 cards and pick up 5 new ones, as your turn: earn double or triple points: and many more. You can also call time when your wagon stock reaches 3, if you want, giving you the advantage of stopping other players completing tickets.

boardgame: Huh, What Is in the Wardrobe?
description:
Somewhere in the world there lives a blue-haired monster who has had enough of scaring people and hearing their teeth chatter every time they see him. That is why we sometimes can't find the cloths we are certain that we put at the right place the day before. Can you remember what you have in your wardrobe? You can find out in this game! A tricky memory game with big and small and cards.

Common goal memory game. A card with several clothes is revealed. After memorizing it the players look at their own cards, and decide if they were represented on the common cards.

boardgame: Cthulhu Wars: Great Old One Pack Two
description:
Description from the publisher:

Cthulhu Wars: Great Old One Pack Two adds four new Great Old Ones to Cthulhu Wars, and all four are available to each faction on a first come, first served basis. If you awaken Father Dagon first, then no one else has access to her...unless they can kill her and reawaken her for themselves. Each Great Old One has its own special ability, plus its own unique spellbook, which is earned separately from your own.

• Atlach-Nacha has a special ability that allows you to place one web token in the area where it is located, and if you have a complete chain of web tokens across the map, you receive its spellbook and win the game.

• Bokrug doesn't vanish if killed, but instead demands that you place it on an area with no enemy units during the cleanup. The drawback is that one of your opponents removes one of your cultists from the map during cleanup as well. Thankfully, you can pass Bokrug to an opponent, thereby forcing the loss of a cultist onto that player instead of you.

• Father Dagon gives you an action that washes enemy units from the shore into the sea depths, and once you have his spellbook in play, you must sacrifice a cultist each turn in exchange for 6 power.

• Ghatanothoa mummifies enemy cultists, and his spellbook lets you do so even faster.

Ninja Camp: Badges and Activities is a mini-expansion that contains eight badge cards and twelve activity cards. Badge cards reward players for collecting the most of the associated card, while activities add a little more complexity to Ninja Camp by adding in a new rule for each game.

A co-operative game for 2-3 players.

The players have to work together to help Mia, Mo and Yuko to save the unicorns populating Centopia.

On their turn the player rolls up to 4 dice setting aside all symbols of the evil Panthea and choose one action fom the remaining dice (mainly moving the characters). Next player can roll only the dice not set aside. If all dice are locked another Munculus is added to the board assigned to guard a unicorn of matching colours. To save a unicorn the players have to match the number of Munculi guarding the unicorn. If there are ever 4 Munculi guarding a unicorn players lose, otherwise they win if all unicorns are saved.

One Page Kill Team is a skirmish game designed to be played with just a handful of miniatures. The game features dynamic mechanics which allow you to jump onto your foes from ledges or kick them into deadly terrain, making for an action packed skirmish.

One Page Fantasy is a strategy battle game designed to be played with WHFB miniatures. The game offers simple mechanics based around alternating phases, allowing you to play highly tactical games of regimental warfare.

One Page Skirmish is a single-paged ruleset designed to be played with just a handful of miniatures. With simple combat mechanics and a dynamic campaign, the game serves as a great way to explore the world of Warhammer Fantasy.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Hammer Wars is a small-scale miniatures wargame inspired by RTS video games. In an alternate universe where battleships from the 41st millennium have crashed on the warhammer world, get ready to fight where fantasy meets sci-fi.

Army Men Combat is a fast and simple ruleset designed for the nostalgic among us, perfect as an introduction to wargaming for young ones. Dust off your old army men, improvise some terrain, and get ready to fight with toy soldiers like you never have before.

Lazy Jack Visual Aids Game is a series of picture and question cards that can be used to play a variety of simple educational games.  Here's what's included:

     1 to 24 Number Game for Beginners: Players match "story cards" with scenes from a story to number cards to try to make the most matches
     Speedy Alphabet Game: Players take turns searching their cards for the letters of the alphabet in sequence, passing when then fail to find the next letter. The player who finds Z wins.
     Family Word Games: Each player chooses and reads a card, then asks the next player to spell one word on the card. The player to spell the word correctly keeps the card. The player with the most cards wins.
     Family Picture Games: In turn, players read the text on one card. The next player must name one character depicted on the card. Another player may challenge by calling out "Lazy Jack" and naming a different character. The correct player wins the card. Most cards wins.
     Family Questions and Answer Game: Each player has a stack of picture cards. Players in turn ask a question from a question card. The player who is able to find the answer on a picture card answers and takes the card. Most cards wins.
     Party Game No. 1 and Party Game With Props: Players choose "party game" cards and perform a challenge. Other players vote on best performances.
     A.E.I.O.U Game: Each player draws a card and must find all five vowels to win the card. Most cards wins.
     Name a Person, Place or Thing Game: Player chooses a card and names a color that appears on the card. Other players in turn try to guess which item of the color the player referred to. Correct guesser keeps the card.  Most cards wins.
     Picture Matching Game: Shuffle 24 each of black and white and color picture cards together. Players take turns drawing a card, trying to match b/w and color cards. Player with the most matches wins.
     Family Reading Game: Color story card are dealt to players.  Corresponding black/white cards are turned up one at a time. Player with the matching card reads the card's caption. If read correctly, the player claims the card. Other players may challenge and then read the card.  Whoever reads the card correctly claims it.  Most cards wins.


- Family Change a Word Game: Black/white picture cards are dealt to players.  Color cards in turn are turned face-up. The player with the matching card reads the caption, but changes a word to change the meaning of the card.  There is no competition involved, so this last "game" is the only one that is not really a game.

Do you like duck! duck! GO! but you have younger children that love the ducks? duck! duck! GO! Junior is a 16-card expansion for the beloved rubber-ducky racing game. These cards are an alternative deck for use with younger players. The cards ease players into the game by including numbers instead of hex space symbols. As players become used to the game, they can gradually switch to increasingly complex hex space cards. The duck! duck! GO! Junior deck is a great way to introduce players to your favorite rubber ducky game!

1 blue dolphin
18 coloured rings (small,medium and large in red, orange, yellow, blue, green and purple)
 * The small rings are able to be placed inside the large rings
1 coloured dice

The dolphin is placed on the table with it's back facing down, like a see-saw. The 18 rings lie next to it. Players take turns to roll the dice and place a ring of the required colour on the dolphin's tummy. The player that makes rings fall off the dolphin must drop our. The remaining players then continue to play without the eliminated player and the fallen rings become part of the game again.

1 frog
6 yellow and 12 green cylinders
1 dice with symbols

The object of the game is to place the cylinders on the frog without the animal tipping over or one of the cylinders falling off.

Rolling the dice determines the symbol on the end of the cylinder which is to be placed on the frog. A player that causes a cylinder to fall during their turn is out of the match. The winner is the player who put the last, and hence the most difficult, cylinder on the frog without knocking it over.

1 Cat
18 Balls of thread
4 reels of thread
1 coloured dice

The object of the game is to place the brightly-coloured balls or blocks on the cat without the animal tipping over or one of the balls falling off.
Rolling the dice determines the colour ball which is to be placed on the cat. Each player takes turns throwing the dice, moving in a clockwise direction.
 A player that causes a ball to fall during their turn is out of the game. The winner is the player who places the last ball of thread on top of the pile without causing anything to dislodge and fall or who is the last player to remain in the game.

1 Ark
12 animals (in 6 pairs)
1 dice with symbols

The object of the game is to place the animals on the Ark without the boat tipping over or one of the animals falling off.
A dice with symbols is rolled to determine which animal should be placed on the Ark.
If a player causes pieces to fall off the boat, then the player is out of the match.
The winner is the player who puts the last piece on the Ark without knocking it over

boardgame: Frostgrave: Thaw of the Lich Lord
description: Thaw of the Lich Lord is a complete campaign for Frostgrave that will challenge both new and veteran players. Through a series of linked scenarios, players discover the existence of a new power in the Frozen City, one who was old when the great city was still young, and who saw both its rise and its disastrous fall. Warbands will confront the Lich Lord's minions, race against his agents to seize possession of mysterious artefacts, and brave the perils of Frostgrave in search of his lair. Eventually, they will need to muster all their courage to venture into the depths of the city and face the Lich Lord himself. Not all wizards will seek to stop the Lich Lord, however, and full rules for giving into his corruption and following the dark road to becoming an undead lich are presented for those who crave power and immortality above all else. While the campaign presents many new threats against which wizards and their warbands must test themselves, including an expanded bestiary, it also offers additional resources, such as new henchmen that can be recruited and unique magical treasures that can spell the difference between survival and oblivion.

With this new supplement for Frostgrave, players lead their warbands into the vast network of catacombs, sewers, and dungeons that run beneath the Frozen City. It was in these dark confines that ancient wizards experimented on living creatures, creating strange hybrids and deadly monsters, many of which still roam the forgotten passageways. Along with a host of new scenarios, treasures, spells, and creatures, the book also contains rules for running games of Frostgrave set in dungeons, including traps and secret passages. With the wonderful and rare magical treasures to be discovered, will players risk taking their warbands down into the Breeding Pits?

During the Depression, the streets of Retrocity were controlled by the iron hand of Don Picolotto ... But he died. Now, four families are fighting each other to control the organized crime in the city.

O Gângster de Retrocity is a competitive game for 2-4 players where each one assumes the role of a mafia boss that seeks to dominate the crime of Retrocity.

Only one player can become the big boss and to achieve this goal, each player must create a strategy by joining board movement, card's special effect and attacks on their rivals.

The game uses modular boards, which allows the city to have a different setting every new play.

The deck of 60 cards is divided in three types: Action, Equipment and Building. All cards have a special effect and Action and Equipment cards can be used in combat against a rival.

The length of a game is 30 to 60 minutes and as players become accustomed to the rules, the game becomes increasingly wild!

Arena: Blood &Glory is a competitive game for 2 players where each player, who roleplays a Gladiator, seeks to win his opponent to achieve fame and recognition or perhaps freedom!

The game uses a deck of 54 cards (divided into Combat, Events and Equipment cards), a board, two tokens and seven damage markers.

To win, the player need to cause 4 damage in the opponent. For his, the player uses board movement and cards management (the cards can be used to attack, defense and special effects).

Arena: Sangue & Glória é um jogo competitivo entre 2 jogadores onde, cada jogador interpretando um Gladiador, busca vencer o seu oponente para alcançar fama e reconhecimento ou, quem sabe, liberdade!

O jogo utiliza um baralho de 54 cartas (dividido em cartas de combate, eventos e equipamento), um tabuleiro, dois peões e sete marcadores de dano.

O objetivo é causar 4 de dano no oponente e para isso o jogador usa movimento em tabuleiro com uso de cartas (que servem para ataque, defesa e efeitos especiais).

LONG DESCRIPTION

Everything or almost everything is possible in this game, based on the theme of World History figures from 13 important areas: governors, the military, astronomers, philosophers, scientists, explorers, mathematicians, pioneers, writers, painters, musicians, Nobel Prizes and Human Rights.

HOW TO PLAY
- EUREKA (GAME 1)

OBJECTIVE
It is played by two teams of two players. The aim of the game is to gather 4 people from one of the 13 sets: the military, astronomers, etc…
When the player gathers the 4 cards they secretly signal their partner who then says EUREKA.

Moving forward...

1. At the beginning of each round, turn 4 cards over (from the deck) on the table. 
2. All together, players count up to 3. 
3. After finishing the count, each player can switch the cards held in the hand with the ones on the table (he/she can switch as many cards as desired). 
4. A round ends when players do not wish to exchange more cards. 
5. The 4 cards on the table from the previous round are discarded. Turn 4 new cards over from the deck and repeat the procedures from step 2 forward.

- FAMILY GAME (GAME 2)

OBJECTIVE
Gather as many Families as possible. Each Family is composed of 4 characters. Each character is associated to a colour: green, red, yellow and blue.

Moving on…

Step 1

The first player asks another player for a card of his/ her choice. This request must respect the following rules:

     the player already has a card of this Family;
     the player must always say: first, the name of the Family to which the card belongs; then, the colour associated with the desired character: green, red, yellow or blue.


Step 2

One of the following options occurs:


     if the player succeeds in getting the asked card: then, he/ she asks a player for a new card;
     if the player cannot get the asked card: he/ she picks up a playing card from the deck placed on the centre of the table and stops playing. It is the next player’s turn.
A single tile promo for Neuroshima Hex given to members of the Shut Up and Sit Down Gold Club. The tiles allows players to send the mighty reference pear into battle on their behalf.

Monster Mix consists of two games: "Monster Mix" and "Monster Express", both of which use cards that show monsters and cards that show only the top, middle or bottom of a monster.

In "Monster Mix", players each hold a monster card in hand and take turns adding monster part cards to the table, trying to assemble the parts they need to match the card they hold. If they do, they score 1 point and take a new monster card. Whoever scores 3 points first wins.

In "Monster Express", all of the monster cards are laid face up on the table, and the top, middle and bottom cards are placed nearby as three decks. One card from each deck is revealed, then players race to grab the monster card that matches the combination of cards shown.

Description from the publisher:

Pirates Attack is a collaborative strategy game in which two players face the pirates together. The first player to neutralize the pirate threat is the winner.

Pirates will attack your ship, which means that you must be on your guard to obtain the best possible results as you play.

boardgame: Backup Storage War: Exagrid Vs. EMC Data Domain
description: Very simple roll and move game that while you play tries to convince you that Exagrid's backup solution is better than EMC's.

Description from the publisher:

Look, react, swipe to win! Take a swipe at speedy victory with this addictive game of high energy and fast hands! When the chips are down in Swipe Out!, be the first to swipe yours off the board to match the pattern on the Swipe Out card. Win the card to win the round. Collect five Swipe Out cards to win the game!

Time's Up: Title Recall – Expansion 1 contains more cards for use with the Time's Up: Title Recall base game.

Based on the popular game Time's Up!, Time's Up: Title Recall challenges players to guess the titles of books, films, songs, and more. Players try to get other players to guess the same set of titles over three rounds. In each round, one member of a team tries to get his teammates to guess as many titles as possible in 30 seconds. In round 1, almost any kind of clue is allowed. In round 2 no more than one word can be used in each clue (but unlimited sounds and gestures are permitted.) In round 3, no words are allowed at all.

Time's Up: Title Recall – Expansion 2 contains more cards for use with the Time's Up: Title Recall base game.

Based on the popular game Time's Up!, Time's Up: Title Recall challenges players to guess the titles of books, films, songs, and more. Players try to get other players to guess the same set of titles over three rounds. In each round, one member of a team tries to get his teammates to guess as many titles as possible in 30 seconds. In round 1, almost any kind of clue is allowed. In round 2 no more than one word can be used in each clue (but unlimited sounds and gestures are permitted.) In round 3, no words are allowed at all.

description:
Description from the publisher:

In Picmix, each player has a set of four cards on which are shown sixteen items. Each turn, a goal card with three or four of these objects is revealed. Be the fastest to position your cards in such a way that only the objects appearing on the target card are visible. Keep your cool to find the right combination first.

Budō is a two-player mixed martial arts card game that challenges each player to manage a dojo of martial arts skills while striving to knockout the opponent. Learn to manage a dojo in ten minutes then spend a lifetime mastering the martial way. Adapt your mat position and craft a deck of attack and defense skills to combat your opponent. Balancing tactics and strategy, play the player across the tables as much as the dojos.

Players balance the use of Stamina and Experience Points to build a deck of attack and defense cards from their dojo of skills in order to adapt to an opponent's evolving strategy. Holds create an opening for an attack and often work in combination with other moves. Stuns break an opponent's rhythm leaving her without enough stamina to pull of offensive combos. Takedowns are advanced moves that put the opponent on the mat defenseless.

The linear mat component allows players to control distance to avoid damage and get in range to strike their opponent providing an additional level of tactics and strategy.

Lasting about 30 minutes, no two fights are ever the same. With each new bout, new strategies emerge

Hashtag Me! is an interactive storytelling game for small to medium groups. Everyone has their favorite stories to tell, but some stories have been buried over years of neglect. It’s time to dust off those old pages and share ‘em. A set of Ninja throwing stars you got for Christmas when you were 12? An excursion into the snowy wastelands in a beat up old car? Tell your stories and other players will interact by hashtagging your story!

Everyone starts with 6 hashtag cards in their hand. Players take turns being the storyteller. The storyteller draws two question cards and chooses which question to tell their story about. Once the story has begun, the rest of the players lay down hashtag cards during the story as they become relevant. At the end of the story, points are awarded and the role of storyteller passes to the next person.

It's a fun party game that's easy to pick up. It’s great for an icebreaker or as a way to get to know your friends and family better.

Raise the curtain for the funny freak show with cute creatures for little monster fans!

In MonStars, 45 colorful "MonStars" are crowding the stage, ready for the big show. Only an absolutely distinctive style will leave a lasting impression with the jury. Who will be among the Top 10? Each jury member holds a colored card set. By overlapping the cards, details can be combined so that different MonStars are created. Everyone plays simultaneously and tries to copy the candidate shown on the current card.

One thousand expressions, a small board with a rubber band, an erasable pen — and time is running out!

You have to think back to front in this latest variant of the popular party game. Activity Krazy Kritzel offers a new challenge even for weathered Activity veterans. Blind and back to front, they have to give a shape to expressions on a board they wear on their foreheads. Scribblings or art? It doesn't matter...as long as it's recognizable!
A lot of people don't know how awesome and complete the Japanese list of animal sounds is. Rabbits, dragonflies, and foxes actually say something in Japanese, and their sounds will be on the cards!

This deck will help players learn Japanese kanji and gain points by building words made up of several characters. The kanji's meaning and dictionary-style examples of each character will be in the rules document, as will many word-building options and the bar codes to scan for pronunciation. So the cards will have full functionality, and will definitely still be usable by less advanced learners with some effort, but the card faces will be minimalist, focused on the beautiful art of written Japanese.

Speaking to children requires a kind of Japanese that is both loving and authoritative. The Children Expansion will teach you words you don't use when talking to adults... unless they're acting like children (which happens). It will also help you tell them to sit still, play nice, take turns, and brush their teeth.

boardgame: Japanese: The Game – Household Items Expansion
description: People love learning vocabulary that's easy to remember, which is why one of the most requested decks is a Household Items Expansion. You'll learn the words for things you see every day so you can repeat them in your head. You could even place the Fridge card on the fridge with a magnet. But don't put the waste basket card in the waste basket.

boardgame: Japanese: The Game – Martial Arts: White Belt Expansion
description: Words valuable in martial arts will be included for study and hilarity. Mixing and matching it with other expansions can result in glorious sentences like "If Godzilla attacks, our honorable grandfather will roundhouse kick him into an office building."

Japanese culture has a strong social structure that is reflected in their language. You use different pronouns and verbs when referring to people of a higher or lower social level. The Intermediate Expansion: Respect will include names, titles, and verbs with varying degrees of respect to help you speak appropriately in different situations.

Contains nouns like Arm, Shoulder, Leg, Neck, and verbs like Turn, Reach, and Point. This is very useful vocabulary and the explanations and example sentences will help you make sure you're using them properly.
Counting is a little different in Japanese and this expansion deck will help you learn your numbers and how to count people, cards, bikes, books, and other every-day objects!

In the dexterity game Universe, up to four players use marbles, dice and disks as ships and flick, roll, or spin their way into the Universe. It's shuffleboard, pinball, marbles, crokinole, craps, bocci ball, and even a bit of golf — but with the fingers — synchronized into one.

You can go for points, planet ownership, or the black hole, and whoever has the most points at the end of a set number of rounds wins.

Universe includes two additional mini games, giving you three games in one.

The Suicide Deck is an expansion to Disturbed Friends that contains 40 Question Cards, 8 unique #WINNING cards, and a set of 4 bloody player cards.

It was initially available through Kickstarter as an addon to the Disturbed Friends game.
The Booster Pack for Disturbed Friends was achieved as stretch reward through Kickstarter. It contains 12 Question cards, 3 #WINNING cards, and an additional 3 Kickstarter #WINNING cards.

Disturbed Friends: Mini Game / Expansion is both a small version of Disturbed friends for 4 players that can be played stand alone or mixed with the base game Disturbed Friends and/or previously released expansions such as the Suicide Deck or the Booster Pack.

It contains 90 cards: 4 sets of player cards, 20 #WINNING cards, and 56 Question cards.

Object:
Travel through various biblical eras (primeval history to early Christianity) answering questions about people of the Bible that address the who, where, when, what, how, and why of their places in salvation history. Players determine length of time and game play by choosing from any of the designated starting and stopping points on the board--the Garden of Eden, Egypt, the Promised Land, the Temple--with New Jerusalem as the final destination. The team or individual that collects the most character cards wins the game. Board game includes a free Bible!

This game is designed to be played two different ways:

Learn-as-you-play:
If you're new to the Bible, the learn-as-you-play mode will help you get acquainted with Breakthrough! The Bible for Young Catholics and all the wonderful stories, characters, and lessons it holds. When faced with questions, you'll be free to look up the answers in the Bible.

Test-your-knowledge:
If you're more experienced, you'll be able to test what you know about the Bible by answering the questions from memory with the test-your-knowledge mode of play.

Karawanix challenges players to climb atop their dromedaries and make their way through the desert together, enduring sand storms and mirages on their way to the oasis.   The goal of the game is to bring your camel first to the oasis. To do this, you will have to complete several missions based on the roll of the dice.

This game helps develop touch, auditory memory, visual perception and tactile recognition. A game very often used by speech therapists.

boardgame: Till Eulenspiegel: Schabernack mit Schuhen und Schalk
description:
In Till Eulenspiegel: Schabernack mit Schuhen und Schalk, the playful tightrope act stimulates the brains of acrobats both large and small, with players trying to pick out matching pairs from 32 motley shoes. The first player to collect four or five pairs of shoes wins.

Till Eulenspiegel includes three game modes: a memory game, "Till pilfers", and the quick-playing "criss-cross".

A Game of Capital Gains!

Invest in the Stock Market!
Buy and Sell Real Estate!
Beware!
Money Loves Nobody!
Wallets Grow while Pockets Empty!

The Millionaires' Club is a game for 2-5 Players taking about an hours worth of time to play.

Games are played in Rounds, with Players taking Turns within each Round. A Round starts with every Player collecting a crisp One Million Dollar Bill.

Players then take turns engaging in intriguing business decisions; Competing over ownership of the largest share in various companies, as well as, Buying and Selling Real Estate Properties.

The Millionaires' Club will make you feel like a wealthy entrepreneur in no time!

After every Round, the Starting Player rolls Two 'Market Dice', One Red; One Blue. The Red affects the Stock Market: A Low Roll can Crash a Company, while a High Roll Skyrockets Stock Values. The Blue manipulates the Real Estate Property Market: A High Roll Creates a Bubble of economic prosperity, gaining Players a bonus Cash Pay Out! from the Bank. Reversely, a Low Roll will Burst the Bubble; Forecloseing Properties, costing Players their hard earned money.

Afterwards, the Starting Player shifts Clockwise to the next Player, Starting a new Round.

The Game continues, Round after Round, with Players Conducting Business and Rolling the Dice. The Player with the highest valued Estate at the End of the Game Wins!

This game focuses on making friends, being a good friend, and resolving disagreements in a win-win way. The game is designed so that players cooperate and help each other as well as answer questions about friendship to win points for themselves. The game also helps shy and socially delayed students explore friendship issues. Features two different games that use the same board but have separate questions. The second game involves more strategy, harder questions, more advanced vocabulary, and more opportunities for negotiation.

A “Teachers Guide” with complete lesson plans for both versions of the game is included. Students practice reading skills (reading the cards) and math skills (handling the point certificates). Students are challenged to use critical thinking in answering questions and in forming their strategies for playing the game. During the post-game discussion students link their experience in the game with real life.

Includes game board, six game tokens, one die, Making-a-Friend cards, Good Times cards, Solving Problems cards, and instructions

Knights and Knaves is a supplement for the Battlesworn: Bid for Victory miniatures rules.

18 new Classes: Tested against the basic classes of Battlesworn: Bid For Victory, Knights and Knaves adds rules for Acrobat, Alchemist, Assassin, Bard, Beast, Berserker, Chaos Warrior, Construct, Druid, Giant, Monk, Paladin, Pistoleer, Priest, Ranger, Swarm, Witch, and Witch Hunter.

More Magic: This book adds generic magic melee and ranged weapons, Talismans, Amulets, Potions, and 12 new spells, all formatted as cards that can be printed or photocopied.

Solo Play: Guidelines for solitaire play are included.

Description from the publisher:

A tremendous earthquake has diverted the raging river that has separated the Ablas and Ublas for decades, reuniting the two tribes. The only problem is that each tribe's language has changed over the years, and they cannot understand each other. Queen Abla and King Ubla, needs you work together in a series of crazy challenges in order to communicate!

“Abla Ubla!” is a family/party game for 4-12 players, for ages 10+. The duration of the game is approximately 30’ minutes. Players form 2 teams, in which they write down words and try to describe them to their teammates as quickly as possible, but must abide by the rules and the challenges of the Abla-ublas, like speaking with their tongue hanging out their mouth or keep their teeth clenched together while talking, in order to work out what on earth their team-mates are trying to describe! The game contains 85 Category cards that can be erased when filled up and 15 Challenge cards that will keep players excited for a long time! There are 2 modes that we can use the Challenge cards, for even greater replayability. The team with the most Victory Points wins the game. So get ready to Twist your tongue and tangle your words!

Sipario! is a card and strategy game set in the glittering world of opera.

Get ready to stage the most dazzling and prestigious opera season
ever! To achieve this, you will have to engage the most
renowned singers, put up with Prime donne, tantrums, invest
in the best advertising, hire paparazzi, pay the claque, and most
importantly throw spanners in the works of competing theater
managers. After you receive the titles of the three operas you
are going to stage, start opposing anyone who tries to stop you
from conquering the Hall of Fame!
Sipario! is a card and strategy game for 2-5 players, where each
player thinks of ways to outsmart their opponents and at the
same time advance their own plans. The only thing you should
NOT have, to be the best in the business, is scruples…
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
In the dice-based game Rocket Lander, players are challenged to plot a graph on a three-dimensional axis, with them escorting their ships, searching and overtaking opponents, and defending their home turf.

In more detail, each player controls a group of rockets exploring the vast world and outer space, and during play they plot information on an X, Y and Z-axis, then interpret that info to draw conclusions about what to do next.

A Murder Most Fowl is a peculiar card game about crypto communication and people reading.

In A Murder Most Fowl, 2 or 3 teams of 2-players each (which means the game is played by either 4 or 6 players) compete against each other to solve the murder of Colonel Robin Poor, who is a bird of international importance.

3 investigation agencies will be racing each other to solve the murder, or to steal the solution from other agencies. Play either as Charlotte Holmes and Vet Watson from Dogland Yard, Agent Murder and Agent Scurry from Federal Beavers of Investigation (FBI, for short) or Inspector Purrouseau and Queen Cat'O from Brigade Félinationale de la Sécurité.

Before a game session, the teams meet in secret and devise strategies for crypto communication. The teams can use body language, singing, humming, tell obscure stories, remind each other of past memories, speak unintelligibly, in numbers or form sentences that make no sense at all to the other teams –but hopefully passes information amongst themselves.

To call a murder, the teams will need crypto-communication, since callling a murder involves combining a murder scenario with a suspect, a location and a weapon as a team. However the other team(s) must be unaware of your selections since the solution could very well be stolen from you.

The game is loosely inspired by the English nursery rhyme "Who Killed Cock Robin" – especially Henry Louis Stephens’ 1865 book version which is named; "Death and Burial of Poor Cock Robin".

Two players try to destroy each other's Core in a highly strategic battle by employing creatures, spells and dungeon upgrades.

Creatures can have up to three of eighteen special properties, such as Coward (roll a d20 to determine the success of an attack), but also Mug (steal Gold on attack). Spell Cards add one-time actions to potentially turn the game. Dungeon Upgrades not only provide utility (such as sacrificing cards to gain resources), but also let the player shuffle new cards through his deck.

Key novelty: build your deck while playing the game. This allows for a dynamic and strategic gameplay.

Frantic Flip
12 Double-sided animal tiles and 1 board game! Which side of the tile to use to fill out the board? Sounds simple! you will be surprised! A challenging and fun game for the fast of sight and mind.

1. Each player receives 12 double sided animal tiles.
2. The game board is flipped and then each player tries to place all of his tiles on the designated spot on the board, filling it out.
3. Pay attention! the tiles are double sided so you might havet to switch sides to fill out the board! Be fast so you don’t waste time! whoever places all of his tiles first wins the game.

A day at the track is a Grid Movement/Dice rolling 9 card game about horse racing. The game is made up of 9 double-sided cards that consist of different sections of the race track. As you move around the track you will move these card section around to produce a full race track.  Each player has his own d6 and two tokens; one to represent his/her horse on the track and the other to represent the Jockey Boost token.

Components:
[9 cards] - on [1] A4 letter size Double sided PDF sheet 
[1] - A4 Letter size Double sided PDF sheet - Rules
[4] - d6 dice [1 Green, 1 Blue, 1 Yellow and 1 Red]
[8] - Wood cubes - [2 Green, 2 Blue, 2 Yellow and 2 Red]

GAME PLAY:
It's racing day is played in turns with each player rolling his/her d6 and moving his horse token the same number of spaces, as rolled on the d6, around the track.  The player with the lowest die roll moves his/her horse token first, which also means that player could roll the lowest die and still cross the finish line before a player with a higher dice roll!

On each turn a player can decide to use one of his two Jockey Boost actions, which would allow the player to reroll his/her d6.  If a player decides to take this action the move their Jockey Boost token on the 1st token space and rerolls their d6 die.  The player MUST use this new roll, even if it is less than the previous roll, to move his/her horse token around the track.  Each player is only allowed a maximum of two Jockey boosts per game.

CROSSING THE FINISH LINE:
As the players get closer to the finish line each die roll becomes critical.  Since the player with the lowest die results moves first, this player could cross the finish line before a player who is closer but rolls a higher number!

If two or more players roll the same number they move there pieces at the same time.  If those two or more players can all cross the finish line with their die results, the player that is closest to the finish line will cross first followed by the next closest player and so on.

The BLUE player is one space away from winning.  The RED player is two spaces away from winning and the GREEN and YELLOW players are both three spaces away from winning.  The following dice results are rolled: BLUE=5, GREEN=3, RED =3, and YELLOW=1.  Since the YELLOW player rolled the lowest die roll he/she moves first but still doesn't cross the finish line.  Since the BLUE player rolled the highest die roll he moves last.  Now we come to the RED and GREEN players who both rolled 3, which means they move their horse tokens at the same time.  Since the RED player is one space closer to the finish line, he/she ends up crossing the finish line one length ahead of the GREEN player.  The results so far are RED player comes in 1st, the GREEN player comes in 2nd, and the BLUE and YELLOW players still need to take another turn to finish the race.

ADVANCED GAME PLAY:
It's racing day can also be played with an ADVANCED race track layout by using the Hedge cards.  Hedge cards add an hazardous obstacle to the game.  Whenever a player tries to jump over a HEDGE space and they roll a 5 or 6, their speed is too fast, causing their horse to stumble and slow down.  The player must immediately stop their horse token directly beyond the hedge space and lose their next turn.   A player could avoid this roll by using their Jockey Boost action but if the second roll is low enough to prevent them from reaching the hedge, they would still have to roll again on the next turn to try and clear the hedge.

WINNING THE GAME:
To win the game a player's horse token must be the first to pass the finish line before any other player!  As each player passed the finish line they will place their horse token in the corresponding winners box.  Each player continues to roll his d6 until he passes the finish line and his/her horse token is placed in one of the four winners boxes.

Never put a Dark Elf and an Orc in the same room — unless you're playing Epic PvP: Fantasy, that is! With Epic PVP: Fantasy Expansion 1, you can add the devious Dark Elf and the easily peeved Orc races to your games of Epic PvP: Fantasy as well as the disciplined Monk and the brutal Barbarian classes.
