These new and unique decks are fully compatible with all previous Epic PvP: Fantasy decks, allowing for some powerful (and sometimes ridiculous) new character combinations.

Get back to the arena as the battle is on!

A small expansion that can be added to Xenoshft: Onslaught or the  Xenoshyft: Dreadmire.

This hellscape world’s inhabitants have evolved to survive it’s blazing temperatures, and the Xenosathem NorTec craves has only made the aliens a bigger threat. These new foes are known as the Cinderkin and are ready to burn NorTec’s efforts to the ground. A new Fire Tracker is being introduced that will increase enemy abilities and threaten the very base itself as the fire progresses.

Of course, NorTec won’t just stand idly by. It’s developed some new special items that will help on this terrible world. These Emergency Supply Items differ from the equipment you’re familiar with because they are available to everyone at the start of the game and only come with one charge, requiring resources to be put into them to recharge.

Xenoshyft: Immolation comes with the following:

52 Wave 1 Cinderkin Enemies
52 Wave 2 Cinderkin Enemies
52 Wave 3 Cinderkin Enemies
3 Emergency Supply Item Cards
1 Fire Tracker Card
1 Plastic Fire Counter

Board football team management game. Matches, players trading, random and non-random events determine the ultimate champion.
The players starts witn an amount of 40 million euros and obtain players with a range of 5-9 ability. As more the ability as much the money needed for buying. After that the players (if want) expand the capacity of their soccer field so they can sell more tickets on the home matches they play. The match is played by one dice and the winner gets 3 points instead of 1 for tie and 0 for lose. The winner is the player who gets winning points with the follow system .
10 winning points for the champion
5 winning points for the second place in the championship
6 winning points forr the Cup winner
2 winning points for the Cup finalist.
0,5 points for every 1.000.000 remain in the final treasury of the team.
In the middle of the season the players can change and axchange players.

Also there are random facts with cards that can change the rank of the players.

Επιτραπέζιο παιχνίδι διαχείρισης ποδοσφαιρικής ομάδας. Αγώνες, αγοραπωλησίες παικτών, τυχαία και μη γεγονότα καθορίζουν τον τελικό πρωταθλητή.

Giocco dal tavolo gestione della squadra. Giochi, negoziazione giocatore, casuali e non casuali eventi determinano il campione finale.

boardgame: Japan (fan expansion for Ticket To Ride)
description:
Ticket to Ride™: Japan is a fan-made expansion to the Days of Wonder, Inc. Ticket to Ride™ series created by Alan R. Moon. A tribute both to Ticket to Ride™ and the country of Japan, this expansion is designed for players who are already well familiar with Ticket to Ride™’s standard gameplay and mechanics.

Published by permission for non-commercial use. All rights to the Ticket to Ride™ name, gameplay, mechanics, and imagery are reserved by Days of Wonder, Inc., who have not endorsed or playtested this expansion.

"Welcome to Question time! A game of Australian politics, political history, intrigue and rat cunning. Every player has a chance to win whether you have been cabinet minister, a student of political science, a member of the press, a regular punter or just voted in your first election. This is politics, the idea is not to be nice to your opponents but to beat them - at any cost - just like the real thing!"--Box.

A mix of trivia game, hand management and strategy, 2 or more players or teams move around the board (the seating layout of the Chamber) using dice (if on the Backbench) and a spinner (if on the Frontbench). The aim of the game is to present three Acts to parliament. An Act is three cards of a particular type, gained when on the Frontbench. When on the Backbench, players also draw Question Time and Stuffups & Scandals cards (general knowledge), Party Room and Press Gallery cards (luck), and filibuster cards (must speak for 60 seconds for or against the motion on the card).

Dinner's Ready! is a family culinary card game promoting healthy eating by challenging players to collect recipes and their necessary ingredients.

Players are dealt 5 recipe cards. The objective is to collect a "balanced meal:" an appetizer, an entree, and a dessert that together contain one ingredient from each of the food groups (fruits, vegetables, grains, meat, dairy). Players choose their "best" three and discard the others.

The objective is to collect the ingredients to make your appetizer, then the entree, then the dessert. You must go in order!

Each player is dealt four ingredient cards. On their turn, players may ask for a particular ingredient from another player ("Go Shopping"), pick up the top ingredient card on the "Compost Pile," or draw a replacement Recipe card.

The first player to successfully prepare their "balanced meal" wins, exclaiming "dinner's ready!"

The goal of the game is to educate players on the versatility of healthy ingredients, the basic concepts of cooking, and fun facts about food. If children are exposed to food at an early age, they are more likely to eat healthier as adults.

Who says you can't play with your food?

The Combat In Kowloon set, from new publisher Inner Kingdom Games, saw a change in the venerable Shadowfist card game. First, it is no longer sold as randomised boosters. Instead it is a Dynamic Card Game: sets are sold as fifty card decks. Second, it removed the two-player option (dueling) for tournament play and is now focused on 3 to 4 player matches. Third, the factions of the Secret War are set to: the Ascended, Dragons, Eaters of the Lotus, Four Monarchs, Guiding Hand, and the Jammers.

The Combat In Kowloon boxed set included starter decks for the Ascended, Dragons, Eaters of the Lotus, and the Guiding Hand, play-mats, counters, as well as a new rulebook. (The Jammers and Four Monarchs factions would be included in the follow-up set Back For Seconds.)

boardgame: Florenza: The Card Game – Ingegnere Promo Card
description:
A promo card that came with Bretagne.

Ingegnere is an Arte card. It allows you spend one less resource of your choice when you build an Edificio di Rione, Chiesa di Rione, or Palazzo di Famiglia card

boardgame: Firestorm Taskforce Starter Set: Aquan Prime vs. The Directorate
description: Firestorm Taskforce Starter Sets are an exciting way for players to start gaming spaceship combat in the Firestorm Galaxy. Inside this set are 14x highly detailed spaceship models (2x Medium, 1 of which can be upgraded to a more powerful design using modular components) and 5x Light Frigates. Also included is a Firestorm Taskforce fast play Rulebook, 15x Dice, Token Sheet, 2x Acrylic Movement Templates and 14x Flight Stands.

boardgame: Firestorm Taskforce Starter Set: Sorylian Collective vs. The Relthoza
description: Firestorm Taskforce Starter Sets are an exciting way for players to start gaming spaceship combat in the Firestorm Galaxy. Inside this set are 14x highly detailed spaceship models (2x Medium, 1 of which can be upgraded to a more powerful design using modular components) and 5x Light Frigates. Also included is a Firestorm Taskforce fast play Rulebook, 15x Dice, Token Sheet, 2x Acrylic Movement Templates and 14x Flight Stands.

boardgame: Firestorm Taskforce Starter Set: Terran Alliance vs. Dindrenzi Federation
description: Firestorm Taskforce Starter Sets are an exciting way for players to start gaming spaceship combat in the Firestorm Galaxy. Inside this set are 14x highly detailed spaceship models (2x Medium, 1 of which can be upgraded to a more powerful design using modular components) and 5x Light Frigates. Also included is a Firestorm Taskforce fast play Rulebook, 15x Dice, Token Sheet, 2x Acrylic Movement Templates and 14x Flight Stands.

boardgame: Small City: Character Meeple Expansion 2 – The Godzillas
description:
Smoke billows from the chimneys of your industries. Produce more and cheaper! Why wor- ry about pollution? After all, citizens are eager to work in your factories. But all this production might just wake a few monsters from the Earth of old... Goooooodziiiiiilaaaaaaaas!

Godzillas are now awaken and are going to walk around your city!!!

During the first 4 rounds, the Godzillas are still sleeping. At the end of Round 5 and each Round till the end of the game, all the players roll one  6 Die, the highest roll will host one Godzilla. The Godzilla first appear in the Harbour, then in Factories level 3, 2 and so on... Then they must move in the city, the player still decide the direction. Each citizen on his path are swallowed and each Building becomes inactive.
The players track the Radioactivity with black discs. 
It is possible to clean 2 black discs if the player choose the Engineer action.

Also... Good Luck...

boardgame: Small City: Player boards Expansion #2 – The Rivers
description:
4 New Player boards printed both side (Easy and Expert) for the game Small City.
The rivers cut the player board into two parts and the players will have to build bridges to cross the water...

boardgame: Small City: Player boards Expansion #3 – The Forests
description:
4 New Player boards printed both side (Easy and Expert) for the game Small City.
Players need to spend money to deforest the player boards, but they will earn Building Materials with the wood...

boardgame: Tanto Cuore: Kickstarter Big Box and Maid Randomizer
description:
A box designed to fit the first four Tanto Cuore sets, including the core set Tanto Cuore, and the expansions Expanding the House, Romantic Vacation, and Oktoberfest, as well as the new Maid Randomizer deck which it comes with. It is a limited release item from the Oktoberfest Kickstarter.

The Randomizer deck improves gameplay by allowing for randomly balanced games and unpredictable combinations, leveling the playing field for veteran players and new players by forcing both to form unique strategies. This was previously only available through a fan-made website, but is now a great facet of the official game.
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
Lead Rome's best gladiators to glory in Lex Arena – a historic tabletop strategy game with great design, crafted from wood and linen.

Lex Arena is a tabletop elimination game for 2 to 4 players. You take the role of a Lanista – an owner and trainer of gladiators. Following a simple set of rules, it is your duty to lead your fighters to glory.

The goal of the game is to have at least one gladiator as a sole survivor in the arena.

Each turn a player draws an event card that can affect the gameplay and lasts for one turn. Even cards can be beneficial or harmful.

Lex Arena provides balanced gameplay so knowing yourself and your opponents is crucial for victory.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Cinelinx: A Card Game For People Who Love Movies – Horror Expansion
description:
Adds over 70 new genre specific movies, actors, and directors to your Cinelinx game.

Includes blank Cinelinx cards to customize your deck even further!

boardgame: Cinelinx: A Card Game For People Who Love Movies – Movie Buffs Expansion
description:
Adds over 70 influential or otherwise notable movies, actors, and directors to your Cinelinx game.

Includes blank Cinelinx cards to customize your deck even further!

boardgame: Cinelinx: A Card Game For People Who Love Movies – Superheroes & Comics Expansion
description:
Contains over 70 new genre themed cards to add your favorite movies, actors, and directors to your Cinelinx game.

Includes blank Cinelinx cards to customize your deck even further!

boardgame: The Divine Wind: A Dexterity Battle of Martial Farts
description: 'The Divine Wind' is a light-weight dexterity game where you play as Samurai, taking to the field of battle to engage in the ancient tradition of He-Gassen, or “fart battling.” You will ride your horse around the battlefield, positioning your posterior to blow away your opponents with your superior flatulence. Using bean buns to increase your strength, sake to increase your duration, and special cards to tip the battle in your favor, you will fight to be the last Samurai standing. Every card that is not used becomes a shield to protect from your opponents. With positioning and a good ole flick of the wrist, you will corner your opponents, pierce their defenses, and blow them down with a divine wind.

One player has to defend the citadel with 16 black pawns against four allied enemy armies of seven red, blue, yellow and green pawns each. The pawns may move one space or capture (in any direction) an enemy pawn by jumping over it. The defender may not move into the citadel, unless it is under threat by an attacker. The attackers move only forward or sideways, never backward. Movement is one space on the red lines or sideways on the black lines and one or more spaces forward or backward (only the defender)on the black lines.

The defender wins the game if he can occupy oll 4 spaces of the citadel in the center of the game board.

The allied attackers win, if they can occupy the four spaces of the citadel.

The game was published in the 1930s or 1940s.

This expansion consists of 50 new cards and rules to supplement the original game.

You must still make advantageous marriages for your ladies and gentleman but now with the inconvenience of an undead apocalypse which threatens to make a bloody mess of your wedding plans. One player starts the game afflicted with the Curse of the Living Dead.

The Cursed player draws cards from the new deck - both on their own turn and when anyone gets married. This deck includes many Zombie cards, which transform the Cursed player's ladies and gentlemen into flesh-eating monsters! If the player runs out of characters to infect, they must pass the Curse onto someone else.

Gift cards send useful items to other players - including weapons and the means to protect themselves from zombie attacks. Event cards may unleash a horde of ravenous Zombies on polite society or help the poor survivors to persevere with their essential tea-drinking and courtship.

It is very difficult for the Cursed player to win, but not impossible - if they can keep their Zombies well-fed with a stocked up Slaughter pile...

The Earth is under attack by the fearsome Eclipse and his 14 Zodiac Colossi. Their mission is to take over the world, and the Super Galactic Battle Mounties are the only ones who can stand in their way! That’s why Eclipse has launched an all-out offensive on the city of Kingsmount–home of the Battle Mounties and the source of their power. If the Mounties’ Command Center on Mt. Royal falls, there will be no one left to stop Eclipse. Save Kingsmount; Save the World!

Super Galactic Battle Mounties is a fully co-operative city defense game that features a battle system emphasizing cooperation and combination. As the Zodiac Colossi swarm into the city, aided by their minions, you will work together to protect it. Alone, you are not powerful. It is only when fighting with your teammates that your true power is realized. You win if you survive the onslaught by defeating Eclipse and his Zodiac Colossi. You lose if your Command Center falls, destroying the source of your power.

SGBM consists of 12 episodes (rounds). Each episode, in turn, consist of 4 phases which the players take simultaneously:
Spawn, where you will find out your monster(s) of the week and the episode sub-plot.
Plan, where you will deliberate strategy and move your Mounties to where they need to be.
Battle, where you will all roll your dice to fight monsters and lemmings.
Retaliation, where the Zodiac Colossi fight back and damage the city.

Classix is a 6-suit deck of 93 cards. 
It has:

     0 and 1 cards added to each suit
     10 card upgraded to the Damsel face card


6 games have been designed for this 6-suit deck, and a standard set of components. These games include:

    Rummycross (Rummy)
    Gambit (Trick-Taking/Comparing)
    Jacob's Ladder (Shedding)
    Damsels & Dragons (Dungeon Crawl)
    Crowns (Comparing)
    Klondike 6 (Solitaire)

Players are theater owners in the Vaudeville circuit circa 1890 – 1910. They will book acts from the booking agent or the circuit to earn cash and fame. Cash buys more acts, and fame progresses the player on the fame track and unlocks bigger shows and allows more acts in the finale. Act category values are manipulated by the player during the game in the attempt to make their acts more popular and their opponent’s acts less popular.

The Finale is triggered when the turn token reaches the final space on the turn tracker.

Earn the most fame in the Finale to win.
Simple roll-and-move game. The first player to reach space 100 wins the game. On the red spaces, events from the novel hinder or benefit the players.

Contents: 4 Puppy Playing Pieces, Spinner, Game Board

Object: To be the first player to return his puppy to his doghouse.

Before Play: Each player selects a spotted puppy playing piece and places it on the matching doghouse. Blue puppy goes first.

Play: First player spins the spinner and moves his puppy that number of spaces, counterclockwise around the board.  After each player has spun once, the first player then counts the number of spots on the puppy on which his playing piece is resting.  He moves his puppy that number of spaces.  Play continues in this way.  If a player lands on a bone, he then spins the spinner again and moves that number of spaces.  If the arrow points to the puppy's head, he has to return to his doghouse. (The spinner is used only at the start of the game and when a player lands on a bone.) If a player lands on a space occupied by another player, he moves to the next space.

To Win: The winner is the first player to go completely around the path of puppies and land on or pass over his dog house again.

boardgame: Ohio (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
Ticket to Ride: Ohio is a fan-made expansion to the Days of Wonder, Inc. Ticket to Ride series created by Alan R. Moon. A tribute both to Ticket to Ride and the great state of Ohio, this expansion is designed for players who are already well familiar with Ticket to Ride’s standard gameplay and mechanics.

Published by permission for non-commercial use. All rights to the Ticket to Ride name, gameplay, mechanics, and imagery are reserved by Days of Wonder, Inc., who have not endorsed or playtested this expansion.

Unofficial Fan made expansion - Symbols & Graphics Copyright 2004-2016 Days of Wonder, Inc. Produced with permission of Days of Wonder, for non-commercial use only.
The first player to reach space 51 wins the game. Movement is controlled by a spinner. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players.

Life: School Years is a deck building game about real life! Everyone starts the game with only Mom and Dad to help them, but can acquire new friends and properties to help them defeat jerks and succeed in life. Milestone cards also add a press-your-luck mechanic and a way to remove unwanted cards from your deck. The player with the most success points at the end of the game is the winner at Life!

Life: Adult Years is the sequel/expansion to Life: School Years. Players build a deck during the game by acquiring cards from a shared pool. This time around, the stakes are higher as players can get married, divorced, have a crummy office job, retire and finally, death. As before, the goal of the game is to have the most success points at the end and be the most successful at Life!

Seven Cat Cards are dealt to each player. The game starts with the youngest player.

In clockwise order, each player takes a turn to roll the die, and acts according to the image shown on the die result.

The game ends immediately when one player gets rid of all Cat Cards in hand.

This Kickstarter Edition combines the following in one package:

    Cat Tower,
    the Cat Tower Lite expansion,
    the Cat Tower Plus expansion.


It also contains several Kickstarter exclusives, including:

    Extra cats to play with seven players,
    Champion Cat,
    Boba Tea Cat,
    Panda Cat,
    Chihuahua,
    Maine Coon Cat,
    Three other exclusive cats.

Players represent rail transit companies in turn of the century New York City, racing to connect Lower Manhattan to the Bronx. Build subway lines, connect stations and entice riders to use your subway.
Players acquire permits, hire workers and purchase materials to build lines, and then populate those lines with trains to generate revenue. Build the most lines in a neighborhood to claim its victory points. Be the first to connect lines to all the key stations to win the game.
This promo was available at GenCon 2015 at the HABA booth and consisted of 4 Pigs for the game Animal Upon Animal. The Pig figures have a HABA logo on one side and a QR code on the other.
This promo was available at GenCon 2015 at the HABA booth and consisted of 4 Rhinos for the game Animal Upon Animal. The Rhino figures have a HABA logo on one side and a QR code on the other.

'Arby' is a game created by Neptune Meter Company and appears to be a sales/promotional tool extolling the benefits of one of their products (the ARB) that allows water meter readers to do their job electronically, automatically, and remotely.

From the box:

"The ARBY game can be played by 2, 3, 4, or 5 players. One player is the ARB meter reader who reads the meters without entering the house; the others are conventional meter readers who must enter the house to obtain the reading. The playing board is arranged in four WATER DISTRICTS or routes of six houses each with the WATER OFFICE in the center where the game is started and finished. The goal of each player is to read all his meters and arrive back at the WATER OFFICE before the other reader (or readers). The ARB reader must read the meters in all 24 houses on the board; the conventional reader reads only six houses (or one fourth of the board)."

The players all start in the Water Office where they each roll a die. The highest number is the ARB reader. The others choose their routes in order of the die number rolled.

When the ARB reader player reaches a dot outside a house they draw a card which will either give them the meter reading or present an obstacle. ARB player does not have to enter a house to get the reading. Once read they move on to next house.

When a conventional reader player reaches a dot outside a house they draw a card which will either allow them to enter the house or give them some reason they cannot. If allowed into the house they wait until their next turn and then rolls the die. If an EVEN number is rolled they put their pawn on the inside dot where they 'read' the meter by drawing another card which allows the reading or presents an obstacle. Once the meter is read player must roll an ODD number to exit the house. On the next turn they head to the next house.

This continues until a player reaches their quota (6 for conventional or 24 for ARB) at which point they race back to the WATER OFFICE. First one there by exact roll amount WINS.

Components:

- Playing board
- One die
- Five meter reader characters, One ARB reader and Four conventional readers (pawns)
- 24 Lockout Markers (plastic discs)
- 30 matching colored cards for each conventional meter reader (18 large, 12 small - in 4 colors)
- 24 white cards for the ARB meter reader

There is apparently a follow up game called 'Carby'.

In the game of InWords, players spin the wheel in the game board to select a common, everyday word for the round (e.g., Cold, West, Grand, etc.). They then compete to produce answers containing that term which fit into one of the categories listed around the game board (People, Places, Things, TV, Movies, Music). As answers multiply, the challenge and the point totals get larger and larger.

Designed by Yumiko Endoh.

The game starts at the moment of the Honnō-ji Incident, forced suicide of Nobunaga Oda.
Three factions, Uesugi, Tokugawa and Hojo battle to take over the former Takeda territory.

Although there are 3 factions, this game is played by only 2 players.
The faction each player controls is decided randomly just after the first turn.  And this information can be kept secret as long as a player wants. Until that moment, each faction is played by both players alternatively.

The victory is achieved when the faction that a player controls has conquered a determined numbers of areas.  If no player can achieve victory, then both lose the game.

Time scale: ?
Game period: 1582-1583
Counters: 90
Map: half standard



Published in Game Journal #51.(from BGG user)
A promo card for 100 Swords, given out during the Kickstarter campaign. This item lets you defeat any non-boss monster.
A promo card for 100 Swords, given out during the Kickstarter campaign. This item lets you acquire any non-boss item.

boardgame: Small City: The Big Tiles Expansion #1
description:
Small City: The Big Tiles Expansion #1, the first big expansion for the Small City base game, consists of 54 new building tiles:


    9 palaces
    12 Leisure tiles : 4 Stadiums, 4 Cinemas, 4 Swimming pools
    12 Statues
    4 Tramway Stations
    4 Airports size 9 and size 8
    4 Banks
    4 Residence of Level 6
    4 Recycling Centers
    5 Promise cards
    Rules

From the box (Translated):
"To play Cuan Bestia Soy no special skills are needed , only names of trees, of countries, of artists, of things in a library , etc. With some of these issues and an initial letter each round is long... but hurry up! because only the fastest players can upgrade. The others will go down.
Is a game for fun ... so much fun and a good opportunity to demostrate intelligence , wit, memory and speed. "

In each turn, a player throws the dice and moves the pawn on some of the letters. Another player will be in charge of drawing a card which indicates the subject (eg characters of history, capital cities, islands or mountains). Once listed, write a word or phrase that meets these conditions and put his hand on the mark. The first will come to the throne and the last, will go down a step.

The goal is to reach the top of the board: the Throne or make any of the rivals down everything and get crowned Beast.

From the box (Spanish):
"Para jugar 'Cuan Bestia Soy' no hacen falta conocimientos especiales, sólo nombres de árboles, de países, de artistas, de cosas que hay en una librería, etc. Con algunos de estos temas y una letra inicial se larga cada ronda... pero ¡apúrense! ya que solo los más rápidos podrán ascender de categoría.
Los demás se van para abajo.
Es un juego para divertirse... para divertirse mucho y una buena oportunidad de demostrar inteligencia, ingenio, memoria y rapidez."

En cada turno, un jugador arroja el dado y mueve el peón sobre alguna de las letras. Otro se encargará de sacar una carta donde indique el tema (por ejemplo: Personajes de la historia, Capitales de Países, Islas o Montañas). Una vez indicados, se debe escribir una palabra o frase que cumpla estas condiciones y colocar la mano sobre la marca. Los primeros se acercarán al trono y los últimos bajarán un escalón.

El objetivo del juego es llegar a la cima del tablero: el Trono o lograr que alguno de los rivales llegue abajo de todo coronándose Bestia.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: No Lo Testeamos Ni Un Poco: Picante
description:
User summary (Translated): 
This expansion is for players over 18 years old.
Includes 69 cards that are mixed in the common deck and others that are divided into 4 decks (Picantor (Spiced Up) 1, 2, 3 and 4).
Whenever you lose you can draw a card from the special decks, and if you fulfill the challenge, you are back to the game. It also comes with a special die because you have to use when the cards do so.

User summary (Spanish): 
Esta expansión es para mayores de 18 años.
Incluye 69 cartas que se mezclan en el mazo común y otras que se dividen en 4 mazos (Picantor 1, 2, 3 y 4).
Siempre que perdés podes robar una carta de los mazos especiales y si cumplís la consigna, seguís en juego. Además trae un dado especial que tenés que usar cuando las cartas así lo indiquen.
family: Game: We Didn't Playtest This At All

description: This is a 10 card pack of historical items and relics what were made available in the deluxe edition of Scoundrel Society. These cards add more complexity, variety, and historical element to each game.

In After Days: Survival is Everything! each player takes the role of a pair of Post-Apocalyptic survivors working cooperatively with other players to provide for the group and deal with the threats arrayed against them. Each turn players send one of their two survivors, each has a specific ability, into harm's way to gather supplies by pulling three random dice and rolling them. Each dice has four possible resource outcomes: food, protection, shelter, and scrap which can be converted and used by certain Survivors.

Survivors must then nullify the threats arrayed against them such as devastating weather breakouts which cause a wound for each resource not met. Roving bands of marauders that continue to come back on subsequent turns if not dealt with swiftly. Finally, the risen dead refuse to go down until all of the dead have their resources met.

To nullify threats Survivors must provide the resources required on each threat card either using rolled dice or Survivor card abilities. Deal with all the threats and take no wounds. Fail to deal with a threat and it injures one of your Survivors, which can cost you the game. Survive six turns with at least one Survivor still alive and you win the game!

Each version of the game supports up to four players so feel free to combine two games to have up to eight players fight for their survival!
publishers: Dice Made Me Do It! LLC

boardgame: Devil Tree Station: Skin That Smokewagon!
description:
In Devil Tree Station: Skin That Smokewagon! each player takes the role of either a Wanted gang outlaw or justice fueled Posse member in a lead filled shootout in the dirt covered streets of the Wild West town of Devil Tree Station. Out think your adversaries through decisive actions and straight shooting.

Each player selects a character card to portray them, either a Posse Member or Wanted Outlaw and teams emerge The Posse and the Wanted Outlaws. Each team has a four card action deck from which each player on the team selects an action to play each turn. Also of interest is each character has a action card that if played by them allows them to reroll any or all of their action dice once. This is their "tell" as the other side sees this as well. Will you follow you tell and risk the other team playing an opposing card or do you avoid it and surprise them but forfeit the chance to reroll. If the other team plays the correct corresponding action they steal half of your action dice, so the risk and rewards are very real.

Each player plays an Action Card face down and places some or all of their dice on the action Card becoming their Action Dice...the remainder goes on the Character Card. Play then proceeds from fewest Action Dice to most...there's the fast and the dead.

The Active Player flips over their Action Card while designating a target on the other team who flips over their card. If the Active Players Action Card counteracts the Targets, the Acting player immediately steals half the Target's Action Dice and places them on their Character card.

Next the Active player rolls their Action Dice looking for icons that match their Action Card. For example, if they played the Cover Action they look for Cover icons on the dice. Each rolled die that matches the Action card allows the Active Player to move one of the Target's Action Dice to the Active Players Character card. Then the next lowest number of Action Dice goes and so on...Last Team of Player with dice remaining wins!

The real beauty of this game is that it can be played as either an every player for themselves free-for-all or as a team based battle fighting for dominance of the town. Each version of the game supports up to four players, but feel free to combine two games to have up to eight players fight for supremacy!
publishers: Dice Made Me Do It! LLC

boardgame: Pantheon Ascension: Even Mythical Family Trees Need Pruning
description:
In Pantheon Ascension: Even Mythical Family Trees Need Pruning each player takes the role of a minor Norse deity backstabbing their way to the top of the family tree by stealing temples, brainwashing followers, and robbing their kin of prestige.

Players start with three random dice and one Major Deity card. Each turn they choose two of four possible actions in an attempt to get sets of four temples, prestige, or followers, while avoiding three mockery icons. The four possible actions are: Reroll any or all dice, add a random dice to dice pool, play Deity card, and Duel another player. Players cannot perform any action twice!

Getting and rolling dice is self explanatory, while playing a God card involves picking a target for the effect on the card, playing it and resolving the effect, and drawing a replacement card. Some cards have good effects while others have detrimental ones, but the only way to replace a God card is to play it.

Dueling involves both players rolling all of their dice, which is a nice way to ruin someones plans if they're showing three of a kind. The winner is the player with the most non-mockery icons showing and they get 1/2 of all the dice rounded up, keeping all the rolled faces, while the loser gets the remainder. This is a great way to get more dice if you only have a few and Duel someone with many, and if you win you can even grab a set of scoring dice from those rolled.

If at any time your dice show three or more mockery icons, immediately throw away all of your dice, draw three new dice, roll them, and end your turn. This includes Duels: When one or more players shows at least three Mockery icons during a duel, the duel is immediately canceled, each player retains their dice, and the player(s) showing 3+ mockery need to follow the steps at the beginning of this paragraph.

Scoring sets of dice occurs automatically at the end of a players turn and awards a single victory token. Get three victory tokens and your Ascend to Major God status and win!

Each version of the game supports up to four players so feel free to combine two games to have up to eight players fight for supremacy!
publishers: Dice Made Me Do It! LLC

boardgame: Shadow Wars: The Information Arms Race
description:
Shadow Wars: The Information Arms Race is an adversarial game of backstabbing, assassination, and asset manipulation set in 1980s, the height the Cold War. The overall goal is simple, be the first to gather three pieces of intelligence and dominate the Shadow War. To accomplish this task, players secretly target other players/nations and roll dice, which represent agents in an attempt to gather intelligence.

Players start the game with 1 Asset Die, 1 Ghost Die, and 1 Attack Die. All of these dice are identical except that they have one face that shows 2 x their specific type (i.e. Ghost die has a face with 2 x Ghost icons). They also start with one piece of Intel. The Intel goes on their Home nation card along with all dice.

Each turn players put some or all of their dice on a hidden target card of their choice. They then roll some or all of their Target card dice along with 0-5 World dice, which differ from the other dice by not having a double icon face and and extra blown cover icon...Expendable agents are very risky, but allow players with few dice to continue to play and be competitive. The goal is to get three icons of the same type, Assets, Ghosts, or Attacks, without getting three blown cover which forces players to reveal their target.

When players get three of a kind, they reveal their Target nation card, who then gets to defend. If the defense is unsuccessful, players steal an Intel token from the victim along with inflicting one other insult: 3 Ghost icons = No defense allowed; 3 Asset icons = Steal 1 die; 3 Attack icons = Kill one dice.

The winner is the first player with three Intel tokens. How will you deal with the myriad of threats arrayed against you and win the information war…the choice is yours!
publishers: Dice Made Me Do It! LLC

boardgame: Kepi & Pickelhaube: A Study in 19th Century Warfare
description:
Kepi and Pickelhaube is the TAC-50 game that brings the era 1835-1880 to life.

No specific battles are provided in the rule book

This fast play rule set contains rules for the 19th Century Warfare. The game uses inches with 15mm, 20mm, and 25mm figures. For 5mm & 10mm you can substitute centimeters for inches. It is designed to have an evening of fun with no paperwork involved. The ratio of figures to actual men is 1:50, a model gun represents a battery, and staff represent individuals.

38 page rulebook with two player aid cards.

Scale is 1" = 25 yards. 2-30 mm. 
1 model soldier = 50 men in "reinforced Skirmish order" (infantry) on 5/8" x 1" stand and 2 x 25 man lines (cavalry) on 7/8 x 1 2/4 stand

Henceforth is a cooperative, campaign-driven card game where you and your fellow players take on the roles of characters trying to survive after a deadly virus known as "Blue Ruin" wiped out 3/4 of the population, turning ordinary people into insane, howling monsters known colloquially as "Blues". While stand-alone rules exist, Henceforth is best played in a campaign format which can take place up to (and sometimes over) 15 game sessions or “stays.” Over the course of these stays players will earn experience to exchange for new skills and discover useful gear and weapons to aid them as they begin to unravel the game’s overarching mystery.

Each stay sees your characters holing up in a somewhat safe location lovingly dubbed the “home base.” Here your characters will spend action points to attack the Blues, build defenses, search for supplies, and more.

A stay ends when there are either no characters left to take actions, either because they have all evacuated or (worse) been killed, or a story trigger occurs. Story triggers are special events tied to the game’s campaign adventure book. This book consists of a series of scenarios which will help guide your characters to their next point in the campaign, eventually leading the group to one of Henceforth: The Living and the Dead’s many story endings.

The game is driven by an element of push-your-luck that greatly rewards players that take risks but can seriously endanger the entire group if those same risk-takers get too greedy (or desperate).  It is very easy to draw attention to your home base in Henceforth; one wrong move, and a herd of insane, blue-skinned monsters will quickly surround your characters making it impossible to escape unscathed.

Warning: Henceforth is excruciatingly unforgiving.

For starters, each character only has six hit points. With each point of damage your character takes, the chances of that character suffering a long-term injury increases. Next, food and ammunition are both extremely limited and only become harder to find the longer you all stay in one place. So you gotta keep moving. Finally, you must keep track of your character’s sanity in the game as suffering too much or seeing too many horrible things can drive them mad, making them easy targets for the blues.

boardgame: Sri Lanka (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
This map is an unofficial fan made expansion. Symbols & Graphics Copyright © 2004-2015 Days of Wonder, Inc. Produced with permission of Days of Wonder, for non-commercial use only.
This follows the principles of the basic Ticket to Ride game on a special board with special cards and rules. The object of the game is to gain the most points by travelling various routes and outwitting other players. You will need the train pieces and the train cards from either the US or Europe base games (or you can buy them from the Days of Wonder shop
There is an extra set of Chance cards: some are for the cities, each with a value to be earned by visiting that city: others give you extra facilities to complete your tickets. These cards are dealt/picked up alongwith tickets in the same quantity and with the same 'keep/dispose' rules.There are also up to 9 bonuses for the end-of-game scoring.
The board does not have any grey tracks or parallel tracks - colour choice and parallel routes are in the hands of the Gods. Thus there are God cards to help you.
There are many new options in the rules: exchange all your cards at the start but keep all the new ones: discard 5 cards and pick up 5 new ones, as your turn: earn double or triple points: and many more. You can also call time when your wagon stock reaches 3, if you want, giving you the advantage of stopping other players completing tickets.
Jobs is a betting game that includes eighty dream jobs.

boardgame: Dark Red Flags: A Filthy Expansion to Red Flags
description:
This is an expansion for Red Flags. Dark Red Flags adds 75 really, really nasty Red Flag cards to your game.

Here are some heavily censored examples:
_________s every time they smile
Has ________s instead of eyes
Sweats _________

This deck isn’t for the weak stomached or easily offended. We apologize in advance for the trauma you’re about to put your friends through.
An 36-card booster pack exclusive to Kickstarter backers

This deck contains a selection of simple words from the various parts of speech (nouns, verbs, etc.) that will help you learn Tokyo-related and general vocabulary at the same time as Japanese sentence structure. The backgrounds of the cards are photographs of the city and the example sentences on each card be of a Tokyo-specific flavor. This is an excellent complement to the Original Core Deck; using them together will teach you over 50 new words and allow you to add two or three more players to the game!

The Bushido Expansion will teach historical facts and legends about Samurai, their era and way of life, and the culture surrounding them. The art for the 36-card Bushido Expansion is by Legend of the Five Rings artist Troy Graham.

Yōkai are the monsters of Japanese folklore. In America some people look out for Bigfoot. In Scandinavia some look out for trolls. In Japan there are scores and scores of different creatures, and they're becoming more popular in Western storytelling. The art for its 36 cards will be provided by none other than the incredible Matthew Meyer, creator of The Hour of Meeting Evil Spirits.

boardgame: Japanese: The Game – Weapons and Warfare Expansion
description: This expansion adds 36 cards about traditional Japanese weaponry and battle. You'll have cards for nouns like Katana and verbs like Ambush. The art is by acclaimed illustrator Héctor Herrera from the card game Legend of the Five Rings.

Baden-Württemberg: Suchen Sie Buchen - is a trivia game focusing on topography of the German state of Baden-Württemberg. Players draw cards describing towns, popular sights or landmarks, etc. and are then asked to pinpoint them on the game board which is a map of Baden-Württemberg. However, no place names are included on the map.

The map is divided into several zones of varying sizes. Players can try to gain more points by choosing a smaller (thus more concrete) zone, or play it safe, by only choosing a general area (larger zone) for the location in question.

Baden-Württemberg: Suchen Sie Buchen is a local variant of the more known "Deutschland: Finden Sie Minden", which was published on the occasion of the 50th anniversary of Baden-Württembergs founding.

Ticker Tape is a stock hand management/bidding card game. You will vye for the most popular stocks while also dreading the stock market crash.

Components:

     1 deck of stocks and action cards on which to bid. This deck consists of stocks in five different colors and various action cards that will give dividends or make the market crash.
     5 Stock base value tiles that inform how much a stock is currently worth as well as how much you can buy or sell stocks of certain colors.
     234 coins in various denominations


Game Play:
Every round three cards are drawn from the stock deck. Every player uses closed bidding for the three cards. After resolution of the auction the player pays the companies for the stock he just purchased. Play proceeds through the stock deck until all cards have been depleted. During the game players may buy, sell, or trade with one another at any time, but may only buy or sell with the stock companies when it is their turn. When the stock deck is depleted, players are awarded bonus money from the stock companies depending on who had the most, second most, and third most of each stock. The winner at the end of the game is determined by who has the most money.

Salvo! River Plate is a solitaire WW2 naval wargame containing rules, game tables and warships simulating the Battle of the River Plate in December 1939.
You will need three dice (d6), paper and pencil to play.

The first (and only) expansion set of college teams for Bowl Bound.

Includes 20 charts of the greatest teams from the 40's, 50's, and 70's.

				
				
					Teams
     1940 Stanford
     1945 Army
     1947 Michigan
     1951 Maryland
     1951 Tennessee
     1952 Michigan State
     1954 UCLA
     1954 Ohio State
     1955 Oklahoma
     1959 Syracuse
     1971 Nebraska
     1972 USC
     1973 Notre Dame
     1973 Penn State
     1975 Arizona State
     1976 Pittsburgh
     1977 Kentucky
     1977 Arkansas
     1977 Texas
     1978 Alabama


Nineteen teams were new, the 1940 Stanford chart was a bonus giveaway in Vol. 2 Issue #1 of Avalon Hill's All-Star Replay publication.

Hashtag is an interactive storytelling game for small to medium groups. It’s great for an icebreaker or as a way to get to know your friends and family better. Everyone has their favorite stories to tell, but some stories have been buried over years of neglect. It’s time to dust off those old pages and share ‘em. A set of Ninja throwing stars you got for Christmas when you were 12? An excursion into the snowy wastelands in a beat up old car? Tell your stories and other players will interact by hashtagging your story!

Players take turns telling stories based on prompts as the rest of the players lay down hashtag cards that are relevant to the story. At the end of each story, players receive 2 follower tokens for each hashtag card they played, while the storyteller receives 1 follower token for every hashtag the other players laid during the story.

In Tous au Poulailler, players flip face-down tiles to try and gather as many eggs as possible.

On their turn, each player flips one tile. If it shows an egg, the tile remains face-up on the table. If it shows a basket, the player takes all face-up eggs on the table. If it shows a hen, all eggs are discarded. If it shows a broken egg, the player discards one of their eggs.

The game ends when all tiles were flipped. The player with the most eggs wins the game.

Variants (memory and push your luck) allow the game to evolve as the child grows.

boardgame: Halo: Fleet Battles – Covenant Commander Pack
description:
This boxed set is the ideal way for aspiring Covenant Commanders to start gaming the Halo: Fleet Battles tabletop miniatures game. Included in the box is a full colour, easy to learn 128-page Core Rulebook, Supreme Commander Rho 'Barutamee Data Sheet, 5 Covenant Command Dice, 2 Six-sided Dice, Token Sheets, Covenant Statistic Sheet and Reference Sheet.

1 128-page Rulebook
    1 Reference Sheet
    2 Covenant Token Sheets
    5 Command Dice
    2 standard six-sided dice
    1 Fleet Commander Card
    1 Statistics Sheet

boardgame: Halo: Fleet Battles – UNSC Commander Pack
description:
This boxed set is the ideal way for aspiring UNSC Commanders to start gaming Halo: Fleet Battles tabletop miniatures game. Included in the box is a full colour, easy to learn 128-page Core Rulebook, Vice Admiral Michael Stanforth Data Sheet, 5 UNSC Command Dice, 2 Six Sided Dice, Token Sheets, UNSC Statistic Sheet and Reference Sheet.

1 128-page Rulebook
    1 Reference Sheet
    2 UNSC Token Sheets
    5 Command Dice
    2 standard six-sided dice
    1 Fleet Commander Card
    1 Statistics Sheet

boardgame: Halo: Fleet Battles – Covenant Assault Carrier (CAS)
description:
Assault Carriers are monumental symbols of Covenant power, and among the deadliest implements wielded by the Prophets in their war against humanity. Each Assault Carrier is a potent instrument of conversion and compliance, capable of deploying thousands of troops and numerous fighter craft, backed by an array of devastating energy projectors that can burn entire continents to ash: a single CAS is sufficient to quell all but the most pernicious heresy, rebellion, or parasite outbreak. Colossally expensive to construct and few in number, the rare loss of an Assault Carrier is mourned by the entire Covenant.

Every major Covenant fleet has at least one Assault Carrier at its heart, if only because carriers themselves require a significant number of support vessels when operating away from High Charity or major outposts. Multiple carriers in a single fleet is unusual, but not unheard of. For example, the crusading Fleet of Sacred Consecration, commanded by the Prophet of Regret himself, is led by twin Assault Carriers, the Solemn Penance and Day of Jubilation. Together, these massive warships have scoured dozens of human worlds and bring righteous destruction to those within the Covenant who dare stand in the way of the Prophet’s holy mission.

1 resin Assault Carrier
    1 Medium/Small Sprue
    1 custom Formation Base
    1 Formation Sprue
    4 Covenant Token Sheets
    1 bag of 10 Halo Dice
    2 Statistics Sheets
    A5 Formation Overlay Sheets

boardgame: Halo: Fleet Battles – UNSC Punic-Class Supercarrier
description:
The Punic and its sister ships were the first of a new breed of warships drafted to fight a new style of colonial war in the waning years of the 25th century. Officially designated Space Control Vehicles, these massive carriers could transport and sustain entire Colonial Military Authority (CMA) expeditionary and interdiction campaigns in the remote Outer Colonies for months at a time, crushing dissent and terrorism before it could fester and spread. At least, that was the intent before Covenant fleets threatened humanity with extinction.

Punic-class supercarriers have served the UNSC well in numerous campaigns and holding actions during the Covenant War, including the heroic last stand above Estuary and as base stations for the decisive counterinsurgency and evacuation operations on Charybdis IX. Unfortunately, the destruction of each supercarrier also means an irreplaceable loss of elite crews and materiel that slowly erodes the UNSC Navy’s overall effectiveness. By 2552 only a handful of Punics’ remain spaceworthy, including the Trafalgar, pride of Reach’s defense fleet.

1 resin Supercarrier
    1 Medium/Small Sprue
    1 custom Formation Base
    1 Formation Sprue
    4 UNSC Token Sheets
    1 bag of 10 Halo Dice
    2 Statistics Sheets
    A5 Formation Overlay Sheets

boardgame: Greater Evil: The political bullshit game
description:
Greater Evil is a strategic version of the game “Bullshit,” with a political twist. Players shamelessly pander, lie, call each other liars, and play Presidential Candidates in a reckless fight to win the support of campaign Backers.

Each Backer on the board is susceptible to different kinds of Rhetoric cards, which come in five categories: Promises, Exaggerations, Sweet Talk, Facts, and Lies. Players play Rhetoric cards face down, but announce their play to the table (e.g., "two promises"). You can lie about what you're playing, but be warned: if another player calls you out as a liar, they'll get to steal your cards for themselves.

Presidential Candidate cards such as Asshat, Patriotic Pony, Shit Burger, Politibot 2016, and Jesus Toast can turn the tide. Their special powers can attack your opponents or save yourself.

Win a Backer and collect its points by being the first to play enough Rhetoric cards on it. The player with the most points wins the game.

Trivia game with 280 questions about wine. Contents:
56 quizcards
4 winecards
1 die

The goal of the game is collecting 8 grapes for the winecard by correctly answering wine-themed questions. A small element of chance is added by the die: On a roll of 1-2 the player gets a multiple choice question (which rewards 1 grape), on 3-5 an open question (which rewards 2 grapes). The player's turn is lost on a roll of 6 or a wrong answer.

Description from the publisher:

They thought the secret of Warwick mansion had been solved and the spirit had found peace, but now new signs have emerged that were previously hidden. New suspects, places, and objects that do not fit into the picture — and the presence of the ghost is strong once again.

In Mysterium: Hidden Signs, the spiritualists must return to the old mansion and investigate these disturbing visions. Will they understand all the instructions this time and give the ghost its final rest?

Fleet Commander: Genesis

"Become a Fleet Commander, pick your fleet, choose your weapons and enter the battle. Lead your mighty warships to victory and your name will stand as a wise strategist and an enlightened tactician."

Fleet Commander: Genesis is a space battle game for 2 to 4 players. The game is fast and aggressive; set it up in less than five minutes and play it in less than one hour! The rules are so simple and the mechanics so deep that you can teach it to young children, yet still experiment with epic tactical team battles for experienced players.


The Kickstarter Edition includes:


     64 space tiles
     57 engraved dice
     78 ship miniatures (39 per faction = 32 Frigates, 16 Destroyers, 8 Cruisers, 8 Battleships, 6 Heavy Battleships, and 8 Technical Ships)
     KS only Campaign book
     More tokens, boards, etc.
     It supports a six player count
     The first expansion: Avatar
     The second expansion: Salvation


The Kickstarter Edition began shipping in July 2017.

boardgame: Mauseschlau & Bärenstark: Fit und Clever – Das knifflige Bewegungsdomino
description:
From the back of the box:

Who will manage to balance the game box on their head, clap their hands behind their back or walk like a robot? And who knows what fits together: Horse and Banana or Horse and Dog? Hat and Goat or Hat and Coat?

Two games in one package which will not only make you fit but clever, too!

On one hand the well-loved Fit and Clever movement domino with all-new actions for the younger kids, on the other hand the new Fit and Clever allocation domino for the older kids.

A game that trains logical thinking. The game can be played as a betting game on the truth of implications or just to be the first to find if an implication is correct or not.

Example:
A statement and an implication is selected:
When Betty is reading a book she does not want to be disturbed.
Betty does not read a book. Therefore she can be disturbed.

With the game material (logical matrix) you can prove that this implication is not correct: ((p --> -q) and -p) --> q

If the implication is revised in the following way, the implication is true ((p --> -q) and q) --> -p:
When Betty is reading a book she does not want to be disturbed.
Betty can be disturbed. Therefore she is not reading a book.

This is a game for true mathematical nerds!

From the box:

ALSO makes you

     fit in controlled thinking
     precise in phrasing
     invulnerable in discussing
     excellent in solving brainteasers


ALSO-players know the rules of precise thinking.

Psychological game or training for self-discovery and group dynamics.
Up to 8 players can participate. On a players turn he draws a card of a selected difficulty level (beginners, advanced, professional) and decides to fulfill one of the tasks of that card (red = creativity, yellow = interaction/confrontation, blue = self-discovery, green = articulation). The other players decide how good the task was performed and the active player received rewards in coloured tiles that he can place on the board in the pattern he likes.
Over the time the board is filled with the patterns of the individual players. You can play either until the board is filled or for a certain time. All players are winners as they know more about themselves than before.

Simple dice game. The setup consists of each player placing his pawns in a row on one side of the board. The goal is to be the first in reaching the opposite side with all his pawns. Pawns are moved by rolling a die and can only move within the own column. You can throw back other players pawns by ending your move on the same field.

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 1 – Sopwith Camel
description:
The Sopwith Camel was a British single-seat biplane, introduced on the Western Front in 1917. It was a superlative fighter, credited with shooting down 1,294 enemy aircraft, more than any other Allied fighter. It also served as a ground-attack aircraft, especially near the end of the conflict. in the air-to-air role by newer fighters. In the hands of an experienced pilot, its maneuverability was unmatched by any contemporary aircraft. Major William Barker's Sopwith Camel became the most successful fighter aircraft in the history of the RAF.

Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW1 Wings of Glory Airplane Pack is a ready-to-play model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW1 Wings of Glory game product. In each pack you will find all you need to play with the airplane: a special base with gaming stats, a variable altitude flying stand, and a specific deck of maneuver cards.

– Description from the publisher.

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 2 – FW-190 D-9/D-13
description:
Publisher Blurb:

The Focke-Wulf FW-190 Würger (Shrike) was a German single-seat, single-engine fighter aircraft designed by Kurt Tank in the late 1930s and widely used during World War II. The FW-190 D (nicknamed the Dora) was intended to improve on the high-altitude performance of the A-series enough to make it useful against the American heavy bombers. However, the circumstances of the war in late 1944 meant that fighter-versus-fighter combat and ground attack missions took priority. Great Aces that gloriously piloted this aircraft included Franz Götz and Waldemar Wübke. Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW2 Wings of Glory Airplane Pack is a ready-to-play model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW2 Wings of Glory game product. In each pack you will find all you need to play with the airplane: a special base with gaming stats, a variable altitude flying stand, and a specific deck of maneuver cards. Larger maneuver cards (63 x 88 mm) are included with this model, to handle the fast movement of this airplane.

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 2 – P-51D Mustang
description:
Publisher Blurb:

The North American Aviation P-51 Mustang was an American long-range, single-seat fighter and fighter-bomber used during World War II. The P-51s were used by the USAAF's Eighth Air Force to escort bombers in raids over Germany. The RAF's 2 TAF and the USAAF's Ninth Air Force used them as fighter-bombers, roles in which the Mustang helped to achieve air superiority in 1944. This aircraft was used in North Africa, Mediterranean Sea and Italy. Great Aces that gloriously piloted this aircraft included John Landers, Williams Saks and Spurgeon Ellington. Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW2 Wings of Glory Airplane Pack is a ready-to-play model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW2 Wings of Glory game product. In each pack you will find all you need to play with the airplane: a special base with gaming stats, a variable altitude flying stand, and a specific deck of maneuver cards. Larger maneuver cards (63 x 88 mm) are included with this model, to handle the fast movement of this airplane.

By 2213, the religions and governments of Earth have separated into various factions competing for resources and technology. The most powerful of these empires, The Genesis, employ strict laws and draconian influence. More than half their population has been imprisoned aboard hulking generation ships. Treated as separate colonies, these ships vie for their rightful access to dwindling basic needs. Among the rising tensions is a small group of outlaws guided by a mysterious puppet master. Could they be the key to saving the galaxy? Or are they part of its endless entropy? 

Rogue Moon is a branching path co-operative game set in a dark science fiction universe.

Players take on the role of one of several criminals that have been jettisoned in an escape pod when their transport ship was mysteriously attacked. As a team, the players will complete objectives guided by a mysterious figure calling himself the "Broker".

Players explore derelict space stations made of quantum matter - making every station different from the last.

Rogue Moon leverages a scenario book full of campaign missions with branching paths based on individual decisions and completed or failed objectives. Throughout the course of the game, players will discover the hidden secrets of each character and their roles in a galactic-wide conflict.

Form a team

     There are heroes among the bad as well as the good. Play as criminals including a smuggler, bounty hunter, and war profiteerer
     Different characters unlock different paths in the storyline


Fight for survival

     Each character has unique abilities powered by a shared pool of limited energy
     Depleted energy entropies into the fuel of enemies
     Resolve combat and defense in a single dice roll


Discover the mystery behind your choices

     Through decisions and actions, some, all, or none of you can earn your freedom
     Unravel the identity of the Broker and his plans for you and your crew
     There's more than one way to serve a life sentence


Featuring miniatures and vibrant sci-fi artwork, Rogue Moon is another tale ready to be lost to the infinite of space.

Fleet Commander: Forge

With the Forge mini-expansion, you will be able to add to your fleet some of the most powerful and sophisticated warships ever seen in the galaxy. Using the hulls of standard warships, the "Forge" managed to develop a new mix of capacities according to the requirements of their customers. As a Commander of the Hegemony of Amycles, you can now add the dangerous class 2 Assault-ship and class 3 Gun-ship to your fleet. As a Phoeban Commander, count on the fast Strategic Cruiser and the sturdy Turtle Ship to master the battle zone. Finally, intelligence agencies intercepted transmissions about "specific special weapons" inbound to system PA-FF-104. Has the "Forge of Fendrix" already equipped Battleships with such unique devices, or are they still only plans? The answer is in the battle to come.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
From the publisher:

"The Guédé", a small uncharted archipelago in the Western Atlantic.  Ship-wrecked seafarers wash ashore in droves to the small rocky island chain.  These scared islanders cling together in small groups, drinking rain water and hunting for any food they can find on the islands.  Something is in the water however, and  after only a few days a paranoia hijacks the islanders.  Compelled by a blinding bloodlust, the islanders begin to butcher one another.  Day after day the pale sun rises to watch these islanders hunt each other in a demented fury.  And day after day, more islanders wash ashore this forlorn archipelago only to be tainted by a toxic curse that festers in these waters…

Forlorn is a ‘Survival, Team Strategy Game’. There are several different game modes that are played using ‘Islanders’.  There are 31 Islanders that each function differently. Use your Islanders to compliment one another to defeat your opponents.

Forlorn is typically played with 2-4 players, however Last Man standing can be played with 8. The game modes are Capture the Flagon, King of the Hill, Regicide, and Last Man Standing.

Each game mode has a different objective, winning the objective earns a player 5 victory points and ends the game. Additionally each islander kill is worth 2 Victory points, and a sailor (a minion) is wort 1 Victory point.

This way a player that engages strategically and racks up kills can beat a player that camps and wins the game objective.

Forlorn has been tested for nearly 2 years and has come to a version that favors strategy, decisive thinking and psychology.  Forlorn ultimately is a bloody strategy game with 4,495 possible different team combinations, 4 different game modes and 2-8 possible players.

Ninjas! Their very name conjures images of black-clad, highly trained assassins. Now, the mystique and power can be yours to use as you see fit.

Ninja: Silent But Deadly is more than just a game; it's a metagame to be played in the background with ninja-like skills, making it perfect for a game night, casual party, or other social gathering.

To play, hand out one “You Lose!” card to every player. Each player has until the end of the game session to slip their card somewhere where another player will be forced to find it. Amongst other cards of a game currently in play, in their chips, etc. Be creative! And don’t get caught! When a "You Lose!" card is found by another player, that player is out. The last player standing wins.

—description from the publisher


The Kickstarter Even Silenter 5-card expansion is included in all editions:


     Ninja Love Interest
     Ninja Star
     Ninja Mitosis
     Oh Snap! Samurai!
     Platonic Ideal of a Ninja


Shh! Do you smell that? Ninjas! Five optional cards to add to your games. You can now play up to 17 people, or remove up to 5 standard Ninja Cards from the game and substitute these Even Silenter cards in their place.

—description from the publisher

Bukkake! takes a classic fetish and turns it into a fun card game that mixes strategy and luck. It's weird, addictive, and makes for a very interesting game night. The goal of the game is to "come" on the player in the middle of the circle and get them out. If you're the last player standing, you win! Despite how it sounds, Bukkake! is not erotic and does not require anyone to literally ejaculate. While the theme of the game is clearly sexual, that's about as far as we take it. None of the cards are pornographic-all imagery is euphemistic. For example, the genital named "the spicy pepper" is just an image of a chili pepper. The only exception is the vagina card, which depicts an anatomy textbook-style vagina. It's not pornographic. We felt society is underinformed about female anatomy and wanted to educate in a weird way.

Legend of Vyas is a two-player strategy card game based on the the Mahabharata.  Each player chooses a hero from the story and builds a deck of warriors, weapons, spells, astras, advisor, artifacts, and formation. Each player then uses these cards on the Kurukshetra battlefield in an attempt to reduce the opposing hero's health to zero. Cards are in general closely linked to the way the characters, spells and weapons are used in the epic itself.

In game's initial release, the available heroes are the legendary bowmen, Arjuna and Karna.

Each game set comes with 3 unique Indian stick-dice that determine a player's energy allowance for using cards.

5150 Fringe Space is about living life on the edge. Sometimes that means salvaging space wrecks, hunting space pirates, or hauling cargo and passengers from one world to the next.
But it also means carousing in a city, rescuing crewmembers from Alien abductors, and fistfights in a low end bar. 5150 Fringe Space is a RPG Lite game that can also be played as a man to man or ship to ship combat game. Inside you’ll find:

A variety of Classes, Attributes, Professions, and Alien Races to make your characters unique.

Man to man combat rules for when you’re on a planet.

Ship to ship combat rules for when you’re out in Space.

Rules for character improvement that rewards you when you succeed and brings you down when you don’t.

Light bookkeeping Campaign and Economic systems that enhance the game instead of bogging it down.

Color paper character and space ship counters.

And much more…

5150 Fringe Space is designed to be played solo or same side against the game mechanics, but also works just as well when played head to head against your friends.

Playable with any figures you may already have or just with the paper counters we’ve included. Play it any way you want, just play it!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
A game played with a Duelo Primigenio deck. It is a changing puzzle where players will have to find the best position to place a card in the card matrix.

Players will place one of their cards ortogonally to other card on the table, forming a matrix of cards with a limit of 4x4 cards.

After playing a card, the player will check the column and row where his card is placed, then he will take to his side every card in that row and column that have the same color and suit of the card played.

The objective of the game is to have the least amount of cards, and with the lower numbers in the side of the player.

Spanish:
Consiste en un puzzle cambiante donde los jugadores deberán encontrar la mejor posición para colocar cada carta en el tapiz de cartas, para así llevarse las cartas que más les convenga para ganar la partida.

From the publisher:

Does everybody have a limit? You'll find out playing this lively party game. No matter how well you know someone, you will know them better after playing this game. It measures the limits you didn't even know you had. So go ahead and answer that age old question, what's it gonna take? But if you kiss a boa constrictor, we had nothing to do with it.

In this party game the active player draws two random hypothetical situations and a money card depicting an incentive.  The active player has to decide whether they would be willing to do either, both, or neither of the situations  for that specific reward indicated on the Money card.

All other players cast their votes by using voting cards  to indicate which Hypothetical situations they think the Reader would do for the reward. All players responses are revealed and scoring Chips are earned for correct predictions of what the Reader Player would do. Each player at the table takes a turn, and then count up their chips. The winner is the person with the most scoring chips at the end of each round.

Game covers battle of Sochaczew, fought 13–16 of September 1939. Sochaczew was the last city on the way to Warsaw.

Scenarios:
"Sochaczew 14 September"
"Sochaczew 15 September"
"Sochaczew 15 September"

Game was published in the 41 issue of the "Taktyka i Strategia" magazine, in the both Polish and English editions. It uses the B-35 System (Bitwy II wojny światowej 1943-45).
family: Series: Bitwy II wojny światowej 1943-45 (Dragon / Taktyka i Strategia)

description:
Race against the clock to form as many words as possible by adding vowels and consonants to letter cards. Earn points for only those words that have not been written down by any other player AND include, in any order, ALL three consonants in play on the Letter Pot Board. The longer the word, the more points won. At the first sighting of an Oh Bleep! Card, be the first to shout, “Oh Bleep!” and gain advantages over other players.

Oh Bleep! is not your grandpappy's word game. Select one of three game-play options: (1) Zany, (2) Brainy, or (3) Bleepin' Crazy (combining both types of challenges). Depending upon the game selected, be prepared to perform comical, daring physical feats and/ or overcome brain-busting challenges. But watch out! Don't let yourself be distracted from your word-creating, opponent crushing goal. After all, only the player with the most points wins.

boardgame: Ca$h 'n Guns (Second Edition): Full of Surprises
description:
This promo card was available with the release of the expansion     
Ca$h 'n Guns: More Cash 'n More Guns.

After choosing your Surprise card, take all the Surprise cards from the discard pile and choose two more.

You, the merchants of Istanbul, have come up with a new and quite lucrative way of earning extra money: delivering messages to the shopkeepers of the bazaar! While doing so, you can catch some useful information here and there that you can sell to the secret society for rubies. In order to keep your regular business running, you have hired a companion who is actively supporting you. True, he is a little slower than you would like but in return he does not require any assistants.

Istanbul: Letters & Seals is an expansion for Istanbul.
family: Theme: Mail / Stamps / The Post Office

description:
Polynesian Adventure is a fun and educational board game for families. The game is a voyage through the Pacific Ocean visiting several of the islands of Polynesia. Players race to the island of Hawaii as they learn about numbers, islands in Polynesia and life in the Pacific.

Gameplay
Every player starts their Pacific island voyage at the bottom of the board game. To advance, each player draws a card in turn and follows the directions provided on the cards. Number cards have varying amounts of fish on them, along with the accompanying numeral to help children learn to count and associate numerals with the counting. The player then uses that number to follow the fish along the path. There are also "bonus" cards in the deck. These cards can instantly transport a player to an island or move other players around the board. For example, the "Hurricane" card moves the player at the front of the game back six spaces. There are also bonus spaces on the board that allow players to take shortcuts on their voyage or to lose turns. The first player to arrive in Hawaii wins.

This is a game where thinking - and talking - fast is always an advantage.  Can you describe a list of ten people, places or things to your team mates before your minutes is up?  Can you keep a game of work association going without any hesitation?  It's a hectic game where getting your tongue in a twist could be your downfall!

Players are split into teams.  Each card has two parts.  First you have to describe a list of ten things - these might be movie actors, sports people, cities, countries or things like herbs and spices, to give a few examples.  Sometimes there are limitations to what you can say, such as describing actors without mentioning the films they've been in.  Then the second part of the card is "Don't Hesitate", when two players keep up a game of word association for as long as they can, from the given start word on the card.

Reo is a dice game for 2+ players based on the classic dice game Liar's Dice and the goal remains the same--be the last player standing!  Reo introduces a set of community dice used by all players to change the dynamic and allow customization of luck versus skill.

In all variants, players begin with five 8-sided dice each and a cup to keep their roll hidden from the other players. Players then take turns bidding according to the rules, until one player's bid is challenged.  The key to the game is using the results of your roll, probability, and your ability to read others to figure out what the best possible bid for that round is (the most number of dice with the highest number on them), and bid it first.  Challenges are resolved by revealing all the dice, as well as the community roll of 3 additional 8-sided dice that Reo adds.

The most popular version rolls the community dice after a bid is challenged.  This version emphasizes luck a little more, as a player can roll themselves out of a bid gone wrong jam.  The other popular variant rolls the community dice before the bidding, where all players can see the community dice for the "Texas Hold' Em" version.  Your bidding must be more precise when played this way, emphasizing skill versus luck. Any way you play, Reo is a fast-paced, fun game that is quick to pick up, and hard to put down!

The country of La Guerre des Moutons (the war of sheep) is full of fields and forests. There are sheep in the fields and wolves and hunters in the forests. Each player tries to make the largest pens with his own sheep inside by placing his own tiles. You can easily close small pens, or you can be ambitious and try to build large farms... but if you cannot enclose the fields, your sheep are likely to be eaten by the big bad wolves roaming in the nearby forests. Each player's animal is secret at the beginning of the game, but the soonest you reveal it, the more points you get.

This edition includes the base game and an expansion.

The Black Knight's will introduce 4 new characters to the world of the Red Dragon's Lair plus three new game enhancements.

Gold will be added and the party member with the most Gold at the end of the game is declared the true victor!

Pets will accompany all characters into battle and will fight at their sides!

Dungeon Master Deck will introduce players to a whole new way to play. The Dungeon master will take on the role of the dungeon and a boss. Then with the assistance of a Dungeon Master deck the player will work to permanently kill all adventures and win the game.

Shazia Barnes had come to the far end of a ruined mall’s parking lot, searching for her husband when she heard the cry. She spun around, watchful for alien locusts, and the dreaded flying ‘squids’. Shazia heard the woman’s cry again as she swept the mall, carbine at the ready. The weapon was the only companion who’d made it this far, and she’d scored ‘him’ once with her knife for each deathworm she’d put an explosive-tipped caseless round into. A megalithic starship was visible on the horizon, hanging like cast-iron doom over the distant mountains. Towering columns of black smoke roiled upward from the city in front of her. Shazia studied the landscape, stone-faced. It was clear to her now that her husband must be dead.'

CAN MANKIND OVERCOME THE ALIEN THREAT?

Invaders: Armageddon is an expansion for Invaders, comprising four expansions in one package.

Armageddon expands the number of possible stratagems available to each player, from the board-set ones available in the base game of Invaders. Due to the hidden nature of these powerful additions, both players are kept guessing as to their opponent’s overall plan of attack – until it might be too late!

Mankind has also gained a new phase, allowing him to attack the vast alien mothership, and possibly increase his shot at victory against the alien overlords. This addition alters the way both players fight the invasion of Earth:
Will the Invader move scarce resources to protect the mothership - which has an energy source available to help play Invader cards, or can fire a mega-death super-weapon.
How many cards will Mankind play to directly attack the mothership, because every robot or tank moved to strike the starship is one less card available to defend Earth from other attacks...
