Chef Dog is proud of his fabulous sandwiches that will have everyone drooling with hunger, but before he can deliver them, the monkeys have torn them apart and scattered them across the table. Who can reassemble and deliver more sandwiches?

Sandwich Mix-Up consists of forty "bread slices", with twenty of these slices having raised areas that represent the sandwich fillings: cars, rubber ducks, butterflies — you know, the types of things that dogs would use in sandwiches. The other twenty slices have indentations that match the shape of the fillings.

To set up, lay out all the bread slices with the filling sides face up and the indentations face down. At the same time, players start taking the face down slices and feeling the indentations to try to identify which fillings belong to this slice. If they think they know, they can grab that filling slice to try to complete the sandwich. If correct, they keep it; if not, they return the filling to the table.

Whoever finishes the most sandwiches wins!
Super Math Spy involves secret missions, decoder glasses, and lots of calculations.

Little Monsters is a quick game of cards specially designed to tap on the excitement of 'risk-taking elements' from press-your-luck mechanisms. Players take turns drawing as many cards as they want from the deck, scoring a range of 1-5 Baby Points for each card drawn. There is no limit to the amount of times a player can draw cards, but as soon as she draws a Monster Parent, she loses all her cards in her Baby Zone and is forced to end the turn.

But wait, it is not as easy as it sounds. First of all, cards drawn are secretive and only known to the player drawing them. Players can guess at the total Baby Points of each other by the number of cards they drew, but not the exact Baby Points acquired. A player with 8 cards might only have an actual total of 9 Baby Points while a player who drew 5 cards could already be sitting on 10 Baby Points! This is where players try to match the amount of cards by drawing at least as many cards or more than their opponents in order to ensure their victory.

Play special abilities, sabotage other players' turns and store items to help you pave your way to the top of the Hill.

Laughing Kookaburra tests your memory with two games in one box.

In "Eggspressions", players divide the cards equally among themselves. One player turns over an Eggspression card, then points to the face on the game board that matches this image. The next player covers this card with a card of their own, then points to the first face, then the second face. Players continue this way until someone can't remember the sequence and is penalized by placing all of the cards at the bottom of their stack. Whoever runs out of cards first wins.

In "Laugh Out Loud", players use the same cards, but flip them to the other side, with each card showing something silly like "Hee", "Hoohoo", "Woah", and so on. Gameplay works the same as above, but now players must repeat the chain of sounds.

In both games, sometimes a card presents the active player with a challenge, and the player must first complete this challenge prior to doing the memory test — and splitting your focus this way is tough!

The Tric Trac TV scenario created by CROC is a short print and play (PnP) scenario available on the french gaming web site trictrac.tv.

The agents need to resolve a mystery in the local offices of Tric Trac TV.

boardgame: Doc McStuffins: Ich packe meinen Arztkoffer
description: Another Ravensburger mini game. Doc Mcstuffins is a toy doctor, and she has her own toy practice in the garden of her parents. She lost her syringe, the stethoscope, the magnifying glass and the scissors. All players are small Doc McStuffins, who are trying to find the missing medical instruments, and put them into the suitcase. They do this by dice rolling, and look for the symbol they got, into 3 covered section in the box. The one who found them first is the winner.

boardgame: Once Upon a Time: Animal Tales
description:
In Once Upon a Time, players tell a story together using cards that show fairytale elements and endings. Now add more possibilities to your game with the 55-card Animal Tales expansion.

Ending Cards:
All agreed the animal was the wisest ruler they ever had.
And that's why those two creatures still hate each other.
As a thank-you they invited the tiny animals to live with them.
But he was terrified of the tiny creatures for the rest of his life.
Every summer and winter the villagers thank the animals with a feast.

boardgame: Breakout to Paris: Operation Blücher, May-June 1918
description:
Breakout to Paris 1918 depicts the third Aisne German offensive. It followed Operation Michel in March. Ludendorff was determined to win the war and these offensives were Germany's last attempts. Michel was a near catastrophe for the Allies and Blucher would be just as dire and a near miss: the Germans were within hours of reaching Paris.

Some claim the two American divisions that had just arrived saved Paris from calamity for the Allies because within a period of a few days, German troops had created a huge bulge and gained over 35 miles and had taken 50,000 prisoners.

The map scale is about three miles per hex. Each combat unit a battalion( tank only), regiment or brigade. A game turn
represents one day.

Each team has a Transmitter and Mind Reader.
Transmitters draw a card with six words. Mind Readers try to guess the words by asking questions but transmitters may only shake or nod their heads until the word is guessed. The team who guess all six words first get scores. The team that gets 20 points first is the winning team.

boardgame: Chosen Men: Military Skirmish Games in the Napoleonic Wars
description:
Chosen Men is a set of fast-action skirmish rules detailing the bloody skirmishes between light troops in the Napoleonic Wars. The primary focus of the game is on soldiers and NCOs in light "flank" companies, as they scout ahead of larger forces and take part in man-to-man actions against enemy skirmishers.

Although the game allows for the formation of accurately sized companies of light infantry and cavalry if you wish, these formations are broken down into small groups of up to a dozen men. For the most part, officers are not swashbuckling super-heroes, but staunch commanders who rally and direct their men to achieve the battlefield objectives. Although the games uses an alternating action turn sequence, officers can use their influence to multiple units at the same time in an effort to steal the initiative. With all rolls resolved using standard 6-sided dice, this game combines a classic wargaming feel with modern wargame mechanics.

boardgame: Madeira (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
This map is an unofficial fan made expansion. Symbols & Graphics Copyright © 2004-2015 Days of Wonder, Inc. Produced with permission of Days of Wonder, for non-commercial use only.

This follows the principles of the basic Ticket to Ride game on a special board with special cards and rules. The object of the game is to gain the most points by travelling various routes and outwitting other players. You will need the train pieces and the train cards from either the US or Europe base games (or you can buy them from the Days of Wonder shop.

There is an extra set of Chance cards: some are for the cities, each with a value to be earned by visiting that city: others give you extra facilities to complete your tickets. These cards are dealt/picked up alongwith tickets in the same quantity and with the same 'keep/dispose' rules.There are also up to 9 bonuses for the end-of-game scoring.

There are many new options in the rules: exchange all your cards at the start but keep all the new ones: discard 5 cards and pick up 5 new ones, as your turn: earn double points: and many more. You can also call time when your wagon stock reaches 3, if you want, giving you the advantage of stopping other players completing tickets.

This game has a subtitle :"Everyone's friend, is nobody's friend" and that is very appropriate and descriptive !

SJUFT is a classic board game where players have a number of pieces, that they have to try to bring from their base and into the center of the board.

To reach the targets and thus have the chance to win the game , you however have to work together with the other players. If you do not help, or receive help from the others in the game - You are not going anywhere in this game.

Two players square off to reign supreme in the wizard world. High point total wins a point. You must win 3 points to win the round. Win 5 rounds to win the game. Each player squares off with 45 Wizard cards and 18 Wacky Wizard cards. Each round consists of 5 Wizard cards placed face down in front on the player. Each player sets the order before they lay them down. You can never look at them again. You draw two Wacky Wizard cards that you hold in your hand. The Wacky Wizard cards modify your main Wizard cards. Such as changing the cards color, adding or subtracting from your card or your opponents card. You each reveal your top Wizard card and let the battle of wits begin. How will you use your two Wacky Wizard cards to ensure that you win 3 points? Did you create a order of your Wizard cards that best uses the abilities of your Wacky Wizard cards?

Wacky Wizards may also be played in teams of Red verses Blue when more Wacky Wizard Decks are added. You may add two people per Deck added.

Promo Set #3 includes extended, full-art, English versions of five maids: Sofia Marfil, Beatrice Escudo, Liliana Giornata, Milly Violet, Mika Yakushido.

This set has been distributed with purchases of Tanto Cuore: Romantic Vacation.

A pack of 12 bonus cards for use with Stones of Fate. These cards were reached in the Stones of Fate Kickstarter project.

Brazilian gaming personality, Jovem Nerd (http://jovemnerd.com.br), has made an appearance during the zombie apocalypse.  This survivor/zombivor character is a promo that was made readily available in Brazil.

Brazilian gaming personality, Azaghal, from the Jovem Nerd website (http://jovemnerd.com.br), now fights against the zombies.  This survivor/zombivor character is a promo that was made readily available in Brazil.

This box was specially made for Brazilian website, Jovem Nerd, and contains a three-mission campaign with special "gaming night" rules, exclusive dice and miniatures. All you need to have a nice Zombicide night! This Gaming Night Kit features the Brazilian personalities, Jovem Nerd and Azaghal. It includes 3 survivor and zombivor miniatures of each new character, the dashboard, a skull tracker, and 6 special green Zombicide dice.

Right to The White House is a comedic party game. Its cards are themed after the Republican 2016 primary debate. Each card includes a debate topic, a picture of a candidate, and a quote from a televised debate.

The game's starting tableau consists of a draw deck, a discard pile, and a hand of seven cards for each player. Players compete to be the first to discard all their cards, similar to Uno or Skip-Bo. Play proceeds in turns around a circle. Each player must discard or draw one card on his or her turn. Players can only discard a card if the debate topic marked on it matches the topic of the card on the top of the discard pile. When one player discards all their cards, each remaining player accrues points based on the number of cards left in their hand. The game is then reset and continues for three more rounds (or until one player's score exceeds an agreed maximum score).

Two special card types introduce a mechanic that requires all players to speak and act ultra-right wing at all times when playing. The Conservative Challenge cards allows one player to challenge another if they detect any behavior that is not sufficiently conservative (as judged by a vote of all other players). The loser of a Conservative Challenge must draw extra cards. A second special card, the Trump Card, allows players to automatically win any conservative challenge.

From the box:
Crash! With the most tremendous noise of splintering wood and broken tiles Mr Willie Wonka's Great Glass Elevator has just torn through the roof of his Chocolate Factory! Through the glass elevator floor, charlie can see all the doors to all the rooms...but they are mixed up! Which room is which? Each of your cards shows three lift buttons that lead to different rooms. Race around the factory, opening and closing doors as you go. And see if you can find the rooms in the order shown on your cards! The more buttons you can match, the further you'll go!

Match your card to all the rooms in Wonka's Chocolate Factory to win!
family: Books: Charlie and the Chocolate Factory

description:
Memo with two levels of play, featuring George the Fireman to help.
Theme is based around occupations and related objects.

All the tiles are laid face down, each player takes it in turn to show two cards. In the first level the cards have to match i.e. Tennis player & Tennis player. In the second level, the players match the occupation with its object i.e. Tennis player & Racket.

Once a player has made a match they can place George on any tile to help them remember a tile. With each matched pair, George can be moved.

Once all the tiles have been claimed, the player with the most matched pairs wins!

Alphabet game with two levels of play, featuring Marie to help.

The mini game board is assembled and all the letter tokens are sorted into 4 matching coloured piles. Each player is dealt an even number of books. Players take it in turns to roll the die and move Marie around the track. Pick a coloured tile matching the space and call it out. In the first level the players can use the pictures and letters to recognise the letters. In the second level, only the letters can be used.

Once a player has completed all their books they win!

Dominos with two levels of play, featuring Sonia to help.
Theme is based around animals and vets making them better.

Each player gets 4 dominos to start, the rest are placed in stock. One tile is used from the stock to start the game. Players take it in turns to match the animals building a chain from either end. Once a player has placed all their tiles. Sonia is placed on the last tile and no more tiles are laid.

A Heart token is now placed on any animal with a bandage. Players now roll the die and any heart token they pass over or land on they pick up.

Once all the hearts have been picked up the player with the most wins!

The second level is played using the paw print side (players have to count the number of paws to make a match).

This 5 card set includes 2 new followers (The Hunter and The Hound) and 3 new weapons (Ripper Jack, FABS - Executioner, and The Rapier). These 5 cards were given to ALL Kickstarter supporters and are only available now via the BGG Store or possibly via the publishers at tradeshows.

The big ol' hungry Bug is out to ruin your picnic!  The player who defends his/her food the longest, by blocking him from their corner of the picnic blanket, is the WINNER!

The game uses a large, fast, battery powered bug that moves around the game board erratically, while players use a plastic hand piece to block the bug from hitting their pie.  The hand piece re-directs the bug towards their fellow picnickers and away from their own food.  The bug moves back and forth around the board until a player fails to block it.  When all 4 food pieces are knocked out of position for a player, they are out of the game.  Play proceeds until there is only one player left with any food untouched.

A dicegame for 2 players with a Curling theme

Each player has 8 dice in their color.

Players alternate their turns by first calling  "Draw" or "Take Out" and then roll 1 dice. The object is to have more dice with 
lower values than your opponent.
An additional dragon, with a unique level 2 upgrade ability option. Files provided freely by the publisher, and unpainted meeple made available separately.

boardgame: Tinga Tinga Tales: Swinging Monkey Race Game
description:
Join the Tinga Tinga Tales Monkeys as they swing from tree to tree and be the first monkey to reach the finish line.

Create your own game path of trees - make it straight or curvy. Roll the dice to match the fruit to swing from tree to tree. Land on another monkey tale and hold them up a turn. First monkey to swing through all 15 trees wins.

boardgame: But Wait, There's More!: Geek Expansion
description:
The Geek Expansion is a pack of three mini expansions:

Fantasy RPG Mini-Expansion
Superhero Mini-Expansion 
Video Game Mini-Expansion

Every mini-expansion contains six new features and four new products that can be added to the appropriate game decks separately or combined.

From the back of the box:

Hi-Ho and away we go! It's learning fun the GOLDEN way as children race two alligators around a plastic game tray, taking turns matching colors on the Gators' backs. Designed to help children learn to recognize and match basic colors, Hi-Ho! Gator Race combines fun and learning, offering a snapping good time for all.

User summary:

You are a new student at Stanford. The object of the game is to graduate (by accumulating 180 or more units) and become the most successful. Success is measured by three different criteria: Final GPA, Social Stature, and Resume Stars.

Players make a few decisions on paths to follow, but the game is largely dice rolling luck. Strategically little more advanced than Candyland or Chutes & Ladders. You do get to travel around a campus map. Probably ONLY of any interest to Stanford grads and fans.

Flimsy and shoddy components and construction.

From the publisher:

Build it tall, don't let it fall. Players take turns rolling the die and adding ingredients according to the number rolled. The higher the burger the harder to add ingredients without making the burger fall.

Agents & Operatives is a board game of epic conflict between the world's most covert powers: The Agents, The Operatives, and various deadly Mercenary groups for 4-8 players!

NEWS FLASH: Global intelligence indicates imminent international safety threats! In response, leading nations convened to combine their top spy agencies' forces into a single elite counter-terrorism Agency Faction [Codename: WATCHDOG], their Agents’ mission -- identify and eliminate all Operative threats to mankind.

In response to this alliance, leading evil Operative organizations agreed to collaborate under a single terrorist umbrella Faction, S.H.A.D.O.W. Their plan -- infiltrate vital international Zones to eliminate Agents for global control. The combat stakes are at an all-time high as both the Agents and the Operatives seek to eliminate each other.

Meanwhile, skilled Mercenary Factions operate in the shadows of strife, seeking to strategically maneuver within the embattled Zones for their own gain. However, what is unknown yet to all is that all lines of communication within the Zones has collapsed, so no one will know who their allies or enemies are upon entry...

Control your Faction’s field Units, retrieve vital Intel to discover your Allies and Enemies, and enter restricted Zones to gather Weapons, Gadgets, and trigger Location effects with major Global ramifications. Agents & Operatives is a unique combination of secret team/social deduction gaming and worker placement design featuring spatial strategy and upgrade management to create a thrilling, combative, and unforgiving real-time espionage experience from your chair.

Danger has never been higher and trust never lower.

The wargame with miniatures. The box contains map of ancient world, miniatures, a two six-sided dices, markers of flags and money, bookrule.

You can conquer the ancient world using simple rules and pretty miniatures.

Players may conquer neutral or opponnent's provinces, get taxes from them, build forces (infantry, cavalry, chariots, elephants), fleets, fortresses.

Armies can fight under overlord command only.

Quality of overlords are restricted and players should care about them.

Simple combat system uses a two 6-sided dices to determinate "licky" bonus

The player who conquers the all provinces is winner.

Party game based on UK BBC television game show that aired from 1954 to 1956. Two players are secretly given details of an imaginary link between them and other players have to deduce it. A chairperson is required to moderate. The game contains cards with six links, including backgrounds, and cards for the chairperson and panelists.

boardgame: Get Adler! Deduction Card Game: Whispers
description:
This expansion adds The Mole (player 7) and French Agent Bonnet (player 8).

The objective of The Mole is to pass vital information to Adler (and Kate Collins), while the mission of Gigi Bonnet is to intercept enemy communications and pass that intelligence to her colleagues.

From the publisher:
Fireside Tales is a collaborative storytelling game for 4 to 8 players. The goal of the game is to tell an enjoyable story together by suggesting appropriate story elements represented by the cards you play. If you wish to win, in addition to hearing an awesome story, suggest the cards that fit the story best - so that the other players choose to incorporate them in the story. Whoever gets the most elements into the story is the victor.

Imagine Once Upon a Time meets Cards Against Humanity, then add deeper storytelling and wacky rules - and you'll get Fireside Tales.

Divine Prophecy, included in Commissioned: The Call (as a Kickstarter stretch goal). Also given away separately as separate promo card at board gaming events.

boardgame: Enid Blyton's Little Noddy Party Game
description: Players race to find hidden images in three pictures of Noddy's world.

The classic party game of kings with a piratical twist.

Players take turns drawing cards to reveal their fate.  Will it be a singin' contest, or a walk o' the plank?  If ye be lucky, ye might add a rule to the Pirate Code and force your crewmates into all manner o' silliness, but beware being Keelhauled and don't forget the Mop Bucket...

The Viticulture Moor Visitors Expansion, designed by famed designer Uwe Rosenberg (Agricola, Caverna, Patchwork) and Viticulture designers Jamey Stegmaier and Alan Stone, adds 40 new visitors to any version of Viticulture.

Early editions also included corrected reprints of the Promoter blue visitor (for the base game) with rewording allowing to spend 1 grape OR 1 wine to gain VP and Residual, instead of having to spend both grape and wine. And Harvest Machine orange construction card (for Tuscany), fixing a misprint where cost was blank (now 2). Later prints of Moor Visitors do not have these two cards. Back of the box with the description will clearly state if they are included. If the description only mentions 20 Summer and 20 Winter cards then they are not included.

description:
From the publisher:
Pick a team. Use the cards to enhance your team, or to sabotage others. Battle to dominate the board and be the last team standing.

user summary:
Start by drafting a squad of six Stick cards, each with it's own attack/defense rating and special ability, then placing their corresponding Stick Markers on the battlefield. Play then goes around the table with each player moving their Stick Markers into position, playing cards to enhance their Sticks or harm their opponent's Sticks, and rolling the dice to resolve battles until only one squad remains on the board.

In this game of strategy and control for 2 to 6 players, move your pieces and protect your king to attack and defeat other creatures to win.

Based with triangular tiles on an hexagonal board, the game mechanic is as simple as move and attack. The opponent may retreat unless he is blocked by other pieces or the outside rim. The dead creature is placed in the attacker's pantry. it will be eaten later to increase movement or number of attacks.

With a master jumbo card, each player choose 2 menus each turn.
On this card, you will use counters to track your points, stars and available menus.

The game mechanics is based on card drawings which randomly brings customers with different tastes and preferences. If they match, you earn points (dice rolled). Then, you can spend points to invite food critics by drawing food critics cards, if your menu matches, you earn a star.

You win when your restaurant reaches 3 stars.

From the publisher's website:

Grand Fleets is a tabletop game of naval warfare during the age of the battleship. From the ironclads of the late 1800s to the floating fortresses of World War II, players can take command of the ships that altered the course of history.

In addition to the basic rules for surface actions between 1890 and 1950, Grand Fleets includes rules for using submarines and aircraft alongside your battlewagons. Advanced rules are also provided for things like RADAR, smoke screens, critical hits, fleet morale, night fighting, and destroyer flotillas.

All distances in Grand Fleets are given in thousands of yards (kyds), giving you the flexibility to adapt the tabletop scale to whatever size miniatures you have on hand. The rules are also written to accommodate hex-based play, if desired.

The rulebook contains 160 ship data cards representing over 50 different classes. Best of all, you'll never have to wait for official publication of your favorite ships: comprehensive, step by step guidelines are provided to generate game stats for any vessel that put to sea during the battleship era.

So, raise the battle ensign, sound general quarters, and damn the torpedoes — your destiny awaits!"

Grand Fleets: Third Edition Rulebook is a set of naval miniatures rules that can be used for ships from 1:1800 to 1:6000 scale.  The system can be played on a tabletop or hexes.  Certain aspects of the system have been streamlined for the third edition, such as separate gun mount arcs have been combined and armor piercing values have been reduced from three to one.  The rules can be used to simulate naval battles from the Spanish-American War era to WWII.  The main rulebook includes scenarios from the Russo-Japanese War, WWI, the Spanish Civil War, and WWII,

description: The expansion of the board game Medieval Battle. The token of the Commander appears on a battlefield and can fight like the other units but the attendance of the Commander allows also to surprise the opponent by the special command or maneuver.

Spazio: 1999 is a game that recreates the space voyages of the Alphans, the stranded inhabitants of Moonbase Alpha, in their search for a course to return to Earth. The game is inspired by the '70 ITV/RAI English/Italian produced TV series "Space: 1999" / "Spazio: 1999".

Introduction to the game, translated from the Italian rulebook:

"In the 'Spazio: 1999' game, the players must move the Moon on the main Gameboard and visit alien planets with their Eagle spaceships, to acquire the Course cards that will enable them to build, on the Stellar Monitor secondary gameboard, the right Course to return to Earth. The first player able to build a complete Course will win the game".

Munchkin Marvel 2: Mystic Mayhem, an expansion for Munchkin Marvel, lets players journey into mystery with Doctor Strange and The Defenders as they fight villains like the evil Baron Mordo and the Dread Dormammu. This expansion includes new Doors and Treasures as well as twelve oversized Dungeon cards that introduce powerful new threats and Allies and add a new level of mayhem and madness to the game.

description:
Game covers battle of the Quatre Bras, fought against Ney and Wellington during the Waterloo campaign.

Game was published in the magazine "Dragon" as an expansion of the "Waterloo 1815". Contains two scenarios, historical and fictional, with random arrival of the units and possibale usage of the Prussian forces as well as French D'Erlon corps.

Munchkin Marvel 3: Cosmic Chaos, a cosmic-themed expansion for Munchkin Marvel, allows players to team up with Star-Lord and The Guardians of the Galaxy and take down some of the heaviest hitters in the Marvel Universe, including The Beyonder and Thanos. New Portals transport players to far-flung Dungeon locales like Xandar and Knowhere.

This expansion contains 90 new Door and Treasure cards as well as twelve new Dungeon cards.

description:
Each player starts with three pigs in his pigpen. The goal of the game is to get rid of the pigs. Movement is determined by two dice and comences on the track of each players color around the game bord ant into the meadow in the center. If at least one of the dice shows an 1 and one of the player's pigs is in a segment of his color, he discards the pig to the meadow, or if a pig is in the color section of another player, it may be put in this players pigpen.

The game ends when the last player gets his last pig onto the meadow. So even if one player is the first to get rid of his pigs, he may still get new ones until the last player ends the game and may himself lose the game.

Kingdom-building, monster-fighting, food-vending, gold-stealing PIXEL fun for everyone!!

The Daring & The Doomed is a fast-playing, easy to learn card game for the whole family. The print quality and fun pixel art have been a huge hit where we go to play! Now, we need your help to get this cool little game out for more people to enjoy! The game basics can be learned in a minute or so, with rules cards included right in the box. There’s even some special kids rules and drinking game rules to make things even crazier for your table. The starter deck is 144 cards strong! This means you can play 3 or 4 complete games with no repeats! All you need is a few six-sided dice and something to track gold like pennies, M&M’s or tiny plastic goats. Your boxed, shrink-wrapped starter set includes Heroes, Monsters, and Kingdom cards.

description:
The players place number tiles on their own and the shared game
board and try to gain the most points possible as a result. They get points on their own game board for horizontal and vertical rows with tiles in four different colours. Equally, the get points for rows with tiles with four identical numbers and tiles of their own colour.

boardgame: The Secret Of The Atomic Bomb
description:
The goal of the game is to built an atomic bomb.
The game is played in three phases. In the first phase the players try to collect 4 cards of one country by exchanging cards between neighboring players by the roll of an even number. If a player collects 4 cards of a country he contolls this country and enters phase two.

In the second phase the players move around the game board, starting at their own country. Some spaces hinder or benefit the players. They collect fissionable material (atoms) to build an atomic bomb by placing two atoms on any space he occupies in any country that has entered phase two. If a player has collected 11 atoms, his country enters phase three.

In the third phase the players build an atomic bomb by moving the 11 collected atoms from the white dots inside the factory to the blue dots.

The first player to finish his atomic bomb, wins the game.

The Game was published between late 1945 and 1947.

Orion’s Gate is a space battles wargame from the creative mind of Austin Peasley. The game features an innovative fleet building system that let’s you blow up enemy ships piece by pice. The game can be used to play larger battles (like BFG) as well as dogfights (like X-WING).

boardgame: B-Sieged: Sons of the Abyss – Defender Level Kickstarter Rewards
description:
This is a box of goodies that was given to backers of the Kickstarter Campaign.
Extra figures: Elite Gargoh*, sculpted versions of the Avatar of the Abyss* and Ikomoth the Dragon*.
Alternate sculpts: Standard Gargoh, Elite Krohn, Standard Krohn, and Mölen.
Sculpted tokens: First player, morale, food, gold, Divine Grace, Time, Messenger, Alternate Messenger sculpt, protected building, damaged building, destroyed building.
Extra heroes: Thorlak the Barbarian, Astrid the Princess, Donatto the Cook, Dahlia the Peasant, Viggo the Captain, Igor the Quack Doctor, Kirk the Jester, Eivor the Noble, Damiana the Innkeeper, Waldo the Monk*, Kaira the Seer*, Rolf the Executioner*.
Zombicide: Black Plague ID Cards - allows B-Sieged and Zombicide: BP crossover.

Here are the details on the Crossover with Black Plague:

All of our backers just got for free the Survivor ID Cards for the 8 B-Sieged Heroes from both Sons of the Abyss and the Darkness & Fury expansion to play in Zombicide: Black Plague, our next Kickstarter campaign!

The Heroes that will come in this set are:

Orobox, the Mercenary

     These items will be available to non-Kickstarter backers.

boardgame: Pass auf!: Die Gefahren der Straße
description:
Each player starts on one of 6 starting points, has to visit all the other starting points in numerical order and end the game on his own starting point. Movement is determined by a die. On some spaces traffic situations hinder or benefit the players, dependent if they are motorists, motor cyclists or pedestrians.
The first player to return to his own starting point wins the game.

This game was likely published between 1912 and 1927.

Cards Against IT is a fan-made version of Cards Against Humanity that is designed for Information Technology Professionals. Like the regular game, a player selects a red card with fill-in-the-blank text and each player selects one of their white cards to try and find the funniest result. The person playing the red card chooses the funniest response.

The game can be played independently or integrated with Cards Against Humanity. Unlike the original game, the humor is very tame in nature and is designed to be Safe for Work so it could be played in the office. The cards all relate to funny situations that people who work in Help Desks or other IT fields might encounter.

This is a promo (Pack B) for Blood Rage produced specifically for the 2016 Kickstarter campaign for The Dice Tower (season 12).

This adds a new god with the following ability: "Invading Hili's province with any figure, by any means, costs exactly 1 Rage (regardless of any other cost modifications)."

From the publisher:

The Tidal Lord reigns supreme while standing in Water regions on the map. Enemies sharing its region suffer 10 damage each turn, while Allies seeking the Tidal Lord's protection recover 10 health each turn while in its region.

Like the non-elite Fen Lord, the Tidal Lord can slow down enemy units. The Riptide attack reduces enemy movement by 3 for one round, but if it shares a Water region with the Tidal Lord this duration is 2 rounds.

The Tidal Lord can also utilize a Pull attack like its non-elite brother -- but the Churning Sea has the option of targeting Allies, in which case it gains Accuracy and does zero damage. Combined with the Tidal Lord's passive ability, it can either pull enemy Golems to their watery doom, or re-position allies for healing.

The abridged card game of the centuries-old game mahjong!

A rummy-style card game based on the classic game of mahjong. Be the first to complete a hand by collecting sets, runs, and a pair. Play to win, but also play to not give up the winning hand!

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Planechase: Primordial Hunger
description:
Primordial Hunger is a red-green deck built around devour, from Shards of Alara.

Planechase was originally released in 2009. This deck was released in 2012.

Planechase is a card game and a variant of Magic: The Gathering with an emphasis on multi-player games. The set utilizes new oversized Plane cards, cards that are based on various locations (Planes) within the Magic multiverse, to modify the rules of gameplay.

To play a game of Planechase, each player needs his or her own planar deck, consisting of at least 10 different oversized cards. The players may also use a "shared planar deck," which requires ten cards per player. Players cannot use more than one plane with any given name. When the game begins, the player going first reveals the top of his or her planar deck (or the shared planar deck), which is the plane the game starts on. Each plane card has a name, a type, one ability that applies at all times (either to all players or to whoever is the turn player at the time), and one "Chaos" ability.

Players also need a six-sided die, called the "planar die." Each Planechase preconstructed deck comes with a "planar die" that has four blank faces, one "Planeswalk" face with the "Planeswalker" symbol, and one "Chaos" face with its own symbol unique to Planechase. During a player's main phase, that player can roll the planar die, with three possible outcomes. A blank roll of the planar die does nothing. If "Chaos" is rolled, the "Chaos" ability of the current plane card triggers. If the "planeswalk" symbol is rolled, the player "planeswalks away" from the current plane (put it on the bottom of its owner's planar deck) and he or she reveals the top card of his or her planar deck, which becomes the new plane that has been "planeswalked to." A player can roll the planar die as many times as he or she likes, but successive rolls cost an additional one mana for each time the planar die has been rolled this turn.

Other than the above, the normal rules of the game are followed, and Planechase can be adapted to any multiplayer variant.

Planechase 2012 introduced the "phenomenon" card type. A player may have up to two different phenomenon cards in his or her planar deck, which cannot have the same name. When a player planeswalks to a phenomenon, he or she follows the instructions on the card, then immediately planeswalks away from it.
Cinderella themed version of standard UNO. One Exclusive Glass Slipper card.
family: Versions & Editions: Versions with IP after the original release

description:
In this board game the players are not playing against each other. Instead they collaborate and play against the rise of an occult, worldwide dictatorship that is ruled by an evil elite hierarchy. Up to twelve players can participate in the resistance against the rise of evil. By playing the game the players will become informed and mentally prepared to resist the push for a New World Order.

The game is played by throwing a common dice and moving around a board on a path called the path of the astrological ages. Players have a chance of overcoming the rise of the evil hierarchy by answering question cards correctly.

If a player steps on a square marked with the all seeing eye, the evil hierarchy will move up one step on the 'rise of evil ladder'. If on the other hand the player steps on a square marked with an 'I', 'P' or 'M' symbol, the player has the chance of ascending the 'resistance ladder' by answering a question correctly. If the elite moves to the top of its ladder first, the game is lost. If the players manages to move to the top of the resistance ladder first, the game is won.

If you have a printer you can download and print the game.

In Vanuatu, you are a Vanuatuan who wants to prosper during the eight turns of the game. In order to prosper, you have to manage with natural resources, rare items, vatus (local currency) and tourists. To earn money or prosperity points, you may also draw on the sand*, carry tourists all over Vanuatu islands, or trade cargo with foreign countries.

On each turn, the archipelago expands and you have to program your actions with five tokens. You put one or more of them on the chosen action spaces, and on your turn, you will only be able to play an action if you have the majority on its space. Thus, sometimes have to wait for other players to remove their tokens by playing their actions. If you are not in majority anywhere, you have to remove all of your tokens from an action and give up hope of playing this action. A good strategy lets you block other players; for example, they won't be able to sell fishes if they haven't caught them first – order matters! Islands and sea resources are rare, so be quick and take them first!

There is no such thing as a rich Vanuatuan. When you reach ten Vatus, they automatically transform into 5 prosperity points and again, you are poor. Life is hard, but you may encounter ten men to help you; on each turn you choose one of them to increase your benefits from an action. As always, being the first player lets you choose before the others.

In short, Vanuatu is a strategy game of programming and majorities that features development, blocking, and a lot of interaction between players.

This Quined Master Print edition brings you the wonderful original game, as well as the Rising Waters expansion and the extra character of the Governor, with completely new artwork by Konstantin Vohwinkel. The Rising Waters expansion adds a whole new level of tension to the game: Global warming threatens Vanuatu. Many islands are in danger of disappearing under the sea due to rising waters. Players will therefore have to unite to protect the islands by building dikes. However, the interests of one are not necessarily the interests of others.

(*) Sand Drawing in Vanuatu belongs to the Masterpieces of the Oral and Intangible Heritage of Humanity of the UNESCO.

Fortress is a game in rounds for 2 to 5 players. The concept is to protect royal cards in your hand while trying to capture your opponent’s royals. This is done by placing non-royal cards on the left and right of the royal cards --thus building a fortress. In hand cards are used to break down an opponent's defenses, making the player's own defenses weaker in the process.  Protected royals and captured royals are worth points that are tallied after each round.
The game can be played without a table, and only requires a single standard 52-card deck (removing the Jokers).

Crossfire is a territory control game that only requires two decks of standard poker cards and a table.
The game involves two players starting at either end of the table making his way towards (and through) his opponent. Cards are setup on the table as a grid that players build upon to reach the other end. Players must maintain a line of "powered" cards to continue moving forward. There is a high level of strategy and tactics to obtain victory.

The Cat Herding Card Game!

This simple, fast-paced, simultaneous play card game is all about balancing speed and perception.

Players race to be the first to round up 150 cats (points).There are 60 cards with point values ranging from 5-75 in increments of 5. Players try to collect 4 cards of equal value to earn that many points.

All gameplay is simultaneous. The 60 cards are divided evenly among all players so that each player has their own draw pile. At the shout of "Get 'Em" all players quickly draw 4 cards. Players can only hold 4 cards at a time. Players can put cards face up on the table, one at a time. Players can pick up cards, one at a time, Either from their draw pile or any face up card on the table. The first player to have 4 cards with matching point values in hand shouts out "Got 'Em" and scores points equal to the number on the 4 cards. Then all the cards are shuffled and redistributed and a new round begins. The first player to accumulate 150 points wins.

Weltkrieg allows you to re-create well-known (and not so well-known) battles from the wars of the 20th Century on your tabletop, and change the course of history! Scaled for Grand Tactical representation (one miniatures base = 1 company of troops), this first book of the Weltkrieg series includes:

The game series rules, divided into Basic, Advanced, and Optional sections for ease of learning;
Rules that cover basic combat mechanics, command attributes and troop quality, the use of specialized troops (artillery, scouts, aircraft and anti-air, etc.), night actions, airborne operations, engineering functions, and more;
Roughly 180 Data Cards containing the game stats for German and Soviet forces on the Eastern Front, 1941-45, plus a dozen pages of unit organizations for both armies, and a special insert containing the game-equivalent TO&E of Panzergrenadier Division Grossdeutschland at the beginning of the 1942 Fall Blau offensive; Five battle scenarios covering actions throughout the war; Seven 'generic' scenarios that can be used for pick-up games, or as the starting scenario for one of two different types of campaign games; Comprehensive game charts, play aids and markers.

Weltkrieg uses a semi-alternating turn sequence that keeps players involved in the game instead of waiting around for their opponent to finish their turn before being permitted to take any actions, and incorporates the use of specific unit orders and Command Points that may be expended to perform certain game functions; formations having better command and control will be able to accomplish more during an average game turn than those with lesser force coordination.

Written primarily for 6mm and smaller figure scales, Weltkrieg can accommodate figure scales up to 15mm with minimal rules modifications.

The Basing seems compatible with Flames of War so this gives an alternative to use with your 15mm miniatures.

Case Blue Free Demo version is available.

The Worst Type Of Person is an adult party game that lets players amuse each other with their humor and creativity.

In this entertaining game, players use one of the descriptive cards in their hand to create the funniest person they can by matching it to a person on a card.

Players choose from their hand a description on a card to make the funniest and most ridiculous, ludicrous, absurd, inappropriate, preposterous, and and “worst type of person” they can. Then all of the players discuss why their choice, or another players choice is the “worst”.

Players decide which other player they think made the “worst person”, and award them their played card. The player with the most awarded cards at the end of the game wins.

Players will have fun playing their cards and laughing at the cards other players play. But the most fun comes from the hilarious discussions players will have about their choices.

The description on your card may not seem to be the worst at first, but a clever or creative explanation can make your description the worst.

Making funny matches with the cards is entertaining, but the group discussion of everyone’s choices is where the game really becomes fun.

The games are fairly short if played for only 1 or 2 rounds. With short games, players can more easily leave or join the game. Short games also allow other players to join in easier.

The Worst Type of Person is available in 2 volumes at The Game Crafter for $19.99 each.

In Quantum Link, 2 players take turns placing their dice on empty spaces of the board with a fixed face value, betting on how many friendly dice will surround each of them at the end of the game. Quantum Link is the evolution of Quantum Leap and Adaptoid, by the same designer.

An innovative, dice driven game of arena combat. As an Occulite Chief it's up to YOU to defend your tribe's honour.

Eye for an Eye is an gladiator-like skirmish game played in an arena with spaces marked by hexes. Players control one character (usually an Occulite Tribal Chief) trying to settle a dispute with another tribe or tribes (the other players).

Unlike other skirmish games, Eye for an Eye can be played in both real time with a soundtrack timing the action, or in a turn based format. So you play the game at your own pace! 
In the Realtime mode, there are no turns and players can call out and resolve actions whenever they want to trigger them. ​Actions are powered by allocating dice to different spaces on the player’s board and cards. Each character has its own set of skills and restrictions on dice placement. It is likely that you will quickly form a bond with your favourite. 

In the turn based mode, players break up the action into short stints, allocating dice in realtime and then grabbing a turn order marker. Players then resolve 2 actions each in an order based on who grabbed which turn order marker.

Play a single round in five minutes and an entire tournament in one hour in realtime OR play a turn based game in 20 to 30 minutes. The choice is yours!

With several different game modes including:

     Every Eye for Themselves
     Alliances
     Nymphlet Ceremony
     King of the Hill
     Domination


and 6 totally unique characters (with more on the way), Eye for an Eye has a huge amount of replayability.
family: Setting: Rise of the Occulites Universe

description:
Fans of the classic naval wargames War at Sea and Victory in The Pacific have for many years combined the two into an ad hoc simulation of the global naval struggle that was World War II. There were issues, however: a wide variety of house rules, battle resolution that drastically changed depending on the sea area in which it took place, to say nothing of numerous historical "anomalies". Now these two games have been reimagined, re-researched, and recombined into a single seamless contest for either two or four players. This ain't your Daddy's War At Sea.

Can the Royal Navy protect the shipping lanes of Britain's far-flung empire long enough to turn the tide against the U-boats? Or will the Kreigsmarine and Supermarina cut England's merchant marine lifeline? Will the French remain loyal to the Allies, or will they bring to life Churchill's greatest nightmare and fight alongside the Germans? Can the Americans withstand the Japanese onslaught long enough to bring their mushrooming strength to bear? Or will Japan complete its conquests in the Pacific and force the Allies to negotiate for peace?

Among the new features


     A movement & combat system consistent over both the Atlantic and Pacific
     All ships evaluated uniformly
     Redesigned counters
     Added sea areas including a completely new Atlantic map
     More emphasis on U-Boats
     Designed from the start as a single game


New Rules Require New Tactics:
Players employing "classic" strategies may quickly find themselves in trouble. Atlantic Jutland-esque battles are replaced with a tense balancing act: Only patrolling ships can combat U-Boats, but raiding ships are needed to prevent German surface raiders. An IJN pressing the early edge too hard may be met with Atlantic Fleet reinforcements, making a Hawaiian invasion more realistically difficult.

description:
From the publisher:

Our designers have done it again! Reaching into the most horrendous and disgusting parts of their minds and the internet to bring you our first expansion!

One player reads a Question Card out to the group, replacing "Insert Name" with the name of a person at the table.

The other players then pick a highly-offensive answer card from their hand to give to the reader. The reader then reads the card again, one-by-one, filling in the blanks vwith the provided answers.

Once everyone is done p******* their pants laughing, players then vote on the answer they want to win! No dictator B.S. here; every one of the terrible people playing the game gets a say!

description:
You are all workers in a Top-Secret underground corporate research facility of D.O.A. (Dynamic Oscillating Architecture) when all of a sudden the quarantine alarm sounds. You know what that means. The "Cleaners" are on their way. You only have so much time to get what you need and be where you need to be to enact your plan or else they will "take care of" you. Don't let anyone get in your way.

Each game of Quarantine D.O.A. will be played on a selfishly player made map that will change every game. With a large selection of special room cards all with unique conditions to randomly choose from, no one will know what will be placed until the map is complete.

Players will take turns spending actions trying to find the items required to enact their plan. Those that succeed will win. There can always be multiple winners but you don't really know who might help and who will get in the way so to be safe don't trust anyone. Search, steal and push to ensure that you are one of the lucky few, but remember, sometime the no one escapes and the "Cleaners" win.

Defend the walls of your Dojo from invading Pirates that want to steal the ancient artifact of great power you protect.  Play one of six ninjas all with their own special abilities and work together to prevent the fall of the dojo.

This is a worker placement tower defense co-operative game where the Ninjas take turns fighting off wave after wave of Pirates until they have defeated four of six Pirate Captains. Build and upgrade walls to slow down their advance because if they enter the Dojo they will start damaging the Ninjas instead of just the walls and turrets. You can always use bombs and ballistas but you only have have a limited number to use.

Each end phase the Pirate ships will attack with their cannons and send in reinforcements.  The location of that attack and the direction and number of new pirates is randomly selected by three small decks of cards that will be reshuffled every time a captain is found.  The difficulty of the game can be changed by adding or subtracting to the Pirate number deck.

Thick as thieves they say but what happens when informants infiltrate your thieves' guild? What happens when they are your friends?

Informants is a strategic game made for 2 to 6 players. Compete with and against other players in an effort to collect all of the items you have been tasked to collect. Trade, steal and bluff your way to completing your mission but be careful helping out the wrong team could lose you the game. All you can trust is their word.

Players take the role of either a thief or an informant and are tasked with recovering a number of items.

Each turn you must decide between collecting tools to aid in your mission, trading with another player or attempt a robbery. The round ends when one player collects all of the items they have been tasked with collecting. Players are then rewarded with money for every item they have collected and the winning team is awarded bonus cash for completing their mission. The game ends after all rounds have been played and the player with the most money wins.

boardgame: Atlantis: The Lost Empire – The Great Adventure
description:
Who will manage to get first into Atlantis?
To achieve this, players will have to travel across the dangerous underground river in order to get supplies and get into the lost empire. But watch out! The titans are lurking.

Players roll the die and move on the board accordingly. When a player lands on a block with a picture, draws a card from the "direction cards". When a player collects the 3 special cards needed, he can open the gate of Atlantis. Then, he chooses and flips one out of the seven cards from the center of the board. If the card shows Atlantis, the game is over and the player is the winner. Otherwise, the game continuous until a player finds the card with Atlantis.

boardgame: Lock 'n Load Tactical System: Modern Era 1960-Present Demo
description:
Lock ’n Load Tactical’s System Demo Version 4.1 (v4.1) Modern Era edition.  This demo uses the latest set of guidelines and regulations for the game system written by Jeff Lewis, Lead Developer of the Lock ’n Load Tactical Series.

The demo comes with a basic printed rule book, printed map with extra large hexes, one scenario and seventy 1” counters to help you get into one of the easiest tactical systems available.  Everything you need to try out LnLT is here.

The Modern Era Edition takes you to Vietnam in 1969, where you can play either the US Army or the Viet Cong forces.

description:
Lock ’n Load Tactical’s System Demo Version 4.1 (v4.1) World War II Era edition.  This demo uses the latest set of guidelines and regulations for the game system written by Jeff Lewis, Lead Developer of the Lock ’n Load Tactical Series.

The demo comes with a basic printed rule book, printed map with extra large hexes, one scenario and seventy 1” counters to help you get into one of the easiest tactical systems available.  Everything you need to try out LnLT is here.

The World War 2 Era Edition takes you to Normandy 1944, where you can play either the US forces or Germans.

Battaglia Spaziale "simulates" a tactical "man-to-alien" scale fight beween Space Monsters and Human Space Legion troops, using a checker-like mechanism. The player pieces can eliminate the adversary figures by moving on their squares to capture them.
The game uses 32 soft plastic 2" figures form the "Gli Spaziali" toy soldiers series produced by the same Atlantic.

Junk In My Trunk is a casual game based on the classic Swedish card game Vändtia, an ancestor of the card game Palace.

The circus grounds are a mess after a great show. Clean up duties fall to the elephants, and using their trunks like vacuums, they suck up all the junk. The first player to get rid of all of their "Junk" (cards) wins. Special action cards also add to the fun by allowing you to peek, swap and flip.

boardgame: Legends of the American Frontier: Bounties of the American Frontier – Expansion Pack #1
description:
This expansion pack introduces "bounties". There are two ways to have a bounty placed on your head.

Firstly, when you fail an Adventure, instead of taking a Failure card, you may take an appropriate Bounty card. Taking a Bounty card this way means you avoid the immediate penalties of failure, which can be quite an advantage. Secondly, you may succeed at one of the several new Adventures included in the expansion, that while lucrative, are a little bit shady, and also "award" you with a Bounty card.

Also included in the expansion are Bounty Adventures. These are adventures that players may go on to claim bounties placed on other players heads -- to bring them to justice, if you will. Claiming a bounty rewards the player claiming it, and incurs a penalty on the player that had the bounty on them. If you get to the end of the game without a bounty on your head being claimed, you have succeeded in avoiding your dark past being unearthed.

And as a successful bounty hunter, you will add to your Legend.

Poison Bottles is a card game with many classical elements entwined. Each player has a 3x3 grid of facedown cards in front of them, for which they will try to secretly change as the game progresses in hopes to achieve the lowest score possible.

There are five distinct phases in the game, starting with trick taking, where a high card of the first played suit will win. If a poison bottle is played in the trick, the rules are reversed and the low card wins.

In the next two phases, peeking and swapping, every player is allowed to look at any one card on any players board, followed by the losers of the trick swapping one card from their board with a card from their hand.  A marker is then placed on one card of each players board to protect it from being attacked.

The attacking phase follows, where the winner of the trick then takes a card from an opponents board, and swaps it with one from his hand, leaving the card face up for the rest of the game.  This is repeated for each opponent.

The final phase is the drawing phase, where players draw back up to a five card hand. These phases are repeated until a player cannot draw a card, upon which the game ends immediately. Players then reveal their boards and count up their points, however, if a player manages to get three of the same number card in a row or column, that row is eliminated and now worth 0 points. Similarly, if a player manages to get a row or column of three poison bottles, that row or column becomes worth -30 points. Poison bottles played in the trick taking phase are worth -3 points to that player at the end of the game.

There are elements of hearts, nines, and memory in the game, with a focus on bluffing and heavy player interaction.

Chickamauga: Bloody September, 1863 (CBS) is volume four of Paul Koenig’s Bloody Civil War Series. It is a two-player wargame simulating the second bloodiest battle of the war and the biggest defeat for the Union in the West. As the Confederate player, you are in the rare position of outnumbering your enemy. On the first day you must take Thomas' initial blow, and then knock him back, setting up for
the decisive battle on Day 2 after the arrival of Longstreet. As the Union player, you must hope that Thomas can be the Rock of Chickamauga once again. Your army arrives slowly, and you will be hard pressed to hold back the Rebels.

description:
Folded is an abstract origami themed card game for two players. The play area consists of four stacks of cards in the center of play representing squares of origami paper to be "folded" to make creations.

The game comes with many creation cards, each with four steps to complete a creation. These steps must be found in order by flipping cards in place to reveal the steps in sequence and complete chosen creations.

Players move clockwise around four locations using balloon markers to fulfill journey task cards. These journey task cards simply show the symbol that must match the category when chosing the four creations. Players may force their openent to use a differend search method from four fold reference cards to hider their search.

The first player to complete all four creations is declared the winner.

Folded has brightly colored cards in this print and play game that can be played by many ages. It is also compact enough to fit in a mint tin to bring anywhere.
Trick tacking and set collection game where collecting the most pairs of gods is the goal before running out of cards.  Played with a 72 card deck with 18 different gods from ancient Greece.

A mini-expansion for The Networks, consisting of cards unlocked by Kickstarter stretch goals.

Contents:
7 new Season 4-5 Shows
7 new Stars
8 new Network Cards (2 copies of 4 cards)
2 new Ads
6 blank Season 2-3 Shows
1 blank Star
1 sticker sheet with stickers for the turn order and score markers.

This is not a Kickstarter exclusive; it will be available for purchase once the game comes out.

From the publisher (English):
A yahtzee-like game where players represent Tango dancers in a tournament, they try to acomplish the best choreography in order to win.

From the publisher (Spanish):
El juego (inspirado en la generala) trata sobre un concurso de Tango, donde los jugadores representan parejas tratando de hacer diversos pasos de baile, y de ser posible coreografías de diversa complejidad, quien mejor lo haga será el ganador.

"Cosmoscacchi" is a chess/checkers-like wargame that utilize the 5" characters of the Galaxy Series, produced by Atlantic in the '70s years of the last century.

The players can move their pieces on the board in different ways based on the type of Character used (no dice rolls involved). The Characters can shoot each other if there is a direct stright or diagonal line between them. The shooting is resolved revolving the "Fire Wheel": if the wheel shows a number equal to the distance between the Characters, the round hit the choosen target, otherwise it miss. If the wheel stop on a space with no number the shooter's weapon jam.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Zooloretto Würfelspiel: Affenstall

Translated.... Zooloretto Dicegame: Monkeyhouse

Available as AbacusSpiele Catalog Edition 17.
Also as a download on M.Schacht's website, where he calls it Variante: S.

It is a different score pad for the Zooloretto dice game. There is an additional monkey enclosure on this pad and fewer spaces for most of the enclosures. Three enclosures score bonus points by filling these first and the three others earn coins instead.

Carcassonne: Star Wars – Erweiterung 1 is an expansion for Carcassonne: Star Wars that includes 18 new space tiles featuring First Order faction symbols plus 6 scoring tiles as well as components for a sixth blue player (Kylo Ren).

Players now have a lightsaber and a blaster token, and when placing a meeple they may add one but not both of these tokens to help with dice rolling. The lightsaber adds 1 to a die roll and the blaster uses a custom yellow die numbered from 3 to 8.

A solo card game inspired by the 80's computer game Elite.  The player assumes the role of  a spaceship captain, travelling about and trading between worlds.

Decks of custom cards are used to record the status of the player's ship, as well as generate the space they are travelling through, the marketplaces they trade at, the opponents they fight and missions they can complete.  Combat utilizes a combination of resource management and dice rolling.

The game is intended to be played over several sessions with the ability to be quickly packed up and then resumed, whilst being stored in a compact fashion.

boardgame: Das kleine Gespenst: Wettlauf zur Burg Eulenstein
description: Das kleine Gespenst: Wettlauf zur Burg Eulenstein is a cooperative memory game based on the well-known book by Otfried Preußler.

From the publisher:

The Race is a fast paced drag racing game where each player tries to get to the finish line first! Pick a color, take the matching components and roll the dice. With a little luck you might become the ultimate racing champ!

The Race features a fun dice mechanic where everyone rolls their dice at the same time and compares results to determine how their race cars move along the track. The Race is heavily based on chance, but uses a bit of strategy with a well timed boost.

It's a great filler game that can be played by nearly all ages. For shorter or longer games all you have to do is change the track length, so it's modular as well! Pick up The Race today and see who, among your friends and family, is the ultimate racing champ!

A fast-paced, movie matching card game. Match the Popcorn Cards in your hand to the famous films on the Movie Cards. First player to play out all of their Popcorn Cards wins.

60 Popcorn Cards and 120 Movies

Epic Resort: Villain's Vacation, the first expansion for Epic Resort, introduces villains, new tourists, new workers, and an epic monster!

The darkest souls in the land have been busy plotting schemes and casting spells, but such extreme dedication to conquering heroes can be exhausting. So this time, the villains decide it's their turn for a tropical getaway and it's up to you to build a resort fit for vampires, witches, and crazed Alchemists most foul.

description:
From the manufacturer: 
Twizmo Cards is all about creating poker-style hands using a combination of the “cards” on the cube and the cards from the deck.  This combination of the 2 sets creates a new card playing dynamic that allows for a come from behind win.  It’s poker in 3 dimensions!  Invent  variations of your favorite games with this new paradigm that allows for endless possibilitiesupply.

In each round players compete to create their best hand using the 3 cards they have been dealt from the deck and any 3 “cards” on the top face of the cube.  Cards used on the cube must be 3 in a row either vertically, horizontally or diagonally.

Players now use the cards they’ve been dealt as betting chips.  The player with the best hand collects all the cards that have been bet for that round.  Careful which cards you bet and how many lest they fall into the wrong hands!

When all the cards from the deck have been dealt and won, players use the cards they’ve collected to create one final hand for the win.  It’s entirely possible to come from behind to take it all!

Be the first to clean up Little Critter's room!  Spin the spinner and move around the track in either direction.  Land on a toy space, find the matching token in the center of the board, and put it in your toy box.  Be the first to put away 6 toys and you win!  But beware of the cobwebs and dust bunnies.  You can't win if they're in your toy box!

Game covers two battles fought between Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth and the Kingdom of Sweden during the Polish–Swedish War (1600-1611). Both battles were famous because of the use of the Polish heavy cavalry known as Winged Hussars.

The battle of Kircholm was decided in 20 minutes by the devastating charge of Polish–Lithuanian cavalry, the Winged Hussars.[3] The battle ended in the decisive victory of the Polish-Lithuanian forces, and is remembered as one of the greatest triumphs of Commonwealth cavalry.

During the battle of Kokenhausen Polish forces defeated the Swedish relief force and captured the besieging force, relieving the Polish garrison. The battle is notable as one of the greatest victories of the Polish hussars, who defeated their numerically superior Swedish adversaries.

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 2 – Nakajima Ki-84 Hayate
description:
From the publisher:

The Nakajima Ki-84 “Hayate” was a single-seat fighter used by the Imperial Japanese Army Air Force during World War II. Nicknamed “Frank” by the Allies, the Ki-84 had excellent performance and high maneuverability, and was considered to be the best Japanese fighter to see large scale operations during World War II.

It was able to match any Allied fighter, and to intercept the high-flying B-29 Superfortresses, and its powerful armament increased its lethality. Great pilots that gloriously piloted this aircraft included Kenji Fujimoto and Takeshi Imoto.

Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW2 Wings of Glory Airplane Pack is a ready-to-play model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW2 Wings of Glory game product.

In each pack you will find all you need to play with the airplane: a special base with gaming stats, a variable altitude flying stand, and a specific deck of maneuver cards. Larger maneuver cards (63 x 88 mm) are included with this model, to handle the fast movement of this airplane.

From the publisher:

In the super-fast card game Supermarket Shuffle, you are shopping at the supermarket and trying to grab the best combination of items in your shopping cart. Score points for collecting the most apples. Watch the Daily Specials to help you pay for you groceries using less money And once you're done shopping, you might have won a special prize! But be careful which groceries you allow your friends to take; it might be just what they need to beat you! And make sure you have enough money to purchase your groceries or you have to give them back.

Supermarket Shuffle takes card-drafting mechanisms and distills them into a 20-30 minute game that anyone can play. The dynamics of "draft and pass" are brought to the fore, while keeping the rules to a minimum.  As you see the first few hands of cards, you must quickly assess the make-up of the round and decide which type of groceries you'll go for. Then, each turn you'll need to weigh which cards to keep and which to pass on. The different scoring combinations allow for some clever plays and nasty blocks.

Spurs : Legends Expansion is the second expansion for Spurs: A Tale in the Old West.

The Legends Expansion includes 15 new Challenge tokens, a deck of Train cards, and two new female characters: the  Cowgirl and the Legend herself.

boardgame: Commands & Colors: Napoleonics Expansion #6 – EPIC Napoleonics
description:
EPIC Napoleonics is the sixth expansion for GMT’s Commands & Colors Napoleonics game system. EPIC Napoleonics allows for fighting larger battles with more units. EPIC Napoleonics is actually two game systems in one – the Epic Battles system, and the La Grande Battles system. Although the scope of our Napoleonic game has changed size wise, the historical feel and the basic rules of play, for the most part, remain the same. To experience the maximum enjoyment with these two new Napoleonic game formats, both EPIC Napoleonics and La Grande Battles scenarios are best played using the Command card and Tactician card decks that were part of the Generals, Marshals and Tacticians expansion.

Included in this expansion are six standard sized scenarios that focus on key phases of the battle of Austerlitz, 2 December 1805. This expansion also features 12 EPIC sized engagements and two La Grande Battle scenarios. Also included are two double-sided mounted maps. The EPIC sided battlefield is larger, and at 11 hexes deep by 20 hexes wide, it is almost double the size of the standard size battlefield of 9 hexes deep by 13 hexes wide. When laid side-by-side to form the La Grande battlefield, the battle area is a whopping 11 hexes deep by 26 hexes wide.

In an EPIC Napoleonics game, one player on each side assumes the role of the Commander In Chief (CIC). Each CIC has a hand of Command cards and on a turn must play one card from his hand to order units in one section of the EPIC battlefield. Also on the turn, the CIC must select one card from the Courier rack to play. The card selected from the Courier rack will also order units in one section of the EPIC battlefield. There are only three sections in the EPIC battlefield (left, center and right) and with two Command cards played each turn, the action quickly becomes very fast and furious.

The Courier rack is a new game mechanic, and starts each battle with five Command cards that are visible to both players. Each turn one card is taken from the rack, so the choice of cards a player has each turn is reduced, until there are only two cards remaining on the rack. The rack is then filled back to five cards before the next player’s turn, and the card selection process from the Courier rack will start again.

EPIC Napoleonic will allow two or more players to enjoy a truly Epic size battle, but it is important to note in order to play most EPIC Napoleonic scenarios, players will need a copy of the Commands & Colors Napoleonic game and its expansions (Spanish, Russian, Austrian, Prussian and Generals, Marshals and Tacticians) This EPIC Napoleonic expansion will only provide enough units and terrain to fill any gaps.

description:
Ghosts roam around the attic playing wildly and (unfortunately) tearing their sheets in the process. Their parents patch them up, but now it's easier to tell who's who when you spot them passing by, and your job in Spooky Wooky is to find pairs of ghosts quicker than anyone else.

To set up, all the tiles are laid face down. Players take turns revealing one tile, and as soon as someone spots a pair of ghosts with patches of the same shape, that player keeps the matching tile newly revealed. Players contain playing until no more matches can be made, then whoever has the most tiles wins.

Alternatively, players of Spooky Wooky can look for ghosts with patches of the same shape and color, or for ghosts with the same number of identically-shaped patches.

Rough-and-Tumble is a boardgame with a new and unique game mechanic: it's a chess game with hexagonal boxes and a little innovation that will make the game unique and intriguing, ideal for one on one challenges to discover who's the best strategist!

You will choose between two teams: orcs or dwarves, each faction will offer different powers and mechanics, what faction you are going to use and which one is the best, is exclusively up to you!

From the back of the box:

The game "The Chief of Police" has been created by a serving police officer.  By playing this game in a family environment everyone will gain a valuable insight into the rights and wrongs of behaviour in everyday life.

Whilst playing you become aware of Crime Prevention, Traffic Laws and Road Safety.  You experience the problems faced by Police Officers and the decisions which have to be made by them when on duty.

After starting patrol from the police Station each "Police Officer" will endeavour to be the first to win 10 Commendations and become "The Chief of Police."

It's fun - it's educational -it's a challenge for the whole family.

Can you become "The Chief of Police"

Basketball toy court where a small ball is  driven into the baskets by manipulating mechanical side levers. The Winner is the one with most points on the scoreboard at a given time. A personal foul is committed every time the ball is thrown out of the court. The contrary is given two attempts at a free row.

From the publisher:

In Ancient Sands, players will play as treasure hunters digging for valuable artifacts in a hostile desert. The game is played on a 4x4 grid of treasure card stacks. At the top of the stacks are easy to recover simple treasures. Each one of these will give you a small number of points and an additional bonus or way to manipulate the desert. As you take these, more valuable treasures are revealed deeper in the sands. Should you manage to reach the bottom and gather the workers needed to dig one, you'll find ancient artifacts of tremendous power. Recover one and you'll not only get a huge boost to your score, you'll be able to turn its power on your rivals!

Each turn, you will choose a worker to recruit and play for both progress towards recovering treasures and for a variety of effects depending on who you chose. Alternatively, many workers can be instead discarded for stronger powers, but no progress towards digging. Once you have gathered enough workers in play you can send them out to dig up a treasure from the desert grid. Each treasure is worth a number of victory points along with a wide variety of special effects from recovering them. You might find functioning tools that make your work easier or uncover old tomes that point to where more valuable treasures lie. Be warned though, the Dead Reaches are not a safe place to be. Each round an event card will be drawn that could radically change the situation and have everyone scrambling to recover. Worse still are detrimental weather cards that could bring your progress to a screeching halt. It will be up to you to carefully use your workers to mitigate the risks that lie in the Ancient Sands.
