boardgame: Indiana (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
Ticket to Ride: Indiana is a fan-made expansion to the Days of Wonder, Inc. Ticket to Ride series created by Alan R. Moon. A tribute both to Ticket to Ride and the great state of Indiana, this expansion is designed for players who are already well familiar with Ticket to Ride’s standard gameplay and mechanics.

Published by permission for non-commercial use. All rights to the Ticket to Ride name, gameplay, mechanics, and imagery are reserved by Days of Wonder, Inc., who have not endorsed or playtested this expansion.

Unofficial Fan made expansion - Symbols & Graphics Copyright 2004-2016 Days of Wonder, Inc. Produced with permission of Days of Wonder, for non-commercial use only.

A card game where players attempt to turn over and find colored sets of jungle animal cards in order to win the monkey card associated with that color. Each monkey card is worth a certain amount of coconuts. But players need to beware of the Tiger card which speeds up the game considerably. The player with the most coconuts on his or her cards at the end of four rounds is the winner.

Both players control a "snake", using two rotating disks to guide it. Using the snake, they have to catch metal marbles inside a labyrinth, and carry them back to their base. The player who catches the most marbles wins the game.

This is a simple print-and-play fantasy Card Game with pre-constructed decks, simulating small scattered battles in the everlasting greater war of good versus evil.

Each player chooses a side (good or evil), and tries to win the battle by discovering and researching powerful resources to eventually use in the greater war, such as spells, relics and enchantments. Players can hire heroes to hinder the opponent’s exploration, but for that they will have to face enemy dungeons and their challenges, such as guardians and traps, built to protect the resources being processed inside.

So, the goal of each player is basically build dungeons by placing challenges over the table, and then trying to process resources inside. While keeping these resources protected against enemy invasions, the player will also want to disrupt the progress of the opponent, which is acquired by forming a party of heroes to raid the enemy dungeons. During invasions, six-sided dice are rolled to decide success or failure.

Winter, a game of ice and falling snow, is an 18 card and 18 token game divided in two phases: freezing and thawing.

First each player will help to freeze the lake while falling flakes of their color. When all the cards have been placed, the thaw starts and you will have to get your flakes to survive while your opponent's flakes melt.

How many snow will be at the end of winter?

Master of Spies As a player, you take the role of a powerful spymaster in the world of Eldinmoor™, using your underlings to infiltrate the four Powerful Factions of the world. By moving them higher and higher in the courts, you can amass more secret information. But be careful, for other Spymasters are about, and they will try to thwart your every move! Master of Spies is a card game of luck, skill, and betrayal set in the World of Eldinmoor™. The game is for 3 to 6 players and takes about 30 to 45 minutes to play.

boardgame: 1944: Race to the Rhine – Red Ball Express
description:
1944 Red Ball Express is a mini-expansion for the game 1944 Race to the Rhine. It reduces randomness in the gameplay and introduces threats which the allied leaders feared: sudden bad weather and the launching of an Axis counter-offensive.

The expansion includes nine new pursuit and Axis cards and new special rules sheet similar to those of section 15 in the game instructions.

description: Glass Face is a fast-paced, set collecting, dice and slapping game. The characters in the game are trapped in the cards. You need to slap them and set them free and place them into your care. There are 64 characters, on cards numbered 1-8. 8 of each number. There are 8 rounds in the game. Each round there are 8 cards in a line. Each player has 3 die (1-3) Everyone races to roll numbers corresponding to the numbers on the cards. When you roll the same number, you slap it and take it. If you roll a 9 you are frozen, so be careful when rolling 3 dice. Each card you collect is worth one point and if you have more of one number than anyone else, you get the point value of that card. Whoever has the most points at the end wins!

It happens in an instant. The awakening of a world. When events urge that world into a point of sudden chaos, and clarity. In this instance, in this world, it was called Windfall.

Windfall was the sudden manifestation of Aether – raw and unstable magical energy. Unleashed by the whim of the gods, it's presence has changed the world forever.

Windfall is a strategy card game for 2-4 players, where each player takes on the role of a powerful Guardian. These mighty individuals battle for control of the Aether using amazing spells, powerful relics and deadly creatures.

In Windfall, there are two primary ways to play. The first is the more casual Adventure Mode, where players will be playing from a shared group deck, making the best play out of the cards they are dealt. The more advanced mode of play is Drafting, where each game session starts with a unique drafting process before the players battle it out.

Each turn, as your power grows, you will summon creatures, equip weapons, harness ancient relics and cast deadly spells. Each player will battle the other guardians, reducing their life until only one remains standing.

boardgame: A Game of Thrones: The Card Game (Second Edition) – Across the Seven Kingdoms
description:
From the publisher:

Across the Seven Kingdoms is the first Chapter Pack in the War of Five Kings cycle for A Game of Thrones: The Card Game!

In the aftermath of Robert and Eddard’s deaths in King’s Landing, the Great Houses of Westeros have pulled away from each other. Joffrey Baratheon has been crowned king, and now sits the Iron Throne. Robb Stark has already been proclaimed King in the North, and Stannis plots from Dragonstone, brooding on a throne that should have been his. It is the calm before the tempest: war in Westeros is imminent, and above it all, the Dragon’s Tail burns a blood-red trail across the sky.

Enter the War of the Five Kings with Across the Seven Kingdoms and the War of Five Kings cycle! As the Westeros cycle broadly followed the events of A Game of Thrones, so the War of Five Kings cycle moves forward to highlight the thrilling battles and intrigue of A Clash of Kings. Each House calls upon its loyal bannermen in this cycle, and a multitude of new Kings arise to claim the Iron Throne. What’s more, Across the Seven Kingdoms begins to introduce effects based on the Summer and Winter plot traits, allowing the long-lived seasons of Westeros to play a more integral role in the game. With new King versions of Robb Stark and Viserys Targaryen entering the game alongside characters like Arry and Janos Slynt, this Chapter Pack is an explosive beginning to the War of Five Kings cycle!
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: A Game of Thrones – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
From the publisher:

All-Star Wrestling Rejects is a fast paced collectible card game that features a roster of six not-so-super stars competing for the coveted ASWR Championship Gold!

If you're on Offense your objective is to create a sequence of moves that inflicts maximum damage to your opponent and that will, eventually, lead to a pin. Each move (card played) deals a certain amount of damage to your opponent. You play a card by matching up the specific color with the appropriate side to the Transition Move (last Offensive card played). Be careful what move you play, as the higher the damage that is dealt, the more sides that are blacked out. With 48 different Offensive moves to unleash, every match is a unique challenge! On Defense? Then prepare to use your Block cards to limit the options on, and hopefully completely block off, your opponents offensive sequence. Or you could always play that Reverse card . . .

Throw Foreign Objects such as Kendo Sticks, Steel Chairs, Rubber Chickens, and Sporks into the mix to take your opponent out, but watch out! If your opponent plays a Referee Block card it could be game over. Each wrestler also has two Custom Finishers (for a total of 12 included in the game)! Can you survive The Hunchback Hillbilly's "Hurrican-Moron-A", Abe Sherman's "Hip Replacement", or The Bulkster's "Leg Plop"?

The Starter Set includes everything you need to begin playing immediately!

boardgame: Golem Arcana: Gudanna – Raiders of the Blasted Land
description:
From the publisher:

Three new Golems for your Golem Arcana Army:

The Burrow Claw is a highly evasive Warsprite, using its Tunneling Blast to slip away from a melee opponent, ending up in any Region with No Cover that it chooses.
    The Terra Naga is indispensable for dislodging enemies from key locations on the map -- using Scattering Sirocco, a short-range AOE, to displace all Golems from a Region and then using a free displacement on itself, possibly to take their place! 
    The Ravager relentlessly moves through the enemy's lines, leaving destruction in its wake by combining its passive ability that cools down movement when entering an enemy Region and its Duststorm Blade that causes damage to enemies when the Ravager leaves an enemy Region.

Also included are three TDI Golem Cards (matching the figures), two double-sided Victory Point tokens, and four new Relic Cards that can be used to augment any Golem army:

Zyama’s Anointing: allows a Golem to sacrifice some of its own Health in exchange for more Damage on its next attack.
    Davati’s Sacrifice: offers a temporary boost to damage equal to the Golem's armor value, but permanently reduces armor to zero.
    The Lantern of Valli: summons forth the Arena of Valli, a Construct that boosts Accuracy and Damage to all Melee attacks in the region.
    Metr’s Blood Well: calls forth the blood of the earth, allowing its controller to gather Mana from that region until it is destroyed.

Anatolian Fleet is a simulation game of modern day combat for control of the eastern Mediterranean and the Black Sea-Balkan sub-theatre by Naval Task groups. NATO, Russian and Turkish Fleets are pitted against each other and the other minor navies of the region. You must form your ships into Task Forces and attempt to cripple the enemy’s capital ships, carriers, and submarines. Your air assets must be carefully assigned to defensive and offensive missions. Most important, you must protect your supply and replenishment ships, or your fleet will be unable to maintain combat effectiveness.

Each 8-hour game turn is broken into 3 distinct phases (sub, surface, and air) and each player takes turns choosing which phase they wish to conduct. Ex. if the NATO player chooses "Air", they conduct all bombing, shipping strikes, anti-sub, and interception actions for all their aircraft.

Players also use their units to attempt to "detect" enemy forces so that an attack can be made. Ships and aircraft are always detected once a unit moves close enough to them, but players have to roll to try to detect subs.

Optional rules include nuclear weapons and logistics.

Anatolian Fleet is a comprehensive simulation of the myriad elements involved in modern naval command and control. From Technical Term Game Company Anatolian Fleet continues the line of naval games originally from Victory Games Fleet Series.

description: KiBosh! is a classic 3-Card game with Explosive Action! Kibosh31 is a new card game based on a classic 3-card game of 31, also known as Scat or Ride The Bus. The object of the game is to build your 3-card hand to a total of 31, or as close as you can, before your opponents do by drawing and discarding from the deck or discard pile. KiBosh! includes Skip and Reverse cards similar to UNO, and a unique KiBosh card, that when played, forces the next player to dicard their entire hand and draw three new cards. Players are eliminated after losing all their chips in round play and the last player wins the pot.

This game is an example of a typical boy's "Pop-Gun" game or kit that were released by Gabriel & Sons (as early as 1910), then by Parker Brothers, Milton Bradley, and by Built-Rite. It has has a metal pop-gun, so it was manufactured before the depression.

Games like this remained in print through the 1950's before being supplanted by electronic target games like Wild West Shootout!

New threats have reared their monstrous heads in the land of Valeria! Scouts have reported seeing fiery hounds to the South and beasts that bring with them winter's deathly bite to the North. The King's heralds have been dispatched to all of the corners of Valeria to call upon new Adventurers for you to recruit while the workers lay the foundation of new Domains.

Valeria: Card Kingdoms – Flames and Frost adds two new powerful Monster stacks, a new set of Citizens to recruit, and new Domains for you to purchase.

The Kickstarter version of this expansion included Valeria: Card Kingdoms – Expansion Pack #03: Agents and Valeria: Card Kingdoms – Expansion Pack #04: Peasants & Knights
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The year is 1525 in the Republic of Florence, and the city-state prospers under the rule of the Medici family.  It is a time of artistry, of education, and of invention.  All over the land, people are vying to find a patron, one of the rich noble families who will support and encourage a creative soul with their wealth, fame, and good name.

One of the members of these illustrious nobility is looking for a fresh, young mind to carry into the halls of history.  To find the best and brightest, the Patron has organized a challenge.  All geniuses should seek to emulate the greatest man of their time, Leonardo da Vinci, and therefore, the most worthy artist will be the one who can turn Leonardo’s dreams into reality.

As one of the Patron’s hopeful aspirants, you have been invited to one of daVinci’s private laboratories, to compete against other artists in their goal to follow in the late Master’s footsteps.  Your job is to maneuver around the puzzle that is daVinci’s lab, traversing the rotating gears of his workshop, all the while assembling a team of expert engineers to study and be the first to build a replica of one of da Vinci’s designs.  The first recruit to successfully assemble and test the proposed machine will win the coveted spot in the Patron’s household.  Do you have what it takes to win?

The object of the game is to move around a moving gear board, with each gear offering up a different resource for your build - plans, engineers, tools, and materials.  The plans tell the players what other resources they need to collect.  Plans are played with cards, engineers and tools are played on tokens (so they can be left on a board location), and materials are played with shards.

Once a player has all of the resources necessary to complete his build, they can move on to the final gear to assemble their creations, and then return to the beginning of the room.

boardgame: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Back to the Sewer
description:
Turtles travel freely (the number rolled on the die, in any direction) around the game board fighting Shredder's henchmen.  The goal is to try to win enough battles, collect enough cards, and beat Shredder first.  Each board space has one of a handful of actions associated with landing there.  Sewers are used as portals to jump across the board and manhole covers can be used to block those portals.

Game rules borrow heavily from Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Pizza Power Game (1987).
family: Comic Books: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles

description:
Fighting the Islamic State was researched and designed to show why the war in Iraq and Syria is causing so many refugees, who is fighting and why, and what outcomes may occur. The game covers the period 2014-2017, and so projects into the future. In order to accommodate that, we'll update the rules and charts periodically online to reflect what is happening in the field.

The game counters (playing pieces) include combat units for: the Islamic State ("ISIS," "ISIL," "Daesh"), the Syrian Regime (including Russians), the Syrian Rebels, the Kurds, the Iraqis, the Iranian-backed forces (Shia Militia and Hezbollah), and the US/Coalition forces (USA, France, Britain, Turkey).

The map covers all of Syria and Iraq and the border areas. Depicted are the power plants, dams, oil fields, refineries and archaeological sites that the Islamic State exploits for cash. The map is color-coded to reflect the primary religious sects that divide loyalties there: Shia and Sunni Muslims, plus "Tolerants." Fighting in an area that matches your Faction's religion helps you; fighting in the other areas helps your opponents.

The game is for 2-6 players. "Momentum" is a key concept - the player with Momentum starts and ends each turn. The Islamic State also commits "Acts of Terror" every turn that increase the likelihood for Intervention, mostly from the U.S.

The dance arena is ready, and it's time to put on your dancing shoes!

Dance Arena 200 is a hilarious social game. Out of 200 dances from all over the world, it's your job to make your partner guess the correct dance.
Demonstrate! Dance together! Teach! Explain! And best of all - have fun!
Attention: once the sand timer is turned over, your teammate must name as many dances as possible. The fastest players win! With various difficulty levels, this game is suitable for everyone, no matter what your dancing skills are.

V.I.P.E.R. is a super-secret espionage agency based in North America whose directors are managing groups of agents running black ops all over the world.  Operations include surveillance, planting moles, extracting VIPs, sabotage, stealing secrets, frame-ups, staging coups, assassinations, and computer attacks.

V.I.P.E.R. Ops is a strategy board game for 2-4 players in which you take on the role of one of seven agency directors, each with different specializations, and vie with other directors for missions and gathering of resources such as operatives, agents, gadgets, deployment assets, and intelligence.  But plan well, because the other agency directors are competing with you for the same resources and only the most cunning director will be named the new chief director!

During the course of play, you compete with other players to claim missions. Players then use their budget to train agents, claim power cards and resources to help complete missions or even increase future budget. Completed missions give victory points and other rewards that give new missions or help complete other missions. At the end of 8 rounds of play, the director with the most points is named the new chief director and wins the game.

boardgame: Roll for the Galaxy: Bountiful Gaming Grid Promo Tile
description:
Bountiful Gaming Grid is a new home world tile for Roll for the Galaxy.

It is a gray world, and reads: Begin the game with $3 instead of $1 and gain a Novelty (cyan) die into your cup.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: Emblems is a trick-taking and set-collecting card game about grouping country flags. Call out attributes of specific flags and collect as many cards as possible. Earn additional points along the way by using special bonus cards that will boost your total score. The player with the highest score wins.
family: Organizations: The Game Artisans of Canada

description:
Project Baikal is a collaborative village-building eurogame inspired from tower defence video games.
It is a family-friendly card game for 2 to 4 players, 10+ y.o. and one hour in length.

Project Baikal has an original gameplay where all the players can execute their actions at the same time (no player turn).

Theme
The dark winds of war, famine, plague and catastrophic weather have swept the planet, almost scouring it clean of humanity. 
One small enclave still survives though, in a village on the bank of the Lake Baikal in Siberia.
From such humble beginnings the human race can be reborn. And not make the mistakes from the past.

You and your friends are the villagers, keeping out the remnants of the old world and nurturing the hopes of the new. 
You will protect the village from wolves, mercenaries and internal pressures like racism and corruption. 
You will welcome strangers with needed skills, grow your village and rebuild civilisation anew.

Gameplay
From 4 locations, bad events (Sabotage, Wolf pack, Disease, etc) slowly progress towards your newly-formed village.

To defeat those events before they do damage, you will:
- Collect action cards (Move, Meet, Draw, Swap, Build and Give),
- Move to locations (Forest, Lake, Mountain and Road) and
- Encounter those events.

You can also acquire good events (old couple, family, dog, etc) who will help to build useful buildings (Town Hall, Lab, Hospital, etc) using gathered resources (Wood and Stone).

You have 6 turns to complete 6 buildings and win the game.
But beware: you can lose instantly if your village takes too much damage!!!

Acknowledgment: Theme written by Ian Triffit.

boardgame: When Tigers Fight: Tiger of China
description:
In this alternate history, Nazi Germany and Japan were victorious in WW II have turned on each other to start WWW III in 1948.

A small German force helped by Indians attack the Japanese in China. They enter through west and north China or via airlanding, naval movement.

From the publisher:
The goal of Go, family, go! is to foster a greater relationship between the members of the family, a passage of information (such as, for example, the discovery of little secrets, sharing of memories and desires, the confession of our own fears, etc.) and a new-found complicity.
Go, family, go makes available to participants (2 to 6 players) some cards depicting typical family experiences: watching a movie at the cinema, a picnic in the countryside , football game in Tv, stadium or in the garden. 
All the experience cards have multiple interpretations.
At the beginning of the game the family chooses the most eligible candidates experiences, but only one will actually be one you will live; in essence, then, the game goes beyond the same time to perform it and continue with the won experience sharing.

From the publisher (Italian):
L’intento di Go, family, go! è quello di favorire una maggior relazione tra i componenti del nucleo familiare, un passaggio di informazioni (quali, ad esempio, la scoperta di piccoli segreti, la condivisione di ricordi e desideri, la confessione delle proprie paure, etc.) e una ritrovata complicità. 
“Go, family, go” mette a disposizione dei partecipanti (da 2 a 6) alcune tessere raffiguranti esperienze tipicamente familiari: dalla visione di un film al cinema, al picnic in campagna, alla partita di calcio da vedere allo stadio o semplicemente da giocarsi in cortile. Tutte le tessere esperienza hanno molteplici chiavi di lettura.
All’inizio del gioco la famiglia sceglie le esperienze più papabili, ma solo una sarà effettivamente quella che si andrà a vivere; in sostanza, quindi, il gioco travalica il tempo stesso per svolgerlo e continuerà con la condivisione dell’esperienza vinta.

From the publisher:
Twisted is an immersive new tabletop skirmish game for 2 or more players, with a world-class range of 32mm miniatures representing your characters.

Set in an uniquely interesting steampunk universe, Twisted’s absorbing gameplay and linked story-driven missions create a real emotional connection between you and your characters on the tabletop. Every character has an original backstory and distinctive role within the Twisted universe: guiding them through our richly-developed environments and exciting encounters will have you on the edge of your seat and wanting more!

But beware the power of the Engine! The ever-present influence of the mysterious steampunk Engine pervades the world of Twisted. What makes playing Twisted excitingly unique is that the Engine itself brings a new and intriguing element to every encounter: the Eye of the Engine card deck and the unique ‘Favour of the Engine’ game mechanic give the Engine an active role in the actual gameplay.

Maintain the favour of the Engine to enjoy rich rewards, or tempt fate and risk it all!

The Engine is the hidden source of steampunk power permeating the world Twisted. But it is more than just a god-like machine: it has a selfish consciousness seeking to transform the world according to its own designs, and a jealous ego unwilling to share this power!

Rumours of the Engine’s influence are spreading throughout the world of Twisted, and competing factions have emerged seeking to discover the secret access points to the Engine’s power: the Nodes.

The first faction to stumble upon one of these Nodes is the Dickensians: their discovery of an Engine Node hidden deep beneath the darkest sewers of London triggers an explosion of raw steampunk power as the Engine – sensing danger – fights back by summoning the Servants of the Engine!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
DECEMBER 1944, THE BELGIAN ARDENNES...
With the German army seemingly on the verge of defeat, the Führer ordered it to stage a new surprise attack, aimed at splitting the Allied army's advancing on Germany. Timed to utilise the winter weather, Operation Wacht Am Rhein committed Germany's last reserves in an ambitious gamble to turn the tide of the war.

On December 16th, the offensive struck in the Ardennes forests. The unexpected attack would become the largest American battle of the war in Europe...

• BACKGROUND

A general overview of Operation Wacht Am Rhein, from the initial infantry offensive on day one, to clear the routes for the following panzer attacks on day two. It  covers; 6th Panzer Army in the north in hard fighting for the Elsenborn ridge and the deep penetration of Kampfgruppe Peiper. 5th Panzer Army's drive in the centre and the surroundings of St Vith and Bastogne. 7th Army's supporting attack in the south.

• ARMY LIST

Designed to be used with the army lists already published in Battlegroup Overlord, this book contains a single new army list, for the freshly raised Volksgrenadier Divisions, as well as many new additions to the Overlord army lists for refighting the battles of late 1944. It also includes units for the German disguised infiltration teams of Operation Greif.

• SPECIAL RULES

New special rules to adapt the core Battlegroup game to the winter battlefields of 1944-45. The rules recreate the character of the Ardennes fighting, with German infiltration tactics, the morale effect of their powerful surprise attack and the winter weather's limiting of Allied air support, along with the snow falling to impede operations on the ground.

All help to create games with the distinctive feel of the bitter 'Battle of the Bulge'.

• SCENARIOS AND CAMPAIGN

Nine new historical scenarios, including a detailed narrative campaign for Kampfgruppe Peiper's  ill-fated race to the Meuse river.

72 page softback campaign supplement detailing the Battle of the Bulge in the Ardennes December '44 - January '45

Please note you need the Battlegroup Overlord campaign supplement and the mini ruleset to play Wacht am Rhein.

From the publisher:
The Predalien is a Xenomorph spawned from a Predator host. It is the death that nearly all Predators fear, allowing themselves fall victim in such a way is deemed a dishonorable death.

The Predalien is much larger and stronger than the human-spawned Xenomorphs and appears to hold no role within the Hive and instead acts independently hunting and killing until Death!"

User sumary:
The title is a german nursery rhyme. Something like "in love - engaged to be married - married".
3 to 8 players (on the box it is written incorrectly as 3-12 players) can do this funny boardgame with many hearts and luck.
First you have to select, if you are a man (turn your pawn clockwise) or a woman (turn your pawn anticlockwise). Everyone gets 10 hearts, the money in this game. Roll the dice and move your pawn along the coloured fields. On the yellow fields you get a yellow card with your interests (jazz, football, politics). On the green fields you get characteristics (angry, smart). If two players meet each other, they can fall in love (romantic) and check their performance (economically). Sometimes you have to pay with red hearts or get black hearts (white fields) and you have to pay for optimizing your interests and characteristics.
After a specified time all players sum up their red and black hearts and the winner is the heart with the most.

Iron League Junior is a version of the Iron League first Edition board game for kids.
This version was made for parents and kids who have never played with board games before or parents and kids who have thought that the Iron League First Edition rules were very difficult.

Year 2100, the world industry has focused on war machines called IRONS and countries compete to have the best robot Iron teams.
Laboratories look for the best weapons and clubs look for the best pilots.
Countries fight in the famous and hardest league in the world: the IRON LEAGUE.
And there are hundreds of teams that fight in each country to compete in the Iron League.
You are an Iron team coach and you have been hired to take the team to victory and win the Iron League title.
Management and sponsors place their trust in you; we know you are the best and you will take the team to victory.
The decisions related to the selection of your team and of your cards, together with the luck of the dice and of the events cards will be decisive to be able to win or lose.
Score more times with the plasma ball than the opposing team; you can also damage all the rival Irons or hit them with the plasma ball to win points.

The game is for 2-6 players. 
Recommended for 2 players; if you have 2 Iron League games you can join them together and create a fantastic game. 
Recommend age: 7 years up.
 Objective of the game
The aim of the game is to have fun! To win the game you must score higher than the opposite team.
The team that manages to put the plasma ball into the goal area 3 times will win.

Is it really there.. and can you find it?

This is similar to Dobble in that there is a set of cards (of different shapes) with symbols, words and shapes on them. The difference is that there is a second set of cards which have descriptions of objects on them:

"Something that has a tail"

Against the timer each player has to determine if there is an item that matches the description or not.

To make things even more complicated there are also 2 dice which determine which shaped cards you are using in the round.

Each player represents a claimant to the Throne of Vintha'las. The King, in his wisdom has decided that the claimants shouldn't have a civil war to figure out who wins. No, they should prove their mettle by besting one of the horrific beasts that wanders the kingdom. One slight problem, the beasts have killed all the legendary heroes, so the claimants will have to make due with...the rest.

The rest are the Misfits, a group of drunken washed up heroes who now haunt the tavern The Drunken Artist, and are your greatest hope for claiming the throne. Here goes nothing!

There are two ways to win. Be the first team to kill the monster, or be the last player standing.

Players draw cards from the community pile as they pursue the big bad monster, sometimes drawing cards that help them, sometimes drawing cards that help the monster. With their hand they discard cards to utilize different types of moves, like attacking the monster or stealing cards from the other groups of misfits questing after the monster as well. Defeat the monster yourself or wait until it devours everyone else, the choice is yours. Whoever lands the last hit gets the kill, so be weary of opportunistic players!

The evil Slime Queen has stashed her most valuable treasures within her fortress known as Slime Castle. This has attracted many adventurers from far and wide, including yourself, to try and nab her majesty’s treasures! Will you be quick enough to get your treasure and escape before everyone else, or will you be trapped inside Slime Castle forever?

OVERVIEW
The adventurers are using keys produced by their Magic Key Ring to explore the castle. They hope to collect Krystal Keys, the only keys that can unlock the rooms holding all of the Queen’s treasure.

OBJECT OF THE GAME
The object of the game is to steal one of the Queen’s four treasures and be the first player to escape Slime Castle.

The Princess Bride Scwamble Game resembles a certain other word-creation game with a similar name as players take turns putting wooden letter tiles on a grid in order to score points, but in addition to the normal words one might find in the dictionary, players can also score for spelling "mawage", "togeder", and "foweva" as well as iconic phrases such as "Tru Wuv".

boardgame: Alien vs Predator: Alien Queen Expansion
description:
From the publisher:
Queen, monarch, profanities, no matter what you call her the xenomorph queen inspires terror wherever she goes. Inspiring Drones and Soldiers with hive intelligence she can turn a colony thousands strong into chestburster food in mere weeks. And if you are foolish enough to enter her lair, your screams as she rips you apart will be the last sounds you ever make.

boardgame: Alien vs Predator: Alien Crusher Expansion
description:
From the publisher:
The Crusher caste encountered on Acheron (LV-426) Still remains a mystery to USCM. No obvious host of Origin can be determined, originally presumed to be an Adolescent Queen this theory was quickly dismissed.
The Crushers large head crest, which at this time is believed to be bulletproof, Becomes the Prime Weapon of Choice, Charging its prey to inflict massive blunt force trauma.

boardgame: Alien vs Predator: Alien Royal Guard Expansion
description:
From publisher:
Strongest and most feriocius of them all are known to guard Queen herself.

From the publisher:
Caterpillar P-5000 Powered Work Loader, commonly referred to as a Power Loader.
This useful piece of equipment  can be found at most human colonies.
Primarily used to lift and relocate heavy objects the power loader is a true asset to Human engineering and infrastructure.
You can watch that puppy scream as you throw it out an air lock.

boardgame: Alien vs Predator: Predator Hellhound Expansion
description:
From the publisher:
The Hellhounds are a canine-like species domesticated by the Predators for use in hunting.
 Much like The Bloodhounds of Earth they are used to chase down prey allowing a swifter, more effective hunt to be executed. That said the Predators have also been known to release the hellhounds with intent to cause panic and thin the ranks of their opponents during battle."

language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
In Anaxi, players are presented with category prompts, and they want to think of words or phrases that fit multiple categories at the same time — but without matching another player's answers.

In each of five rounds, players draw a single blue, red and orange card. These round cards have clear plastic on one-third of them and a word written on the other two-thirds. Players overlap these cards on a scoring chart that shows the value of a word or phrase that encompasses two or three of these categories. Players have a limited amount of time to write down as many words or phrases as they can for each pair of categories and for all three categories. They then compare answers and score points for those answers unique to themselves.

Whoever has the most points after five rounds wins!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Players in Spirot play pixellated cards from their hands in order to create sequences of three cards that have the same color (or all different colors) as well as the same pixel pattern (or all different pixel patterns).

To set up, place the nine board cards in a 3x3 grid, shuffle the spirot cards, and deal six cards to each player. Reveal spirot cards from the top of the deck until you find a matching image card that can cover each of the four corners of the board card. Each spirot card has one of the nine images on the board cards, with this image in one of three colors and one of three pixel patterns.

On a turn, place a spirot card from your hand onto a board card. If you create a sequence, you score one point, then take another turn. If you don't create a sequence, your turn ends. At the end of your turn, refill your hand to six cards.

Once someone has five points, complete the round so that everyone has had the same number of turns. Whoever has the most points wins!
Melvin + Simon is a collage art memory game.

Spectracube offers six different games, using a set of primary and a set of secondary color cubes.

SPECTACULAR SHUFFLE
Roll primary and secondary Spectracubes to create the highest scoring spectrum straights, pairs and 3 of a kind! Do you take the risk to roll again?

GREEDY GRAB
Be the quickest player to roll colors that match the center cubes and grab them! Roll again before other players and grab what’s left!

YOU SCORE!
There are so many ways to score in this game that you may think you’re way ahead of the competition! But watch out—a few quick rolls, and your competition could catch up!

BLOTCH OUT!
Try not to Blotch Out! Avoid the “Color Blotch” Spectracubes. Sound easy? Not so fast…You’ll learn a lot about chance in this game!

RUMMY ROLL
Quick eyes and fast rolls get you winning—as you place your Spectracubes into Rummy straights and sets!

SPECTRA-SLAM!
Roll the Spectra-Slam cubes to win. This game gets more intense as players approach the 15 point threshold to winning!

boardgame: Zanzibar: Unguja (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
This map is an unofficial fan made expansion. Symbols & Graphics Copyright © 2004-2015 Days of Wonder, Inc. Produced with permission of Days of Wonder, for non-commercial use only.

This follows the principles of the basic Ticket to Ride game on a special board with special cards and rules. The object of the game is to gain the most points by travelling various routes and outwitting other players. You will need the train pieces and the train cards from either the US or Europe base games (or you can buy them from the Days of Wonder shop.

There is an extra set of Lucky Dip cards: some are for the cities, each with a value to be earned by visiting that city: others give you extra facilities to complete your tickets. These cards are dealt/picked up alongwith tickets in the same quantity and with the same 'keep/dispose' rules.There are also up to 9 bonuses for the end-of-game scoring.

Zanzibar has two islands and Unguja may be played stand-alone or with Pemba. The boards do not have any grey tracks or parallel tracks. There are flight options as an extra enhancement.

There are many new options in the rules: exchange all your cards at the start but keep all the new ones: discard 5 cards and pick up 5 new ones, as your turn: and many more. You can also call time when your wagon stock reaches 3, if you want, giving you the advantage of stopping other players completing tickets.

boardgame: Zanzibar: Pemba (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
This map is an unofficial fan made expansion. Symbols & Graphics Copyright © 2004-2015 Days of Wonder, Inc. Produced with permission of Days of Wonder, for non-commercial use only.

This follows the principles of the basic Ticket to Ride game on a special board with special cards and rules. The object of the game is to gain the most points by travelling various routes and outwitting other players. You will need the train pieces and the train cards from either the US or Europe base games (or you can buy them from the Days of Wonder shop.

There is an extra set of Lucky Dip cards: some are for the cities, each with a value to be earned by visiting that city: others give you extra facilities to complete your tickets. These cards are dealt/picked up alongwith tickets in the same quantity and with the same 'keep/dispose' rules.There are also up to 9 bonuses for the end-of-game scoring.

Zanzibar has two islands and Unguja may be played stand-alone or with Pemba. The boards do not have any grey tracks or parallel tracks. There are flight options as an extra enhancement.

You can also call time when your wagon stock reaches 3, if you want, giving you the advantage of stopping other players completing tickets.

"Bigside pocket" is standalone game of bigside. not expansion for bigside*


あなたはオールドボ-ス!
しかし、あなたには信頼を置ける部下がいない。
周りにはあなたの位置を狙う奴、 また、ソイツのために、あなたのことを裏切ろうとする奴らばかり! 奴らを見つけ出し、最後までボスの地位を守り切ろう!

Your are a old boss !
You must be find traitor and new boss !!
That's the way you survive.

Bigside pocket is a party game for multiple people together.One of the players becomes the Old Boss. The other players draw 2 cards. In turn, they use one of the cards. The card left on your hand becomes your identity. There are 8 cards total (2 per each Character) so it is possible to guess others' card. After all players use their own card, the Old Boss chooses a player to find the betrayer. Players get the income according to the result. The game ends after all players once played the Boss. The player with the highest money wins the game.

This game is release 2016 kobe game market.

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 2 – B-17
description:
From the Publisher:

The Boeing B-17 “Flying Fortress” is a four-engine heavy bomber aircraft developed in the 1930s for the United States Army Air Corps (USAAC). The B-17 was primarily employed in the daylight precision strategic bombing campaign of World War II against German industrial and military targets. From its introduction in 1938, the B-17 Flying Fortress evolved through numerous design advances.

Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW2 Wings of Glory® Special Pack is a ready-to-play large size model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW2 Wings of Glory game product.

In each pack you will find all you need to play with the airplane: a special base with gaming stats, a variable altitude flying stand, and a specific deck of maneuver cards, as well as specific rules and components necessary to use the airplane.

Bonus contents in this pack include one double-sided industrial complex card and special rules.

Comes in a Memphis Belle version and Bit o Lace version.

The game of Sovrano is a straight-forward and easy to learn game of strategy and cunning.  However, beneath the relative simplicity lies a depth of complexity that will keep the game interesting and re-playable. Assemble your guards and prepare to lead your emperor to the throne and absolute power!

Sovrano is a game for two players that typically lasts between 15 to 30 minutes.  Each player has 11 pieces (1 Emperor, 8 Guards, 2 Archers).  The center space of the board is the Throne, while on opposite ends of the center row lie the Tower-spaces.

The objective of the game is to be the player who scores the most points by capturing the Tower-spaces (1 point granted per space held at the start of each turn).

The second objective of Sovrano is to be the first player to move their Emperor onto the center space (the Throne-space) and hold it until the start of his or her next turn.  This grants that player 3 points and ends the game.

In Yalla you compete to be the best falafel stand in the Old City!  Take and fill orders to garner praise from reviewers and customers to be the first five star falafel stand!

Game play is a furious mix of dice and card drafting.  Yalla is a very simple to learn and quick playing strategy game..

From the publisher:

Welcome to 1879! The British Empire, passing through an interdimensional portal, has found a new world, the Gruv, chock-full of resources and land to be exploited. In the process, though, they've run headlong into the Samsut, descendants of ancient Babylonians who've been living there for over three thousand years. Now the might of the Empire clashes with the weird science of the Samsut. The Saurids, a reptilian race native to the new world, contest with both empires to retain their ancestral lands. Who will control the Gruv? That's up to your battles to decide! This miniatures game focuses on the military aspects of the period providing the Universal 18 ruleset and basic information about the British and Samsut forces, and a series of Forcebooks detailing the various military forces on Earth and on the Gruv.

boardgame: Legends of the Ancient World: Ebon Rebirth
description:
Ebon Rebirth is a programmed adventure using Dark City Games Legends of the Ancient World rule set. These rules provide gameplay very similar to that of Melee and Wizard from Metagaming. This adventure is compatible with those games as well.

Battles are fought with counters on a hex map that is included with the game.

From the publisher:
"...You are here for one simple reason; these are lean times. Resources are scarce, not enough for the various peoples of the Wildlands to survive, much less the scavengers. Symbonn has a reputation for providing opportunity to the desperate. It will take some bloody work, but you are confident that you will rise to the challenges that you will face, and survive..."
family: Series: Legends of the Ancient World (Dark City Games)

description: Mage Wars: Academy – Priestess Expansion includes a new mage for use in both Mage Wars: Academy and Mage Wars: Arena. Now you can play as a holy Priestess competing to prove that the way of light is absolute by letting her smite the wicked and summon virtuous allies.
This mini-expansion introduces the Elementals that can change the suit of a card you play on your turn giving you more control on when to score!

This Bonus Pack introduces a new Event mechanic. Players shuffle in Events in to the deck and when they are revealed through card drafting, players do the action on the cards. Along with the new Event cards, the Bonus Pack also includes 4 new Adventurers - Baron, Champion, Bishop, and Sorceress.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Jugger TTG: Dogskull Heroes is a tabletop board game simulation of a real-life modern sport which is based on a fictional gladiatorial sport featured in a 1980's cult movie, "The Blood of Heroes" (also known as "The Salute of the Jugger" and "Salute to the Jugger").

The 1989 movie, "The Blood of Heroes", filmed in Australia and starring Rutger Hauer portrays a post-apocalyptic world, similar to the Mad Max movies, in which the central focus of the bedraggled society is a brutal gladiatorial game called "The Game". In this movie a rag-tag team of players, called "Juggers", fight their way through the barren lands to earn the right to play in the big league of The Nine Cities.

The cult following of the movie saw the emergence of Jugger as a sport where the brutal weapons in the movie were replaced with padded foam weapons (also known as spars or pompfen). First emerging in Germany, Sport Jugger quickly spread through out Europe, Australia, and the Americas. So popular has the sport become that in 2014 the German World Masters Tournament, The Deutsche Meisterschaft, attracted 74 Jugger teams from across the world.

In 2015 Jugger fanatic and games designer, Stuart Shaw, took on the challenge of creating a tabletop board game by fusing his game system, Siostam, with modern Sport Jugger rules. The result is an exhilarating, fast paced action tabletop game, Jugger TTG: Dogskull Heroes, where you get to take charge of your own Jugger team and lead them to victory and glory against the best Jugger players in the world.

User summary:
理想の納豆 (Risou no Natto) means Ideal Natto in English. Nattō is fermented soya beans, a side dish which people either love or hate.

Players work to make their ideal bowl of Natto rice by adding the toppings they like, avoiding the toppings they don't, and mixing their Natto to the perfect gooey consistency.

Each player gets two "preference" cards that only they see. These dictate the toppings they want at the end of the game. Players take turns drawing and playing topping cards and mix cards onto several piles in the middle of the table. Whenever they feel the time is right, they can choose to dig in and "eat" their Natto by choosing a pile from the centre of the table. Once all players have decided to eat, scores are totalled, and the person with their most ideal (based on the score from topping cards) and sufficiently mixed Natto (based on the value of the mix cards) is the winner.

The game was first published in 2014 and a new edition produced for Spiel 2016 with Japanese rules in the box, and English, German and French rules added outside the box for buyers.

Publisher's summary
In the card game Ideal Natto, you mix the packets of natto on the table with different toppings like green onion and raw egg, stirring it multiple times until it is prepared just so. And then you must grab your favorite natto before someone else does!

This is a game of reading other players’ intentions and secretly setting your eyes on your “Ideal Natto” – eat the most delicious natto to win the game!

Our game 'Ideal Natto' is officially endorsed by the Japan Natto Cooperative Society Federation.

Guild Halls introduces 4 new buildings - 1 for each type. When built, a Guild Hall will reward the player with Victory Points if they have the most of that Guild Hall's building type.

Guild Halls also introduces rapid-recruit Adventurers that only require 1 building type icon in your village to recruit. They offer some quick but low points and immediate rewards.

Guild Halls will build upon the Event cards mechanic. Skeletons, Jesters, and more!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Ultra Coins is a collectible coin game that uses solid brass coins to represent a player's army that is comprised of characters from fantasy, science fiction, and horror. A coin can represent a force of soldiers or a powerful individual.  Some coins are more powerful than others and all coins have different special abilities.

Each coin is 2 inches in diameter. The front of a coin has the artwork representing the character.  On the outer perimeter are numbers that represent the character's attack and defense values.  The inner ring contains the character's name, rank, and special abilities.

The back of the coin has a tip which allows the coin to spin.  The coin can spin on a game mat, notebook, table, etc.

The spinning of the coin represents the random element of an attack or defense.  After the coin is spinning, a players stops it with their finger and uses the number next to their finger.

There is no game board, no dice, and no markers.  The only thing you need are the coins.  The game is modular which allows players to battle with only a few coins.  They can expand the battles as they collect additional coins.

Description from the publisher:

In times of yore, loyal knights would ride out to do their king's bidding. These knights have stumbled into the dungeon and are now in your way. Draw your steel and show them who's the king around here. Munchkin Knights has fifteen shiny new cards to add some Knight to your day!

Description from the publisher:

Show your Munchkin pride in any game using meeple-type figures! The Munchkin Meeples set includes six different meeple designs, each of which has one Mega-Meeple and four Non-Mega-Meeples — and they have in-game bonuses!

Quadropolis: Game Store is a single tile that functions like a shop in Quadropolis — meaning that it earns its owner points at the end of the game based on the number of customers in it — but it features a black die in one corner that provides additional scoring when used in combination with Quadropolis: Tric Trac, a public utility tile that earns its owner one point for each game store on that player's board.

Quadropolis: Game Store exists in 23 different versions, with the only difference among them being the store's name and logo. These tiles were distributed to customers who bought Quadropolis from the associated game stores in France.

You can get the Quadropolis: Game Store in these 23 variations: AgoraJeux (AJ), BellaCiao, C’est le Jeu, Descartes Bordeaux, Descartes Lyon, Espace-temps, La Diagonale du Fou, Labyrinthe, Le Bazar du Bizarre, Le Village du Jeu, Ludibay, Ludifolie, Ludocortex, Les Ludovores, Librairie Martelle, Meisa, Philibert, Le Repaire du Dragon, Rocambole, Sur La Route du Jeu, Sortilèges, Starplayer and Troll2Jeux.

Quadropolis: Tric Trac replaces Public Service tile 1416, and in addition to functioning as a Public Service for scoring, this tile gives its owner one additional point for every Quadropolis: Game Store tile in that player's city.

Quadropolis: Tric Trac was distributed at the Tric Trac booth at the 2016 Festival International des Jeux.

Fight the elements and each other in a race to become the greatest Bone Hunter in the Badlands!

Based on Dinosaur Fossil hunting in the late 1800's, Dinosaur Wars is a board game for 2 - 4 players with a playing time of around 2 hours.

Object: You are a seasoned bone-hunter on assignment to a renowned museum.

Your mission is to field expeditions into the Badlands to plunder the Dinosaur Digsites there. Landing on a Digsite enables you to pick up or “excavate” Bones Cards.

To maximize your haul of Bones Cards you will need equipment and the services of a sometimes disreputable crew. The more crew and equipment you have the greater your haul of bones cards. The race is then on to get your precious cargo back to your museum where complete sets of bones cards have a points and money value. Money enables you to fund your expeditions and points are recorded on the pad provided. The game continues until there are no more bones cards left to “excavate”. The winner is the player with the most points.

But beware! The attentions of Bone Rustlers and Claim Jumpers, the pitfalls of Calamity Cards and shady dealings at the Bones Bazaar may leave your collection in tatters, trust no-one and have no regard for the ethics of bone digging!

description:
Element Circle is the tactical, magical card game. The game is availlable to play digitally online and you can buy a paper version to play with your friends.

In Element Circle the players battle each other over the control over the four elements Earth, Water, Fire and Air. In the circle appear four elements at random that can be used by any player and the players obtain spells from the same deck. The players then choose their innate elements that only they can use and take turns to use their spells to beat the other players. Each element has their own signature effect.


     Earth is a solid and cannot affect the elements.
     Water is a fluid and alters the state of the game.
     Fire is destruction and intense.
     Air is motion and mobility.


Element Circle is played in rounds. Depending on how the players act, a round can be very short or relatively long. The trick to winning Element Circle is to be able to see how to play your hand best, predict the actions of your opponents and to best judge the strength of your own hand as well as those of your opponents. There is a lot of randomness in the rounds that keeps it interesting to play. Even the best players will lose some rounds, but will eventually win more rounds than others. Normally the first player to win three rounds wins the game, but when playing online each win increases your score and rating.

Dungeon War is a game for 2-4 players, where the player control groups of Heroes and Monsters in an expanding dungeon and must gather as much treasure as they can, while using their monsters to defeat the other treasure Hunters.

The game uses tiles to expand the dungeon so no two game will ever be exactly the same. Using a quick and simple turn based system, even with 4 players the game is so fast paced, no one will get bored waiting for their turn. The 62 Event cards add constant challenges to the expanding dungeon and will make even the most seasoned treasure hunter squirm as the dungeon deals its next deadly torment their way.

When the event deck is exhausted or if only one hero remains in the dungeon the game comes to an end and its time to count up the treasure and whom ever has the most will win the game.

Monopolis (eng. Monopoly) game was made in Lithuania when this country was part of the Soviet Union, like the other Baltic states.

Just before the end of Soviet Union, politics became softer and allowed more things from the western culture like this game for example (not actual Monopoly version). Such things became popular among people, but the rule that everything had to be written in Russian remained, that's why this game's Rules are written in Russian.

The yellow lid of the game box only show the "shareholder cards" as well as some of the little "Chance cards". 
Each side of the little game board has 3 company spaces, 1 railwaystation and a "destiny" space (showing a yellow circle with some image).

The 23 spaces on the board are from onwards: 
Pepsi-Cola -10%-circle - Marlboro - Knïb (Kiev) - Las Vegas - a Jail corner - Philips - policeman in circle - Opel - Alma-Ata - Ford - cowboy on totem pole (Destiny corner) - Parker - sun and cube in circle - Toyota - New York - Asahi - judge corner - Atlantic - sun and cube in circle - Honda - London and Panam.

The instruction on the destiny spaces respectively reads:

     side 1: pay 10% of your funds and put it in the "destiny pool".
     side 2 with the policeman: pay a fine of 500 and put it in the "pool".
     side 3 and 4: test your luck by taking a Chance card.  It is remarkable to see that the "Destiny corner" is officially used as the "Free Parking corner" in the unofficial family rules in Parker Brother editions!


Besides, there is an inner circle in the center of the game board, called Klondike, being an essential part of the game. Klondike can be entered any moment, by paying the Bank 500. Starting point is the pointing hand as in the main part of the game. The Rules for this section are as follows:

     Landing on each of the empty yellow spaces player receives 1000 from the Bank.
     Landing on the space with the gold coin player receives 5000 and the right to return to the main game.
     Landing on the space with the money bag player receives 10000 and the right to return to the main game.
     Landing on the space with the pistol player loses all his money received in Klondike and has no right to leave the Klondike.
     Landing on the space with the black cat player loses all his money received in Klondike and must return one of his most precious shareholder cards to the Bank as well as one of the cheapest he owns, but he still has the right to return to the main game.
     Landing on the space with the pointing hand player must return to the main game with the money he collected in Klondike.
     Philips shareholder card. Instead of property deeds players receive a shareholder card  from the banker the moment they buy shares of a property or station. 


How does that work? Let us take the Philips property to explain:
The first share, together with the "shareholders card"  (like a "property deed" in Parker Bros. Monopoly) can be bought for 16 the moment you land on this free space. A next player landing on this space have to pay you 4.

When you are short of cash you can pawn this card with the Bank for 8.

A second share can be bought from the Bank for 160 after which a visitor must pay you 40. The value of this share however is 140. In order to remember how many shares you own on this property you place the blue plastic paperclip on line 2.

In this way one can collect up to 5 shares by finally paying 6400. That moment the value of your total shares is 3200 and any visitor has to pay you 1600 each time he lands on this property. Should you run out of cash you can pawn this card with the Bank for 3200. 
There are 18 small (56x69 mm) yellow Chance cards, to be placed on the red dot in the centre of the board.

Some of the instructions on these Chance cards read:

     Konkurentu dovana gangsteriui 1000$ = Player receives 1000 from each other player and he gets the right to throw the dice 3 times. 


In addition he is allowed to rob another player, f.e. the moment he lands on a space owned by another player the owner must hand over all profit to this player. 

     Nenumatytos pajamos 50% = Player receives 50% of his own properties (stocks and stations) from the Bank.
     Mafija 1000 $ = Player has to proceed to the Destiny corner and pay 1000 fine.
     Air Mail = Player does not move per space but per board side. On doing so he is allowed to land on a space he likes, except on the corner spaces. However, he does receive 200 passing the Start corner.


Black cat = Player must return one of his shareholder cards to the Bank, without the right to redeem it.

The procedure with the stations is somewhat different: When buying a station player has to pay 800 to the Bank and in the same time he receives a hexagonal, plastic chip. The more stations a player owns the bigger profit he gets. The moment another player lands on your station he owes you 200 per station. Should an owner runs out of cash he can, like with the other properties, pawn a station with the Bank for 400 with the opportunity to redeem the station when he has enough money again.

The money consists of rather a number of plastic coins in various colors and diameters. The 11 denominations are: 1 - 2 - 5 - 10 - 20 - 50 - 100 - 500 - 1000 - 5000 and 15000.

boardgame: Epic Card Game: Tyrants – Draka's Rage
description:
The Dargothian Mountains may seem completly wild and untamed, but Draka reigns supreme there. Those who don't submit to his rule risk a fiery death from above.

Epic Card Game: Tyrants is an expansion for the Epic Card Game that's packaged in four non-random booster packs (Draka's Rage, Markus' Command, Helion's Deceit, and Raxxa's Revenge), with each booster pack containing twelve new game cards and a double-sided token.

boardgame: DC Deck-Building Game: Crossover Pack 5 – The Rogues
description:
The DC Comics Deck-Building Game Crossover Pack series is a way to breathe new life into your game with just a few tweaks. Grab your favorite DC set and instead of using the Super Heroes and Super-Villains from that set, use the ones from this pack instead!

DC Comics Deck-Building Game: Crossover Pack 5 – The Rogues features a full set of new playable Super Villains, Super Heroes to defeat, and new Main Deck cards such as Equipment, Locations and Super Powers.

description: Match Quilt is a card game where players choose quilt blocks to arrange in their sampler quilt to make the best final product. Blocks earn points for matching various aspects of neighboring cards, like color and difficulty level. Final scoring also rewards an even distribution of colors and patterns. The advanced mode brings in special card effects like one-time actions and ongoing abilities to make choices even more interesting for the quilters. Make it sew!

Players draw various cards permitting them to gain points, steal cards from others, block steal attempts, and force others to discard their hands. The objective is to acquire a requisite number of points while blocking the efforts of other players to do the same.

The game moves very quickly, focused on fast-paced action rather than high-level strategy.

Everyday Strife features 104 unique playable cards, each with its own value and function. Players attempt to win by holding specific categories, combinations, or values of cards. They draw cards and also attempt to steal them from other players. Various mechanisms are provided for countering steal attempts and sabotaging the efforts of others.

The game integrates rock-paper-scissors for some of its competitive events, as well as special requirements for playing certain cards, such as using phrases and accents.
SezWho? is a trivia game in which players attempt to identify quotations from famous individuals in the fields of Gossip; Politics; Arts, Science & Literature; Sports; Movies, and TV.

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 2 – Kawasaki Ki-61 Hien
description:
The Kawasaki Ki-61 Hien (“flying swallow”) was a Japanese World War II fighter aircraft. Over 2.500 Ki-61s were produced, first seeing action around New Guinea in 1943, and continuing to fly combat missions throughout the war. From 1944, Ki-61 were also employed effectively in aerial ramming attacks against B-29 Superfortresses.

Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW2 Wings of Glory™ Airplane Pack is a ready-to-play model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW2 Wings of Glory game product.
In each pack you will find all you need to play with the airplane: a special base with gaming stats, a variable altitude flying stand, and a specific deck of maneuver cards.
Tile laying game.  Each tile has a letter and a value.  A = 1 ... Z = 800.  Tiles are laid scrabble fashion onto the playing surface to create words and match values.

boardgame: Krimi total: Die Yacht der Macht
description:
General info on Krimi Total: Murder mystery game in which players act out their role and try to solve the mystery.

Info on this particular case: Blue skies, clear waters, endless white beaches frame the beatiful island republic of Bananas Grandes. For three dacades El Presidente Emilio Mentirosa has governed this trobical island. He likes to be celebrated by the powerful, beatiful and rich. Tonight the champaign is on ice, latin rhythms come from the speakers on the sundeck of the presidential yacht. Tonight El Presidente ceöebrates his 60th birthday. As the party is about to begin a body is found on board. Is El Presidentes life in danger? Or maybe even the future of Bananas Grandes?

In Die Yacht der Macht there are 8 obligatory roles (can't be played with less than 8): 4 male, 4 female. One optional role, the inspector, can be either male or female. The roles include: El Presidente, his wife and son, the son's girlfriend, the presidents couselor, the general, a rich heiress to the banana plantations and an american buesinessman, looking to bring tourism to Banans Grandes.

Modular track system 1/64 scale

You can build every NASCAR speedway for a low cost.

Scale: 1 space by second. (Except Bristol 2 spaces by second)

An expansion is planned to build any pitlane, but this is not necessary for the game
since pitstops are resolved by Action cards.

For most of Ovals, there are enough inside sections to build pitlane and a 3rd inside lane.

Can be used by children to play with their NASCAR and INDYCAR diecast 1/64.

Modular track system 1/87 scale

You can design every F1 tracks, from the actual or future seasons for a low cost.

An expansion is planned to build any pitlane, but this is not necessary for the game
since pitstops are resolved by Action cards.

IntroductionAlpha is a territory and connection game for two players: Black and White. It's played on the cells of a hexhex board, which is initially empty. The suggested board size is between 5 and 8 cells per side. Both players must have access to a sufficient number of black and white stones.

				
				
					DefinitionsA group is a set of connected, like-colored stones. A single stone is also a group.

				
				
					PlayBlack plays first, then turns alternate. On their turn, a player must place one stone of any color on an empty cell.

The game ends when the board is full. A player's score is the product of the size of the largest group of their color and the number of distinct groups of their color. The player with the higher score in the final position wins. In the event of a tie, whoever has fewer stones of their color on the board wins.

				
				
					Pie ruleThe pie rule is used to make the game fair. This means that White will have the option, on his first turn only, to change sides instead of making a regular move.

Les Poilus: Aux Ordres! is an expansion for The Grizzled that consists of three elements:


    Rules for solitaire play.
    New rules for playing with two players.
    Mission cards that present players with new challenges that scale in difficulty based on their experience.


On this last topic, mission cards come in easy, normal and hard difficulty levels, and based on the experience of the players, you set up a deck of missions.

Each round, the mission leader draws two of these cards, then decides which one to attempt. These missions might affect the number of starting cards in a round or allow players not to lose a round due to a specific condition, such as night or rain.
