description:
In RanaDrama, the Apprentices of an old Wizard got accidentially polymorphed into Little Frogs. Still in the Wizard's Tower, they have to try to overcome all the defence mechanisms that are targetting them now as "vermin". The final cure is outside of the Tower but only one of them can win. Be either clever or fast ... preferably both.

The game works with a modular board, where the different rooms of the wizard's Tower can be influenced and moved with different magic spells. While exploring the rooms for spells and wonderous items, the frogs have to avoid the guardians and traps of the tower... and sometimes even the rival apprentices, which are in the same Situation now too.

RanaDrama is a highly competative game where creative use of the numerous spells is the key.

At the start of every turn a Player has to determine by a dice roll, which persona (the one of the wizard's apprentice or the frog's) is dominant. Based on that, and modified by certain magical effects the movement Points are determined.

A Player can use theese Points to hop around on the room Cards, casting spells or searching their square on the room Card to find the key out of the Tower or other useful magical artefacts.

Only the Player with the Crystal key can pass the Guardian at the end of a floor, but all the other Players can hunt him down to get the key from him.

From the publisher:

Halo: Fleet Battles, Ensign Edition makes it even easier for players around the world to begin gaming exciting spaceship battles set in the fantastic Halo Universe. Inside this 2-player box set players will find all the models and rules they need to start playing. The set allows for six Battle Groups (four UNSC and two Covenant) of highly detailed spaceships to be fielded, providing players with a superb base for future fleet expansion.
The Ensign Edition consists of the following components:


     128-page Core Rulebook
     16x UNSC Plastic Ships
     8x Covenant Plastic Ships
     Plastic Formation Bases
     Token, Statistic and Overlay Sheets
     Punch-Out Scenery Sheet
     2x Fleet Commander Data Sheets
     30x Custom Halo Dice and 2x Standard D6
     Reference Sheet
     Assembly Instructions


Enough gaming pieces for hours of fun!

Battle Monkeys is a 2-4 player strategic combat card game, with Monkeys! Players build their army of Soldier monkeys with one goal in mind....Complete...World....Domination!

Players build their "Army" of 4 soldiers that are used to both attack enemies and defend their general. Soldiers are placed face up in front of each player and have varying power levels.

On your turn, you draw a card, have the option to place a soldier in your army, attack an enemy player. Attacking/Defending is similar to most TCGs. Players also have spell cards that allow them to perform a variety of task such as stealing soldiers from another army, drawing more cards, destroying everyone's hand or army, and more.

Each player starts with 20 total life and the game continues until there is only one player left alive.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Political Story Telling Game. Free Print-to-Play. Designed by Ronald Villaver. CC Attribution-NonCommercial (CC BY-NC) Are you sure about your bets this coming election? What makes your candidates better than the rest? Use showmanship, quick wits, and satirical selling skills to convince the voting public that your team has what it takes to solve our nation’s current issues.

DISCLAIMER:
This is a work of fiction. Names, characters, businesses, places, events, locales, and incidents are either the products of the designer’s imagination or used in a fictitious manner. Any resemblance to actual persons, living or dead, or actual events is purely coincidental.

Setup:
Shuffle the Political Issues and Candidate Cards into their respective piles. Give each player a starting hand of three Candidate cards. First player goes to whoever recently tried to weasel their way out of a violation and got away with it.

Basic Game: 3 – 5 Players
Plataporma is played in a series of rounds where players try to get the vote of the Voter player to earn points. The first to 3 points wins!

Step 1 : Reveal the Issue
The Voter chooses a card in their hand that will be the Current Issue for the round. They then read it aloud and put it on the table for everyone to see.

Step 2 : Promote Candidates
Each other player (starting from the player to the Voter’s left) chooses 2 cards from their hand to play as their candidates (President and Vice-President pairing). They must explain to the Voter why their candidates are the best to address the Current Issue. Once per round, if a player doesn’t like the candidates in their cards, they may discard as many cards from their hand to draw that many new cards.

Step 3 : Vote for favorite Candidate
After each player has taken their turn to promote their candidates, the Voter chooses which pair they liked the most. The player whose pair was picked wins the round and gets a point! They put the Current Issue card face down in front of them to track their points.

Step 4 : Prepare for the next round
Everyone discards all used cards and then draws back up to 4 cards. If there are no more cards in the draw deck, shuffle the discarded cards back into the draw deck.

Step 5 : Start the next round
Start a new round with the player that won the last round as the new Voter for the round. The first player to get 3 points wins!

Debate Variant: 6 – 10 Players

The round begins with the Leader also known as the Chairman, drawing a Political Issue and reads out loud.

The players to the Chairman’s right and left are now debate opponents and selects their respective electoral match up. The rest of the players are called voters. The opponents have 2 minutes to explain why their pair is better than the other in addressing the political issue.
The chairman counts to 3; voters simultaneously raises their right or left hand to indicate their preferred pair in reference to the chairman’s right or left. The Opponent with the most votes wins a point. Chairman breaks ties.

Players shuffle back played candidate cards and draws up to their beginning hand. Player to the chairman’s left is the new leader for the next round. Play continues until a player reaches 3 points, that player is the winner.
Party Variant: 10+ Players

It plays the same as the basic game except for a few things: The voter draws 3 political issue cards and chooses to play 2. Players must address both issues.` The rest of the players only chose one candidate card and must declare to the other players their selection. Next they must convince someone else to be their running mate. If they are unable to get a partner, they are forced to use just one candidate. Players must do this selection as quick as possible.
Play goes as normal, except they now have 2 minutes per team, and both are making their presentation in tandem. Speaking and acting in character is highly recommended.
The voter chooses the winning pair, both players get a point each by giving them their Political Issue card. If a player was forced to use one candidate, they only get 1 point, represented by the Political Issue Card.

This work is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International License. To view a copy of this license, visit http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/. All characters appearing in this work are fictitious. Any resemblance to real persons, living or dead, is purely coincidental. Opinions expressed by players playing this game are solely of their own and do not express the views or opinions of anyone else.

If you liked this game, please check out my other designs at http://www.scripturedyou.com

For updates and print-to-play files, always check the repository at https://github.com/rvillaver/PlataPorma

boardgame: Hokkaido (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
This map is an unofficial fan made expansion. Symbols & Graphics Copyright © 2004-2015 Days of Wonder, Inc. Produced with permission of Days of Wonder, for non-commercial use only.

This follows the principles of the basic Ticket to Ride game on a special board with special cards and rules. The object of the game is to gain the most points by travelling various routes and outwitting other players. You will need the train pieces and the train cards from either the US or Europe base games (or you can buy them from the Days of Wonder shop.

There is an extra set of Chance cards: some are for the cities, each with a value to be earned by visiting that city: others give you extra facilities to complete your tickets. These cards are dealt/picked up alongwith tickets in the same quantity and with the same 'keep/dispose' rules.There are also up to 9 bonuses for the end-of-game scoring.

The board does not have any grey tracks or parallel tracks - but you can travel to Tokyo by air or shinkansen for extra points.

There are many new options in the rules: exchange all your cards at the start but keep all the new ones: discard 5 cards and pick up 5 new ones, as your turn: earn double points: and many more. You can also call time when your wagon stock reaches 3, if you want, giving you the advantage of stopping other players completing tickets.
Be the first to discover the answer to a question whose letters are contained in the last word.

This edition contains Quartermaster General and the Quartermaster General: Air Marshal together in one box.


Quartermaster General is a fast-paced game that puts you in command of the major powers of the Second World War. In the game, supply is crucial to keep your armies and navies fighting; destroy your enemies' supply lines and their forces will surrender!

During play, you control one or more countries on either the Axis or Allied team and try to score as many victory points (VPs) for your team as you can through the use of cards or by occupying the starred supply spaces. Each major power has a unique set of cards with which to marshal its forces, which are represented by wooden army and navy pieces. After twenty rounds of play, the team with the most VPs wins.

Quartermaster General: Air Marshal adds two entirely new mechanics to Quartermaster General.

Bolster cards are played directly from your hand at a time specified on the card, adding a whole new element of surprise and challenge to the game.

Your Air Forces provide an additional dimension to battle. Air Force pieces are deployed to the board through play of a Deploy Air Force card. Once in play, Air Forces may support Army or Navy units in the same space and apply additional pressure to adjacent enemy pieces.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
User summary:

After dinner at a local restaurant, you and your friends find that no one has their wallet. The manager is none too happy about it, and proposes a small competition. The first one to wash enough dishes gets to walk away, while everyone else will be turned over to the police.

On their turn, players must decide whether to 'carry' or 'wash' dishes. If 'carry' is chosen, up to two cards are played face down to the table. 'Wash' simply allow you to add cards to your hand at the end of a round.

After all players have taken a turn, 'carry' cards that have been played are simultaneously revealed. These are then checked against the carry rules and resolved. Finally, player's who chose to 'wash' draw 2 cards.

This continues round after round until a player successfully washes 10 dishes.
publishers: Install Games (インストールゲームズ)

boardgame: Trivia On The Go: T.V. Edition
description: A trivia game including a deck of 50 cards. Each card has questions and answers on them, all multiple choice. Players take turns asking questions and answering. Players getting a correct answer are given the card. The player with the most cards at the end of the game wins.

OBTINEO is a 2-6 player, abstract strategy game, with a modular terrain field (so the game is always different), three dimensions of play, resource-driven expansion, and several ways to play and win.

OBTINEO has 3 phases of play: Build, Expand, and Conquer.

In the Build phase each player places one of their hex tiles per turn until the OBTINEO island is complete. Once the island is complete during the Expand & Conquer phases (which will run together)each player turn will go as follows.

1 Player Collects Resources according to where pawns are at beginning of turn, 2 Player purchase new units, 3 player moves pawns, and 4 if Player has captured enemy units by moving into diamonds they occupy and collecting resource cards, player can purchase and move onto the island additional troops.

OBTINEO is won by conquering each enemy by either capturing all of their troops or by holding and blocking their ports.

boardgame: Champions of Midgard: Virtue Promo Card
description:
A promo Rune Card for champions of Midgard.  Given away as part of a promo pack at BGG con 2015.

This Rune Card is worth 3 Glory at the end of the game and its use allows the owner to either return 1 Blame token to the supply or force an opponent to take 1 Blame token from the supply.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Tactics Resource
description:
The Tactics Resource is a participation prize given to players who participated in The Void Episode OP Event. It is considered a Resource and costs 5 squadron points.

When using this Resource, place 2 Evade, 2 Scan and 2 Battle Stations Tokens on the Resource to start the game.  Each round, one ship in your fleet may take one of these tokens and place it beside it as if it had performed that Action as a free Action.  If the ship does not have the given Action on its Action Bar, it may still place the token, but it is considered a Standard (not free) Action and the ship also receives an Auxiliary Power Token.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Structural Damage Check Resource
description:
The Structural Damage Check Resource is a participation prize given to players who participated in The Doomsday Machine Episode Organized Play Event. It is considered a Resource and costs 5 squadron points.

When using this Resource, place 5 Auxiliary Power Tokens on it to start the game.  After every ship has moved, you may choose to place 1 token from this Resource beside a ship in your fleet (once per round).  If you do so, you may:

1) Repair 1 damage to that ship's Hull. OR

2) Flip 1 critical damage that is assigned to that ship face down.

Remove the Resource from the game when all the tokens are expended.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – General Orders Resource
description:
The General Orders Resource is a participation prize given to players who participated in Month 1, "The Wrath of Khan," of the Star Trek: Attack Wing Storyline Organized Play Event, The Classic Movies.

When using this resource, you place 5 battle stations tokens on it to start the game.  During movement, if an enemy ship ends its move with one of your ships in firing range, you may place one of the tokens from the resource by your ship, even if it already has a battle stations token.  The resource is expended after all 5 tokens have been used.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Emergency Power Resource
description:
The Emergency Power Resource is a participation prize given to players who participated in Month 2, "The Search for Spock," of the Star Trek: Attack Wing Storyline Organized Play Event, The Classic Movies.

When using this resource, place 5 mission tokens on it to start the game.  When performing a green or white maneuver with any ship in your fleet, once per round you may spend one of these tokens and perform an additional maneuver from its dial with a speed of 1 or 2, which you treat as a red maneuver.  The resource is removed from the game once all 5 tokens are expended.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Improved Hull Resource
description:
The Improved Hull Resource is a participation prize given to players who participated in Month 3, "The Voyage Home," of the Star Trek: Attack Wing Storyline Organized Play Event, The Classic Movies.

When using this resource, you may put a mission token on any number of ships in your fleet.  Each of those ships is treated as if it has +1 hull value for the duration of the game.

The resource costs an amount equal to the total starting hull value of all ships in your fleet (before using the resource) divided by 2 (rounded up).  E.g., if your fleet's total combined hull value was 15, the resource would cost 8.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – U.S.S. Cairo Expansion Pack
description: This ship is the competitive prize for the "Tin Man" episode Organized Play event.  It is an Excelsior Class ship.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – I.R.W. Rateg: OP Prize
description: This ship is the competitive prize for "The Corbomite Maneuver" episode Organized Play event.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – U.S.S. Reliant Expansion Pack
description:
This ship is the competitive prize for Month 1 of the Star Trek: Attack Wing Organized Play tournament, The Classic Movies.  It is a Miranda Class ship.

The ship is distinct from the retail variant, being a part of the Independent faction and coming with all new cards.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Kruge's Bird-of-Prey Expansion Pack
description: This ship is the competitive prize for Month 2 of the Star Trek: Attack Wing Organized Play tournament, The Classic Movies.  It is a B'Rel Class ship.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – H.M.S. Bounty Expansion Pack
description: This ship is the competitive prize for Month 3 of the Star Trek: Attack Wing Organized Play tournament, The Classic Movies.  It is a B'Rel Class ship.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – U.S.S. Enterprise-A Expansion Pack
description: This ship is the grand prize for the Star Trek: Attack Wing Organized Play tournament, The Classic Movies.  It is a refit Constitution Class ship.

boardgame: Daishō: Skirmish Wargaming in Mystical Japan
description:
Have you ever wanted to replicate the incredible feats of swordsmanship you see in movies like 47 Ronin and 13 Assassins? Stand surrounded by half a dozen angry Ashigaru and then, in a single fluid motion, strike them all down?
Bring forth fiery darts from heaven and hurl them at your enemies? Face demons from the pits of hell? Disarm and hammer an arrogant Samurai lord into the dust with your bare hands?
You can do this and much more if you play Daishō, skirmish wargaming in mythical Nippon.
This game allows players to field forces of five to fifteen miniatures in a game you can easily complete in an evening on a space only a yard square. The rules are short and simple to learn – few players need to refer to them after their second or third game.

This book provides:

     The core rules of play.
     The complete points system so you can create your own forces.
     Full listings for twelve unique forces.
     Armour, weapons and equipment, both mundane and magical.
     Listings of skills, heroic ki powers and magical powers.
     Fifteen scenarios, supported by seventeen scenario complications.
     Ten detailed landscapes for you to play across, and
     Simple, yet rewarding campaign rules.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Marvel Universe Miniature Game: The Avengers Starter Set
description:
From the publisher:

This game is a small scale skirmish level game where Superheroes and Villains face off against each other in a 3D tabletop set up. It could be a city, the Wakandan desert, the Savage Lands, the blue area of the moon or anything you like. In Marvel they fight anywhere.
It is played on a 36" x 36" board so will fit on most tables without a problem.

In a game you will usually have between 4-7 characters on your team. Each of these characters act individually using their own Power to carry out different attacks and use Special Powers.
Many can help or hinder each others though with boosts and actions like a Fastball Special.

The aim of the game is to win set scenarios by completing different tasks and scoring Victory Points. These could be anything from take down / save the highest Level model in the team or rescue and keep a Cosmic Relic.

Each character comes with their own Stat Card which shows everything they can do. Attacks, Defence, Skills, Power, Damage and such.
These are then used in a 2 D8 mechanic during the game. For example, 2D8 plus Attack Stat vs Defence Stat or 2D8 against your own Agility.

Earth's Mightiest Heroes joined forces to take on threats that were too big for any one hero to tackle. With a roster that has included Captain America, Iron Man, Ant-Man, Hulk, Thor, Wasp and dozens more over the years, the Avengers have come to be regarded as Earth's No. 1 team.

Feared and hated by humans because they're different, the X-Men are heroic mutants, individuals born with special powers who've sworn to use their gifts to protect mutants as well as humans.

description:
From the publisher:

This game is a small scale skirmish level game where Superheroes and Villains face off against each other in a 3D tabletop set up. It could be a city, the Wakandan desert, the Savage Lands, the blue area of the moon or anything you like. In Marvel they fight anywhere.

These are then used in a 2 D8 mechanic during the game. For example, 2D8 plus Attack Stat vs Defence Stat or 2D8 against your own Agility.

A group of cosmic adventurers led by Star-Lord, the Guardians of the Galaxy protect the universe from threats all across space. The team also includes Drax, Gamora, Groot and Rocket Raccoon!maybe required.

Description from the publisher:

The dogs of war aren't just loose...they're adorable, too! Get your chew toys and your armor ready as their bite is far worse than their bark! Munchkin Puppies has thirty new cards to teach your old deck new tricks.

"Fireball! Fireball! Fireball!" is a light card game of magic mischief.
Mages hurl spells at each other in an attempt to defeat their opponent with superior spell casting.
With their lightning reflexes, seconds feel like minutes - making them able to block incoming attacks with their own spells.

All players starts with three Fireball cards and get to choose two cards each from one of the eight different Schools of Magic.
Each turn consists of three moves.
A move can either be to draw a card or to play a card.
When an opponent plays a card against you, you can attemt to block the with your own cards.
The objective is to get all other players down to 0 health, kept track of on a health card.

Features 35 distinct playing cards, at a total of 68 cards in the deck.

Valhalla Cluster is the first block of card sets in the Force of Will trading card game.

Valhalla Cluster's beginning was on December 1st, 2012 with the release of The Dawn of Valhalla expansion in Japan. The cluster ended with the release of The Shaft of Light of Valhalla expansion on July 25th, 2014.

List of Sets:
    The Dawn of Valhalla
    The War of Valhalla
    The Shaft of Light of Valhalla

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – Nightmare Deck: The Nîn-in-Eilph
description:
From the publisher:

Middle-earth and The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game are full of adventures, and your road goes ever, ever on. Sometimes, when the mood strikes you—or when your heroes take a wrong turn—you may see that road darken… and then darken again.

Nightmare Decks allow you to revisit your favorite scenarios recast within the darker and deadlier shadows of Nightmare Mode. Each Nightmare Deck introduces new encounter cards that you exchange with specified cards from the adventure's standard encounter deck. The result is a more challenging adventure that comes full of novel and thematic new twists.

The Nîn-in-Eilph emphasizes the difficulties of making swift passage through the marsh with a series of quest stages featuring the Time X keyword that shuffle back into the quest deck if you can't fully complete them in time. Accordingly, it's vital to make swift progress through each Stage 2 and Stage 3, lest you find yourself in danger of threating out as your heroes begin to sink in the mire.

Rather than alter the quest structure in any significant way, The Nîn-in-Eilph Nightmare Deck ratchets up the tension by introducing a number of effects that reinforce its central concept—your repeated cycling of quest stages. In Nightmare Mode, you don't just have to move from quest stage to quest stage, you have to do so while your staging area fills up with Creatures and locations that trigger disruptive Forced effects.
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: The Lord of the Rings – The Card Game (LCG)

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – Nightmare Deck: Celebrimbor's Secret
description:
From the publisher:
Middle-earth and The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game are full of adventures, and your road goes ever, ever on. Sometimes, when the mood strikes you—or when your heroes take a wrong turn—you may see that road darken… and then darken again.

Nightmare Decks allow you to revisit your favorite scenarios recast within the darker and deadlier shadows of Nightmare Mode. Each Nightmare Deck introduces new encounter cards that you exchange with specified cards from the adventure's standard encounter deck. The result is a more challenging adventure that comes full of novel and thematic new twists.

The heart of Celebrimbor's Secret is the tension of the race to locate Celebrimbor's abandoned ring mould among the ruins of Ost-in-Edhil. As your heroes explore the ruins and its various locations, the Dark Lord's forces conduct their own search, led by an agent named Bellach. If you don't quickly explore the locations that make their way to the staging area, Bellach and his Orcs might explore them first. While your exploration places progress on locations as normal, your enemies' exploration of locations places damage on them, and any locations with damage equal to their printed quest points are placed facedown underneath The Orcs' Search (Celebrimbor's Secret, 125), where they force your threat to rise more sharply at the end of each round.

By providing Bellach and his Orcs with even more ways to damage locations and add them to The Orcs' Search, the Celebrimbor's Secret Nightmare Deck forces you to act with more urgency than ever.
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: The Lord of the Rings – The Card Game (LCG)

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – Nightmare Deck: The Antlered Crown
description:
From the publisher:

Middle-earth and The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game are full of adventures, and your road goes ever, ever on. Sometimes, when the mood strikes you—or when your heroes take a wrong turn—you may see that road darken… and then darken again.

Nightmare Decks allow you to revisit your favorite scenarios recast within the darker and deadlier shadows of Nightmare Mode. Each Nightmare Deck introduces new encounter cards that you exchange with specified cards from the adventure's standard encounter deck. The result is a more challenging adventure that comes full of novel and thematic new twists.

Even after they recover Celebrimbor's ring mould, your heroes must continue along their quest, for they have many miles to travel before they can return the mould to Saruman in Orthanc. However, their journey is interrupted in The Antlered Crown when they come upon the battles between warring clans of Dunlendings. In order to successfully complete their task, then, your heroes must battle through waves of hostile Dunland enemies before they can establish a lasting alliance with the Dunlendings who swear their loyalty to Chief Turch.

As the conclusion of The Ring-maker cycle, The Antlered Crown focused heavily on the cycle's Time X keyword, using it to trigger a number of effects, including the reveal of enemy cards from the Raven deck that you create at the beginning of the scenario. The Antlered Crown Nightmare Deck reinforces this focus on the Time X keyword and the Raven deck, so that both of the scenario's signature elements are more challenging and impactful than ever.
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: The Lord of the Rings – The Card Game (LCG)

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – Nightmare Decks: The Road Darkens
description:
From the publisher:
Middle-earth and The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game are full of adventures, and your road goes ever, ever on. Sometimes, when the mood strikes you—or when your heroes take a wrong turn—you may see that road darken… and then darken again.

Nightmare Decks allow you to revisit your favorite scenarios recast within the darker and deadlier shadows of Nightmare Mode. Each Nightmare Deck introduces new encounter cards that you exchange with specified cards from the adventure's standard encounter deck. The result is a more challenging adventure that comes full of novel and thematic new twists.

Bundled as one sixty-card product, The Road Darkens Nightmare Decks modify each of the three scenarios from The Road Darkens Saga Expansion. Because each Saga Expansion endeavors to stay as faithful to the books as possible, it may sound like a daunting task to create sixty new cards to reshape the Fellowship's travels through the second half of The Fellowship of the Ring, but lead developer Caleb Grace assures us that the Nightmare Deck afforded him a welcome opportunity to include many of the story elements he was pained to cut from the scenarios in Standard Mode:

I'm always excited to design Nightmare Decks for our Saga Expansions because there are always things that I had wanted to include, but we simply didn’t have the room. While working on The Road Darkens, I found myself saying things like, “This will have to go in the Nightmare version” whenever I had to cut something that I liked. For example, serious fans of The Lord of the Rings may have lamented the exclusion of the Watcher in the Water’s many tentacles, or the absence of the Balrog’s fiery whip and flaming sword. Nightmare Decks give us the opportunity not only to increase the difficulty of the scenarios, but to appeal to our fans by introducing more of these memorable parts of the story.
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: The Lord of the Rings – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
Superstar is a sports-themed family game that actually consists of five different sub-games, viz. Cross Country, Swimming, Shooting, Shot Put and Basketball. Each of the sub-games is represented by it's own roll-and-move, or dice rolling, track on the game board. Players get rewarded by game money ("Superdollars") for relative success in each sub-game - but those who land on the feared "Doping Test" space will be immediately disqualified from that particular sport! The player with most Superdollars at the end will be declared "Superstar" and given a quadruple hurrah by her fellow players.

The catch of the game is that the 18 chance cards actually instruct players to do physical exercises, such as push-ups, stretching etc., and a player unable or unwilling to do those will have to pass a turn.

At the start of the 19th century, the exploration and exploitation of the African continent began in earnest. During this deplorable period in world history, the major European powers — Britain, Holland and France — would mercilessly explore, colonize and fight over its many resources for a hundred years. Naturally, native populations would come to resent and rebel against their foreign exploiters and seek independence, and so it is in this swift-playing game for two, where you emulate a European nation and compete for a continent.

Players get a hand of cards in four types: Explore, Colonize, Exploit and Conflict. Some cards are restricted to a region, others to a single area. Exploration leads to colonization, which leads to exploitation, and sometimes to a conflict between the rival powers. Hand management and timely card play is important in this game.

If more colonists arrive in an area than it will accept, a native uprising will occur. The roll of a die and simple charts settle uprisings (and conflicts) quickly. Choosing the right time to initiate negative action against your opponent can be crucial.

INFILTRATE                                   ANTICIPATE                            CAPTURE

Illyricum 6 - 9 CE

During the period of Rome’s bloody civil wars, a divided army fought for control of one of the mightiest empires on earth. Take sides in a game that balances skill and chance. Anticipate and plot your next moves, as you scheme to infiltrate and plunder enemy territory. Ambush and capture your opponents, but beware! Fickle Roman gods may take sides and intercede to seal your fate.

Your moves are determined by the cards you draw. Uncertainty haunts your every turn, because sprinkled among the tiles are Roman gods that may aid or impede your efforts.

The perfect balance between strategy and chance in the unpredictable Ancient World. Young players will particularly love the unpredictability and surprise while older players will treasure and master the infinite playing strategies.

Played on an 8 x 8 board with marble pieces and a stack of cards full of mystical and powerful gods.

To understand what you get, we uploaded a great range of photos on this BGG page. The Production Version is the standalone box set. Capere is a Social Abstract Game SAG for 2 or 4 players.

CAPERE IS ALSO AVAILABLE WITH WOODEN TILES AS PART OF ANCIENT WORLD MULTI GAME SYSTEM FROM PLAYFORD GAMES

"The legends of old spoke of a great samurai who donned no armor. He moved faster than the wind, his sword whistling soundlessly. Because no one knew his ream name, they called him by his most prominent feature - a large straw hat covering his features."

TRAITS: Beauty, Great, Happy, Male

POWER: Once per game at the start of his turn, Straw Hat Samurai may immediately move to any location on the game board. If he does this, his turn ends immediately after placing onto the new location.

The Straw Hat Samurai is a limited edition promotional character who will be printed in VERY limited quantity, and available first to Kickstarter backers as an add-on, after which remaining units (if any) will then be sold at rare conventions worldwide. The add-on bundle comes with his own miniature as well as his set of items to become a playable character in Hitman Holiday.
—description from the publisher

Since Magic Realm Light 30 is a light version of Magic Realm that plays in 30 minutes, it makes sense that any additional material that will ramp up the game a bit (and requires some extra Effort) should be called the Magic Realm Light 30 Asterisk Expansion, or MRL30*.

This expansion introduces Sunlight Actions, the Alert action for Magic chits, Treasures at Dwellings, 6 Weapons/Armor, 6 Treasures, 2 Spells, the Chapel along with a Knight denizen, and the Wish, Curse and Power of the Pit tables.

"Light 30 Asterisk" (or L30*) is a Magic Realm game term, meaning "something Light-weight that takes up 30 units of time and costs one Effort Asterisk".

Overview (by the publisher): At the tournament place, the knights want to prove themselves. The colored symbol dice are thrown into the dice tower, showing new challenges every turn. The various symbols and colors have to be sorted. Which is the largest set? Skill is necessary when flicking the pieces. The more dice match the target space, the further the Knight can move.

Gameplay: The active player throws the six dice and a small wooden cube into the dice tower. The player then flicks a wooden disk. If he hits one of the target spaces, he claims the dice that show the matching result (color or symbol). Then the other players in turn order also flick the disk to try to claim some of the remaining dice. After this, the next player becomes the active player, and continues with throwing all dice into the tower.

Each die collected moves the player's knight one step forward. The first knight reaching to other side of the tower wins.

There are also rules for a variant in which the players bet which symbol they will roll, get points for matching symbols, and are able to change their bet by paying dice.

Put yourself in place of the greatest heroes and commanders of Argentina's history and free this land with honor, wisdom and strategy. Oid Mortales el grito sagrado. LIBERTAD!

Oíd Mortales is a trivia game with strategy combat in a board. Players will have to manage tokens, playing infantry and artillery cards which will fight in surprise trivia, where be the expert historian is not necessarily good. The tactic board gives an expansion to the game far beyond questions and answers, where movement and intelligent use of resources is important as the battle itself.

Each player will take the side of the "Patriotas" and the other the "Realista´s" side. During the game the players draw cards and put or move the battalions on the map in relation to the action points (AP) obtained in the die roll.

Each team will have 4 commanders that have special fight habilities, one of them will be the Gold Commander.

The dice roll give action points (AP) to the players. The actions cost from 2 AP to 6 AP, depending on the type of action. For example take an artillery card cost 2 AP but an event card cost 6 AP.

When a unit is next to an enemy unit, the player can attack. Each artillery or reinforcement card, wich are used to attack, include an historical trivia. The attacker increase or decrease his AP by answering de question.

The first player who eliminate the enemy Gold Commander enemy wins.

Battle Roads Miniatures is a table top board game set in a post apocalyptic world where civilization is rebuilding itself. A plague whose origin is not widely known wiped out most of the population. Attempts to isolate and purge the cause resulted in wide spread use of major nuclear arsenals and other weapons of mass destruction. Not much was left. Very few people were left. Those that survived found that they had undergone changes.

The technology in the cars is 20 to 40 years past the "weld on a gun, add some steel plate and go get stuck in" that immediately followed the plague and attempted purges. Vehicles are purpose built for their combat role and are far more deadly. Driver protection is at a premium so motorcycles have mostly been relegated to the few remaining fringe gangs. The mortality rate for motorcycle users is so high they are now called "coffins".

The goal is to survive the game and take out as many opponents' cars as possible. Game play is very fast with each turn lasting 3 to 10 minutes depending on how many cars are on the board. Each player uses a control board for each car that tracks turn speed, two phases of movement, component damage, and driver skill use (offense or defense). For the convention play version of the game these control boards are made of wood. For the mass production version (digital image in gallery), the control boards will be on silk screen impregnated boards (~5mm thick). Players track their car status and maneuvers by placing colored pegs on the control board.

Combat is via opposed dice roles. The attacker gets a pool of dice based on the weapon being shot and the range, speed and direction the target is moving. Driver skill can also add to the attack dice pool. The defender gets defense dice equal to their remaining armor rating and can get additional dice for range, speed and driver skill. Once rolled, any offensive successes over defense score damage. Critical damage is a big part of the game. Any time an attack rolls any successes their is a crit chance. It is extremely unlikely an undamaged car will take a critical hit but a medium to heavily damage car is highly likely to take a critical. The bad news with critical damage is the attacker assigns the damage location. Basically, the attacker hits what his/her driver was aiming for. Critical hits are to be feared and avoided!

Rules can be played at the Quick Play level, standard level or with all optional rules. The largest change between levels is how to handle collisions. Many diagrams are included in the rules to simplify the learning process and reduce the "rules lawyering" that may occur when questions arise.

Each game is played via individual scenario or players can play the long term campaign where they control an entire tribe including all their cars as well as drivers and advanced facilities. As drivers score damage, score critical damage, make kills and win scenarios they gain points that allow them to increase in driver skill or obtain upgrades for their cars. The core version of battle roads miniatures includes basic upgrades for the base cars like a bigger engine, bigger weapons, etc. A later expansion will include full design and upgrades of the cars.

Autocross is a vintage dice rolling board game, up to 6 can play. Players try to avoid the green, yellow and red spaces. Three lights shows on the right of the board, when a car steps onto one of these spaces.

Green light: one space back and miss one turn
Yellow light: two spaces back and miss two turns
Red light: go back to start

The first to get the finish, wins.

Contents:
6 cars
1 dice
1 board

You need a 4.5 V battery to use the lights (not included).

Each player has 6 meeples, which he must get from his starting spaces to the 6 numbered home spaces of his color. Each meeple has to move once around the board. Movement is determined by a die. Meeples are brought into play by rolling a 1. The blue spaces on the track are neutral and can hold more than one meeple. On the yellow spaces, meeples can be thrown out. But this is actually a good thing, because they are put on the home spaces of their color. Throwing out is mandatory. If a Player forgets to throw another meeple out, one of the meeples in his home is put back to a starting space. If a meeple is thrown out on the space of his own color in front of his home, the meeple is put back on a starting space. The first player to bring his 6 meeples home wins the game.

The players start with 6 meeples at the bottom of the game board. Movement is determined by a die. Any meeple that has reached space 25 can advance to a winning space by the apt roll of the die. For instance, on the roll of a 2 it can occupy a "100-space" or on the roll of a 6 the "1000-space". Each winning space con only be occupied by one meeple. The game ends when all winning spaces are occupied or all meeples are on winning spaces. The player with the highest point count wins the game.

boardgame: Star Wars: Imperial Assault – The Bespin Gambit
description:
From the publisher:

The Bespin Gambit is a new expansion for Imperial Assault!

The effects of the Galactic Civil War can be felt on every planet of the Star Wars galaxy. On industrialized planets, the inhabitants may be forced to work in brutal conditions, slaves to the construction of the Imperial war machine. Other worlds may be torn by war as legions of Stormtroopers and massive assault walkers squelch any dissension against the Empire’s rule.

On other planets, however, the war is fought in secret, in the shadows of dark alleys, disused conference rooms, and secret meetings. Information is exchanged between faceless Rebel spies, even as the terrifying Imperial Security Bureau searches for their undercover foes. Now, The Bespin Gambit brings this shadow war to your games of Imperial Assault.

In The Bespin Gambit, you’ll find a wealth of new content to take your campaigns and skirmishes into the ritzy hotels and industrial underworks of Cloud City. Iconic characters like Bossk and Lando Calrissian join the game for the first time, gorgeous new map tiles portray some of the most memorable Cloud City locations, while new companions and a new condition change the flow every game. With two new Rebel heroes, one new Imperial class, new items, a new mini-campaign, and a new focus on your Spy characters, The Bespin Gambit offers an indispensable bonus to every Imperial Assault player.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 2 – Bristol Beaufighter
description:
The Bristol Beaufighter was a British long-range heavy fighter derivative of the Bristol’s earlier Beaufort torpedo bomber design. The Beaufighter had a long career and served in almost all theatres of war in the Second World War, first as a night fighter, then as a fighter bomber and eventually replacing the Beaufort as a torpedo bomber.

Great Aces that gloriously piloted this aircraft were Archibald McNeill Boyd, Michael James Herrick and Paul Yettvart Davoud.

Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW2 Wings of Glory® Airplane Pack is a ready-to-play model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW2 Wings of Glory game product.

In each pack you will find all you need to play with the airplane: a special base with gaming stats, a variable altitude flying stand, and a specific deck of maneuver cards.

Comes in a Boyd version, a Herrick version, and a Davoud version.

boardgame: Star Wars: Imperial Assault – Bossk Villain Pack
description:
From the publisher:

Finally, you can take up the hunt with Bossk, a deadly Trandoshan bounty hunter! Bossk was one of the bounty hunters searching for the Millennium Falcon in The Empire Strikes Back, and with this figure pack, you can harness his unbridled ferocity. Whether you’re taking contracts for the Mercenaries faction in a skirmish or working for the Imperial player in a campaign, Bossk will quickly prove himself a dangerous hunter.

During your campaigns, the Bossk Villain Pack offers new challenges and thrills with an additional Agenda set to supplement your other cards. You can use these Agenda cards to brutally hunt down the Rebels or smuggle guns through the Toydarian wilderness, but no matter how you use them in the game, Bossk is sure to endanger the heroes with his wild attacks and regenerative talents.

You may also include Bossk in your strike team for a skirmish game. Two new skirmish missions introduced in this figure pack challenge you to navigate the dangers of a Wasskah Hunting Ground, and an assortment of new Command cards introduce unexpected and unpredictable maneuvers. For instance, you may play Wild Attack when you declare an attack to immediately add one red die to your attack pool! In exchange, however, you must add a white die to the defending figure’s dice pool. If you need to destroy a powerful figure in a single attack or suffer the consequences, Wild Attack might give you the edge you need.

This is not a standalone product. A copy of the Imperial Assault Core Set is required to play. Includes missions that require The Bespin Gambit expansion.

boardgame: Star Wars: Imperial Assault – Agent Blaise Villain Pack
description:
From the publisher:

ISB agents in Cloud City are led by none other than Agent Blaise. a methodical warrior and an expert interrogator. With the Agent Blaise Villain Pack, you can bring this Imperial warrior’s menace and precision into any game of Imperial Assault.

Whether you’re stalking the halls of Cloud City or engaged in another campaign across the galaxy, Agent Blaise can be a menacing opponent for the Rebel heroes. First, this figure pack contains a brand-new Agenda set, Imperial Intelligence. This Agenda set invites you to take advantage of your espionage and observation of the Rebel heroes—for instance, Under Surveillance allows you to record a hero’s favored attack methods and unleash your knowledge when the heroes least suspect it.

You may also add Agent Blaise to your skirmish strike team to cut off support from the Rebel forces. Two new skirmish missions bring you into the terrifying depths of ISB Headquarters, and new Command cards offer tricks and unexpected tactics for any strike team. In addition to his reputation as a warrior, Agent Blaise offers several opportunities to interrogate your foes and discard their Command cards, ensuring that you always hold the upper hand.

boardgame: Star Wars: Imperial Assault – ISB Infiltrators Villain Pack
description:
From the publisher:

Across the galaxy, few Imperial institutions command more fear than the Imperial Security Bureau. Your neighbor or your family member may secretly serve the ISB. The man you pass on your way to work or the talkative woman in the cantina could be an informer. You can’t trust anyone, and in this atmosphere of stifling fear, few dare to speak of rebellion. Now, you can enforce the status quo and punish sedition in your games of Imperial Assault with the help of the ISB Infiltrators Villain Pack.

If the heroes are unlucky enough to encounter them on a mission, the ISB Infiltrators prove to be dangerous combatants, using the new Hidden condition to reduce enemy accuracy and gaining surges to deal more damage and increase accuracy. More than that, each ISB Infiltrator benefits from the Coordinated Raid ability, which allows another figure in the group to interrupt and perform an attack. Obviously, more attacks can significantly increase your damage output over the course of the game.

Whether you’re using their unique talents in a campaign or a skirmish, this Villain Pack offers additional cards and missions to supplement your adventures in the Star Wars galaxy. A new Agenda set, Command cards, Reward cards, and a skirmish upgrade offer you additional tools for any mission, from the ISB’s vaunted training grounds to an Imperial facility on Bespin.

boardgame: Star Wars: Imperial Assault – Lando Calrissian Ally Pack
description:
From the publisher:

As the Baron Administrator of Cloud City, Lando Calrissian has access to considerable resources and influence. Despite his recent rise to respectability, Lando is still a smuggler, a gambler, and an outlaw at heart, and he now chooses to exercise his influence in the aid of the Rebel Alliance by helping their operatives elude the Imperial Security Bureau agents.

Your Imperial Assault campaigns can certainly benefit from having a charismatic scoundrel like Lando Calrissian by your side. A new campaign side mission introduced in this figure pack invites you to investigate missing shipments of supplies and help Lando root out corruption, but no matter where you employ the help of Lando Calrissian, his resourceful nature and cheerful disregard for the rules are certainly useful assets to your heroes.

Skirmish games can also reap the benefits of Lando Calrissian’s support. An entirely new skirmish map invites you to travel to a Lothal Safehouse, where you’ll struggle to recover artifacts or hidden shipments of weapons before your opponent. You may also take advantage of new Command cards like Tough Luck . Any figure can play this Command card after your opponent rerolls a die in order to remove that die instead! Between Tough Luck and the other cards in this Ally Pack, Lando Calrissian’s unconventional tactics can quickly turn a battle in your favor.

This is not a standalone product. A copy of the Imperial Assault Core Set is required to play. Includes missions that require The Bespin Gambit expansion.

In Alpha Bet players take turns writing words in a shared grid. Each player has three copies of the alphabet to draw letters from, plus an additional copy of the alphabet and pool of vowels available to all players.

When a player creates a word they first write it down in a separate area and bet points per letter. Their opponents then write down letters they think will appear in this word and also make per-letter bets. The player will gain the points bet for letters no opponent guesses and lose them for guessed letters; their opponents gain points for correct guesses and lose them for incorrect guesses.

In Cross-Wordsmith players enter words into a grid. Unlike in most such games each grid space does not contain a letter but a section of a word: a prefix, root, or suffix. Many of the grid spaces are color-coded to show which of the three they must contain, while others can have any section.

A list of allowable sections is provided; on their turn a player chooses at least one of these to add to the grid and marks off all they use (each one can only be used a certain number of times). All adjacent word sections must combine into words, but this does not necessarily have to be done linearly; it is possible to turn corners when forming words.

Each word section has a point value and players score for all words they form on each turn.

A set of three games for two players (hence the title). All are public domain games:

Dots & Dashes is Boxes.
Sting is Jotto.
Maneuvres is Battleship.

Side-Swap is an abstract strategy game with similarities to Checkers. The pieces start on squares of the opposite color and have to cross the board to their home area on the other side, at which time they can move onto squares of their own color; after they do this they cannot move again. All pieces can be jumped and jumps can be chained. Opposing pieces can only be captured once they have moved onto their color spaces.

The game can be played by two players, four players in partnerships, or four players individually. When playing with four the pieces are turned over to show numbers; pieces must end up on the spaces with matching numbers. The four-individuals game has a few extra rules about when pieces of the same color may be captured.

Do you like gnomes? Do you like cookies?? Then prepare yourself for the most EPIC CARD GAME EVER! Steal the Cookie is a fast paced, intense party card game. The object of the game is to be the last player with the Almighty Cookie in your hand, when the final Cookie Jar Card is drawn.

The game consists of three types of cards... Action cards, Stop Cards, and Interrupt Cards. Each player must use the cards in his hand to prevent the other players from obtaining the cookie. The game is easy to learn, but hard to master. Use all of your skills to overcome your opponents and steal the COOKIE! But watch out! Your opponents have an array of spells and tricks up their sleeve! The object of the game is to be the last player to be holding the Almighty Cookie when the final Cookie Jar card is drawn. The game is fast, simple, and incredibly easy to learn. There are over 20 different card types for endless hours of fun! So grab your friends and family and get ready to STEAL THE COOKIE!

We’ve just arrived at 221B Baker Street when Dr. Watson takes us aside. "Gentlemen, I’m deeply concerned about Mr. Holmes’s mental state. For two days now he’s been acquiring strange books and spending all his time at the coffee table playing patience with a deck of cards. I’m worried that he’s been seized by the idea of reading the future thus, which you will no doubt agree is quite illogical. Also, please don’t mention this madness of not speaking until he’s flipped a card."

Wiggins asks Watson if he can say when exactly the most brilliant mind of our time was overcome with this folly. "Precisely two days ago, after reading The Times." Wiggins looks over at us conspiratorially and whispers in our ear: "I rather think we’d do well to read that edition of the Times and start investigating, don’t you?"

So begins "October 1891," a single extra case expansion originally published in Role Mag' (#5 - March-April 1991).

An alternate main case title was added to the English and Spanish editions:
 * Spanish: El Caso de la Baraja
 * English: Case of Bad Fortunes

Czech board game about rally racing. 
Players roll the dice and drive through track that consists of 3 stages. Every stage has speed trials, precise stops, failure traps and other tasks that can cause penalties to player. When all players finish stage, next one can start. Players count their penalties in the finish of race. Winner is player with the least penalties.

Trivia game about the German Tv-Series "Tatort"

Crime Scene - The game is for everyone who can't get enough Crime Scene. But don't be afraid of gaps in your knowledge! With a little luck, even crime scene laypeople can beat the crime scene experts. How it works? Sometimes the players have to answer multiple-choice questions, sometimes they have to guess or answer a question with yes or no. And if you fall behind, bonus cards will help you to get back to the front. It never gets boring here: because everyone is playing at the same time! Would you like a little crime scene?

Includes 336 questions in 6 categories: Classics, Faktencheck, Kuriositäten, Modern Times, Städte, Backstage.

Mixed Nuts is a party/trivia game with a unique voting element. Each of the trivia cards is double-sided with a question on both sides and the answer to each question on the opposite side; therefore, the person reading the question can play along, too. Each card features an odd trivia fact—and somewhere in the text, there are two possible choices—one is a fact, the other is a fib (these choices are a different color than the rest of the text).

The active player picks one (the fact or the lie), and reads the sentence aloud to the other players. Here's an example: “Al Capone's vault was opened on live TV by (Barbara Walters/Geraldo Rivera) and there was virtually nothing of value in it.” Players then decide if the given answer was correct. For instance, the active player includes “Barbara Walters” in their statement. If a player thinks that's right, they put the voting chip in their hand. If a player thinks that’s incorrect, they put nothing in their hand and have an empty hand. All players then reveal what's in their hand. The correct answer is “Geraldo Rivera,” so those players with empty hands receive one point—in the form of a nut—to put into their score cup. After three rounds of each player having had a turn to read a card, the player with the most nuts wins.

description:
With the shopping cart in the supermarket. Where is the shelf with the bananas or the milk? Colored chits have to be sorted into the correct shelves and counters, before they can be added to the cart and paid at the checkout.

boardgame: The Pyramid of the Flying Marbles
description:
The Pyramid of the Flying Marbles is a Shibumi game for 3 players derived from The House of the Flying Blades, by the same designer.

Players take turns capturing a marble of the next player in turn or passing. When all players pass in succession the game ends. The player with more marbles remaining on the board wins.

Mauseschlau and Bärenstark want to recover a treasure from a ship wreck deep in the ocean. But the diving is not without risks. There are aggressive sharks, and a mysterious creature guards the wreck. By he who can answer questions and perform tasks related to ships and oceans, and has a little luck with the dice, will be first to bring his treasure to safety.

It is a roll-and-move game where the players follow a track through the sea, from the ship to the diving bell, from there to the ship wreck, and upwards to the surface.

On many spaces, a question related to the sea will be asked; younger childs can select between three answers on the card, older ones have to find the answer without help. On other spaces, a small pantomime is requested. In both cases, oxygen tanks can be won. These are needed on the shark spaces. Without air, the player has to retreat to the diving bell.

When reaching or passing the wreck, the player gets a piece of the treasure.

On addition to sharks, the area between the ship wreck and the surface is guarded by a plastic Kraken. It is moved by a special die, and if it catches a diver with treasure, the treasure is lost, and the player is send back to the diving bell. When a player reaches the surface with a treasure, he wins the game.

Changes to the precursor (Mauseschlau & Bärenstark: Abenteuer unter Wasser) include: Less shark spaces, especially in the last part after the ship wreck; an additional die for the kraken, so the players can move the kraken in addition to their own playing piece; the treasure is easier to get, since the wreck no longer needs to be reached with the exact die roll; a variant is included to make it easier to leave the sea with the treasure. As a result, this revised game plays in half the time, and has less luck of the dice.

Munchkin Playmat: Spyke Gets Suckered is a 28"×14" playmat that showcases Spyke tussling with a tentacled terror. It includes spots to keep track of your Class, Race, level, and all your monster-slaying gear, and it features an in-game bonus — Spyke's secret space — that allows you to Cheat! one item per game.

Munchkin Playmat: Flower Cashes In is a 28"×14" playmat that showcases Flower atop a pile of freshly looted items. It includes spots to keep track of your Class, Race, level, and all your monster-slaying gear, and it features an in-game bonus that gives you extra gold when you sell items each turn...but not forever.

Czech game with large board full of castles and paths. 
Players recruit one knight every turn, position of new knight is controlled by dice. After that players can move to consolidate power of knights to groups or fight for castles or other groups/knights or avoid conflicts etc. If someone wins battle for castle chooses carefully hidden defense power of castle (becouse it is limited per player). 
Game ends when there is only last free castle. Players sum their castle points and player with the most wins.

Star is a two-player abstract strategy game designed by Ea Ea as the third out of four games by him extending or generalizing Hex. The four games in publication order: The Game of Y, Poly-Y, Star, and *Star. See further details of the developmental history at the designer's homepage.

The game board for Star is a hexagon with uneven side lengths made up of hexagonal spaces (e.g. alternating edge length of 6 and 7 hexes). On the board, the playing area is surrounded by a ring of "partial edge hexagons" used for final scoring. The two players (black and white) takes turns to place one stone (in their color) on any un-occupied hex space in the playing area. The goal of the game is to create "stars" - groups of connected stones belonging to one player that touches at least three partial edge hexagons. Each star has a score of the number of partial edge hexagons it touches minus two. The final score of a player is the sum of points for all the stars of that players color. The player with the highest score wins. The sum of the two players score is constant for any particular board.

From Wikipedia:
Star is a two-player abstract strategy board game developed by Craige Schensted (now Ea Ea). It was first published in 1983 in Games magazine. It is a connection game, related to games such as Hex, Y, Havannah, and TwixT. Unlike these games, however, the result is based on a player having a higher final score rather than achieving a specific goal. He has since developed a slightly more complicated version called *Star with better balance between edge and center moves, writing "*Star is what those other games wanted to be."

From the cover:


        Mission: Capture Berlin before the Americans/British!
        Method: Massive ground assault from Oder Bridgehead
        Ground Conditions: Mud
        Enemy Strength: Fanatical Nazis, SS troops and Volksturm. Resistance cannot last beyond a few days. Morale seems to be good despite other reports.
        Fuel situation critical. Ammunition limited. We will bury the Nazis.


This is a division-level game of the final Soviet drive on Berlin. The scale is two miles per hex and three or five days per turn (three in April, five earlier).

The game contains an 11"x17" map (on two sheets) and approximately 160 counters.

From the designer:

Create your city and be the best mayor in the history over the 4 years (4 rounds in each year)! Set goals, fulfill promises, create your strategy and grow your city while maintaining the best levels of education, health, sustainability (ecology), security, entertainment and economy.

Each player purchases (with cash) Construction Tiles (houses, apartments, hospitals, cinemas, schools, industries etc) which will enhance the different levels of your city (security, health, education etc).

With high levels the player can complete Goal Cards that he acquired during rounds and also complete the requests of the television media (media cards), earning more points mayor and approval of the population. Implement projects (through project cards) can also help gain mayor points, gain approval of the population and increase the city levels.

To buy Construction Tiles, Goal Cards and Projects Cards, each player uses his meeples (city hall employees). Throughout the game, new meeples may be hired.

At the end of each year, players must have high approval of the population to gain even more Mayor Points. At the end of 4 years, which has more points Mayor is the winner!

From the designer (Portuguese):

Construa a sua cidade e seja o melhor prefeito da história ao longo de 4 anos (4 rodadas em cada ano)! Estabeleça metas, cumpra promessas, crie sua estratégia e faça crescer a sua cidade, mantendo os melhores níveis de educação, saúde, sustentabilidade (ecologia), segurança, lazer e economia.

Cada jogador compra com dinheiro Tiles de Construção (casas, apartamentos, hospitais, cinemas, escolas, indústrias etc.) que irão aumentar os diferentes níveis de sua cidade (segurança, saúde, educação etc.).

Com os níveis elevados, o jogador poderá completar Cartas de Objetivo que ele adquiriu durante as rodadas e também concluir as solicitações da mídia de televisão (cartões de mídia), ganhando mais pontos de prefeito e aprovação da população. Implementar projetos (através de cartões de projetos) também pode ajudar a ganhar pontos de prefeito, obter a aprovação da população e aumentar os níveis da cidade.

Para comprar tiles de construção, cartões de objetivos e cartões de projetos, cada jogador usa seus meeples (funcionários da prefeitura). Durante o jogo, novos meeples podem ser contratados.

No final de cada ano, os jogadores devem ter alta de aprovação da população para ganhar ainda mais pontos de prefeito. No final de 4 anos, que tiver mais pontos de prefeito é o vencedor!

The Craftworlds of the Eldar are scattered across the stars, massive drifting starships that are home to last survivors of a race that once ruled the universe.

This is a supplement for Warhammer 40,000.

The Imperium Chronicles Combat Card Game is an easy-to-understand, fast-paced game between two players. Players alternate between being the Attacker and the Defender, each using a Unit Card and multiple Modifier Cards each round. The winner of each round earns a point and the first player to reach 10 points wins the game.

Note: You only need to buy ONE deck for BOTH players to play! A single deck includes everything you need for two players!

Parallel is a 2-4 four player light-weight matching card game of deduction and strategy. Play consists of being dealt (4) randomized playing cards; followed by the clockwise execution of various actions in a race to match the goal card in the center. This means that each players goal is unique to their perspective of the goal card. After the cards are dealt, each players stands them keeping them secret. Each round consists of checking for matches, and execution of actions until one player claims victory. Players begin with (4) action tokens and lose one for every match. Action tokens replenish at the beginning of each players turn, after their first turn. 
 Their are two types of actions. The Swap and Switch actions effect your cards, while the Flip and Spin action effect the goal card. This is important because players cannot win on the same turn as manipulating their own cards. This means, in order to win, a player must come into their turn having 4 matching, a winning board state, or have the ability to Flip or Spin the goal card into a winning position. Winning a round grants a VP token.

For more on Parallel please follow @ParallelCG on twitter or check out Parallel.Mozello.Com

Mishmash Galaxy is a light strategy game of long-term planning, memorization and card management in which you combine made-believe substances to complete and construct the staples of SF technologies! Make clichés and tropes come to life as you struggle to create FTL drives, time machines, portals and laser weaponry!

The goal of the game is to be the player with the most victory points! Players can earn victory points mostly by completing technologies; whenever a technology is completed, all contributors get the minor score of that technology, and if there is a major contributor, that player earns the major score. On a turn, a player can execute two actions, none of which are obligatory. The actions are:

• Play a Research Card: A player can put one of their Substance Card face down in front of them. At the beginning of their next turn, this card is revealed and its research ability is activated. Then the card should be discarded. You can never have more than one active Research Card.

• Contribute to a Technology: Each player can contribute a card from their hand to two different Technologies. Any card can be contributed (Ingredient or Substance) as long as the resource of the card are correct with the resources needed in that Technology stage. Each Technology is being filled in order of their blueprint, from left to right. You play the cards face down and place your ownership token on the card. Resolving the Technology works as below:


     All cards on the Technology are revealed.
     Cards are then activated from right to left. Owner of the card is the one who resolves the card Technology ability.


The game ends when all Technologies prepared for the game are completed.

In Djumble, players reveal cards from the deck that have descriptions such as "green", "floats", or "weighs more than 1 kg", then everyone races around the house — or wherever they're playing — to find something that fits as many of these categories as possible, then they argue about which things fit best in order to score points.

From the designer:

Control Tower: LAX Airport is a family boardgame about the management of the planes in an airport.
The players are air controllers who must land and take off the planes and distribute those planes in the diferent areas of the airport like repair/maintenance garage or refuelling to satisfice their requeriments.

The game is based on Control Tower with new mechanism.
The players share the LAX Airport while in Control Tower each player manage his own airport. This change makes different feelings between both games.

Control Tower: LAX Airport is an addictive boargame for 2 to 5 players, with easy rules but complex making decisions.

From the designer (Spanish):

"Torre de Control" es un juego de mesa familiar sobre la gestión de aviones en un aeropuerto.
Los jugadores encarnan a controladores aéreos de la gestión interna de un aeropuerto y entre sus funciones están ordenar los aterrizajes y despegues de los aviones y las distribución de los aviones en los diferentes servicios del aeropuerto tales como repostaje y reparación y mantenimiento.

El juego esta basado en "Torre de Control" pero con nuevas mecánicas. En esta versión los jugadores tienen un único aeropuerto común a diferencia de la anterior versión que cada jugador tenia el suyo propio. Este cambio hace que ambas versiones transmitan unas sensaciones de juego totalmente diferentes.

"Torre de control: Aeropuerto LAX" es un juego adictivo de 2 a 5 jugadores, de reglas muy sencillas pero con mucha toma de decisiones.
An expansion pack with 2 new pantheons: Aztec and Nightmare (Cthulhu Mythos). Available as an add-on on KS campaign.

Like Madriga and her companions, your adventures do not stop with the end of the Dwarf King’s Quest. Mantica teems with dark places, great treasures and evil deeds, and this book will help you to construct your own adventures amongst them, whether you wish to create a hero to battle the darkness, or your own villain to rule the world.

Features:

Create a hero - Generate a truly individual Hero or Overlord from scratch, based on any model in your collection. The choice is yours, from name and profession to spells and songs!

Design the Dungeon - Conjure up your very own maps, objectives and backstory. From the cavernous to the claustrophobic, we give you the tools and tips to put together a unique challenge.

Level Up - Watch proudly as your Character evolves from the very first adventure, gaining levels, abilities, and above all great stories to tell! Will you master a discipline, or branch out and learn new skills?

Be Bad - Turn the game on its head by playing as an evil party, heading off to take down a ‘good’ Overlord and his band of so-called Heroes.

Explore - Between adventures, visit one of 13 different downtime locations to rest, replenish and more! Will it be Tavern or the Temple this time?

Play Solo or Full Co-op - These rules allow for a new and engaging single-player experience, in addition to a co-operative mode in which no Overlord player is required!

Tackle Uncharted Dungeons - Venture into the unknown and hold your breath as the map unfolds before you in this exciting variant where no-one knows what lurks around the next corner!

Your Dungeon Saga Awaits you...

boardgame: Dirty Cops: A Game for Scoundrels
description:
Dirty Cops: A Game for Scoundrels is a fast paced 2-4 player game where players work cooperatively against criminals, then stab each other in the back to gain the most money.

Games take place over 3-4 crime scenes, depending on player count, with each player taking on the role of lead detective during the course of the game.

Players choose how much they work together, making (and breaking) alliances with each other to survive the criminals (and other cops). Players also choose how ruthless they want to be with each other, allowing the group to set the tone for the game.

Cops, drugs, and guns...what kind of cop will YOU be?

Real-time game, in which up to 6 players must simultaneously and continuously play cards to its own boards trying to defeat at least one of the different powers of any of the opponent's cards and take them. The effects of the played cards are instantaneous and change the powers and abilities of other cards in play.

It's a fast and frenetic game, that can be played even with kids.
The game is played in rounds until the deck is empty (more players means less time). When this happens, the players count the total strength of the defeated cards, and who has more is declared winner.
It can also be played in constructed deck mode.

boardgame: A Curious Deck for Extraordinary Liars
description:
This is the first of a series of games based on some simple rules...  Here's the description from the back of the box:

A Curious Deck for Extraordinary Liars contains 56 peculiar cards depicting surreal and unintentionally hilarious Victorian art from ads and postcards dated 1880-1910.

Instructions for play:
The first player takes a card from the deck. The card holder may choose to describe what they see on the card or lie about what is on the card. It's up to the other players to determine whether the card holder is lying or telling the truth. Players are permitted to ask questions.

When all players have guessed Lie or Truth, the card holder announces the correct answer and reveals the card.

The player to the left then takes a new card from the deck and continues the game.

Players who guess correctly get 1 point.

The first player to 10 points wins.

Mother Truckers is a CB jaw-jackin', blacktop fast-trackin', madcap dash across 1970s America for 2-4 players.  The goal of the game is for your female trucker to make a name for herself by being the first to gain 12 Fame Points and show the boys you can outhaul, outdrive, and outwit every other gearjammer on the road.  Put the hammer down as you pick up Commodities and deliver them to cities that demand them for Fame.  Earn Action cards for your accomplishments and use them to dominate the highway as you become a truck driving legend!  Just be sure to steer clear of old smokey...

On each turn, a player rolls the Movement Die and optionally the Speeding Die if they wish to attempt speeding.  Speeding can allow for an extra Movement point per turn, but can cause wasted fuel trying to avoid the cops if they catch you.

The player may then split their Movement between physically moving road spaces on the map, refueling, and picking up or dropping off loads in cities.  Each road space costs 10% fuel and players are towed to the connected city of another player's choice if they run out of fuel.

Contracts offer incentives to deliver certain types of goods or travel to specific regions for bonus Fame.  Action cards spice up the game by creating risky scenarios for extra Fame such as Bar Brawls and Street Chicken.  Or, play devious deeds against your opponents such as shooting out their gas tanks and stealing their loads.  Naturally, all cards are flavored with classic 1970s CB jargon.

The players are collectors of old money, and their aim is to collect more and more coins, old bank notes, bills, and assemble a valuable collection of them. They can also sell these collections throughout the game, to make an extra profit by the end of the game. The player who knows the most about the acquired art treasure's historical background, can make lavish benefits quickly, but who has less knowledge can also get one copy released for auction, with the help of an expert. Knowledge or tactics may prove better? Whoever collects the most forints by the end of the game, will be the most succesful numismatic and the winner of the game. The game was created with educational purpose, and its aim is to present and teach Hungarian history. Every thesis has five questions, specific fot that era, which must be answered correctly to win the subject of auction. If someone doesn't know the correct answer for the question, he/she can hire the other players as experts, who will get payment for the right answer from the principal. The biggest income can be reached by selling collections during the game.

Palette Swap is a logic puzzle game in which you are given a 'palette' of primary colors that you mix, match, and swap with your fellow artists in order to complete your schemes to win the game. If you're clever you can complete multiple schemes in a single turn, and close attention is rewarded if another artist creates the scheme you were after when it's not your turn.

On your turn, you may spend two of the five colors in your palette for their effects. Red allows you to match the colors your opponents have, yellow lets you mix your colors and theirs together, and blue lets you swap a color from your palette with one of another player's colors. Playing two primary colors gets you a secondary color, which simply combines the effects of the two primaries. Playing two secondary colors get you a black, which you must use on your next turn and costs you your secondary colors.

As you change the colors you possess and your opponent's, you will be able to complete your goals, all of which are based on actual color schemes such as split complimentary and analogous schemes. Accomplishing all five of your schemes first makes you the victor.

The game also includes cooperative rules for playing with younger children and rules for solo play.
