Pick a power on your card and compare it with the values on the other players´cards. The players win or lose cards on each round, and the last player with cards left wins the game.
This Deck is specifically dedicated to Donatello even if all the Ninja Turtles Characters are present

Description from the publisher:

Stockpile: Continuing Corruption, the first expansion for Stockpile, the critically acclaimed stock market game of insider trading, contains four expansion modules that can be played separately or used all together for more strategic game play.

Module 1: Forecast Dice — Six custom dice offer more excitement by changing the market forecast round-to-round. Roll the dice at the beginning of the round and select the appropriate forecast cards. The dice cause the market to rise or fall on any given turn and ensures that no two games will ever be the same.

Module 2: Bonds — Bond Cards present a new, safe investment option for players. Purchasing bonds can provide a steady stream of interest payments every round. However, it comes at a price. The original money used to purchase bonds cannot be recovered until the end of the game. Be careful not to overspend or you might lose out on valuable stockpiles.

Module 3: Commodities & Taxes — Commodities & Taxes create much more tension during the game. Each round, players add Commodity or Tax Cards face-up to the stockpiles. Collect different types of commodities and dodge taxes to earn substantial end-game bonuses.

Module 4: More Investors — Stockpile: Continuing Corruption adds six new investors from around the world who have heard about massive profits being made, and they want in on the action. Each investor possesses a unique ability that opens up more ways to win.

This game simulates a soccer game. The player who manages to get the ball into the goal of his oppenent wins the game. Movement of the pawns is determined by a die. The moving pawns kick the ball forward and end their movement one space behind the ball. One space can hold more than one pawn and the ball.

Strategy and conquest in Ming Dynasty China

36 stratagems is a game for 2-3 players, which is set in the Ming Dynasty era in China and neighbouring states. You are the leader of one of several rival families aspiring to become emperor of the entire region. You start the game in a capital city, with just an aspiring emperor, a scout and a small army. The aim of the game is to become the dominant dynasty by conquering and maintaining control of capitals, temples and prefectures. Each of these actions will help you in gaining the victory points needed to be the undisputed emperor and win the game. To be successful you will need to manage your budding empire carefully. Manage your economy through taxation and expanding your population, secure stability by maintaining cohesion among the political elite, maintain a strong military through recruitment of units supported by powerful generals and avoid rebellion by gaining popularity with your subjects. You face an uncertain destiny and will encounter many challenges during your reign including plague, piracy and looting. However, you are not alone in your quest, as to help you gain the power you so richly deserve and to undermine your pitiful and unworthy enemies, you can call upon the ancient Chinese wisdom enshrined in the 36 stratagems.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
New Year, New Challenge!Each year the greatest gnome tinkerer, Mastermind, takes on a new apprentice. Becoming this apprentice is a most coveted position, bringing with it the chance to learn skills and techniques that most only dream of; not to mention the prestigious work afterward. You will play as an up-and-coming gnomish tinkerer fighting for your place as Mastermind’s protégé at this year’s competition. Mastermind chooses this apprentice each year by their ability to finish a single task. It will be your job to destroy a Masterbot; a steamwork contraption Mastermind has designed and built to attack your workshop.

				
				
					PlayEach player receives a game board, a player placard for tracking resources, a gnome character, and a starter bot they can build to help defend their workshop from Masterminds Masterbot.  
Players take turns playing their characters and Mastermind. On Mastermind’s turn all the players decide the actions of the Masterbot attacking the player to their left , while the players attempt to defend their workshop from destruction. On the players turn dice are rolled to gather resources. Bots are then bought, scrapped, or built. If a bot has been built, the player then has the option to attack and try to take out Mastermind’s machine.

				
				
					ObjectivePlayers must succeed in destroying their assigned Masterbot before the competition can destroy theirs. Sounds easy enough, but the other competitors will try to sabotage you at every turn even while trying to destroy their Masterbots.

Barely Balanced is a quick game of daredevil antics and reckless stupidity in which you try to create an acrobatic show that would strike fear into the hearts of insurance adjusters everywhere.

Game play - Each round players start with a hand of 6 cards. The goal is to play tricks using the cards in their hand. Players my trade cards from the table or draw more cards from the deck. The player to play all their cards take the round, but all the tricks played by everyone are scored. Once a single player has taken 3 round the game ends. The player who has scored the most points over the course of the total rounds wins the game.

Game play was influenced by French Tarot and Japaneses Hanafuda and other classic card games.

The Barely Balanced card game is based on the Comedic/Acrobatic stunt show Barely Balanced.

Barely Balanced game was designed as promotional product for the group by designer Max Holliday. Max had been a fan of the acrobatic group for a few years, and while watching a show at the Bristol Ren Fair came up with the game. He proposed the game as soon as the show ended.

From the back of the box:

Take your knowledge to the mat with this fascinating trivia game about wrestling!  Young people can play with the old "pros"... Everybody wins! - But there is only one champion!

800 World Wrestling Federation Trivia Questions.  Go for that World Wrestling Federation Championship belt... Flaunt your knowledge... Display your acting talents... Pin you Opponents! Create your own "Dream Team".

At the end of the tournament - Claim the "Prize" (The best spot in front of the TV?) - Until the next match.

Leprechauns Disle Dubh (Black Dice) Expansion adds a bit of light headedness and passing out to your game of Leprechauns. Just add the single black die to your game bag and have a laugh when your opponent is rudely interrupted.

Disle Dubh adds one 6 sided dice to your game of Leprechauns. Two of the faces on the die have new game rules. One called Pass out which stops the players turn immediately and Light Headed which causes a player to start his turn over.

Description from the publisher:

Amallian was an amazing kingdom, with lush forests, amazing coasts, cuddly martial artists, and the best Emperor known. The one downside of living there was the constant infighting of the clans. They not only disagreed about the way the empire was built, but the way they traded with their neighbors, too.

The clans didn't agree on much, but the Emperor decreed that there was to be no open fighting amongst them. This "peace" lasted for many years. Then came the storm of all storms. Sweeping many clans away, the Emperor was lost for solutions — but then in the Osae Valley, a mist had appeared, and those who entered it were transported to a new land. The Emperor decreed that all clans should venture forth and settle their new empire.

As they did, the oversight was little to none...which lead to the clans competing for the favor of the Emperor by any means necessary. Now the clans of Amallian race to win the favor of the Emperor by building her Empire, trading goods, and showing the most strength on the other side!

In Legends of the Mist, you take on the role of a legend from one of the four clans. By rolling dice from your pool, you can work to build the Emperor's new kingdom with new plots, fight to control them, produce goods from them, and gain wealth for your clan and the kingdom. Each die type has a different frequency of letting you perform these actions. The other clans will work to undermine you every now and again and since dice are assigned in secret, your planning and foresight are important.

Should you complete the objectives that the Empress gave you better than anyone else, you will be rewarded as the winner of her favor!

Introduction
Gamble your family’s coin in search of emerging riches! Buy and sell goods at the whim of a wild market that will keep you guessing and laughing with wonder! Will you make your fortune on one big deal or diversify your goods and strike smartly?
Carry silk, wine, spice, and more on your wagon throughout medieval Europe. Borrow funds from the Merchant Bank and accept contracts from wealthy nobles to afford and deliver these prized crates for extra rewards. 
By the end of the year, will you return home as a wealthy winner or a penniless peasant?
Merchants of Medieval Europe is a 3-6 player game designed for casual gamers and families.​


Winning the Game
At the end of the last round, the player with the most coin wins. Subtract from scores if players have uncompleted contracts and/or debt.

Length of Game
The standard game lasts 12 rounds. Exhaust the Market Deck once for one standard game. Lengthen the game in 12 round increments if you
prefer a longer experience.

Setup
1. Place the market good tracker discs on the numbers located around the board’s Perimeter Market matching the Initial Value chart above. 
2. Shuffle the contract cards and make the contract deck. Shuffle the market cards and create the Market deck by drawing 12 cards. Place the remaining market cards back in the game box.
3. Separate the 8 colored good cards and create the Resource Supply by placing all of them next to the board along with all the coin and Banknotes. This is the Merchant Bank of Europe.
4. Each player takes 1 horse mat (one horse side face up), one color matching pawn, and 15 coin.
5. Each player draws 4 Contract cards, chooses 1, and places their pawn on the Contract’s delivery location. Return the Contract cards to the deck and shuffle the deck.
6. The player who traveled the farthest away in the last week is the first player. Play continues clockwise from the first player.

Gameplay
1. Market Phase

        i. The first player flips a market card and places it on top of a market discard deck.
        ii. Players adjust the Perimeter Market good trackers to their new values dictated by the drawn market card.


2. Accounting Phase

        i. Players can upgrade their wagon to two horses.
        ii. Players can take 1 debt banknote.
        iii. Players can pay back any amount of debt banknotes.


3. Action Phase

        i. The first player begins their turn and takes up to 4 actions.
        
        a. End their turn by adjusting the Perimeter Market based on their buys/sells, +1 for buys and -1 for sells.



        ii. The Action Phase continues clockwise until every player takes up to 4 actions and ends their turn.


4. Round Ends

Action Phase
Take up to 4 actions per turn in any order and you can repeat any particular action any number of times until you run out of actions.
A. Move- Earn movement points from your wagon and move your pawn. 1 movement point allows movement from one region into an adjacent region.
B. Buy- Buy as many goods as you can afford from the resources stockpile, so long as you are in a region that sells the desired good, the good is in stock, and you have room in your wagon.
C. Trade- Buy, sell, or trade goods with another player in the same colored territory as your pawn.
D. Sell- From any region, you can sell any number of goods back to the stockpile for the current market value as dictated by the Perimeter Market.
E. Contracts- Accept a contract by drawing 4 contract cards, choosing 1, and discarding the remaining cards. Alternatively, complete a contract at the written destination with the required goods.

Version of Monopoly - Kraków Edition.

The game uses all of the rules and values (prices, tax), money and pawns from the standard Monopoly game.

Changes:
- Names of places to buy (properties, utilities) were changes to the know places in Kraków
- Board is lustrated with photos from the city
- Community chest & chance cards have city related flavor texts

Extra:
- The game contains one Top Trumps card - Wawel Royal Castle

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 2 – Spitfire Mk. IX
description:
Publisher Blurb:

The Spitfire Mk. IX was designed and developed to compete with the Focke Wulf FW-190 which in some regards could out-perform and out-climb previous Spitfire versions. The Mark IX was based on the Mark V airframe with the addition of the new generation of Merlin engines allowed this aircraft to duel with the deadly German enemy. Great Aces that gloriously piloted this aircraft included George "The Falcon of Malta" Beurling, Johnnie Johnson and Stanisław Skalski. Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW2 Wings of Glory Airplane Pack is a ready-to-play model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW2 Wings of Glory game product. In each pack you will find all you need to play with the airplane: a special base with gaming stats, a variable altitude flying stand, and a specific deck of maneuver cards. Larger maneuver cards (63 x 88 mm) are included with this model, to handle the fast movement of this airplane.
family: History: World War II (Battle of Britain)

description:
H.E.A.D. Hunters is a card-driven miniatures game for two or more players. Each player controls their own 5.25" Hero of Every Age & Dimension (H.E.A.D.) Hunter Toy that stores all the components of the game in its head.  Inside each H.E.A.D. Hunter is its own themed deck of cards, battle bases, measuring string and combat dice.

The starter pack includes Erik Thanessen (Viking) & Xeno X9 (Robot). Cards from other H.E.A.D. Hunters can be mixed and matched to customize the player's deck, matching their own play style.

Each character has a unique set of starting terrain as part of their deck that can be placed in a multitude of positions and layouts to create unlimited fields of battle. Once these are placed, the players draw and play cards to attack and defend against other Hunters as they move their toy around the table for position and opportunity; where you attack from is as important as what you attack with!

Game play continues until one Hunter is left standing, but be warned: As players take and inflict damage, unique character abilities are enabled which can turn the tide of the game. When the game is over, all the components fit back inside the head while the toy sits patiently (and attractively) on your shelf, waiting for its next opponent.

From the box:

The original Five Suit Game.

Official American Rules for Five Suit Castle Bridge.

Originated 1933 by Ammiel F. Decker

Each player has a set of four pawns that start in his or her side of the board. The goal is to move the pawns around the board to the "Ziel" section. Movement is determined by a die. All players move clockwise around the same board, so they may capture each others pawns. Whenever a pawn arrives on a space as the result of a die-roll, it advances by the number of spaces shown on that space. If a pawn arrives on a red space, it advances into an inner ring. The pawns have to circle the innermost ring, till they manage to enter their "Ziel" section. Captured pawns are returned to their player's "Start" section. The winner is the first player to move all pawns into the "Ziel" section.
This game was published about 1930.

boardgame: Elections of US America Election: The Card Game
description:
Description from the publisher:

Elections of US America Election: The Card Game brings together the best snark of political website Wonkette with the game design smarts of game developer Auroch Digital’s ‘GameTheNews’ initiative to create the greatest social card game in political history, mostly because it’s the FIRST ONE IN HISTORY TOO! (Maybe.)

You as the player take on the role of a campaign manager backing a candidate(past, present or fictional). Each turn you have a choice, go for votes or play a Wonk card. Wonk cards will either up your candidate's Independant, Republican and/or Democrat appeal, or damage your opponents.

The player with the most voter points at the end of the round wins the state. Each state card has electoral college votes, to win a short game you need to be the first player to reach 70 electoral college votes with at least 4 state cards, or go for the long haul you need to be the first to 270 electoral college votes. 
So, will you play fair and hope winning over the voters will get  your candidate into the White House or sling mud like the best of them and go down fighting?

From the publisher:

WizArt is a strategy card game for 2-3 players where you try to acquire knowledge from 3 different magic schools (the cosmic, the elemental or the temporal) in order to graduate as a mage faster than your opponents. To win the game, you must play cards and invest knowledge crystals. If you played well, you will then be able to level up at the beginning of your turn and acquire more power. Depending on the cards that you play, you will be able to use crystals of your opponents. If you reach mage level 5 or if you have eliminated all other players, you win.

WizArt is an exciting strategy card game with a lot of player interaction which is easy to learn but hard to master. It takes about 30 minutes to play and is suitable for players who are 8 years or older.

The players have to move their pawn from the starting point to one of the yellow or red spaces in the center of the game board. The red and yellow dice determine movement on the game track, while the green symbol die controls movement on the checkered center section by one or two spaces each turn. The results of the yellow and red dice are translated by each player's dice result card. Possible outcomes are forward movement (V), backward movement (Z) or no movement at all (Symbol).

The Corner spaces have the following meanings:
V - move forward to the next red triangle
Z - move backward to the last yellow triangle
Symbol - miss your next turn
black spot - return to the black starting space

The game was likely published in the 1920s.

From the publisher:

Ready, set, sumo! Japan's centuries-old national sport is the main attraction in this paper-based smackdown where creativity and competition collide. The result? Laughs guaranteed! There is an impressive assortment of 39 characters—from traditional sumo wrestlers to animals to monsters and other eye-catching creatures from Japanese lore. Additional characters can be created by drawing on customizable shapes. Then battle it out against an opponent's character on the sumo doyho ring. With each player tapping on his or her side of the doyho, the stakes are high until one of the characters falls and the other wins!

Each player has to move his 16 meeples from his starting positions around the game board and over his launching pad (the five spaces of his own color) to the blue "luck" space at the center of the game board. Movement is done one space along the black lines or by jumping over other meeples, regardles if they're own or opponent ones. Each meeple reaching it's own launching pad, immediately goes to the "luck" space and is taken out of the game. The first player to get all his meeples on the "luck" space wins the game.

boardgame: Star Wars: The Card Game – Ancient Rivals
description:
Description from the publisher:

Ancient Rivals is the first Force Pack in the  Opposition cycle for Star Wars: The Card Game.

Throughout the Galactic Civil War, certain feuds and rivalries have defined countless conflicts. The Jedi battle the Sith lords, playing out the age-old struggle between the light and dark sides of the Force. The armies and navies of the Rebels and the Empire clash on planets and in the skies above them. The galaxy’s smugglers and spies struggle for supremacy against the scum that lurk in the shadows. These dynamic rivalries have shaped the Galactic Civil War, and now, with the the Opposition cycle, they will take their rightful place in Star Wars: The Card Game as well.

Ancient Rivals kicks off the Opposition cycle and simultaneously serves as an introduction to the new themes and mechanics that will be found therein. Here, you will find a host of cards that focus on the struggles between pairs of affiliations: Jedi and Sith, Rebel Alliance and Imperial Navy, Smugglers and Spies and Scum and Villainy. The Opposition cycle also introduces a host of new fate cards, including a new type—fate cards with an affiliation. Ancient Rivals also offers up a host of iconic characters: the heroes and villains of Star Wars: Rebels begin to appear in the LCG, joined by new versions of classic characters like Dengar, Han Solo, and Darth Vader. Immerse yourself in the epic conflicts and battles of the Galactic Civil War in the Opposition cycle, beginning with Ancient Rivals!

Contains objective sets 231-235.
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: Star Wars – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
A monstrous board game encouraging kids & families to have imaginative fun with game play imaginative interaction!

A classic dungeon setting is the backdrop as players assume the roles of tough hunters bound and determined to do three things: to defeat as many monsters as possible, to collect treasure, and to make it out unscathed.

Players become bona fide monster hunters as they delve into dungeons filled with beasts and obstacles. At the beginning of each session, players choose one of three game modes in which they will compete. Treasure Hunt, Monster Hunt, or Dungeon Escape.

At the beginning of each session, players choose one of three game modes in which they will compete:

Treasure Hunt – Be the first to get to the treasure

Monster Hunt – Defeat all the monsters on the board, player with the most captured wins

Dungeon Escape – Be the first player to get to the finish exit

Up to 4 boards can be arranged in numerous ways based on the number of players. Obstacle tiles including corridors, pits, locked doors, and secret doors are added to the boards. The number depends on the game mode being played. Monsters are then added to the board.

Each player rolls a d10 for movement through squares that make up the dungeon boards. When they get to obstacles they must make an extra roll to get past them. Sometimes loosing a turn, or having to turn around and find another path. When players encounter monsters they must reference the Monster's card to see how to defeat them. Examples are an opposing roll of the the die, rolling an odd number, rolling 7 or higher.

boardgame: The Hobbit: the Desolation of Smaug Strategy Battle Game
description:
From the publisher:

The Hobbit: The Desolation of Smaug provides a wealth of supplemental rules, which recreate the thrilling events of the film on the tabletop with your collection of Citadel miniatures. This 64-page, full colour, softback expansion for The Hobbit: An Unexpected Journey includes:


     8 brand new scenarios recreating the action-packed events in The Hobbit: The Desolation of Smaug.
     Rules for linking your games with Thorin’s Company into a dynamic narrative.
     Rules for fighting in the realm of Mirkwood, and 3 new Points Match Scenarios.
     A gallery showcasing several hobbyists’ collections of Citadel miniatures, inspired by the warriors of Middle-earth and the events of the movies.
     An ‘Eavy Metal masterclass of Gandalf™ the Grey.
     Profiles and rules for the Good and Evil miniatures in The Hobbit: The Desolation of Smaug.

boardgame: The Hobbit: The Battle of the Five Armies Strategy Battle Game
description:
The Hobbit: An Unexpected Journey Strategy Battle Game is the tabletop game for two or more players. This free PDF expansion is packed with supplemental rules which enable you to recreate the thrilling events of The Hobbit: The Battle of the Five Armies™ with your collection of Citadel miniatures.

This expansion includes two exciting new scenarios that recreate the pivotal events of The Hobbit: The Battle of Five Armies, allowing you to join in the adventure as Thorin’s Company concludes its quest to reclaim Erebor.

This PDF includes profiles and rules for a host of new Citadel miniatures allowing you to play games with our favorite heroes and villains from The Hobbit: The Battle of Five Armies, from the great and terrible dragon, Smaug, to the mighty heroes of the Free Peoples that form the heart of the White Council.

The updated army lists enable you to field the warring hosts that fought in the climactic Battle of Five Armies on the tabletop, from the valiant survivors of Smaug’s attack on Lake-town to the majestic Elven host led by Thranduil, King of Mirkwood.
family: Fictional Events: The Battle of Five Armies

description: Saami is a semi-cooperative game in which players need to eliminate threats while trying to improve their own personal position in the game.

The players have to move their four cubes from the diagonaly opposite corner to the nest of their color. Movement to a neighboring space is achieved by tilting the cube by 90 degree, thereby changing the Image on the top of the cube. The movement can be continued in a straight line as Long as thre are unoccupied spaces. If two cubes are sitting next to or pass each other, the stronger image kills the weeker one (hunter - Fox - weasel - chicken - frog - gnat). The strongest Image is the hunter but he can be killed by the weakest, which is the gnat. Killed cubes are removed from the game. Cubes that have entered their own nest must show the correct animal on top to be saved. A saved animal counts 3 points and the cubes on the board of the last player count 2 points at the end of the game.

In the game of Fantasy Stables you're trying to make it in the horse business.  Your friends will now be your rivals as you compete to see who will end up on top. The object of the game is to collect the most money in cash, horse value and bonuses. Bonuses are awarded for collecting certain groups of horses. They add another level of strategy to the game. Game play is very easy and intuitive for horse people but no horse knowledge is needed to play.

Everyone begins the game with money and horses to start the fun right away. There are several suggested winning scenarios for you to choose from for one hour of play to days of fun. All the turns are cooperative but competitive to keep things lively!

Collect and show horse cards featuring fabulous pictures of real horses in 8 equestrian sports (Dressage, Driving, Trail, Western Pleasure, Western Performance, Racing, Cross Country and Hunter Jumper).

Add horses to your stable at public auctions where everyone can bid. Bid as high as you dare – you know the range but the horse’s actual value is kept a secret until the winning bid has been paid!
Everyone can enter the shows and choosing the right horse will give you an advantage but anyone could steal the win if luck is on their side.

There are random and yearly horse expenses to watch out for so manage your salary and show winnings well. All the players get a real-life horse job (vet, farrier, trainer etc.) who are paid the expenses to keep those interactive too!

Horses are unpredictable so you never know what will be around the corner – wins or losses!

Bird of Happiness is a simple deck-building game in which players try to collect happy birds, some of which will provide special help to the players. Watch for the four-leaf clover as well since those can also aid you...

From their Evil Lairs / Parent's basement players rule Cityopolis by fear, defeating wave after wave of superheroes with their diabolical creations! Who will defeat the most heroes and rein supreme... from jail when they get caught because... well... Superheroes are awesome like that.

Players get a hand of cards from which they will play or discard 2 per turn. For example minions, like Florence 'Hard Nut' McGee, could be placed on the field and players can start to create fiendishly clever weapons like the Poisonous Death Lamp. Alternatively they could play event cards such as the "Publish a photo of your rival stroking a Kitten" to reduce the fear gripping the city. Finally they could get bored and call up a Superhero to help the city... Then later use them for target practise. Superheroes start a set distance from the City and move closer after every round. If they reach the city they start to reduce the fear count which acts as the game timer.

Reducing fear and calling up Superheroes nets players kudos worth VP's at the end but defeating Superheroes earns you Capes which are worth more VP's and after all you are Mostly Evil.

BuySellorHold? is an intelligent and fun-filled action-packed game of strategy that helps players to learn about the Stock Market in an enjoyable and stimulating way.

Throughout the game, share prices move after Government announcements, world news and company statements, and you will have to decide what to do. Real-life events such as buying a house, tax refunds and work bonuses also make a difference, as does luck, since the throw of a die changes share prices for all players.

Three types of game:


     The Introductory Game – the basic game, simplified with fewer features to help people get more familiar with the game.



     The Beginners' Game – the addition of a few more features such as share dealing from outside investors, 'Life' events and portfolio valuations during the game.



    The Advanced Game – this option takes the game to the next level, and is ideally suited to people who have got to grips with the game and are looking for the ultimate challenge.

The first official expansion for the Legend: History of 1000 Miglia game. Not only it introduces 18 brand new cars, but it TOTALLY reimplements the base game: new dashboards for the base game cars are included in the expansion, so you can play the base game using the new rules.

IMPROVEMENTS:

Now cars are balanced on the base of a yearly budget each player can use to buy the car, the driver (who gives bonus on racing) and spare parts.

Cars are more accurately designed and feature different and peculiar curve speeds so they do not ride the track in the same way and the difference between cars fast on straights and those faster on winding roads is much more significant and influences the fate of the race.

12 Single tracks and races have been included to play the game in one session, as well as the chance to race the entire Mille Miglia in one session of approximately 3 hours.

Using the single tracks, drivers can play the Italian Racing Championship and evolve their racing team year by year purchasing new cars and drivers.

No more calculations or writings, yet the same accuracy. Now the time track is kept with the use of a in-game clock.

Several others improvements to balance and make the game more fluid and enjoyable.

From the rules:
Two people or a carload take the Olympic Trivia challenge. Test your knowledge by competing head-to-head with family and friends.  Learn how the Olympic Movement inspires everyone around the world in friendly competition.

User summary:
In Travel Olympic Challenge, players take turns choosing a category of Olympic trivia (such as countries or events). The player must then choose to try for a bronze, silver or gold medal. The player is then read a question by another player. If trying for gold, the player must answer with no hints. For silver, the player must choose an answer from three choices. For bronze, only two choices are offered. A correct question wins the medal, which is marked on a scorecard. A player's turn ends after a correct or incorrect answer.

On subsequent turns, players may upgrade medals already won by answering another question from the same level already won (e.g. a second silver medal in a category upgrades the player to gold in that category). The first player to win a gold medal in all five categories wins.

To shorten the game, it may be played with a certain number of rounds or a time limit. In this case, gold, silver and bronze medals score 3, 2 and 1 points, respectively. The player with the most points wins.

Your new favourite game to play with friends and family

BLOKD is an easy to play board game, the rules are simple, you have to block your opponent to win, at each turn, the player has the choice to move his cube or to block a square, the cube can be moved like the 'queen pawn' in chess game, any empty square can be blocked.

You win the game when you block your opponent, in other words when all the squares around the opponent's cube are blocked and he can't move his cube any more.

boardgame: Flash Point: Fire Rescue – Fire Prevention Specialist
description:
The Fire Prevention Officer is a KS exclusive from the Honor and Duty expansion. It includes a card and rules but no minis. It has been produced in 4500 copies and here's the rules for this character:

4 AP - This specialist has done his work years ago; he talked to the builders and home owners and convinced them to adopt a fire security plan. On his advice smoke detectors and smoke exhausts have been installed, and emergency exits have been planned and marked clearly. Now these measures prove their worth: People are warned and can move out via marked escape routes, and smoke clears off the building.

Prevent/De-Smoke: Move a smoke token anywhere on the board for 1 AP per space. Movement must be on the shortest route towards nearest exterior door, playerʼs choice if alternative routes exist; Smoke passes closed interior doors at no extra cost. Smoke vanishes when outside the building. Moved smoke ignites immediately if adjacent to fire; if it moves on top of other smoke, it becomes fire.
Prevent/Escape: Move a POI token (only if "?" side up) anywhere on the board for 2 AP per space. Movement must be on the shortest route towards nearest exterior door, playerʼs choice if alternative routes exist. POIs can open doors on their way for 2 AP; they will not use breached walls, ladders etc. (they use the escape route "as marked"). POIs moved outside of the building (=coordinate area) are revealed. POIs will not move into fire.

Note: If playing with multi-floor game boards, "shortest route to exterior door" may be via a stair. Note: In the subway station, exits are all spaces with an ambulance symbol.

2017 update:
Included in Flash Point: Fire Rescue – Tragic Events with a corresponding miniature.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Hishigata Shogi (Diamond Shaped Shogi) is a large board shogi variant played on a 19x19 board like Maka Dai-Dai Shogi. Each player has 31 pieces of 22 different types placed in a diamond shaped starting configuration. The pieces are a subset of the Maka Dai-Dai set (96 pieces of 50 different types), and move and promote in the same way as in Maka Dai-Dai Shogi. By using no pawns and predominantly the more powerful pieces from Maka Dai-Dai Shogi in a much more confronting starting position (only one rank separates the two players) Hishigata Shogi is a much faster and more dynamic game than Maka Dai-Dai Shogi while preserving the flavor of the old large board shogi variant.

The game was published at the chessvariants.com homepage in 2005.
Se also description of Hishigata Shogi at Wikipedia as part of the Maka Dai-Dai Shogi article.
Expansion set "designed for players who have mastered the main boxed game". Additional rules and variations that add complexity, with tougher challenges. Includes a new magical combat system.

Formerly titled, "V-Commandos: Résistance", now renamed, "V-Sabotage: Résistance".

Cooperate with the European WW2 Resistance movements to harry the enemy and prepare for the final victory!

Be the stealthiest heroes of World War 2. Appear like shadows behind enemy lines and carry on outstanding operations to undermine the adversary. Make good use of the commandos’ best weapons: stealth, speed of action and self-control.

V-Commandos: Résistance is an expansion to the base game which adds the following features.

13 new terrains (using 15 new tiles) and 5 operations involving up to 6 commandos (increases player count)

3 new characters with unique abilities: the SOE Agent, the Saboteur and the Spy.

18 new events to face

Request equipment airdrops and use your binoculars to improve your luck

Escort your allies while fighting against German officers and guard dogs.  Beware of deadly gas.
family: History: Resistance during WW2 in Europe

description:
The ants scurry through the grass as the summer sun shines down.  Suddenly, a leaf comes crashing down amidst the hustle and the bustle.  The ants stare in disbelief, for they know what that means.

Winter is coming.

With a wary glance, the ants retreat to their respective clans.  The fast approach of winter means food must be gathered, and as every ant knows, the clan that gathers the most food holds all the power in the Colony.  The race is on! Who will come out on top before the chill of winter sets in?

Autumn Ants is a game where 2 to 4 players take control of a clan of ants competing for control of their Colony.  Using a system of dice selection, players will explore the unknown meadow, gather seeds, build dirt walls to hold back their competitors, and maybe even build shrines to gain the blessings of their Ant-cestors! Hurry though, since winter comes sooner than any ant would like...

In Autumn Ants, players move their ant pawns around a randomly generated world, growing their ant colony to try to out compete their fellow players.  Each round, dice are rolled and each player will select one die.  This die will decide both turn order and a special action that can be used that round.  The player with the most food tokens brought back to their ant hill at game end will be the winner!

Kingdom Builder: Harvest, the fourth boxed expansion for the award winning game Kingdom Builder designed by Donald X. Vaccarino, builds on this already exciting world of skill acquisition and land dominance.

Kingdom Builder: Harvest adds many new strategic possibilities to this already classic game. Farmland is a new terrain type which does not have terrain cards. The only way to expand into it is to build into adjacent terrains and spread into the Farmland. Silos on the board allow players at the end of the game to move up to three of their own pieces adjacent to any empty land hex next to their own settlement. The Cabin ability allows the use of two Scout meeples that can traverse land, water, or mountains. Six new Kingdom Builder cards encourage expansion.

Cupcake Race is a simple children's game for 2-4 players.

Players 'race' to complete their cupcake first, which involves spinning and moving around the board to the 'PARTY' at the finish line.

Stops along the way include Mix it, Bake it, Frost it, and Top it, each with a corresponding accessory to add to your cupcake, along with sprinkles of various shapes and colours.

The contents are of very high quality plastic, with a sturdy game board and box, however the game itself will likely entertain only the youngest players (ages 4+).

From the Box:
There's a party tonight, and you (and your opponents) have been asked to bring the cupcakes. The Race is on! Be the first player to MIX, BAKE, FROST, and TOP your cupcake by visiting all 4 corners, and collect sprinkles along the way. The first player to reach the party with a completed cupcake and a sprinkle of each color wins the cherry to put on the top! "Mix it, Bake it, Frost it, Top it! So Much Fun, You Just Can't Stop It!"

Immerse yourself in the conflicts of the Ancient World.

A great collection of classic and contemporary abstract strategy games is waiting for you to explore. As you discover what’s inside, it takes you back in time and around the world.  Travel to ancient Greece, through Assyria, then the Roman and Persian Empires, until you finally arrive by the great Yangtze River in the Middle Kingdom of China.

Unearth pieces inside the treasure chest to play popular and well-loved games such as Chess and Checkers (Draughts).  Discover Greek and Roman classics like Seega and Latunculi that are still being enjoyed in parts of the world today.  And as you dig deeper, you will also bring to light games with themes of ancient conflict created by contemporary game designers.   You can join the battle for Troy in ILIOS, sink Viking warships in Journey Stones, or expand your kingdom in Cartography. King's Ransom is a lighter chess variant with a prince, a bit of luck and a lot of bluffing! In Quorsum you race your horsemen home while obstructing your opponent's in a tile flipping, dice game. Kush offers a fast moving tile stacking game with an unusual twist: you get to move either your opponent's or your own tiles! Yangtze seems like a simple tile matching game until you  discover to master it both players need to think about the modern concerns of environmental conservation to prevent the river drying up.

Some of these games have also been offered as standalone box sets or Treasure Chest Jewels and some are only available is part of the basic Treasure Chest Crown Jewel. The Treasure Chest World Collection offers everything together with components for  11 classic and contemporary strategy games.

Game pieces are designed for use in multiple games, and can be stored in colorful handmade bags.  All the games have simple rules that take 2 minutes to learn and 10-30 minutes  to play.  In some games, the length and complexity of the game can be adjusted.

Decorate your living room with the Treasure Chest, keep it in sight at all times and entice your guests into a world of Ancient Conflict. Let this classic collection entertain you and create wonderful memories for your family and friends for generations to come.

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 2 – Messerschmitt Bf.110
description:
Publisher Blurb:

The Messerschmitt Bf.110 was a twin-engine heavy fighter in the service of the Luftwaffe during World War II and served with success in the early campaigns, the Polish, Norwegian and Battle of France. Later in the war, it was developed into a formidable night fighter, becoming the major night-fighting aircraft of the Luftwaffe. Great Aces that gloriously piloted this aircraft were Fritz Schupp, Georg Christl and Günther Radusch. Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW2 Wings of Glory Airplane Pack is a ready-to-play model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW2 Wings of Glory game product. In each pack you will find all you need to play with the airplane: a special base with gaming stats, a variable altitude flying stand, and a specific deck of maneuver cards. This is not a complete game. To play, you must own a WW2 Rules and Accessories Pack or WW2 Starter Set.

Comes in a Schupp version, a Christl version, and a Radusch  version.
family: History: World War II (Battle of Britain)

description:
From the designer:

You are the pilot of a top of the line Mechanized Bipedal Combat Platform, commonly referred to as a Mech.  You will customize your 25' tall Mech with a selection of weaponry and upgrades, and then hunt and engage the enemy.

This solitaire game is played over a series of turns where you may engage randomly generated encounters.  Attacks are made through die-rolling that can activate a variety of weaponry and standard attacks.  While you dish out damage to the enemy, you must balance the desire to rack up victory points against the damage and heat that your Mech will accrue.

All that you will need to play this game are 6 standard dice, 2 printed sheets, a pencil and an eraser.

A gracious nod to Drew Chamberlain whose DELVE: The Dice Game was a strong inspiration for this game.

Formerly titled, "V-Commandos: Secret Weapons", now renamed, "V-Sabotage: Secret Weapons".

Isolated in hostile territories, strike at the very heart of enemy facilities and sabotage their most strategic secret projects.  

Be the stealthiest heroes of World War 2. Appear like shadows behind enemy lines and carry on outstanding operations to undermine the adversary. Make good use of the commandos’ best weapons: stealth, speed of action and self-control.

V-Commandos: Secret Weapons is an expansion to the base game which adds the following features.

13 new terrains (using 15 new tiles) and 5 operations involving up to 6 commandos (increases player count)

3 new characters with unique abilities: the Gunner, the Intel Officer and the Butcher.

18 new events to face

Remotely destroy your targets using the powerful Panzerfaust and remain stealthy thanks to the smoke grenade

Fight elite paratroopers and avoid the Goliath mini tanks.

The object of this game is for the players, using dice and playing pieces, to travel on the gameboard from a residence square to the bank and shopping locations shown on their shopping lists. The first player to return to his residence with the travel cards for all locations contained on his shopping list wins.

Colonial Space Wars: Invasion (CSW) is a sci-fi tactical wargame with 18mm scale miniatures that recreates battles between armies called Combat Swords. Each one is formed by a group of soldiers, drones or other creatures, represented in the game by metal or resin miniatures. These models move across a tabletop with scenery elements that emulate open battlefields, military complexes, the interior of buildings or spacecrafts.

CSW battles are resolved by Turns, in which each player acts alternatively. At the beginning of his Turn, one player will add to his pool the COMMAND points given by his LEADER or NODE units. COMMAND points available can be used then to activate units, and each unit requires a number of COMMAND points to be used.

A typical game is a Sudden Death match, where the winner is the player that eliminates their opponent’s ability to activate units. However, in addition to this a description of advanced rules and missions for more experienced players is given.

Alien Uprising: Zothren Invasion, a standalone game in the Alien Uprising family, is played in two phases. During the first phase, the players go through a card-drafting process to build robots which will be used to fight off an alien invasion. Each robot will have six body parts, each with various weapons and armor. Once the robots are fully constructed, the Zothren Invasion begins.

Players work in a voluntary co-op fashion to defend earth from the Zothren. If all of the robots are destroyed by the Zothren, humanity is lost. However, if the requisite number of aliens are successfully defeated, then the player who controls the robot that has the accumulated the most Zothren losses is the victor.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
You are on a mission… to place your precious gemstone into the Treasure Chest. First player to do so wins the game. Sounds simple right?

The trick is to use the 8 supporter stones on the game board to navigate a path to the Treasure Chest. To win you must strategically plan your moves and stop your opponent in their tracks. Before they trap you.

Rapid gameplay and extremely simple rules make for incredible fun! May the best player win.

A trivia-game in which you throw two dice in your turn. You move your pawn the number op pips on the dice and answer a question from the topcard of the deck in the color of the place your pawn is on. The player on your left asks the question.
There are 5 categories:
orange = general
blue = architecture
red = culture/sports
purple = economics/geography
green = history
yellow = free choice of category

When you answer the question right, you get a marker of the color of the question asked. You now may throw the dice again for another turn. You can only ever have one marker of each color. When your pawn gets to a color you already have a marker for, just answer the question but no further markers are earned.

When you answer the question wrong, your turn is over and the player to your left is the next player to throw the dice.

When you reach the finish (spot 160) you have to have collected a marker of each category. The other players decide in which category your final quaetion is asked. When aswering right you win and when aswering wrong you get another shot next turn.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Green Knight Armor Expansion
description:
The Green Knight Armor is a powerful seven-piece armor set. Crafting the entire set requires resources and innovations from the Gorm, Spidicules, Flower Knight and Dung Beetle Knight along with rare gear acquired by beating the Manhunter and Lion Knight.

The individual pieces require the following expansions in order to build them:

Green Helm:             
Dung Beetle Knight only

Green Gloves:           
Gorm & Manhunter

Green Plate:            
Gorm, Lion Knight & Dung Beetle Knight

Green Faulds:           
Gorm, Spidicules, Flower Knight & Dung Beetle Knight

Green Boots:            
Gorm, Flower Knight & Dung Beetle Knight

Griswaldo (Weapon - Sword):    
Gorm, Flower Knight & Dung Beetle Knight

Fetorsaurus (Weapon - Shield):   
Gorm, Flower Knight & Dung Beetle Knight

A funny german Ask & Answer game for kids.

German description: 
Man bildet eine gemischte Tischrunde, d.h. es sollen möglichst immer ein Mädchen und ein Junge nebeneinander sitzen.

Nun kann das Spiel, das aus Frage- und Antwortkarten besteht, beginnen. Die Kärtchen werden verdeckt, d.h. mit der Rückenseite nach oben in die Tischmitte gestreut und zwar die Frage- und Antwortkarten gesondert. Sodann zieht ein Kind eine Fragekarte und stellt an seinen linken Mitspieler die auf der Karte stehende Frage. Dieser Nachbar zieht nun eine Antwortkarte und liest die Antwort laut vor. (...) 
So geht das im Kreis, bis alle Karten verlesen sind. Jede Karte wird nur einmal gelesen und dann weggelegt. Die Fragen können kreuz und weiter über den Tisch gestellt werden.
Und nun viel Spaß: 
Ihr werdet viel zu lachen haben.

boardgame: TRENCH SOLDIERS: A Solitaire Game of Combat in the Trenches of the First World War (1914-1918).
description:
INTRODUCTION: TRENCH SOLDIERS is a solitaire game of Combat and Survival in the muddy trenches in France or Belgium during World War I. The player assumes the role of an Infantry Soldier during the grueling and miserable tour in the Trenches. As a Soldier, you are a member of a Rifle Platoon responsible for securing your Sectors of Front by successfully taking enemy positions and defending yours with the least amount of casualties. As you survive and gain experience, your Soldier will improve his attributes and gain skills to help him further survive the horrors of War in the Trenches.
 GAME OBJECTIVE 
The objective of TRENCH SOLDIERS is to successfully survive a Tour in the trenches along the Western Front during World War I. A Tour consists of a number of Rotations in the Trenches. The Player can play a Short Tour (1 Rotation), an Average Tour (2 Rotations) or a Long Tour (3 Rotations). Each Rotation consists of making a Rotation Deck of 20 “Trench Life” Cards and once all Trench Life Cards have been drawn and resolved, the Rotation is over and the Player totals his Rotation VP and adds it to his Tour VP. Once all Rotations have been completed, the Player totals his Tour VP and then compares it to the Victory Determination Table to determine the level of success. Players can choose to play as the Germans, French, Commonwealth (British, Australian/New Zealanders or Canadians) or the Americans.

GAME FEATURES: TRENCH SOLDIERS features plenty of colorful Game Counters and Game Mat. The Game provides plenty of grim themes associated with the War in the Trenches. Counters include Infantry Soldiers, HMGs, Flamethrowers, MK-IV Tanks, A7 Tanks, FT-17 Tanks, Pillboxes, Aircraft, Sub-Machineguns and Light Machineguns from the various Combatants. In addition to the standard Western Front Campaign Scenario there is, also, a Gallipoli/Palestine Campaign with supporting Terrain Markers and Counters for the Ottoman Turks.

TRENCH SOLDIERS can be found at the following URL:
http://www.berserkergames.net

Doppelt und Dreifach is an abstract card game by an anonymous author. It consists of cards numbered 2-15, with higher numbers being rarer. Each player holds six cards at the beginning. In clockwise order, players play one card each. If it is higher than any other card currently on the table, the lower card is discarded and the player who had played it has to draw a new card. However, if two or more players play the same number, the card values are cumulated. When it's a player's turn and their card is still in front of them, they can discard it without redrawing. First player(s) to get rid of their cards wins.

Doppelt und Dreifach was anonymously sent to various people in March, 2016. The rules or the box give no hint at the identity of the designer, publisher or point of origin. In July, Friedemann Friese revealed that he was the designer (https://boardgamegeek.com/geeklist/210813/futschikato-story-must-be-told).

In TYPHOON-ALERT, each player owns a randomly chosen area in East Asia and tries to avoid damage caused by typhoons, which form continually during the game. The goal of this game is to survive typhoons and earn/keep as many life points as possible.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: The Diavolo Case (fan expansion for Sherlock Holmes Consulting Detective)
description:
When we reach 221B Baker Street, Watson greets us. "Holmes is abuzz this morning, he’s waiting in the lounge."

"Ah, there you are at last gentlemen, have you read the paper?"

We shake our heads. "Direct your attentions to the article 'Mysterious death at the embassy.'"

Holmes pauses while we read...

So begins "The Diavolo Case," Yann Gentil's second extra-case expansion for Sherlock Holmes Consulting Detective.

Available for download on the author's website (in French).

And translated into English by BGG Forum members and freely available in the Files section.

GUJA HUNT! (好獵手) is a board game designed for parties, featuring simple rules, that's fast paced and full of interactive game experiences. It combines elements of ice age civilization and modern sports with a bright art style, allowing the player to enjoy a leisurely, humorous, and fun board game experience as a primitive hunter.

《GUJA HUNT! 好獵手》是一款針對派對場合設計的桌上遊戲，強調簡單的規則、明快的節奏以及互動性。結合了冰河時代背景和現代運動元素，搭配上鮮豔明亮的美術風格，讓玩家在原始獵人無厘頭的狩獵行動中享受輕鬆、幽默且歡樂的遊戲過程。

WORKERS! (工人危機) is a game of observation and balance. In the game, players take turns placing wood and workers — and disturbing the other players — until someone makes the building collapse!

WORKERS!工人危機 是款闔家歡樂的小品遊戲，規則簡單、節奏快速且心機幽默，讓人忍不住一局接一局！遊戲中，玩家輪流擺放木條與工人，確保平穩的同時干擾其他玩家，直至有人弄倒建築出局！

Safari Fun is a children's spin-and-move board game given away at McDonald's restaurants. The game consists of a single sheet of thick paper with various punch-out elements that serve as the spinner, game pieces and 3-dimensional scenery. Play is standard for this type of game, with players moving along a track from start to finish, with some spaces that may move them forward or backward or cause gain or loss of a turn. Some spaces also call for the player to complete an action such as waving arms to chase away flies or pretending to swim across a river. Others feature animal facts. The first player to reach the finish wins.

boardgame: Sentinels of the Multiverse: Complete Hero Variant Collection
description: The Complete Hero Variant Collection contains EVERY hero variant card ever made. To set these cards apart from their original print runs, Adam Rebottaro (artist for all things Sentinels Comics) did ALL NEW ART for every single card.

The snake cheerfully creeps over the intersection. But suddenly, tromp, tromp, tromp someone trudges down her back. Whose feet were those? Players mimic the animals' movements using their fingers and hands on the backs of the other players. Whoever guesses the most animals can cleverly imitate the animal's walking style wins the game.

boardgame: Thieves and Cops: Blink to your Victim
description:
Thieves and Cops in the lonely roads of the town. Will you be the next victim? A quick game about observation and bluff for strong nerves.

Thieves and Cops is a card game.
On each round, the players are in charge of a certain character (Cop, Thief, Victim or the Watcher. With just a simple blink of the eye, the Thief, with the help of the Watcher, tries to steal as many Victims as he can, before he gets caught by the Cop. The players are awarded with money cards, depending on the character they have on each round. The first player, who gathers 600 pounds, will be the winner of the game.

The Thieves and Cops: Blink to your Victim has an original gameplay that keeps players across what course of alert. Easy to learn it, zero time setup , fits in your pocket! A game for large groups that guarantees your fun!

The player, who has the Thief card, sees all players discreetly in the eyes and blinks to the one who he believes that is the Victim so that he can steal him. The Victim that has been stolen waits for a while and then he announces that the Thief stole him, reveling his Character card. The Thief continues to steal the Victims, trying to avoid the Cop.
The round ends when:

     The Cop catches the Thief.
     The Cop catches the wrong Thief.
     The Thief steal all the Victims.
     The Thief steals the Cop man.


When something of the above happens, the players that have won, gain Money cards, and a new round begins. All the character cards are gathered again, shuffled and handed out to the players for a new round. The players, who have gained the money cards, put them aside until the end of the game.

Goal of Each Character

Thief – Tries to steal all the Victims, without the Cop finding him or to accidently steal him.

Watcher – Observes all the players during the game, trying to understand who the Cop is. When the thief blinks to the Watcher, he announces to the rest who he believes that the Cop is, trying to help the Thief. If his prediction is right, he will get at the end of the round Money cards. The Watcher is not a Victim.

Cop – He has to find out who the Thief is. When he finds him, he reveals his card and announces the Thief. If he finds him, he gets rewarded with Money cards and all the Victims that have been stolen get compensated with Money cards. If he doesn’t find the Thief and catches a Victim, the Thief wins and the Victim gets compensation.

Victim – When the Thief steals a Victim he waits until the Cop catches the Thief, so he can get compensation, otherwise he won’t get any money cards. When the Cop catches the Victim, instead of the Thief, he will get compensation.

User summary:
隣接のダイヤ (Rinsetsu no Daiya) is 'Diamonds In A Neighborhood' in English.

Diamonds in a Neighborhood is a territory-occupation game for two players, which uses traditional playing cards. Players are assigned one of two sides, Spades or Hearts, which is determined by auction results. They try to score points by taking Diamonds, Clubs, and honor cards. This game is handed out as an added bonus when purchasing a Garden Games product at the Tokyo Game Market Spring 2016.

Publisher summary:
トランプで遊ぶ２人対戦陣取りゲームです。プレイヤーはスペード陣営かハート陣営となりますが、どちらの陣営を担当するかはオークションで決定します。取得されるダイヤ、クラブ、絵札が得点になります。このゲームは2016春のゲームマーケットのガーデンゲームズのおまけとして配られる予定です。
publishers: ガーデンゲームズ (The Garden Games)

description:
Introducing Thunder Bridge, a thrilling game of strategy and survival for all ages. The bridge consists of 20 two-sided tiles, with a normal side and a broken side, which can be placed face up or down during setup.

Players must navigate the crumbling bridge, hindered by broken tiles that slow them down. The bridge is collapsing, adding an element of urgency to the game.

Thunder Bridge offers multiple modes of play:

Free-for-all: A frantic dash to cross the bridge safely.

Cut-throat: Players must decide whether to advance or push opponents back, aiming to be the sole survivor.

Co-operative: Players work together, helping each other to reach the other side safely.

Encounter Mode: A bonus mode that combines Thunder Bridge with The Encounter, where players must escape a pursuing zombie across the bridge.
With its fast-paced action and exciting gameplay, Thunder Bridge is perfect for all ages and occasions.

It's a race to the finish!

You race down the track at top speeds, adrenaline pumping as the roar of the engines fills the air. Your tires skim just inches from your opponents, the tension between victory and disaster palpable with every twist and turn. The art of careful passing becomes crucial—you need precision and nerve. A reckless maneuver could cause a catastrophic accident, setting you back and leaving your opponents in the clear.

Timing your pit stop can be the difference between victory and defeat. A quick pull into the pit offers a tune-up and a powerful speed boost to propel you ahead. However, this comes at a cost—precious seconds lost in the stop can leave you vulnerable to those who risk staying out on the track.

Yet, the race is unforgiving. One wrong move, one miscalculation, and disaster strikes. A collision or spin-out can strip you of your hard-earned progress, forcing you to start from the basics, clawing your way back to contention. Every decision, every fraction of a second, matters in this high-stakes contest where the line between triumph and defeat is razor-thin.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
User summary:
探偵たちの長い夜 (Tantei Tati no Nagai Yoru) in English literally means The Longest Night for Detectives.

In this game, players are detectives staying in a cottage on a snowy mountain, and a murder happens there. Players have to find out the truth of the murder within a limited time. The game is for three players only.

Victory condition
Before the game ends, players must identify the victim, scene, method, and criminal who committed the murder. The first successful player(s) scores one point. Play three games and the highest scorer is the grand champion.

Investigation and deduction
Each turn, you name a person, method, and scene, and see whether other players have those cards or not. Other players place the named cards in front of them face down. You may see the identity of only one of them. Repeat this process until you are sure which cards involved in the murder (not in any player's hand), then record your answer secretly.

Publisher's summary:
◇概要
雪の山荘でおきた殺人事件をゲーム化したものです。
殺された人物と殺した人物が分からないという状況にあります。
プレイヤーは探偵となり、事件の真相を制限時間内に突き止めます。
◇勝利条件
伏せられた4種類、5枚のカードを全て言い当てられたら勝ちです。
3回遊んで1番多くの勝利コインを集めたプレイヤーが優勝します。
（被害者：2枚、殺害場所：1枚、凶器：1枚、犯人：1枚）
◇調査と推理
調査パートで「人物・凶器・場所」を探り、推理パートで披露します。
designers: Kei Tsukahara (つかはら けい)
artists: Kei Tsukahara (つかはら けい)

description: You have joined a sinister cult. You must learn how to greet the senior figures, called the Keepers, and be swift to condemn Heretics.

8 Seconds is a press-your-luck dice and card game with some strategic decisions for 1-6 players.  There are also three optional expansion modules that can be played with the main game individually or together.  8 Seconds is about the action and excitement of bull riding in a rodeo.  Roll your dice and match your moves to the moves of the Bulls.  But beware the rodeo clowns!  There are only two on staff, so if you roll a third Rodeo Clown you’ll have to fill in and you won’t score your Bull!
Features:
 • Fast Paced
• Press-Your-Luck with Strategy
• Player Interaction
• Unique Theme
• No Downtime (w/ Vegas Showdown option)
 Overview:  In 8 Seconds the eight dice have one of five different values on each face: Turn, Buck, Kick, Blank, or Rodeo Clown.  Rodeo Clowns are kept, Blanks are rerolled, and everything else can either be kept or rerolled.  At the start of your turn you’ll draw three Bull cards and then roll the dice.  You must match your dice rolls to the moves indicated on the cards (turns, kicks, and bucks) in order to successfully ride the Bull.  You can reroll dice as needed, but the cost is discard one of your Bull cards, until you are left with just one Bull to ride.  If you successfully ride a Bull you’ll collect that Bull and earn the points indicated.  Foul Tokens can be earned during the game that allow you to reroll clowns, reroll without discarding a bull, add another bull to their lineup, or score bonus points.

8 Seconds goes until one player has successfully ridden three Bulls, and then everyone else gets a final turn.  The winner is the player with the most points in Bulls and Foul Tokens.

Vegas Showdown:  Vegas Showdown is an optional play mode for 8 Seconds that eliminates a common issue with many multiplayer press-your-luck games – what to do when it’s not your turn.  8 Seconds – Vegas Showdown brings the excitement of betting to the game when it is not your turn to ride the bulls, keeping players engaged throughout the entire game.

Solitaire: 8 Seconds also has a solitaire variant that allows the game to be played solo.  This is a quick diversion that can be played within the space of a lap desk or airline tray.

Additional Gameplay Modes:  The Golden Buckle adds Golden Buckle cards, which are available to everyone, until someone acquires each Golden Buckle.  Golden Buckles are challenging to obtain, but give various rewards.  In The Riders, players get to take on the role of Bull Riders.  Each Rider has a special ability that can be used whenever appropriate throughout the game.
This is a set of nine cards for Superfight that was released at PAX South 2016. Each swag bag contained 1 random card.

What started it all is NEW again and then some! Advanced Tobruk, nominated for 'Board Wargame of the Year' is getting its first makeover in over a DOZEN YEARS and things are heating up in the desert ... the war is breathing again outside of Agedabia ... and breathing new life into Erwin Rommel's quest vs. the Desert Rats. Expansions will bring battles ranging from Beda Fomm to El Alamein!
ALL new counters with camo galore to go with desert sand! New maps with the LARGEST HEXES IN THE HISTORY OF THE SYSTEM in a completely scalable game set that can be expanded literally into infiinity. New overlays to go along with the set, new color AFV Cards, wrecks on the back of all vehicles, all in 3/4" size, all scenarios re-done for the new materials and in COLOR > plus new play aids in desert themed colors, folding > ready for ACTION!
Ownership of the ATS Rulebook 2014 and standard system markers is required to play.
This is a VERY LIMITED SPECIAL EDITION ATS MODULE that you can get, here, by ordering as long as this page is up. Each copy contains:
>> 7 sheets of color die-cut counters, all NEW in Olive Drab, new leaders, and more!
>> A Full set of scenarios depicting the Battle of Gazala > new takes on the core scenarios 1-8 with the new boards and overlays!!
Play Aids and AFV Cards in COLOR.
Color folio with charts on inside, a first!
A complete complement of expansions, Gamers Guides coming, just for the new generation!

Description from the publisher:

Some new allies have helped Sheriff Grothe uncover secrets that point to something amiss in the Fourth Ring circus, prompting the Law Dogs to make their move. But is it too little, too late to save Gomorra?

Doomtown: Reloaded – The Curtain Rises is the tenth Saddlebag expansion for Doomtown: Reloaded.

Description from the publisher:

The Thunder Alley: Crew Chief Expansion brings Thunder Alley to a new level of play by enabling players to set Crew Chief strategies in each segment of the game.

This expansion introduces optional rules, star drivers, season play, Crew Chief Cards and Track Cards all allowing players more control and more strategy in each game.

Call the right strategies and you may find yourself in Victory Lane.

The Thunder Alley: Crew Chief Expansion consists of eight 12-card decks of Crew Chief Cards (one for each player), 4 Lap Cards to extend the length of any race, 24 Track Cards with special rules and special scoring to make each race a new experience and a rule book with numerous modules to allow for variations in your play.
family: Series: Thunder Alley/Grand Prix engine (GMT Games)

description:
Description from the publisher:

What do halflings, cat folk, clerics, and dark knights have in common? Well, nothing aside from the fact they're about to enter the arena and help you achieve mastery over your opponents in battle. Each of the new decks in Epic PVP: Fantasy Expansion 2 is playable on its own, but is also compatible with those in Epic PVP: Fantasy and Epic PVP: Fantasy Expansion 1, allowing you to create unique and powerful combinations for your battles.

This expansion contains two 20-card character decks and two 20-card race decks as well as a board for each deck.

Minifig Melee is a miniature-based board game with fun set-up, fast game-play, and awesome miniatures.

In Minifig Melee, players lead teams of minifig heroes in an epic battle, facing off against the other players and the various dangers & monsters controlled by the game.

Players attempt to gain victory points by gathering treasures & items as well as defeating the other player's heroes while navigating obstacles and completing missions.

Players build teams, manage actions, roll dice, and execute on strategies & tactics.

22ra (translated “to 22” points) is a card-driven game that simulates basque hand-pelota  games between 2 players, or 4 players when playing doubles. The objective is to score 22 points by playing cards, so that the opponent cannot or does not want to reach the ball or makes a foul when hitting the ball.

The game consists of three separate decks of cards: 

         The Player Trait’s cards, consisting of 
             Movement cards (Mugimendu) 
             Force cards (Indar), and
             Skill cards (Abilezia)
        
         The Serve Deck (Sake), with 
             Normal serve cards (Arrunta) and
             Risky serve cards (Ausarta) 
        
         The Hit Deck, which consists of three Subdecks: 
             The Bounce Back cards (Botez Atzera), the ball is hit after it bounces
             The Defence Volley cards (Airez Defentsa), the ball is hit before it bounces
             The Good-play cards (Jokaldia), with different special hits and plays. 
        
    


The board represents the fronton, divided in 7 squares (A1, A2, B1, B2, D, F and R), contains also a scoring track and 5 Timeout spaces for each player (Atsedenaldiak).

Each player chooses seven Player Trait cards, at least 4 Movement cards. Then each player is dealt 4 Good-play cards. A start player is chosen randomly (the server in the first point) and the game can begin: the server chooses a Serve card. If the serve is good, the receiver must hit it back either after the ball bounces (from the same square where the ball is, using a Bounce Back card) or before the ball bounces (from the square directly in front of the ball, using a Defence Volley card). Play continues until one player fails to hit the ball (cannot/does not want to hit or hits a ball badly), therefore losing the point (Falta) or the other player makes a great hit, directly winning the point (Tanto). After each point, the players recover a Player Trait card and replenish their hand of Good-play cards (up to 4 cards). The first player reaching 22 points wins immediately the game.

In double games, the teams are formed by a Forward and a Defender, and the games are also played to 22 points. The rules are the same, except that each player chooses 4 Player Trait cards (at least 2 Movement cards) and that they can exchange the Good-play cards before the start of each point.
